result:
# ct-crypto API Reference for Wallet-Lib Reviewers

## 0. Package topology

Wallet-lib never imports `@hathor/ct-crypto-node` from `src/` at runtime. The runtime dependency is **`@hathor/ct-crypto-provider`** (interface + abstract class), and a provider *instance* is injected by the consumer via `wallet.setShieldedCryptoProvider(...)` (/tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/new/wallet.ts:3758 → src/storage/storage.ts:180). The Node binding is an `optionalDependencies` entry and a devDependency used only by tests (`__tests__/integration/helpers/wallet.helper.ts:12` imports `createDefaultShieldedCryptoProvider` from `@hathor/ct-crypto-node/provider`).

## 1. Version pins — MISMATCHES FOUND

| Where | Pin | Installed |
|---|---|---|
| /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/package.json:17 `dependencies` | `@hathor/ct-crypto-provider: 0.0.1-shielded` | 0.0.1-shielded ✓ |
| /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/package.json:30 `optionalDependencies` | `@hathor/ct-crypto-node: 0.4.0` | **0.0.1-shielded** ✗ |
| /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/package.json:51 `devDependencies` | `@hathor/ct-crypto-node: 0.0.1-shielded` | 0.0.1-shielded |

Flags:
1. **`@hathor/ct-crypto-node` is pinned twice with conflicting versions** — `0.4.0` in optionalDependencies vs `0.0.1-shielded` in devDependencies. npm resolution will favor one nondeterministically depending on install order; this should be a single exact pin (project rule: exact versions everywhere).
2. The installed `node_modules/@hathor/ct-crypto-node/package.json` says `0.0.1-shielded` while the sibling repo checkout (`/Users/pedroferreira/Hathor/hathor-ct-crypto-node/package.json`) says `0.4.0`. The `index.d.ts` of both is byte-identical (verified by diff), so the TS surface matches, but the installed *prebuilt binary* is stale relative to the Rust source of truth (see §3, `verifyBalance`).

## 2. API surface actually called (provider interface)

From `@hathor/ct-crypto-provider` (src/types.ts; all methods `Promise<T>`, all byte params/returns are Node `Buffer`, all values `bigint`):

| Method (param order) | Returns | Wallet-lib call sites |
|---|---|---|
| `generateRandomBlindingFactor()` | `Buffer` 32B scalar (non-zero, from Rust CSPRNG) | creation.ts:91,171,187,188 |
| `createAmountShieldedOutput(value: bigint, recipientPubkey: 33B, tokenUid: 32B, valueBlindingFactor: 32B)` | `{ephemeralPubkey:33B, commitment:33B, rangeProof:~3213B, blindingFactor:32B}` (`assetCommitment`/`assetBlindingFactor` undefined) | creation.ts:83,92 |
| `createShieldedOutputWithBothBlindings(value, recipientPubkey, tokenUid, valueBlindingFactor, assetBlindingFactor)` | same + `assetCommitment:33B`, `assetBlindingFactor:32B` (NAPI returns `null`→abstract class normalizes to `undefined`, abstract.ts:294-304) | creation.ts:178,189 |
| `rewindAmountShieldedOutput(privateKey:32B, ephemeralPubkey:33B, commitment:33B, rangeProof, tokenUid:32B)` | `{value: bigint, blindingFactor: Buffer}` — **throws** if not ours / wrong nonce | processing.ts:189 |
| `rewindFullShieldedOutput(privateKey, ephemeralPubkey, commitment, rangeProof, assetCommitment:33B)` | `{value, blindingFactor, tokenUid: hex string, assetBlindingFactor}` — note **`tokenUid` is a hex string**, converted from bytes at abstract.ts:156; NAPI itself returns Buffer | processing.ts:144 |
| `computeBalancingBlindingFactor(value: bigint, generatorBlindingFactor: 32B, inputs: IBlindingEntry[], otherOutputs: IBlindingEntry[])` | `Buffer` 32B | creation.ts:77,172; sendTransaction.ts:571 (unshield excess); utils/transaction.ts:1413 |
| `deriveTag(tokenUid: 32B)` | 32B raw Tag = SHA256("Hathor_AssetTag_v1" ‖ uid) | creation.ts:139,211; processing.ts:164 |
| `deriveAssetTag(tokenUid: 32B)` | 33B unblinded generator | (via `openAmountShieldedCommitment` composition) |
| `createCommitment(value, blindingFactor: 32B, generator: 33B)` | 33B Pedersen commitment | (composition) |
| `createAssetCommitment(tag: 32B, blindingFactor: 32B)` | 33B blinded generator | creation.ts:141; processing.ts:165 |
| `createSurjectionProof(codomainTag: 32B, codomainBlindingFactor: 32B, domain: {generator:33B, tag:32B, blindingFactor:32B}[])` | proof Buffer | creation.ts:213 |
| `deriveEcdhSharedSecret(privateKey: 32B, peerPubkey: 33B)` | 32B = SHA256(version ‖ x) per libsecp256k1 default | (scan optimization surface) |
| `openAmountShieldedCommitment(value, vbf, tokenUid)` / `openFullShieldedCommitment(value, vbf, tokenUid, abf)` | 33B / `{valueCommitment, assetCommitment}` | composed in abstract class (abstract.ts:234-260) from `deriveAssetTag`/`deriveTag`+`createAssetCommitment`+`createCommitment` — these have **no dedicated NAPI export** |

`IBlindingEntry` is long-form: `{value: bigint, valueBlindingFactor: Buffer, generatorBlindingFactor: Buffer}` — matches NAPI's `BlindingEntry` (napi_bindings.rs:420-425) field-for-field, no translation (provider.js:99-103).

Functions exported by the NAPI package but **NOT in the provider interface** (verifier/advanced surface, unused by wallet-lib src — verified by grep): `htrAssetTag`, `createTrivialCommitment`, `verifyCommitmentsSum`, `createRangeProof`, `verifyRangeProof`, `rewindRangeProof`, `validateCommitment`, `validateGenerator`, `verifySurjectionProof`, `verifyBalance`, `generateEphemeralKeypair`, `deriveRewindNonce`, `getCommitmentSize`, `getGeneratorSize`, `getZeroTweak`, `loadNative`.

## 3. Divergence: installed .d.ts vs Rust source of truth

**`verifyBalance` signature lag.** Installed/repo `index.d.ts:50-53` declares 4 params `(transparentInputs, shieldedInputs, transparentOutputs, shieldedOutputs)`. Rust source of truth (napi_bindings.rs:303-309) takes a **5th param `excess_blinding_factor: Option<Buffer>`** and enforces structural invariants (excess ⊕ shielded_outputs mutually exclusive; excess requires ≥1 shielded input, napi_bindings.rs:355-370). The installed prebuilt binary does not contain the excess-related error strings (checked darwin-arm64 prebuild), i.e. it was built from pre-excess Rust. **Impact on wallet-lib: none directly** — wallet-lib never calls `verifyBalance`; it produces the excess scalar via `computeBalancingBlindingFactor(0n-value?, …)` at sendTransaction.ts:571 / utils/transaction.ts:1413 and ships it in the unshield header for the fullnode to verify. But any consumer using the package for verification cannot verify unshield txs with the installed binary.

No other signature divergences: every other NAPI function matches the .d.ts in name (snake→camelCase via napi-derive), parameter order, and shape, including `bigint` (napi `BigInt`, lossless u64 conversion, napi_bindings.rs:13-22) and `Buffer` in/out everywhere. `NodeShieldedProvider._encodeBytes/_decodeBytes` are identity (provider.js:34-39).

## 4. Validation: Rust-side vs caller-must-pre-validate

**Rust validates (caller may pass untrusted bytes; gets a JS throw, not UB):**
- All 32-byte lengths: tweaks/blinding factors (`parse_tweak`, napi_bindings.rs:24-29), secret keys (:31-36), token UIDs (explicit checks in each fn), shared secrets (:469).
- All 33-byte curve objects with point validity: generators (`parse_generator` → `Generator::from_slice`, :38-43), commitments (`deserialize_commitment`, pedersen.rs:43-52), pubkeys (`parse_public_key`, ecdh.rs:316-323 — length + on-curve).
- Scalar range: `Tweak::from_slice` / `SecretKey::from_slice` reject ≥ curve order (and SecretKey rejects zero).
- `bigint` values: rejects negative and > u64::MAX (napi_bindings.rs:13-22; tested down to the 2^53 boundary, :696-708).
- **Rewind anti-forgery (FullShielded)**: `rewind_full_shielded_output` itself recomputes the asset commitment from the recovered `(token_uid, abf)` embedded in the 64-byte proof message and errors on mismatch (ecdh.rs:269-281). Wallet-lib repeats this cross-check in JS (processing.ts:163-186) — defense in depth, consistent with the client guide §4.3.
- `verify_range_proof` rejects zero-amount proofs (`range.start < 1`) at both the Rust layer (rangeproof.rs:96-101) and the NAPI wrapper (napi_bindings.rs:176-180).
- `create_surjection_proof` rejects empty domain (surjection.rs:17-21).

**Caller responsibilities (NOT checked by Rust):**
- **Value range for proofs**: range proofs are fixed 40-bit, min_value=1 (rangeproof.rs:16,44-53). `create*ShieldedOutput`/`createRangeProof` with value `0` or ≥ 2^40 fails inside `RangeProof::new` (throws), so values must be in **[1, 2^40)** — wallet-lib must enforce this before building outputs; the binding accepts any u64.
- **Identity-commitment panic hazard**: `createCommitment(0n, ZERO_TWEAK, gen)` produces the identity point; libsecp256k1 refuses to serialize it and `PedersenCommitment::new` **panics** (documented at balance.rs:93-99 where the excess path explicitly skips zero tweaks). The NAPI `create_commitment` has no guard. Callers of `openAmountShieldedCommitment`/`openFullShieldedCommitment` must never pass value=0 with a zero bf.
- **Blinding-factor zero semantics**: `ZERO_TWEAK` (32 zero bytes; wallet-lib's own constant at /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/constants.ts:157 matches Rust `ZERO_TWEAK`) is a *valid* `Tweak` (unlike SecretKey). Wallet-lib correctly uses it as the `generatorBlindingFactor` for AmountShielded entries (creation.ts:79,105) and for transparent inputs in the surjection domain (creation.ts:140).
- **Balance-equation bookkeeping**: `computeBalancingBlindingFactor` is pure math (`compute_adaptive_blinding_factor`, balance.rs:133-158) — it does not know which entries are inputs vs outputs or whether the lists are complete. Wallet-lib must pass *all* inputs and *all other* outputs with correct long-form bfs; an omission yields an unbalanced tx rejected by the fullnode, not a local error.
- **Token UID encoding**: raw 32 bytes (hex-decoded), no endianness transform; HTR is 32 zero bytes. Wallet-lib converts `NATIVE_TOKEN_UID` → `NATIVE_TOKEN_UID_HEX` before `Buffer.from(..., 'hex')` (creation.ts:135-138).
- **Rewind "not mine" handling**: both rewinds **throw** on foreign outputs (wrong ECDH nonce) rather than returning null/false — wallet-lib correctly wraps in try/catch (processing.ts:133+). By contrast `verify*` functions return `boolean` for proof failure but **throw** for malformed inputs (bad lengths/points), and `validateCommitment`/`validateGenerator` are pure boolean (never throw, length-checked, napi_bindings.rs:216-230).

## 5. Throw-vs-boolean summary

| Pattern | Functions |
|---|---|
| Throw on any failure | all `create*`, `rewind*`, `derive*`, `computeBalancingBlindingFactor` |
| Boolean result; throw only on malformed input bytes | `verifyRangeProof`, `verifySurjectionProof`, `verifyCommitmentsSum`, `verifyBalance` (balance mismatch → `false`; structural excess violations → throw) |
| Pure boolean, never throw | `validateCommitment`, `validateGenerator` |
| Infallible | `generateRandomBlindingFactor`, `generateEphemeralKeypair`, `htrAssetTag`, `getZeroTweak`, `getCommitmentSize`(=33), `getGeneratorSize`(=33) |

## 6. Wallet-lib call-correctness spot-check

All call sites pass arguments in the declared order and types: `rewindFullShieldedOutput(privkey, ephPk, commitment, rangeProof, assetCommitment)` (processing.ts:144-150), `rewindAmountShieldedOutput(..., Buffer.from(tokenUid,'hex'))` (processing.ts:189-196), `createSurjectionProof(codomainTag, abf, domain)` with the codomain *output's* abf — matching Rust's `codomain_blinding_factor` (creation.ts:211-216 vs surjection.rs:12-16). Last-output balancing uses `ZERO_TWEAK` as gbf for AmountShielded (creation.ts:77-82, correct: token visible ⇒ unblinded generator) and a fresh random abf as gbf for FullShielded (creation.ts:171-177, correct per napi_bindings.rs:487-489 comment). The hex-string `tokenUid` from `rewindFullShieldedOutput` is consumed as hex and re-buffered only where bytes are needed (processing.ts:157-164) — consistent with the abstract-class boundary contract (abstract.ts:154-156).

# Shielded Wire-Format Reference — hathor-core `experimental/shielded-outputs-alpha-v4`

Canonical serialization lives in the vendored **hathorlib** package; `hathor/transaction/shielded_tx_output.py` is a re-export shim (`/tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor/transaction/shielded_tx_output.py:27-43`). All integer fields are **big-endian** (`struct '!...'`).

## 1. ShieldedTxOutput

Source of truth: `/tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathorlib/hathorlib/transaction/shielded_tx_output.py`

### Constants (lines 21-27)
| Constant | Value |
|---|---|
| `COMMITMENT_SIZE` | 33 |
| `ASSET_COMMITMENT_SIZE` | 33 |
| `EPHEMERAL_PUBKEY_SIZE` | 33 (compressed secp256k1) |
| `MAX_RANGE_PROOF_SIZE` | 3328 |
| `MAX_SURJECTION_PROOF_SIZE` | 4096 |
| `MAX_SHIELDED_OUTPUTS` | 32 |
| `MAX_SHIELDED_OUTPUT_SCRIPT_SIZE` | 1024 |

### Mode discriminator (lines 30-34)
`OutputMode` IntEnum: `TRANSPARENT = 0`, `AMOUNT_ONLY = 1`, `FULLY_SHIELDED = 2`. The leading mode byte is the **only** discriminator between AmountShielded and FullShielded on the wire; `0` or any other value inside a shielded output raises (`deserialize`, line 216). JSON consumers are explicitly told to rely on `mode`, not field presence (`hathor/transaction/base_transaction.py:85-90`).

### Byte layout — `serialize_shielded_output` (lines 80-106)
```
mode(1, !B)
commitment(33)
rp_len(2, !H) | range_proof(rp_len)
script_len(2, !H) | script(script_len)
[mode==1 AMOUNT_ONLY]   token_data(1, !B)
[mode==2 FULLY_SHIELDED] asset_commitment(33) | sp_len(2, !H) | surjection_proof(sp_len)
ephemeral_pubkey(33)    -- ALWAYS present; 33 zero bytes if absent (line 104)
```
Deserializer (lines 109-218) enforces: `rp_len <= 3328` (line 130), `script_len <= 1024` (line 143), exact-length reads for every field (truncation errors), and decodes 33 zero bytes of ephemeral_pubkey back to "absent" (`b''`, lines 166, 205). It returns `(output, remaining)` — outputs are concatenated back-to-back with **no per-output length prefix**.

### token_data semantics (AmountShielded only)
- One byte, same convention as transparent `TxOutput.token_data`: low 7 bits = index into `tx.tokens` (0 = HTR), bit 0x80 = authority mask.
- Token index resolved via `token_data & 0x7F` (`hathor/transaction/shielded_tx_output.py:77`; `hathor/verification/transaction_verifier.py:808`); must satisfy `index <= len(tx.tokens)` (`transaction_verifier.py:808-812`).
- **Authority bit forbidden**: `token_data & TOKEN_AUTHORITY_MASK` raises `ShieldedAuthorityError` (`transaction_verifier.py:1203-1215`). FullShieldedOutput has no token_data, so no authority risk (line 1215).
- FullShieldedOutput carries `asset_commitment` (33B blinded asset tag) instead of token_data; token UID + 32B asset blinding factor are recovered from the first 64 bytes of the rewound range-proof message (`hathor/transaction/shielded_tx_output.py:92-99`: `message[:32]` = token_uid, `message[32:64]` = asset_bf).

### Per-output sighash — `get_sighash_bytes` (lines 221-242)
```
mode(1) | commitment(33)
| [AMOUNT_ONLY] token_data(1)  /  [FULLY_SHIELDED] asset_commitment(33)
| script(raw, NO length prefix)
| ephemeral_pubkey(33, zero-filled if absent)
```
Note the field order differs from the wire format (token_data/asset_commitment come **before** script in sighash). Proofs (range_proof, surjection_proof) are **excluded**; ephemeral_pubkey is always included to prevent strip-malleability (lines 238-240).

## 2. Header IDs and tx-level placement

`VertexHeaderId` (`hathorlib/hathorlib/headers/types.py:19-25`):
`NANO=0x10`, `FEE=0x11`, **`SHIELDED_OUTPUTS=0x12`**, **`UNSHIELD_BALANCE=0x13`**, **`MINT=0x14`**, **`MELT=0x15`**.

Tx struct: `funds | graph | nonce | headers` — headers appended after nonce, concatenated with no count prefix (`hathorlib/hathorlib/base_transaction.py:355-363`; `hathor/transaction/vertex_parser/vertex_serializer.py:42-47,114-117`). Headers are covered by the tx hash via `get_graph_and_headers_hash` (`hathorlib/base_transaction.py:387-396`).

Verification rules (`hathor/verification/vertex_verifier.py`):
- Max headers per vertex = **5** when shielded enabled, else 3 (`hathor/transaction/base_transaction.py:277-287`).
- One instance per header type (lines 285-296); headers must be in **strictly ascending VertexHeaderId order** (lines 298-305).
- Allowed on `REGULAR_TRANSACTION` and `TOKEN_CREATION_TRANSACTION` only: ShieldedOutputsHeader, UnshieldBalanceHeader, MintHeader, MeltHeader, gated on the shielded feature (`vertex_verifier.py:246-269`).

### ShieldedOutputsHeader (`hathorlib/hathorlib/headers/shielded_outputs_header.py`)
Wire: `header_id 0x12 (1) | num_outputs(1) | serialized outputs...` (serialize lines 84-93). Deserialize bounds: `1 <= num_outputs <= 32` (lines 61-66); requires the vertex be a `Transaction` (lines 47-50). Sighash: `0x12 | count(1) | per-output get_sighash_bytes` (lines 95-104) — i.e., header id and count **are** signed, proofs are not.

### UnshieldBalanceHeader (`hathorlib/hathorlib/headers/unshield_balance_header.py`)
Wire: `header_id 0x13 (1) | excess_blinding_factor(32)` — fixed 33 bytes (lines 60-86, `EXCESS_BLINDING_FACTOR_SIZE = 32`, line 28). Sighash = full serialization (lines 88-91). Semantics: `excess = sum(r_in) − sum(r_out)` for full-unshield txs; **mutually exclusive** with ShieldedOutputsHeader (docstring lines 33-46). Verifier invariants (`transaction_verifier.py:1020-1045`): (1) cannot coexist with ShieldedOutputsHeader *presence* (not list contents); (2) shielded inputs + no shielded outputs ⇒ header required; (3) header requires ≥1 shielded input.

## 3. Mint/Melt headers (`hathorlib/hathorlib/headers/mint_melt_header.py`)

- `MintHeader` id `0x14`, `MeltHeader` id `0x15` (lines 188, 196); identical wire format.
- Wire: `header_id(1) | num_entries(1) | entries...`; entry = `token_index(1) | amount(8, !Q BE)` (`ENTRY_SIZE = 9`, line 32; serialize lines 61-68).
- Bounds (lines 31, 53-58, 81-120): `MAX_MINT_MELT_ENTRIES = 16`; `1 <= num_entries <= 16`; `1 <= token_index <= 16` (token_index 0 = HTR is forbidden, line 104-107); `1 <= amount < 2**64` (strictly positive — this is what lets the header skip a range proof, docstring lines 43-46); duplicate token_index within a header rejected (lines 116-119). Cross-header bound `token_index <= len(tx.tokens)` is enforced in the verifier, not at parse time (lines 75-78).
- Sighash = full serialization (lines 178-181).
- Rule M1: mint/melt headers require a ShieldedOutputsHeader or UnshieldBalanceHeader on the same tx (`transaction_verifier.py:672-691`). Rule M2: each entry requires a matching transparent authority input (lines 1144-1177; shielded inputs can never be authorities). On a TCT, a MintHeader entry with `token_index == 1` (the newly created token) needs no pre-existing authority (lines 1195-1200).

## 4. JSON / API / event representation

`_shielded_output_to_json` (`hathor/transaction/base_transaction.py:82-113`) — used by `to_json` and resources:
```json
{
  "type": "shielded",
  "mode": <int 1|2>,
  "commitment": "<hex>",
  "range_proof": "<base64>",
  "script": "<base64>",
  "ephemeral_pubkey": "<hex>",        // omitted when absent
  "token_data": <int>,                 // mode 1 only
  "asset_commitment": "<hex>",         // mode 2 only
  "surjection_proof": "<base64>",      // mode 2 only
  "decoded": {"address": "..."}        // only when decode_script and script parses
}
```
Mixed encodings: commitment/ephemeral_pubkey/asset_commitment are **hex**; range_proof/script/surjection_proof are **base64**.

- `BaseTransaction.to_json` emits a top-level `shielded_outputs` array, **separate from `outputs`**, only when non-empty (`base_transaction.py:815-819`).
- **Input indexing**: shielded outputs are addressed after transparent ones — input index `i` refers to shielded output `i - len(outputs)`; `is_shielded_output(index)` ⇔ `len(outputs) <= index < len(outputs)+len(shielded_outputs)` (`base_transaction.py:348-363`). Spent shielded inputs are rendered with the same `type:'shielded'` shape inline in `inputs[].spent_output`-style JSON (`base_transaction.py:864-869`; `hathor/transaction/resources/transaction.py:97-101`).
- Event-queue WS API: pydantic `ShieldedTxOutput` model mirrors the JSON shape and is a smart-union member of `TxInput.spent_output`; `TxData.shielded_outputs: list[ShieldedTxOutput] = []` was added so the event stream (consumed by wallet-service) carries shielded receives (`hathor/event/model/event_data.py:46-69, 160-172`).

## 5. Addresses, keys, recovery conventions

**Core defines NO shielded address format.** Grep over `hathor/` and `hathorlib/` finds no shielded version byte, no scan/spend pubkey encoding (only `P2PKH_VERSION_BYTE=0x28`, `MULTISIG_VERSION_BYTE=0x64`, `hathor/conf/mainnet.py:23-24`). The RFC sketches `scan_pubkey(33) || hash(spend_pubkey)(20)` (rfc-summary.md:11), while wallet-lib implements `Base58(version(1) || scan_pubkey(33) || spend_pubkey(33) || checksum(4))` with version bytes `0x3c` mainnet / `0x5d` testnet+privatenet (`/tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/utils/shieldedAddress.ts:28,40-56`; `src/models/network.ts:13-31`). **This is a wallet-side convention with no consensus anchor and diverges from the RFC sketch (full 33B spend pubkey vs 20B hash)** — reviewers should treat consensus as agnostic here: on-chain, only the `script` (a standard P2PKH/P2SH script parsed by `parse_address_script`, `base_transaction.py:509-510`) and the 33B `ephemeral_pubkey` matter.

Recovery conventions the wallet must match (`hathor/crypto/shielded/ecdh.py`):
- ECDH shared secret = libsecp256k1 standard `SHA256(version_byte || x)` of `priv * EphemeralPub` (lines 34-49).
- Rewind nonce = `SHA256("Hathor_CT_nonce_v1" || shared_secret)` (lines 52-65).
- Asset generators: `derive_asset_tag(token_uid)` with token UID normalized to 32 bytes — HTR's 1-byte `\x00` UID is right-padded with zeros to 32 bytes (`hathor/crypto/shielded/asset_tag.py:8-20,33-37`).
- Recovery path (`hathor/transaction/shielded_tx_output.py:49-107`): AmountShielded rewinds against `derive_asset_tag(tx.get_token_uid(token_data & 0x7F))`; FullShielded rewinds against the output's own `asset_commitment` and parses token_uid/asset_bf from the 64-byte message prefix.

## 6. Other consensus limits relevant to wallet construction

- Range proofs: Borromean, 40-bit (≈3213 B typical; max 3328) (`shielded_tx_output.py:24,41`).
- ≥2 shielded outputs always required (trivial-commitment protection, conservative storage-less rule) (`transaction_verifier.py:1219-1227`).
- FullShieldedOutput requires ≥1 input to form the surjection domain, and a non-empty surjection_proof (`transaction_verifier.py:899-910`); mint-header asset tags are added to the surjection domain (lines 895-897).
- ephemeral_pubkey, when non-zero, must be a valid secp256k1 point (`transaction_verifier.py:795-798`).
- Fees: `FEE_PER_AMOUNT_SHIELDED_OUTPUT = 1`, `FEE_PER_FULL_SHIELDED_OUTPUT = 2` (`hathor/conf/settings.py:36-37`; enforcement in `transaction_verifier.py:568`).
- Tx sighash overall layout (`hathor/transaction/vertex_parser/_transaction.py:64-86`): `signal_bits|version|len(tokens)|len(inputs)|len(outputs)` as `!BBBBB`, then token UIDs, inputs (data cleared), transparent outputs, then each header's sighash bytes in canonical header order (`vertex_serializer.py:138-170`).

# hathor-core Shielded Outputs: Consensus/Validation Rule Inventory

Source of truth: `/tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded` (branch `experimental/shielded-outputs-alpha-v4`). All paths relative to that root unless absolute.

## 0. Dispatch architecture (where rules run)

- **Feature gate (earliest check):** any tx with shielded outputs is rejected with `InvalidShieldedOutputError('shielded transactions are not enabled')` if `params.features.shielded_transactions` is false; Mint/Melt headers similarly rejected with `HeaderNotSupported` — `hathor/verification/verification_service.py:115-124`. Feature comes from `Feature.SHIELDED_TRANSACTIONS` activation (`hathor/feature_activation/feature.py:42`, `hathor/feature_activation/utils.py:81`) plus node setting `ENABLE_SHIELDED_TRANSACTIONS: FeatureSetting = DISABLED` (`hathor/conf/settings.py:40`). Node refuses to start with shielded enabled but no native crypto lib: `validate_shielded_crypto_available`, `hathor/crypto/shielded/__init__.py:41-53`.
- **Basic (storage-free) phase:** `verify_basic` → `_verify_basic_shielded_header` → `TransactionVerifier.verify_shielded_outputs` (`verification_service.py:154-171`); `_verify_basic_mint_melt_header` → `verify_mint_melt_basic` (`verification_service.py:159-184`). `RuntimeError` from the crypto lib is converted to `InvalidShieldedOutputError` (`verification_service.py:170-171, 272-273`).
- **Full (storage) phase:** `verify` → `_verify_shielded_header` → `verify_shielded_outputs_with_storage` (surjection only) (`verification_service.py:253-273`); balance is dispatched in `_verify_tx`: if `tx.is_shielded()` then `verify_no_undeclared_mint_melt` + `verify_mint_melt_authority_inputs` + `verify_token_rules(shielded_fee=...)` + `verify_shielded_balance`, else `verify_transparent_balance` — exactly one balance check per tx (`verification_service.py:333-352`).
- `is_shielded()` = has shielded outputs OR any input index ≥ `len(spent_tx.outputs)` (`hathor/transaction/transaction.py:182-193`).

## 1. Wire-format / deserialize-time rules (hard rejections)

`hathorlib/hathorlib/transaction/shielded_tx_output.py`:
- Constants: `COMMITMENT_SIZE=33`, `ASSET_COMMITMENT_SIZE=33`, `EPHEMERAL_PUBKEY_SIZE=33`, `MAX_RANGE_PROOF_SIZE=3328`, `MAX_SURJECTION_PROOF_SIZE=4096`, `MAX_SHIELDED_OUTPUTS=32`, `MAX_SHIELDED_OUTPUT_SCRIPT_SIZE=1024` (lines 21-27).
- Output format `mode(1)|commitment(33)|rp_len(2)|range_proof|script_len(2)|script|[token_data(1) | asset_commitment(33)+sp_len(2)+surjection_proof]|ephemeral_pubkey(33)` (lines 80-218). `ValueError` on: range proof > 3328 (130-133), script > 1024 (143-146), surjection proof > 4096 (186-189), truncation anywhere, unknown mode byte (216). Ephemeral pubkey is always 33 bytes on the wire; all-zeros means "absent" (104, 158-166, 197-205).
- Sighash for shielded outputs covers mode + commitment + token_data/asset_commitment + script + ephemeral_pubkey but **NOT** the proofs (proofs are malleable without invalidating signatures) (221-242).

`hathor/transaction/headers/shielded_outputs_header.py`:
- Header format `header_id(1)|num_outputs(1)|outputs…`; `num_outputs >= 1` required (lines 80-81), `num_outputs <= MAX_SHIELDED_OUTPUTS (32)` (82-85); malformed → `InvalidShieldedOutputError` (93-96). Header sighash includes per-output sighash bytes (114-123).

`hathor/transaction/headers/unshield_balance_header.py`:
- `EXCESS_BLINDING_FACTOR_SIZE = 32` (line 29); format `header_id(1)|excess(32)`; length check at construction (56-62) and deserialize (81-85). Full serialization is in sighash (106-109).

`hathorlib/hathorlib/headers/mint_melt_header.py` (re-exported by `hathor/transaction/headers/mint_melt_header.py`, which converts `ValueError` → `InvalidMintMeltHeaderError`, lines 107-112):
- `MAX_MINT_MELT_ENTRIES = 16`, `ENTRY_SIZE = 9` (token_index 1B + amount 8B BE) (lines 31-32). Per entry: `1 <= token_index <= 16` (token_index 0 = HTR forbidden, lines 104-111), `1 <= amount < 2**64` (53-58, 112-115), no duplicate token_index within a header (116-119), `1 <= num_entries <= 16` (82-87). The strict positive amount bound is what lets headers skip a range proof (docstring 43-47). Full serialization in sighash (178-181).

## 2. Header acceptance rules (`hathor/verification/vertex_verifier.py`)

- `ShieldedOutputsHeader`, `UnshieldBalanceHeader`, `MintHeader`, `MeltHeader` are allowed on `REGULAR_TRANSACTION` and `TOKEN_CREATION_TRANSACTION` only, and only when `params.features.shielded_transactions` is true (`vertex_verifier.py:247-270`). Not allowed on blocks or OnChainBlueprint.
- At most **one instance per header type** (`HeaderNotSupported`, 285-296); headers must be in **canonical ascending VertexHeaderId order** (298-305).
- Max headers per vertex: **5** when shielded enabled (FeeHeader + ShieldedOutputs|UnshieldBalance + Mint + Melt + Nano margin), else 3 — `TooManyHeaders` (`hathor/transaction/base_transaction.py:277-287`, checked at `vertex_verifier.py:282-283`).

## 3. Basic shielded checks — `verify_shielded_outputs` (`hathor/verification/transaction_verifier.py:613-619`)

1. **Point validation** — `verify_commitments_valid` (750-798): count ≤ `MAX_SHIELDED_OUTPUTS=32` (762-765, redundant with deserialize); each `commitment` exactly 33 bytes and a valid Pedersen commitment curve point via `validate_commitment` (767-775); each FullShieldedOutput `asset_commitment` 33 bytes and a valid generator point via `validate_generator` (776-785); if `ephemeral_pubkey` present, 33 bytes and a valid compressed secp256k1 point (787-798). Error: `InvalidShieldedOutputError`.
2. **Authority restriction** — `verify_authority_restriction` (1204-1217): an `AmountShieldedOutput` with `token_data & TOKEN_AUTHORITY_MASK` set → `ShieldedAuthorityError` ("authority outputs cannot be shielded"). FullShieldedOutput has no token_data, so no risk.
3. **Range proofs** — `verify_range_proofs` (800-827): for AmountShieldedOutput, `token_data & 0x7F` must be ≤ `len(tx.tokens)` (809-813) and the generator is the derived NUMS asset tag for the token UID; for FullShieldedOutput the generator is the asset_commitment. Each output's Borromean range proof must verify against (proof, commitment, generator) → `InvalidRangeProofError`. Crypto detail: proofs are fixed at **40 bits** (`RANGE_PROOF_BITS=40`, values < 2^40 ≈ 1.1e12; min_bits pinned to avoid a size side-channel) — `hathor-ct-crypto/src/rangeproof.rs:12-15,52`.
4. **Trivial-commitment / minimum-output rule** — `verify_trivial_commitment_protection` (1219-1227): if the tx has any shielded outputs, it must have **≥ 2** of them; otherwise `TrivialCommitmentError('at least 2 shielded outputs are required to prevent trivial commitment matching')`. Comment: "Without storage, conservatively require >= 2 shielded outputs always" (1220). Combined with header deserialize (≥1), the effective rule is: 0 or ≥2 shielded outputs, never exactly 1.
5. **Shielded fee** — `verify_shielded_fee` (568-579): any tx with shielded outputs **must carry a FeeHeader** (`InvalidShieldedOutputError('shielded transactions require a fee header')`); declared total fee must be ≥ `calculate_shielded_fee` = `FEE_PER_AMOUNT_SHIELDED_OUTPUT (=1)` per AmountShieldedOutput + `FEE_PER_FULL_SHIELDED_OUTPUT (=2)` per FullShieldedOutput (`transaction_verifier.py:556-566`; constants `hathor/conf/settings.py:36-37`). Note `>=`: overpaying is allowed; fee exactness for the *token side* is checked separately (see §5).

## 4. Mint/Melt basic checks — `verify_mint_melt_basic` (`transaction_verifier.py:621-727`)

- **Well-formedness + Rule M3** (`verify_mint_melt_headers_well_formed`, 634-670): each entry's `token_index <= len(tx.tokens)` → `InvalidMintMeltHeaderError`; a token may not appear in both MintHeader and MeltHeader (Rule M3, 664-670).
- **Rule M1** (`verify_mint_melt_requires_shielded`, 672-691): Mint/Melt headers are valid only on shielded txs, detected via presence of `ShieldedOutputsHeader` or `UnshieldBalanceHeader` → `ShieldedMintMeltForbiddenError`.
- **Nano compatibility** (`verify_mint_melt_nano_compatibility`, 693-727): a single token cannot be minted/melted via both a NanoHeader action and a Mint/Melt header in the same tx → `InvalidMintMeltHeaderError` (cross-token combinations allowed).

## 5. Storage-bound checks

**Surjection proofs** — `verify_shielded_outputs_with_storage` → `verify_surjection_proofs` (729-921):
- Domain = asset generators of all non-authority inputs: derived asset tags for transparent inputs and spent AmountShieldedOutputs; `asset_commitment` for spent FullShieldedOutputs (862-890). Authority inputs are excluded (868-869). Spent shielded output index out of range → `InvalidShieldedOutputError` (874-877, 885-890).
- The domain is extended with one generator per **MintHeader** entry (minted asset can be claimed); MeltHeader does not extend it (892-897, docstring 850-857).
- Only FullShieldedOutputs need proofs; missing proof → `InvalidSurjectionProofError` (905-910); failed verification → `InvalidSurjectionProofError` (911-921). A FullShieldedOutput with an empty domain (no value inputs) is rejected (899-903).

**Balance verification** — `verify_shielded_balance` (923-1060), error `ShieldedBalanceMismatchError`:
- Builds: transparent inputs/outputs as `(value, token_uid_32B)` (authorities skipped, 961-965, 978-982); shielded input/output commitments (33B); **FeeHeader entries are added to the transparent-output side** (987-990).
- Equation (docstring 931-950): mixed/partial-unshield: `sum(C_in) == sum(C_out) + fee*H_HTR`; full-unshield: `sum(C_in) == sum(C_out) + excess*G + fee*H_HTR` with `excess = sum(r_in) − sum(r_out)` from the UnshieldBalanceHeader.
- **Mutual-exclusion invariants** (1021-1046): (1) shielded outputs and excess cannot coexist (keyed on *header presence*, not list emptiness, 1027-1029); (2) a tx with shielded inputs and no shielded outputs **must** carry an UnshieldBalanceHeader; (3) excess requires at least one shielded input. The crypto wrapper re-checks the same plus 32-byte excess (`hathor/crypto/shielded/balance.py:29-36`).
- **Rule M4 folding** (992-1019, `_fold_mint_melt_entry` 1062-1104): each MintHeader entry adds `(amount, token_uid)` to the input side; MeltHeader entries to the output side. DEPOSIT-version tokens fold the 1% HTR deposit onto the output side (mint) / withdraw onto the input side (melt) (1087-1095). FEE-version tokens add one `FEE_PER_OUTPUT` HTR to the output side for **both** mint and melt (1096-1104). Token version resolution failure → `TokenNotFound` — deliberately not a silent bypass, since that would allow inflation (`_resolve_token_version_for_mint_melt`, 1106-1142; TCT's own token uses `tx.token_version`, 1124-1125).
- Final check via `verify_balance` in the Rust lib; `False` → `ShieldedBalanceMismatchError('shielded balance equation does not hold')` (1048-1060). Token UIDs are normalized to 32 bytes (1-byte HTR right-padded with zeros) — `hathor/crypto/shielded/asset_tag.py:8-21`, wrapper at `transaction_verifier.py:547-554`.

**Token rules for shielded txs** (replaces transparent balance):
- `verify_no_undeclared_mint_melt` (581-611): for any non-NATIVE token whose transparent token_dict shows a surplus (mint) or deficit (melt) **and** the tx holds the corresponding authority, the token must appear in the matching Mint/Melt header → `ShieldedMintMeltForbiddenError`. Rationale: without the public scalar the augmented equation could mint from nothing (586-589).
- `verify_mint_melt_authority_inputs` — **Rule M2** (1144-1202): every MintHeader entry requires at least one spent transparent **mint authority** input for that token (→ `ForbiddenMint`); symmetric for MeltHeader (→ `ForbiddenMelt`). Shielded inputs can never be authorities ("parent Rule 7", 1173-1175). TCT exemption for `token_index == 1` (the new token grants its own authorities, 1185-1202).
- `verify_token_rules` (407-430) with `is_shielded=True`: skips the amount-based mint/melt permission check and deposit/withdraw computation for non-native tokens (transparent deficits are expected; `_check_token_permissions` 453-457, `_check_token_permissions_and_deposits` 332-337, HTR-surplus check skipped at 347) but still enforces **fee exactness**: `expected_fee (incl. shielded_fee) == fees_from_fee_header` → `InputOutputMismatch` (427-430). So the FeeHeader total must equal exactly the calculated fee (incl. the per-shielded-output fee), even though `verify_shielded_fee` alone only checks `>=`.

## 6. Token creation with shielded outputs

`hathor/verification/token_creation_transaction_verifier.py:30-75` (`verify_minted_tokens`):
- A shielded TCT **must** carry a MintHeader (`InvalidToken`, 45-49); exactly **one** MintHeader entry with `token_index == 1` for the new token (50-56); amount ≥ 1 guaranteed by entry construction (57-59); the TCT **cannot melt the new token** (MeltHeader entry with token_index 1 → `InvalidToken`, 60-69). Non-shielded TCT keeps the transparent `token_info.amount > 0` check (72-75).
- `vertex_verifier.py:247-260`: TCT may carry all four shielded headers when the feature is active.
- Balance dispatch comment: a TCT with shielded outputs joins the shielded balance branch; the new supply is reconciled against the public MintHeader entry (`verification_service.py:333-344`).

## 7. Shielded inputs (spending) rules

- Input index space is unified: `index < len(outputs)` → transparent; `len(outputs) <= index < len(outputs)+len(shielded_outputs)` → shielded; else `InexistentInput` (`transaction_verifier.py:140-152, 181-192`; `base_transaction.py:348-363`).
- Spent shielded outputs' **scripts are evaluated identically** to transparent ones (`verify_script` via `_verify_inputs`, 202-203) and counted in input sigops (`verify_sigops_input`, 134-157; limit `MAX_TX_SIGOPS_INPUT = 255*5`, `hathorlib/hathorlib/conf/settings.py:421`).
- Double-spend-within-tx, timestamp ordering, input data size (`MAX_INPUT_DATA_SIZE`) apply unchanged (174-210).

## 8. Generic limits extended to shielded outputs

- Script size: each shielded output script ≤ `MAX_OUTPUT_SCRIPT_SIZE = 1024` → `InvalidOutputScriptSize` (`vertex_verifier.py:185-190`; setting `hathorlib/hathorlib/conf/settings.py:408`). (Deserialize independently caps at 1024 via `MAX_SHIELDED_OUTPUT_SCRIPT_SIZE`.)
- Output sigops: shielded scripts counted toward `MAX_TX_SIGOPS_OUTPUT = 255*5` → `TooManySigOps` (`vertex_verifier.py:222-233`).
- Token-list hygiene (mempool-hardened, `harden_token_restrictions`): `MAX_TOKENS_LENGTH=16`, no repeated tokens, and **AmountShieldedOutput token indexes count as "used"** for the unused-token check (`transaction_verifier.py:476-504`). Note FullShieldedOutputs do not mark tokens used (asset hidden).
- `MAX_NUM_OUTPUTS=255` applies only to transparent outputs (`vertex_verifier.py:192-195`); shielded count is bounded separately at 32.

## 9. Mempool/relay-only constraints (`VerificationParams.for_mempool`, `hathor/verification/verification_params.py:37-57`)

- `reject_too_old_vertices`: timestamp older than 36h rejected (`vertex_verifier.py:57,307-315`).
- `harden_token_restrictions` (token checks above), `harden_nano_restrictions`, `reject_conflicts_with_confirmed_txs` (conflict caps `MAX_WITHIN_CONFLICTS=8`, `MAX_BETWEEN_CONFLICTS=8`, `transaction_verifier.py:80-81,506-543`). These are mempool/relay policies, not block-validity rules.
- Caller must compute features via `Features.from_vertex()` so the shielded gate is correct per-vertex (`verification_params.py:40-43`).

## 10. Rules the node does NOT enforce — wallet/client responsibilities

- **Blinding-factor scalar validity:** the node validates commitments/asset commitments as curve *points* only (`transaction_verifier.py:750-785`); it never sees blinding factors. Wallets must generate valid non-zero secp256k1 scalars (client guide: "Blinding factors must be valid secp256k1 scalars — use library generator", `/Users/pedroferreira/Hathor/hathor-wallet-lib/SHIELDED_INTEGRATION_REVIEW/reference/client-guide-checklist.md:121`).
- **Ephemeral pubkey presence / recoverability:** all-zeros (= absent) ephemeral pubkey is consensus-valid (`hathorlib/.../shielded_tx_output.py:103-104,158-166`). A wallet that omits it produces outputs the recipient can never unblind (rewind needs ECDH nonce — `hathor/transaction/shielded_tx_output.py:49-107`). It IS bound to the sighash to prevent stripping (`shielded_tx_output.py:238-240`), but presence is the sender's job.
- **Rewind message layout:** FullShieldedOutput recovery expects `token_uid(32)|asset_bf(32)` embedded in the range-proof message (`hathor/transaction/shielded_tx_output.py:92-99`); the node never validates message contents — a sender who embeds garbage makes the output undetectable/unspendable for the recipient.
- **Value fits 40 bits:** range proofs are fixed at 40 bits (`hathor-ct-crypto/src/rangeproof.rs:12-15`). A wallet must not attempt amounts ≥ 2^40 — proof creation/verification fails, not a graceful node-side message.
- **2-output construction:** the node enforces ≥2 shielded outputs (`transaction_verifier.py:1219-1227`), so wallets must always add a second shielded output (e.g., shielded change, possibly zero-value) when shielding to one recipient; the node does not build or suggest this.
- **Unshield privacy leak:** with exactly one shielded input, the revealed excess is effectively that input's blinding factor; the node accepts this — the docstring explicitly reasons the leak is acceptable, leaving multi-input aggregation/hygiene to wallets (`hathor/transaction/headers/unshield_balance_header.py:42-47`).
- **Recipient-set privacy for FEE tokens:** the per-entry (not per-recipient) FEE charge exists to preserve "the privacy of the recipient set" (`transaction_verifier.py:1096-1104`) — wallets choosing output granularity carry the privacy consequences.
- **Blinding-factor custody & sharing:** `compute_balancing_blinding_factor` and all create-side primitives (`hathor/crypto/shielded/balance.py:46-63`, `commitment.py`, `surjection.py:6-21`) are sender-side; the node never stores or checks who can unblind what. Storage/encryption of blindings, and never exposing blindings of outputs sent to others, is purely wallet policy.
- **Fee overpayment economics:** `verify_shielded_fee` only checks declared ≥ minimum (`transaction_verifier.py:575-579`); exactness comes from the token-side fee equation — wallets must compute the FeeHeader to satisfy *both* (`transaction_verifier.py:427-430`).
- **Address/script reuse, output ordering, decoy values:** no consensus rule anywhere in `hathor/verification/` touches these; standard CT privacy hygiene is entirely client-side (client guide §, `client-guide-checklist.md:125`: "Full nodes must enforce the three transaction-layer invariants — not automatic from crypto verification" — everything beyond those invariants is wallet territory).

## 11. Error type inventory

`InvalidShieldedOutputError`, `InvalidRangeProofError`, `InvalidSurjectionProofError`, `ShieldedBalanceMismatchError`, `ShieldedAuthorityError`, `ShieldedMintMeltForbiddenError`, `InvalidMintMeltHeaderError`, `TrivialCommitmentError`, plus reused `ForbiddenMint`/`ForbiddenMelt`, `InputOutputMismatch`, `TokenNotFound`, `HeaderNotSupported`, `TooManyHeaders`, `InvalidToken`, `TooManySigOps`, `InvalidOutputScriptSize`, `InexistentInput` (imports at `transaction_verifier.py:28-62`).

# Hathor-core (experimental/shielded-outputs-alpha-v4) — Wire-Level API Reference for Shielded Outputs

Source of truth: `/tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded` at HEAD `00a4532b` ("experimental: shielded outptus alpha v4 - events API").

## 0. Core data model recap (what's on the wire conceptually)

- Shielded outputs live in a **`ShieldedOutputsHeader`** (header id `0x12`), NOT in `tx.outputs`. Other shielded headers: `UNSHIELD_BALANCE_HEADER` `0x13`, `MINT_HEADER` `0x14`, `MELT_HEADER` `0x15` (`hathor/transaction/headers/types.py:19-25`).
- Two modes from `OutputMode` (`hathorlib/hathorlib/transaction/shielded_tx_output.py:30-34`): `TRANSPARENT = 0`, `AMOUNT_ONLY = 1` (`AmountShieldedOutput`: has `token_data`), `FULLY_SHIELDED = 2` (`FullShieldedOutput`: has `asset_commitment` + `surjection_proof`). Both have `commitment` (33B), `range_proof`, `script`, optional `ephemeral_pubkey` (33B) (`shielded_tx_output.py:38-60`).
- **Combined output index space**: an input's `index` ≥ `len(tx.outputs)` refers to shielded output `index - len(outputs)` (`hathor/transaction/base_transaction.py:348-363`, `is_shielded_output` at :361-363). Metadata `spent_outputs` / `spent_by` are keyed in this combined space (`base_transaction.py:888-892`).

## 1. REST / tx API JSON shapes

### 1.1 `_shielded_output_to_json` — the canonical shielded-output JSON (`hathor/transaction/base_transaction.py:82-113`)

```json
{
  "type": "shielded",                  // required, literal
  "mode": 1,                           // required int; 1=AmountShielded, 2=FullShielded (NEW at HEAD)
  "commitment": "<hex>",               // 33-byte commitment, HEX
  "range_proof": "<base64>",           // BASE64
  "script": "<base64>",                // BASE64 (same convention as transparent TxOutput.to_json)
  "ephemeral_pubkey": "<hex>",         // OPTIONAL — omitted when empty; HEX
  "token_data": 1,                     // only when mode==1 (AmountShielded)
  "asset_commitment": "<hex>",         // only when mode==2; HEX
  "surjection_proof": "<base64>",      // only when mode==2; BASE64
  "decoded": {"address": "<b58>"}      // only when decode_script=True AND script parses; no value/token_data inside
}
```
Encoding mix is deliberate: commitments/pubkeys hex, proofs/script base64. `mode` is the canonical discriminator; consumers must not infer mode from field presence (docstring `base_transaction.py:85-90`).

### 1.2 `tx.to_json` (used by `/v1a/transaction?id=`, `/v1a/decode_tx`, `/v1a/push_tx` response)

`BaseTransaction.to_json` (`base_transaction.py:788-825`) adds a **top-level `shielded_outputs` key, only present when the tx has shielded outputs**:
```json
"shielded_outputs": [ <_shielded_output_to_json shape above> ]
```
No `value`, no `spent_by` here. Regular `outputs` array is unchanged. `Transaction.to_json` (`hathor/transaction/transaction.py:293-317`) adds `tokens` (hex list), nano fields, and `fees` (from FeeHeader) — but **no JSON exposure of UnshieldBalanceHeader, MintHeader, or MeltHeader**. The `excess_blinding_factor` and mint/melt entries are visible only in the raw struct hex (`serialized['raw']`, `resources/transaction.py:63`). There is **no `to_json` on any shielded header class** (verified: no `to_json` in `hathor/transaction/headers/*.py`).

### 1.3 `get_tx_extra_data` — `GET /v1a/transaction?id=<hash>` and `/v1a/decode_tx` (`hathor/transaction/resources/transaction.py:51-158`)

Response: `{"success": true, "tx": {...}, "meta": {...}, "spent_outputs": {...}}`.
- `tx` = `to_json(decode_script=True)` + `raw` (full struct hex incl. all headers) + `nonce` **as string** (:63-64).
- `tx.inputs[]`: each input is the spent output's JSON with `tx_id`/`index` added. **If the input spends a shielded output**, it's the full `_shielded_output_to_json(..., decode_script=True)` shape (with `mode`, base64 `script`/`range_proof`, possibly `decoded.address`) plus `tx_id` and `index` (:97-105). No `token_data` injection / `value` for shielded inputs. Pinned by `hathor_tests/resources/transaction/test_tx_shielded.py:101-119`.
- `tx.shielded_outputs[]` present at top level with `mode` (test :112-119).
- `spent_outputs` map and `meta.spent_outputs` use the combined index space.

### 1.4 `to_json_extended` — address history, dashboard, mempool, WS `network:new_tx_accepted` (`base_transaction.py:827-898`)

Used by `/v1a/thin_wallet/address_history` (`hathor/wallet/resources/thin_wallet/address_history.py:262`), `/v1a/thin_wallet/address_search` (:147), `dashboard.py:75-78`, `tx` list endpoint (`resources/transaction.py:324`), WS factory (`hathor/websocket/factory.py:215`), WS history streamer (`hathor/websocket/streamer.py:300`), and address-index pubsub (`hathor/indexes/address_index.py:88`).

- **`inputs[]` spending a shielded output** get a *minimal, different* shape (`base_transaction.py:865-873`):
```json
{"type": "shielded", "commitment": "<hex>", "script": "<HEX — NOT base64 here!>", "tx_id": "<hex>", "index": 5}
```
  Note: **no `mode`, no `range_proof`, no `decoded`, no `token`, and `script` is HEX** — inconsistent with `_shielded_output_to_json`. Transparent inputs additionally get `"type": "transparent"` (:877).
- **`outputs[]`**: shielded outputs are **appended into the same `outputs` array after transparent ones** (not a separate key), as `_shielded_output_to_json(decode_script=True)` + `spent_by` (hex or null) + — for AmountShielded only — `token` (token UID hex resolved via `token_data & 0x7f`) (`base_transaction.py:888-896`). So in extended JSON, `outputs[]` is a union: transparent entries have `value`/`token_data`/`token`/`decoded`/`spent_by`; shielded entries have `type:"shielded"`, `mode`, `commitment` (hex), `range_proof` (base64), `script` (base64), optional `ephemeral_pubkey` (hex), `spent_by`, plus mode-specific fields, **no `value`**.
- Top-level fields unchanged: `tx_id`, `version`, `weight`, `timestamp`, `is_voided`, `first_block`, `parents`, `tokens` (Transaction).

### 1.5 Other REST resources

- `/v1a/push_tx`: success response embeds `tx.to_json()` (`resources/push_tx.py:108`) → includes `shielded_outputs` key. Failures are the generic `{'success': false, 'message': ...}` with verifier error strings.
- `/v1a/utxo_search`: **shielded outputs are never indexed** — UTXO index skips anything that isn't a `TxOutput` (`hathor/indexes/utxo_index.py:146-150`, `192-196`). Wallet clients cannot discover shielded UTXOs via utxo_search.
- Tokens APIs: no shielded-specific fields; the tokens index folds declared shielded supply into the existing `total` via mint/melt headers and shielding/unshielding index corrections (`hathor/indexes/rocksdb_tokens_index.py:400-563`). Wire shape of `/thin_wallet/token` unchanged.

## 2. WebSocket / event-queue payloads

### 2.1 Legacy full-node WS (`/v1a/ws`, what wallet-lib's fullnode `Connection` uses)

- `network:new_tx_accepted` (broadcast): `to_json_extended()` + `is_block` + `first_block` (`websocket/factory.py:213-221`) → shielded outputs appear inside `outputs[]` as in §1.4; shielded-spending inputs use the minimal hex-script shape.
- `wallet:address_history` (sent only to subscribed addresses): `{type, address, history}` where `history = tx.to_json_extended()` (`indexes/address_index.py:80-90`, `pubsub.py:145`). **Shielded scripts ARE included in `get_related_addresses`** (`base_transaction.py:508-512`), so a subscribed address receives `wallet:address_history` events for txs whose *shielded* outputs pay it, and inputs are resolved shielded-aware via `resolve_spent_output` (:498-504).
- History streaming (`stream:history:vertex`): `data = vertex.to_json_extended()` (`websocket/streamer.py:300`, message shape `websocket/messages.py:506-511`).

### 2.2 Event-queue API (`/v1a/event` REST + event WS) — what HEAD changed

HEAD commit `00a4532b` touched only `hathor/event/model/event_data.py` + `base_transaction.py` (`mode` field) + tests.

- New pydantic model `ShieldedTxOutput` (`event_data.py:46-68`): fields `type: Literal['shielded']` (required), `mode: int` (required, NEW), `commitment: str`, `range_proof: str`, `script: str` (all required), optional/nullable `ephemeral_pubkey`, `token_data`, `asset_commitment`, `surjection_proof`, `decoded`.
- `TxInput.spent_output` is now a smart union `TxOutput | ShieldedTxOutput` (`event_data.py:71-74`; from commit `f7d78211`) — events for txs spending shielded outputs no longer fail validation/get dropped.
- `TxDataWithoutMeta` / `TxData` gained **`shielded_outputs: list[ShieldedTxOutput] = []`** (`event_data.py:163-172`). Before this, pydantic's `extra='ignore'` silently dropped the `shielded_outputs` key, so the event WS / hathor-wallet-service never saw shielded receives. Event tx data is built from `get_tx_extra_data` (`event_data.py:182-218`), so the shielded objects match §1.1 (base64 `range_proof`/`script`, hex `commitment`).
- Event `headers` list still only models `NanoHeader` (`TxHeader: TypeAlias = NanoHeader`, `event_data.py:93`; population at :202-216) — **shielded/unshield/mint/melt headers are absent from event `headers`**.
- No new event *types*; existing `NEW_VERTEX_ACCEPTED` / `VERTEX_METADATA_CHANGED` carry the enriched `TxData`.

## 3. Address history / address search behavior

- `/v1a/thin_wallet/address_history`: shielded-paying txs are indexed under the script's base58 address (address index uses `get_related_addresses`, which parses shielded output scripts — `base_transaction.py:508-512`); history entries are `to_json_extended()` (§1.4). The address embedded in a shielded output's `script` is whatever the script parses to (standard P2PKH/P2SH parse — core has **no shielded address version byte**; only `P2PKH_VERSION_BYTE`/`MULTISIG_VERSION_BYTE` exist in settings). The wallet-lib "shielded address" version byte is a client-side convention; on-chain the script carries a normal parseable address.
- `/v1a/thin_wallet/address_search`: response gains **`has_shielded: bool`** top-level field — true if any returned tx has shielded outputs (`thin_wallet/address_search.py:138-146`, `:201`). Shielded inputs are skipped in token filtering (`:49-51`).
- `/v1a/thin_wallet/address_balance`: response gains **`has_shielded: bool`** (`thin_wallet/address_balance.py:103-157`). Shielded amounts are **excluded** from `tokens_data` received/spent sums (inputs spending shielded outputs are skipped at `:112-115`; shielded outputs to the address only flip the flag at `:128-133`). So the node's reported balance is transparent-only + a boolean hint.

## 4. Balance / UTXO reporting summary

- The node never reports shielded values anywhere: no `value` field on any shielded JSON; balance endpoints sum transparent only and signal `has_shielded`; UTXO index/`utxo_search` skip shielded outputs entirely. Clients must recover values themselves via ECDH + range-proof rewind (`hathor/transaction/shielded_tx_output.py:49-107`: shared secret from `ephemeral_pubkey`, rewind nonce, generator = `derive_asset_tag(token_uid)` for mode 1 or `asset_commitment` for mode 2; mode-2 message embeds `token_uid` (32B) + `asset_blinding_factor` (32B) at message[0:64]).
- `spent_by` for shielded outputs is reported in `to_json_extended` using combined indices (`base_transaction.py:888-892`), so wallets can detect spends of shielded UTXOs.

## 5. Gotchas for wallet-lib schema review (zod `src/api/schemas/txApi.ts`, `src/schemas.ts`)

1. **Two different shielded-input shapes**: `get_tx_extra_data` (full shape, base64 script, has `mode`) vs `to_json_extended` (minimal: only `type`, `commitment` hex, `script` **hex**, `tx_id`, `index` — no `mode`). Schemas must accept both depending on endpoint.
2. **Mixed `outputs[]` union in extended JSON** (address_history, WS new_tx): shielded entries lack `value`/`decoded.token_data` and add `mode`/`commitment`/proofs; `token` present only for mode 1.
3. `ephemeral_pubkey` is omitted (not null) when empty in REST JSON, but nullable-with-default in event models.
4. `mode` (int 1|2) is required at HEAD in both REST and events — older alpha nodes won't send it.
5. `decoded` on shielded outputs is only `{address}` (no `type`/`timelock`), and only when the script parses.
6. Unshield/mint/melt headers are invisible in all JSON; only `raw` hex contains them (header IDs `0x13`/`0x14`/`0x15`).
7. `has_shielded` boolean added to `address_balance` and `address_search` responses.
8. `/transaction` returns `nonce` as a string (`resources/transaction.py:64`).

findings.title: Zod schemas require `ephemeral_pubkey` on shielded outputs, but the node omits the key for consensus-valid outputs — remote-triggerable sync break / dropped funds-bearing txs
findings.description: An ephemeral pubkey of 33 zero bytes is consensus-valid ('absent') and the node OMITS the `ephemeral_pubkey` key from JSON in that case. Every wallet-lib shielded-output schema declares it required: AmountShieldedOutputSchema (schemas.ts:106), FullShieldedOutputSchema (schemas.ts:116), IHistoryShieldedOutputSchema (schemas.ts:191), and fullnodeTxApiShieldedOutputSchema (txApi.ts:113). A shielded entry without the key fails all three branches of the IHistoryOutputSchema union (TransparentOutputSchema needs `value`), so the whole tx fails IHistoryTxSchema. Consequences: (1) stream history sync uses `IHistoryTxSchema.parse(wsData.data)` (src/sync/stream.ts:754) — the ZodError is thrown inside the WS listener with no catch, so the sync promise never resolves and the wallet hangs in SYNCING; (2) real-time `onNewTx` uses safeParse and silently drops the tx (src/new/wallet.ts:1736-1740), so a tx that also pays the wallet transparently becomes invisible (missed funds, stale UTXO view). Any third party can trigger this by broadcasting a valid tx with zero ephemeral pubkeys (e.g. burn-style shielded outputs) that touches a victim address — wallet-lib itself always sets the pubkey, but the node accepts txs without it.
findings.evidence: wallet-lib: /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/schemas.ts:106 `ephemeral_pubkey: z.string()` (AmountShieldedOutputSchema), :116 (FullShieldedOutputSchema), :191 (IHistoryShieldedOutputSchema); /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/api/schemas/txApi.ts:113; parse sites /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/sync/stream.ts:754 (`.parse`, uncaught) and /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/new/wallet.ts:1736-1740 (safeParse → log + return). hathor-core: /tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor/transaction/base_transaction.py:101-102 `if output.ephemeral_pubkey: data['ephemeral_pubkey'] = ...` (key omitted when empty); /tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathorlib/hathorlib/transaction/shielded_tx_output.py:103-104,158-166 (33 zero bytes on wire = absent, decoded back to b''); /tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor/verification/transaction_verifier.py:787-798 (pubkey validated only `if output.ephemeral_pubkey` — absence is consensus-valid).
findings.recommendation: Make `ephemeral_pubkey` optional (`.optional()`) in all four schemas and treat absence as 'not recoverable, skip rewind' in processing.ts (which already skips on rewind failure). Add a regression test parsing a node-shaped shielded output without the key.

findings.title: `decoded` is required on shielded outputs in the tx-API schema, but the node emits it only when the script parses — /transaction parse fails on non-standard shielded scripts
findings.description: fullnodeTxApiShieldedOutputSchema declares `decoded: shieldedDecodedSchema` as required (txApi.ts:114), and IHistoryShieldedOutputSchema does the same (schemas.ts:192). hathor-core's `_shielded_output_to_json` adds the `decoded` key only when `decode_script=True` AND `parse_address_script(output.script)` returns truthy. A shielded output script is any byte string up to 1024 bytes — a non-standard script is consensus-valid and produces JSON without `decoded`. Any `txApi.getTransaction` response containing such an output then fails schema validation, breaking `getFullTxById` (src/new/wallet.ts:3408 path) and the spent-tx fetch inside `convertTransactionToHistoryTx` (src/utils/transaction.ts:1104-1117). Like WIRE-01 this is remote-triggerable by a third party including one odd-script shielded output in a tx the wallet later inspects.
findings.evidence: wallet-lib: /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/api/schemas/txApi.ts:114 `decoded: shieldedDecodedSchema` (not optional); /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/schemas.ts:192 `decoded: IHistoryOutputDecodedSchema` (not optional). hathor-core: /tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor/transaction/base_transaction.py:108-112 — `if decode_script: ... if script_type: data['decoded'] = {...}` — key omitted when the script does not parse; script may be arbitrary bytes ≤1024 (hathorlib shielded_tx_output.py:27,143-146).
findings.recommendation: Mark `decoded` `.optional()` in both schemas (downstream code already null-chains `shieldedOutput.decoded?.address`, processing.ts:106). Optionally default to `{}`.

findings.title: Combined output-index space not honored on the tx-API fallback path: shielded-spending inputs are rejected by a transparent-only bounds check and `convertFullNodeTxToHistoryTx` drops `shielded_outputs`
findings.description: In core's combined index space, an input with `index >= len(outputs)` refers to shielded output `index - len(outputs)`. `convertTransactionToHistoryTx`'s API fallback (used when the spent tx is not in local storage) checks `if (input.index >= response.tx.outputs.length) reject('Index outside of tx output array bounds')` — using only the transparent `outputs` array, even though the same parsed response carries `shielded_outputs` (txApi.ts:150). Every input spending a shielded output of an API-fetched parent is therefore rejected. Even if the bounds check passed, `convertFullNodeTxToHistoryTx` (utils/transaction.ts:1580-1624) builds the IHistoryTx without ever copying `tx.shielded_outputs`, so `findSpentOutput` cannot resolve the slot and the function throws the 'references a shielded slot...' error (:1198-1202). Edge path (parent usually in storage on the sender-local-insert flow), but when hit the wallet cannot record its own just-sent tx.
findings.evidence: wallet-lib: /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/utils/transaction.ts:1111-1113 `if (input.index >= response.tx.outputs.length) return reject(new Error('Index outside of tx output array bounds'))`; :1580-1624 `convertFullNodeTxToHistoryTx` maps `tx.inputs`/`tx.outputs` but never `tx.shielded_outputs` (histTx literal at :1598-1614 has no shielded_outputs key) despite the schema parsing it (/tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/api/schemas/txApi.ts:150). hathor-core: /tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor/transaction/base_transaction.py:348-363 (`is_shielded_output`: `len(outputs) <= index < len(outputs)+len(shielded_outputs)`), :815-819 (`shielded_outputs` is a separate top-level JSON key, not in `outputs`).
findings.recommendation: Bound-check against `response.tx.outputs.length + (response.tx.shielded_outputs?.length ?? 0)`, and have `convertFullNodeTxToHistoryTx` copy `tx.shielded_outputs` (normalized via `normalizeShieldedOutputs` to convert base64 proofs/scripts to hex) into the returned IHistoryTx.

findings.title: Address validation does not bind payload length to version byte — 25-byte addresses with the shielded version byte (and 71-byte addresses with p2pkh/p2sh bytes) validate as the wrong type
findings.description: `validateAddress` checks length (25 OR 71) and version byte (p2pkh OR p2sh OR shielded) as two independent OR-conditions; any cross combination with a correct checksum passes. `getType` then keys purely on the version byte: a crafted 25-byte address with first byte 0x3c/0x5d returns 'shielded' and `getScanPubkey()` silently returns a 24-byte slice (`subarray(1,34)` does not throw past the end) that only fails later inside ct-crypto's 33-byte check; a crafted 71-byte address with the p2pkh byte returns 'p2pkh' and `getScript()` builds a syntactically valid P2PKH script from bytes [1,21) of the scan pubkey — a spendable-looking output paying an arbitrary hash. Checksums are trivially computable, so such addresses are craftable; impact is bounded (an attacker supplying an address controls the destination anyway), but corrupted/buggy encoders should fail validation, not produce scripts.
findings.evidence: wallet-lib: /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/models/address.ts:99-106 (length: `!== 25 && !== 71`), :122-128 (version byte: any of p2pkh/p2sh/shielded, no length correlation; isVersionByteValid in /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/models/network.ts:169-176), :144-159 (getType keyed on first byte only), :185-191/202-208 (getScanPubkey/getSpendPubkey slice without length re-check). Contrast: encode side enforces 33-byte 02/03 pubkeys (/tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/utils/shieldedAddress.ts:40-51). hathor-core defines no shielded address format (only P2PKH/MULTISIG version bytes, hathor/conf/mainnet.py:23-24), so this convention is enforced solely by wallet-lib.
findings.recommendation: In `validateAddress`/`getType`, require (version==shielded ⇔ length==71) and (version∈{p2pkh,p2sh} ⇔ length==25); additionally validate that scan/spend pubkey slices start with 0x02/0x03 when decoding shielded addresses.

findings.title: No [1, 2^40) value bound enforced before building shielded outputs — range-proof creation fails with a wrapped, non-actionable error
findings.description: Range proofs are fixed at 40 bits with min_value=1; values 0 or ≥ 2^40 (≈ 11 billion HTR-base-units, easily exceeded by custom-token amounts which are u64) make `RangeProof::new` throw inside the native library. `createShieldedOutputs` validates scanPubkey and token UID lengths up front (creation.ts:272-309) but never `proposal.value`; sendTransaction's pre-crypto checks (sendTransaction.ts:504-515) cover only output count. A grep for any 2^40 / RANGE_PROOF_BITS bound over src/ finds nothing. The failure is loud (wrapped at creation.ts:358-364) so no invalid tx reaches the node, but the per-ct-crypto contract explicitly assigns this bound to the caller, and the resulting 'Failed to create shielded output ...: GenericFailure' error hides the real cause from users; it also surfaces only after potentially many expensive crypto calls.
findings.evidence: wallet-lib: /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/shielded/creation.ts:272-309 (upfront validation lacks any value check); no hits for `2**40`/`1099511627776`/`RANGE_PROOF_BITS` anywhere in /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/. ct-crypto: /tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor-ct-crypto/src/rangeproof.rs:12-16,44-53 (RANGE_PROOF_BITS=40, min_value=1 — creation throws outside [1,2^40)); client guide: caller must enforce value range (SHIELDED_INTEGRATION_REVIEW/reference/client-guide-checklist.md, node does not produce a graceful message).
findings.recommendation: Add `MAX_SHIELDED_VALUE = (1n << 40n) - 1n` to constants.ts and validate `1n <= proposal.value <= MAX_SHIELDED_VALUE` in the upfront loop of `createShieldedOutputs`, with an explicit error naming the 40-bit range-proof limit.

findings.title: normalizeShieldedOutputs infers mode from `asset_commitment` presence, ignoring the node's canonical `mode` discriminator
findings.description: When extracting shielded entries from `outputs[]` (the to_json_extended shape), the normalizer sets `mode: output.asset_commitment ? FULLY_SHIELDED : AMOUNT_SHIELDED`, discarding the explicit `mode` integer the node now sends. hathor-core's docstring explicitly instructs consumers to rely on `mode`, not field presence. Today the inference is equivalent (mode-2 JSON always carries `asset_commitment`), so this is forward-compat hygiene, not a bug — but any future mode (e.g. a mode 3) would be silently misclassified as AmountShielded and fed into the wrong rewind path. Related: `ensureHex` (utils/transaction.ts:260-266) detects base64-vs-hex by character set rather than by knowing each endpoint's documented encoding (commitment/ephemeral_pubkey hex; range_proof/script/surjection_proof base64) — safe in practice for these field sizes (collision probability ≲ 1e-15) but worth a comment-anchored test.
findings.evidence: wallet-lib: /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/utils/transaction.ts:192-195 (mode inferred), :260-266 (heuristic encoding detection). hathor-core: /tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor/transaction/base_transaction.py:85-90 — '`mode` ... is the canonical mode discriminator on the wire; downstream consumers should rely on it instead of inferring mode from the presence of asset_commitment/surjection_proof'.
findings.recommendation: Prefer `output.mode` when present and fall back to asset_commitment inference only for pre-mode nodes: `mode: output.mode ?? (output.asset_commitment ? FULLY_SHIELDED : AMOUNT_SHIELDED)`; reject/skip unknown mode values explicitly.

findings.title: Shielded address wire format is a wallet-lib-only convention that diverges from the RFC sketch — cross-client interop must be standardized before release
findings.description: wallet-lib encodes shielded addresses as Base58(version(1) || scan_pubkey(33) || spend_pubkey(33) || checksum(4)) with invented version bytes 0x3c (mainnet) / 0x5d (testnet+privatenet). The RFC sketches a different, shorter format: scan_pubkey(33) || hash(spend_pubkey)(20). hathor-core defines no shielded address format at all (only P2PKH 0x28 / MULTISIG 0x64 version bytes), so consensus is agnostic — on-chain only the script and ephemeral_pubkey matter. This is recorded as info, not a bug: nothing breaks today, but any other client (wallet-service senders, headless, mobile, exchanges) that implements the RFC sketch instead will produce/consume mutually-unintelligible addresses, and the 0x3c/0x5d bytes plus the full-33B-spend-pubkey choice exist nowhere outside this repo. The format (and its version-byte registry) needs to be ratified in the RFC/core docs before external wallets integrate.
findings.evidence: wallet-lib: /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/utils/shieldedAddress.ts:28 (format doc), :53-57 (encode); /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/models/network.ts:17,24,31 (shielded: 0x3c / 0x5d). RFC: /Users/pedroferreira/Hathor/hathor-wallet-lib/SHIELDED_INTEGRATION_REVIEW/reference/rfc-summary.md:11 — 'Shielded address format: scan_pubkey(33) || hash(spend_pubkey)(20)'. hathor-core: no shielded version byte anywhere (only P2PKH_VERSION_BYTE/MULTISIG_VERSION_BYTE, /tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor/conf/mainnet.py:23-24).
findings.recommendation: Upstream the chosen format (full spend pubkey + version bytes 0x3c/0x5d) into the RFC / a core-adjacent spec, or align with the RFC's 20-byte-hash form before any second client ships; add the format + version bytes to the client integration guide.

findings.title: Shielded value blinding factors and recovered plaintext amounts persisted unencrypted, unlike all other wallet key material
findings.description: When the wallet decrypts a shielded output it writes the recovered plaintext `value`, the value blinding factor (`blindingFactor`) and, for FullShielded, the asset blinding factor (`assetBlindingFactor`) into BOTH the persisted history tx (`store.saveTx(tx)`) and the UTXO record (`store.saveUtxo`) as raw hex/bigint, with no encryption applied. Every other piece of wallet secret material — main/account/auth keys and the new scan/spend xprivs — is wrapped with `encryptData(...)` under the PIN before being stored. A blinding factor combined with the on-chain Pedersen commitment (C = v·H + r·G, public) is exactly the commitment opening: a reader of the wallet DB not only sees the hidden amount directly (plaintext `value`) but can also PROVE the amount of any shielded output to a third party and link it on-chain, defeating the amount-confidentiality that shielded outputs exist to provide. Because these fields live on the IHistoryTx object returned by `wallet.getTx()`, any consumer that exports/persists/transmits history (a routine operation for mobile/headless) carries the openings with it. The hathor-core threat model explicitly puts blinding-factor custody on the wallet ('Storage/encryption of blindings ... is purely wallet policy', verification rule inventory §10).
findings.evidence: Plaintext persistence: src/utils/storage.ts:987 `value: result.decrypted.value`, :1005 `blindingFactor: result.decrypted.blindingFactor.toString('hex')`, :1006 `assetBlindingFactor: ...toString('hex')`, :1011 `store.saveTx(tx)`; saveUtxo at :1143-1145 `shielded:true, blindingFactor:..., assetBlindingFactor:...`. Type carriers with no encryption marker: src/types.ts:352-353 (IShieldedOutputEntry) and :472-474 (IUtxo). Contrast — all keys ARE PIN-encrypted: src/utils/wallet.ts:631 `accessData.scanMainKey = encryptData(scanXpriv.xprivkey, pin)`, :636, :727, :729; storage.ts:971-989 decrypts scan/spend xprivs via `decryptData`. hathor-core: hathor/crypto/shielded/balance.py is sender-side only; node never stores blindings (rule inventory §10 'Blinding-factor custody & sharing').
findings.recommendation: Treat blinding factors as PIN-protected secret material: encrypt `blindingFactor`/`assetBlindingFactor` (and ideally the recovered `value`) with `encryptData` before persisting, decrypting only transiently when building a spend or an explicit user-requested unblinding. At minimum document that the host store MUST be encrypted at rest and exclude these fields from any history export/sync path.

findings.title: No upper bound on range_proof / surjection_proof / script sizes on the JSON receive path before native crypto calls
findings.description: The wire deserializer (`ShieldedOutput.deserialize`) carefully bounds `rp_len <= MAX_RANGE_PROOF_SIZE (3328)`, `script_len <= 1024`, and `sp_len <= MAX_SURJECTION_PROOF_SIZE (4096)` before reading, matching hathor-core. But the primary receive path is JSON from the fullnode validated by the zod schemas, where `range_proof`, `surjection_proof`, `script`, `commitment`, etc. are plain `z.string()` with no max length. These strings are then hex/base64-decoded into Buffers and handed straight to the native crypto provider in `processShieldedOutputs`. A compromised/malicious fullnode (or a MITM if TLS is not pinned) can ship multi-megabyte proof strings across up to 32 shielded outputs per tx, forcing large allocations (string → Buffer → native copy) before the native layer rejects them — a cheap memory-amplification vector that the wire path is explicitly hardened against.
findings.evidence: Unbounded JSON: src/schemas.ts:104-106,115-118 (`commitment/range_proof/ephemeral_pubkey/asset_commitment/surjection_proof: z.string()`), :183-194; src/api/schemas/txApi.ts:105-117 same. Decoded and passed to native without size checks: src/shielded/processing.ts:122-124 `Buffer.from(shieldedOutput.range_proof,'hex')` then :144/:189 `rewind*ShieldedOutput(...)`. Contrast — wire path DOES cap: src/models/shielded_output.ts:175-176 (`rpLen > MAX_RANGE_PROOF_SIZE` throws), :187-190 (script), :218-221 (surjection). hathor-core caps identically: hathorlib shielded_tx_output.py:130-146,186-189.
findings.recommendation: Add `.max(...)` bounds to the shielded-field string schemas (e.g. 2*MAX_RANGE_PROOF_SIZE hex chars) or length-check the decoded buffers in processShieldedOutputs before invoking the native provider, mirroring the wire-deserializer limits.

findings.title: Shielded HTR change is routed to the non-rotating current shielded address and is always the last (balancing) output
findings.description: `convertHtrChangeIfRequested` resolves the shielded change recipient via `wallet.getCurrentAddress({}, {legacy:false})`, which returns the wallet's CURRENT shielded receive address without advancing the cursor. Repeated shielded sends therefore reuse the same on-chain spend-derived P2PKH for change. Shielded output scripts are standard P2PKH parsed by the node and visible on-chain, so an observer who has ever seen the wallet's shielded receive address can link every change output back to that wallet and cluster the wallet's transactions — defeating the unlinkability shielded outputs exist to provide. This is compounded by the fact that shielded outputs are NOT shuffled (transparent outputs are, sendTransaction.ts:419-426), and the converted change is appended last to `shieldedOutputDefs`, so the change is deterministically the final shielded output (also always the balancing output in creation.ts). hathor-core enforces no ordering or address-rotation rule (rule inventory §10: 'Address/script reuse, output ordering ... entirely client-side').
findings.evidence: src/new/sendTransaction.ts:1090 `const { address: shieldedAddress } = await wallet.getCurrentAddress({}, { legacy: false })` (no markAsUsed → no rotation), :1102 `shieldedOutputDefs.push({...change...})` appended last; transparent shuffle but no shielded shuffle at :419-426; balancing bf always on the last proposal at src/shielded/creation.ts:74 and :169 (`isLast && createdOutputs.length>0`). getCurrentAddress non-rotating: src/storage/storage.ts:308-310. Core has no such rule: hathor/verification has no ordering/reuse check.
findings.recommendation: Derive shielded change to a fresh, never-handed-out internal shielded-spend address (advance the change/internal chain), and shuffle the shielded outputs array (or randomize the balancing-output position) so the change is not positionally identifiable.

findings.title: Full-unshield with a single shielded input publishes that input's blinding factor, linking the prior private receive to the public spend, with no wallet mitigation
findings.description: The unshield branch fires whenever `shieldedOutputs.length === 0 && blindedInputsArr.length > 0` and publishes `excess = sum(r_in) - sum(r_out)` in the UnshieldBalanceHeader, with no check on the number of shielded inputs and no warning. With exactly one shielded input the excess equals that input's value blinding factor; because the input's commitment C = v·H + r·G is public and v is bounded to 40 bits, publishing r lets any observer recover v and, crucially, confirm WHICH shielded commitment this unshield spends — breaking the unlinkability of the previously-private receive. hathor-core documents this leak and explicitly delegates aggregation/hygiene to the wallet (unshield_balance_header docstring), so the node will accept the single-input tx silently.
findings.evidence: src/new/sendTransaction.ts:542-543 gate, :571-577 `computeBalancingBlindingFactor(0n, ZERO_TWEAK, [...blindedInputsArr, ...transparentInputEntries], transparentOutputEntries)` → shipped as `excessBlindingFactor`; attached unconditionally in src/utils/transaction.ts:961-969 / src/headers/unshield_balance.ts. No minimum-input or decoy logic anywhere on this path. hathor-core acceptance: hathor/transaction/headers/unshield_balance_header.py:42-47 ('with exactly one shielded input the revealed excess is effectively that input's blinding factor ... leaving multi-input aggregation/hygiene to wallets').
findings.recommendation: When constructing a full unshield, warn (or require explicit opt-in) for single-shielded-input txs, and prefer aggregating multiple shielded inputs (or adding a shielded change output to take the partial-unshield path, which publishes no blinding factor) so the excess is a non-invertible sum.

findings.title: Shielded zod schemas have zero tests and are never exercised against the canonical hathor-core JSON shapes (required ephemeral_pubkey/decoded vs core omit-when-absent)
findings.description: The new shielded schemas (IHistoryShieldedOutputSchema, AmountShieldedOutputSchema/FullShieldedOutputSchema inside IHistoryOutputSchema, fullnodeTxApiShieldedOutputSchema, fullnodeTxApiShieldedInputSchema) are not referenced by any test file — grep over __tests__ for IHistoryTxSchema/fullnodeTxApi/IHistoryOutputSchema/IHistoryShieldedOutputSchema returns nothing. All integration tests only exercise JSON shapes produced by wallet-lib itself (which always sets ephemeral_pubkey, always uses standard P2PKH scripts, and replays stored `decoded` blocks via asFreshDelivery/fullWsPayload). The schemas mark `ephemeral_pubkey` and `decoded` as REQUIRED, but hathor-core omits ephemeral_pubkey when absent (consensus-valid: 33 zero bytes = absent) and omits `decoded` whenever the script does not parse. A consensus-valid third-party or adversarial tx with an absent ephemeral pubkey or non-standard script that also touches a wallet address would fail schema parsing on the sync paths that use these schemas (stream sync via IHistoryTxSchema, txApi.getTransaction via fullnodeTxApiTxSchema) — a cheap griefing/DoS vector against sync, and no test would catch it. Additionally fullnodeTxApiTxSchema declares a `shielded_inputs` key (txApi.ts:149) that hathor-core never emits (spent shielded outputs are inlined into `inputs[]`), so that schema member is dead/untestable as written — also unpinned by any test.
findings.evidence: wallet-lib: src/schemas.ts:191-192 (`ephemeral_pubkey: z.string(), decoded: IHistoryOutputDecodedSchema` — both required), src/schemas.ts:106,116 (ephemeral_pubkey required in both IHistoryOutputSchema shielded variants), src/api/schemas/txApi.ts:113-114 (required ephemeral_pubkey + decoded), txApi.ts:149-150 (`shielded_inputs` key).
