Test absence: `grep -rln "IHistoryTxSchema|fullnodeTxApi|IHistoryShieldedOutputSchema" __tests__` → no matches. hathor-core: hathor/transaction/base_transaction.py:82-113 (`ephemeral_pubkey` "omitted when absent"; `decoded` only when decode_script AND script parses); hathorlib/hathorlib/transaction/shielded_tx_output.py:103-104,158-166 (all-zero ephemeral pubkey is consensus-valid 'absent'); inputs spending shielded outputs are inlined in inputs[], no top-level shielded_inputs (hathor/transaction/resources/transaction.py:97-105).
findings.recommendation: Add unit fixture tests that feed the schemas the exact JSON emitted by hathor-core for every endpoint variant documented in the integration guide: (1) shielded output WITHOUT ephemeral_pubkey, (2) shielded output WITHOUT decoded (unparseable script), (3) to_json_extended minimal shielded-input shape (hex script, no mode/range_proof), (4) get_tx_extra_data full shielded-input shape, (5) mode-less outputs from pre-mode nodes. Fix the schemas to .optional() where core omits fields, and delete or justify `shielded_inputs`. Add one integration test that injects an absent-ephemeral-pubkey shielded output via onNewTx and asserts the tx is stored (not dropped) with no credit.

findings.title: No test covers the 40-bit value ceiling for shielded amounts; largest amount exercised is ~2^26, four orders of magnitude below the 2^40 protocol limit
findings.description: Range proofs are fixed at 40 bits, so shielded values must be in [1, 2^40) — this is explicitly a wallet responsibility (the node/crypto lib just throws). wallet-lib has no client-side bound: grep for 2**40/1099511627776/MAX_SHIELDED_VALUE over src and __tests__ returns nothing, and src/shielded/creation.ts passes proposal.value straight to the provider. The 'large amount (1M+ HTR)' test tops out at 80,000,000n (~2^26.3). A custom token with a large supply (token amounts go up to 2^63) shielded in one output crosses 2^40 realistically; the user gets an opaque Rust throw from createRangeProof *after* UTXO selection has marked inputs as selected — neither the error surface nor the selected-UTXO cleanup for a crypto-stage failure is tested (B.12 only covers an insufficient-funds failure that throws before the crypto stage). Relatedly, the client-side >32-output guard message at sendTransaction.ts:510-515 ('Cannot create more than') is asserted by no test (the 32-bound is only pinned at the header/Transaction.validate layers).
findings.evidence: wallet-lib: __tests__/integration/shielded_outputs/core.test.ts:101-103 (value0=80_000_000n, value1=40_000_000n — max tested); src/new/sendTransaction.ts:504-515 (count guards, no value guard); src/shielded/creation.ts (no value bound; grep for any 2^40 constant in src/__tests__ → empty); __tests__/integration/shielded_outputs/utxo_selector.test.ts:319-403 (B.12 fails on amount>available, pre-crypto). ct-crypto: hathor-ct-crypto/src/rangeproof.rs:12-15,44-53 (RANGE_PROOF_BITS=40, min_value=1; create throws for value 0 or >= 2^40). Client guide: SHIELDED_INTEGRATION_REVIEW/reference/... 'Value fits 40 bits' is listed as a wallet responsibility the node does not enforce gracefully.
findings.recommendation: Add a unit test (mock provider or real ct-crypto-node) asserting a shielded output of value 2^40 is rejected client-side with a clear error BEFORE crypto/UTXO selection, plus the boundary success at 2^40-1. Add an integration variant asserting shielded UTXOs are released after a crypto-stage build failure. Also add an assertion for the 33-output rejection message at the sendTransaction layer.

findings.title: V.4 does not test what it claims: double-spend rejection of a shielded UTXO is never exercised, and on-chain voiding of shielded txs is only ever simulated via hand-fed onNewTx flags
findings.description: V.4 is named 'second send of an already-spent shielded UTXO is rejected', but the body performs two sequential SUCCESSFUL sends and the comment admits 'We mainly assert no error is thrown'. It would pass identically if the wallet re-selected spent shielded UTXOs and the node accepted them — i.e. it cannot fail for the reason it exists. No test anywhere builds a transaction that actually re-spends a consumed shielded slot and asserts the fullnode rejects it; and the conflict→void→balance-reversal lifecycle for shielded txs (A.10, C.18) is exercised only by feeding `{...stored, is_voided: true}` through onNewTx — a wallet-crafted payload, not the real fullnode event shape produced by a conflict. The suite header ('server-side: fullnode rejects on push_tx') promises a class of tests the file does not contain. Given shielded UTXO deletion races were a real bug area (L.5, 'should delete spent shielded UTXOs so the next send does not double-spend' in core.test.ts:1730), the genuinely negative path — node rejecting a stale shielded input — is the regression most worth pinning.
findings.evidence: wallet-lib: __tests__/integration/shielded_outputs/shielded_negative.test.ts:138-146 ('We mainly assert no error is thrown'); ws_message_ordering.test.ts:230-255 (A.10 voids by spreading is_voided into a stored tx); cross_wallet.test.ts:181 (C.18 same technique). hathor-core: double-spend/conflict handling is real verifier behavior (transaction_verifier.py:174-210 double-spend-within-tx; conflict resolution voids txs) that the wallet's shielded UTXO bookkeeping must survive — never driven end-to-end.
findings.recommendation: Replace or augment V.4 with a true negative: clone the storage UTXO record (or build a raw tx via transaction utils) referencing the already-spent shielded slot, push it, and assert push_tx rejection. Add one integration test that creates a real on-chain conflict (two txs spending the same shielded UTXO via two wallet instances sharing a seed) and asserts the loser is voided and the winner's balance state is correct.

findings.title: A.6 (full-before-bare ws ordering) asserts nothing: `expect(typeof balAfter).toBe('object')` is vacuously true
findings.description: The test's stated contract is 'the wallet must accept a full payload as the first event for a tx it doesn't yet know about and decrypt correctly', but the only assertions are `expect(after).not.toBeNull()` and `expect(typeof balAfter).toBe('object')` — getTxBalance always returns an object, so decryption correctness is entirely unverified ('It either decoded (credit > 0) or didn't (= 0); we don't crash'). The out-of-order full-first delivery to a fresh wallet is exactly the path where the decode-then-clobber bug class (fixed in A.9) lives; a regression that silently stops decoding on this ordering passes this test. The fresh wallet in this test uses a different random seed, so a deterministic credit assertion IS possible if the shielded outputs were sent to the fresh wallet's addresses rather than reusing walletB's stored tx.
findings.evidence: wallet-lib: __tests__/integration/shielded_outputs/ws_message_ordering.test.ts:170-175 (`const balAfter = await fresh.getTxBalance(after!); expect(typeof balAfter).toBe('object');`). Contrast with A.9 (same file, 185-228) which wipes walletB's storage and asserts the exact 50n credit — the same technique works for A.6.
findings.recommendation: Rework A.6 with the A.9 technique: wipe the receiving wallet's record of the tx (or send to a fresh wallet's own shielded addresses), deliver full-then-bare, and assert the exact 50n credit and idempotence after the bare follow-up.

findings.title: Stream-sync (XPUB_STREAM_WS / MANUAL_STREAM_WS) shielded address derivation is completely untested — and its swallow-all catch makes regressions silent
findings.description: The integration added per-address shielded derivation inside StreamManager's address-item handler, wrapped in a try/catch that logs and continues ('so derivation failures don't crash the queue'). If deriveShieldedAddressFromStorage fails or returns null on this path, the wallet silently never registers its shielded addresses, so every shielded receive is invisible (no rewind is even attempted for unknown addresses — processing.ts skips outputs whose decoded.address is not mine). There is zero coverage: __tests__/stream.test.ts is unchanged from master and contains no shielded reference; no shielded integration suite configures HistorySyncMode.XPUB_STREAM_WS or MANUAL_STREAM_WS (grep over __tests__/integration → no matches); the new pinCode parameter threading through xpubStreamSyncHistory/manualStreamSyncHistory (stream.ts:262-309) is likewise untested. All 23 shielded integration suites run only the default polling sync, so an entire history-sync mode of the wallet has unverified shielded behavior with a failure mode of silent missing funds.
findings.evidence: wallet-lib: src/sync/stream.ts:566-587 (try/catch around deriveShieldedAddressFromStorage + saveAddress, error only logged); src/sync/stream.ts:262-309 (new pinCode param). Test absence: `git diff master...HEAD --stat -- __tests__/stream.test.ts` → empty; `grep -rn "HistorySyncMode|STREAM_WS" __tests__/integration` → no matches. Receive-side dependence on registered addresses: src/shielded/processing.ts (skip when getAddressInfo returns null — pinned by __tests__/shielded/processing.test.ts:138-148).
findings.recommendation: Add unit tests for StreamManager's address handling with shielded keys present (asserts shieldedAddress + spendAddress saved at the same index) and with derivation throwing (asserts the queue continues AND, ideally, that the failure is surfaced beyond a log line). Add at least one integration suite (or parameterize an existing one) that runs a shielded receive under XPUB_STREAM_WS and MANUAL_STREAM_WS and asserts the credited balance equals the polling-sync result.

findings.title: MintHeader/MeltHeader coexisting with ShieldedOutputsHeader is never exercised; 'mint/melt to shielded' test names overstate what the bodies test
findings.description: Header combinations actually tested: Fee+ShieldedOutputs (most suites), Fee+Unshield (U.*, I.10), Unshield+Mint (S.0 TCT, S.4), Unshield+Melt (S.2, S.3). The combination MintHeader or MeltHeader + ShieldedOutputsHeader in the SAME tx — which hathor-core explicitly supports (Rule M1 is satisfied by either header; MintHeader entries extend the surjection-proof domain so FS outputs of freshly minted tokens verify) — is never built or asserted anywhere. The auto-declare logic in attachShieldedHeaders (utils/transaction.ts:985-1071) computes mint/melt entries for any shielded tx, including ones carrying shielded outputs, yet only its unshield branch is ever hit. Meanwhile the test names suggest this is covered when it is not: S.1 'mint more tokens to FS output' is actually a plain sendManyOutputsTransaction of already-minted supply (no MintHeader); K.4 'Mint more tokens to a shielded address' and K.5 'Melt tokens with a shielded change address' rely on the shielded-address→transparent-spend-P2PKH auto-conversion (pinned at core.test.ts:479), so the resulting txs are fully transparent with no shielded headers at all — and neither test asserts which shape was produced. A regression in the Mint/Melt+ShieldedOutputs path (e.g. surjection domain not extended with mint tags, or wrong M4 folding) would be caught by nothing.
findings.evidence: wallet-lib: __tests__/integration/shielded_outputs/mint_melt_shielded.test.ts:113-126 (S.1 body is sendManyOutputsTransaction, not mintTokens); token_creation.test.ts:130-133 (K.4 asserts only balance 125n, never inspects headers/outputs), 421-427 (K.5 same); src/utils/transaction.ts:985-1071 (auto mint/melt declaration for shielded txs, incl. shielded-outputs case at 996). hathor-core: transaction_verifier.py:892-897 (MintHeader entries extend surjection domain — only relevant when FS outputs coexist with a MintHeader), 672-691 (M1 satisfied by ShieldedOutputsHeader presence), 992-1019 (M4 folding applies with shielded outputs present).
findings.recommendation: Add an integration test that melts (or mints) while keeping change shielded — i.e. a tx carrying both a Melt/MintHeader and a ShieldedOutputsHeader — and assert via the raw tx (or header inspection on the returned Transaction) that both headers are present and the node accepted it. If wallet-lib intentionally does not support shielded outputs on mint/melt flows, add a test pinning the auto-conversion (assert the minted/withdraw output is transparent spend-P2PKH and NO shielded header exists) and rename S.1/K.4/K.5 to match what they verify.

findings.title: Unblinding-payload scope for mixed-ownership txs (own change + foreign recipient outputs in one tx) is untested — the exact privacy boundary case
findings.description: The unblinding invariant is: expose openings only for wallet-owned outputs (received + change), never for outputs sent to other recipients. The P suite covers the two pure poles — P.3 (self-send: ALL outputs owned) and P.4 (cross-wallet send with transparent inputs: NO outputs owned, empty payload) — but not the mixed tx where the sender holds shielded change alongside the recipient's shielded outputs. That is the only case where inclusion and exclusion must hold simultaneously, and it is exactly the tx shape the sparse-decode suites construct (N.1 'partial-self-and-other FS send', N.3 'middle shielded output') without ever calling getShieldedUnblindingForTx on it. The current implementation filters on stored blindingFactor (wallet.ts:1154), and convertTransactionToHistoryTx does not persist the sender-side blinding factors of recipient outputs — but nothing pins that: a future change that persists send-time blindings (e.g. for retry/audit) would silently flip the mixed case into leaking recipients' openings, and the unit suite's foreign-output case (hathorwallet.test.ts:1019 'not decoded — no blindingFactor → skipped') merely restates the same storage assumption with a mock.
findings.evidence: wallet-lib: src/new/wallet.ts:1146-1172 (filter is `if (!output.blindingFactor) continue;`); src/utils/transaction.ts:1250-1273 (sender-local insert emits shielded_outputs WITHOUT blinding factors — the implicit guarantee); __tests__/integration/shielded_outputs/unblinding_encoder.test.ts:157-236 (P.3 all-own), 236-276 (P.4 all-foreign); sparse_decode.test.ts:66-129 (N.1 builds the mixed tx, asserts only spendability). User-stated invariant: 'Only wallet-owned outputs (received + change). Never expose blindings of outputs sent to other recipients.'
findings.recommendation: Add P.5: sender sends FS to a recipient with changeShieldedMode set (own shielded change in the same tx); call getShieldedUnblindingForTx on the sender and assert the payload contains exactly the change output's on-chain index (and any owned inputs) and does NOT contain the recipient outputs' indices or vbfs — assert against on-chain indices, not array lengths.

findings.title: Multisig (P2SH) wallets get single-sig shielded key chains derived with zero test coverage; wallet-service wallet shielded handling has only a 1-line test delta
findings.description: generateAccessDataFromXpriv derives and stores shielded scan/spend chains for ANY depth-0 root key, with no walletType gate — a multisig wallet therefore exposes shielded addresses whose spend path is a SINGLE-SIG P2PKH on the spend chain, silently downgrading the wallet's m-of-n custody for anything received shielded. Whether that is intended, blocked, or surfaced is pinned by no test: grep for multisig/MultiSig across __tests__/integration/shielded_outputs and __tests__/shielded returns nothing, and the only adjacent unit test is M.2 ('no-op when xpub-only'). There is no test for: a multisig wallet calling getAddressAtIndex({legacy:false}), attempting a shielded send, or receiving on its derived shielded address. Similarly, the wallet-service wallet path got a behavior change (walletServiceStorageProxy now passes shielded output entries through untransformed) with a +1-line test change, and no test covers HathorWalletServiceWallet receiving a tx containing shielded outputs end-to-end (the known local WS-daemon crash on shielded events makes this the path most likely to break unnoticed).
findings.evidence: wallet-lib: src/utils/wallet.ts:626-637 (shielded chains derived whenever `argXpriv.depth === 0`, no walletType check — contrast the multisig branch at 600-608 which is type-gated); `grep -rln multisig __tests__/integration/shielded_outputs __tests__/shielded` → empty; src/wallet/walletServiceStorageProxy.ts:189-201 (new isShieldedOutputEntry passthrough) vs __tests__/wallet/walletServiceStorageProxy.test.ts (+1 line in the diffstat). Read-only single-sig IS covered (core.test.ts:1363) — multisig is the remaining wallet type with none.
findings.recommendation: Decide and pin the multisig contract with tests: either (a) shielded chains are NOT derived/exposed for walletType p2sh (assert getAddressAtIndex({legacy:false}) and shielded sends throw a clear error), or (b) they are supported and a test documents the single-sig custody semantics explicitly. Add a unit test for walletServiceStorageProxy converting a wallet-service tx whose outputs include a type:'shielded' entry, asserting it survives untouched and the transparent siblings still get the decoded.type normalization.

findings.title: Surjection-proof domain includes authority inputs and omits MintHeader generators, diverging from node verification
findings.description: When building the surjection-proof domain for FullShielded outputs, the wallet emits one generator for EVERY input that has a token (`if (inp.token)`), with no filter for token-authority inputs, and it never extends the domain with the generators of the tx's own MintHeader entries. hathor-core builds the verification domain differently: it SKIPS token-authority inputs entirely, and APPENDS one asset-tag generator per MintHeader entry. A surjection proof is bound to the exact domain (membership + order) it was created over; if the verifier's domain differs in length/order the proof fails. So any FullShielded-output transaction that also spends a transparent token-authority UTXO, or that mints a token, will have a wallet-built proof over a domain the node cannot reproduce, and the node rejects it with InvalidSurjectionProofError after PoW. Normal FullShielded sends (transparent/shielded value inputs, no authority, no mint) are unaffected because the two domains coincide.
findings.evidence: wallet-lib src/new/sendTransaction.ts:459-467 pushes genInfo for every `inp.token` with no authority skip and no mint-entry extension; createShieldedOutputs consumes this list verbatim (src/shielded/creation.ts:129-145 buildSurjectionDomain iterates all inputGenerators). hathor-core transaction_verifier.py:868-869 `if spent_output.is_token_authority(): continue` (authority inputs excluded) and transaction_verifier.py:892-897 `if tx.has_mint_header(): for entry in tx.get_mint_header().entries: domain_generators.append(...)` (mint entries appended). The wallet's MintHeader is attached AFTER proof creation (src/utils/transaction.ts:1070-1075 in _attachShieldedHeaders), so the proof can never have seen those generators.
findings.recommendation: Mirror the node's domain construction exactly: skip inputs whose `authorities !== 0n` when pushing inputGenerators, and pass the tx's MintHeader entries' token UIDs into createShieldedOutputs so buildSurjectionDomain appends their derived asset tags in the same order the verifier uses (inputs in tx order, then mint entries). Add an integration test covering a FullShielded send that includes an authority input and a mint.

findings.title: Value and asset blinding factors persisted in plaintext while spend keys are PIN-encrypted
findings.description: After a shielded output is decrypted, the recovered value blinding factor and asset blinding factor are written to wallet storage as raw lowercase hex on both the appended history output entry and the saved UTXO record. Unlike the wallet's signing keys (xpriv/scan/spend keys are stored via encryptData under the PIN), the blinding factors are stored unencrypted. Possession of (value, token, vbf[, abf]) for a commitment lets anyone recompute the Pedersen commitment and fully unblind every shielded output the wallet owns — recovering amounts and (for FullShielded) hidden token UIDs — and even produce the explorer unblinding payload to deanonymize the wallet's confidential history. An attacker with read access to the persisted store (backup file, IndexedDB, headless wallet DB) defeats the entire confidentiality guarantee of shielded outputs without ever needing the PIN. The node never stores blinding factors; the client guide treats their custody as a wallet responsibility.
findings.evidence: wallet-lib src/utils/storage.ts:1005-1006 `blindingFactor: result.decrypted.blindingFactor.toString('hex'), assetBlindingFactor: result.decrypted.assetBlindingFactor?.toString('hex')` written directly onto the IShieldedOutputEntry; src/types.ts:352-353 (`blindingFactor?: string; assetBlindingFactor?: string;` plain hex on history output) and src/types.ts:473-474 (same on IUtxo). Contrast with PIN-encrypted key material declared at src/types.ts:501-509 (scanMainKey/spendMainKey use IEncryptedData). Client guide section 2.0 / key warnings: blinding factors are sensitive secret material kept by the wallet (SHIELDED_INTEGRATION_REVIEW/reference/client-guide-checklist.md:32,121).
findings.recommendation: Encrypt vbf/abf at rest with the same PIN-derived key used for the main keys (store as IEncryptedData and decrypt on demand inside the send/unblinding paths), or document that consumers MUST provide an encrypted IStore backend. At minimum gate getShieldedUnblindingForTx and any export behind the PIN so plaintext factors are never the default on-disk representation.

findings.title: Shielded output value not pre-validated against the 40-bit range-proof bound
findings.description: createShieldedOutputs validates scanPubkey length and token-UID length up front but never checks that each proposal value is within [1, 2^40), the fixed range-proof domain enforced by ct-crypto (RANGE_PROOF_BITS=40, min_value=1). A value of 0 or >= 2^40 is passed straight into createAmountShieldedOutput / createShieldedOutputWithBothBlindings, where RangeProof::new throws deep inside the native binding. The result is a late, opaque crypto error attributed to a generic 'Failed to create shielded output' wrapper rather than a clear up-front rejection. This is the documented caller responsibility (ground truth section 10: 'Value fits 40 bits ... a wallet must not attempt amounts >= 2^40 — proof creation fails, not a graceful node-side message'). Impact is bounded because it throws rather than silently misbuilding, and 2^40 centi-units (~1.1e10 HTR) is far above realistic single-output amounts today.
findings.evidence: wallet-lib src/shielded/creation.ts:271-309 validates pubkey/token lengths and FullShielded input presence but has no value-bound check; the value flows unchecked into buildAmountShieldedOutput/buildFullShieldedOutput (creation.ts:83-97,178-195). ct-crypto ground truth section 10 and rangeproof.rs:12-15,16,44-53 fix proofs at 40 bits, min_value=1; the binding accepts any u64 and throws inside RangeProof::new.
findings.recommendation: Validate `1 <= proposal.value < 2n**40n` for every proposal in the up-front validation loop of createShieldedOutputs (and for the converted HTR change in convertHtrChangeIfRequested), throwing a clear domain error before any crypto work.

result.notes:
Finding confirmed on both sides.

WALLET-LIB: ephemeral_pubkey is a required z.string() in all four cited schemas: src/schemas.ts:106 (AmountShieldedOutputSchema), :116 (FullShieldedOutputSchema), :191 (IHistoryShieldedOutputSchema), and src/api/schemas/txApi.ts:113. IHistoryOutputSchema (schemas.ts:122-126) is a union of TransparentOutputSchema (requires `value`) + the two shielded schemas (both require ephemeral_pubkey); a shielded entry without the key matches no branch, failing the whole tx via IHistoryTxSchema.outputs (schemas.ts:209) or .shielded_outputs (schemas.ts:215).

Parse sites confirmed: stream.ts:754 `IHistoryTxSchema.parse(wsData.data)` is inside buildListener (registered at stream.ts:813-814) with no try/catch — a ZodError throws synchronously in the WS event handler, leaving the sync promise unresolved (SYNCING hang). wallet.ts:1736-1740 uses safeParse → logs error → return (silent drop of a tx that may also pay the wallet transparently).

CORE (source of truth): _shielded_output_to_json OMITS the key when empty — base_transaction.py:101-102 `if output.ephemeral_pubkey: data['ephemeral_pubkey'] = output.ephemeral_pubkey.hex()`. Both to_json (line 817) and to_json_extended (line 888) use this serializer; the WS history streamer emits to_json_extended (streamer.py:300, factory.py:215), which is what the stream/onNewTx paths consume. Empty ephemeral pubkey is consensus-valid: transaction_verifier.py:787 validates pubkey only `if output.ephemeral_pubkey`. Wire format treats 33 zero bytes as absent/decoded to b'' (hathorlib/transaction/shielded_tx_output.py:104, 166, 205).

Refutation attempts failed: normalizeShieldedOutputs (utils/transaction.ts:185, called at wallet.ts ~1750 and presumably stream path) cannot rescue this — it runs AFTER schema .parse/.safeParse in both code paths, so a tx already rejected by the schema never reaches normalization. No .optional() handling exists anywhere for ephemeral_pubkey in the schemas.

Severity high is appropriate: remote-triggerable, causes stuck SYNCING (uncaught throw) and silent missed-funds drops; absence is genuinely producible by any third party (burn-style shielded outputs)."

findings.title: Wallet schemas require ephemeral_pubkey/decoded keys that hathor-core legitimately omits — consensus-valid shielded txs become unparseable and break sync
findings.description: hathor-core treats an all-zeros ephemeral_pubkey as 'absent' and OMITS the JSON key entirely in both _shielded_output_to_json and to_json_extended; it also omits `decoded` whenever the shielded script doesn't parse. Such outputs are fully consensus-valid (a third party can craft them with off-the-shelf tooling and pay any wallet address in the same tx). Wallet-lib's zod schemas make these keys REQUIRED: AmountShieldedOutputSchema/FullShieldedOutputSchema (outputs[] union, used for the extended-JSON shapes) require `ephemeral_pubkey`; IHistoryShieldedOutputSchema requires `ephemeral_pubkey` AND `decoded`; fullnodeTxApiShieldedOutputSchema requires both. Consequences: (a) WS real-time path — IHistoryTxSchema.safeParse fails and the ENTIRE tx is silently dropped, including its transparent outputs paying the wallet (src/new/wallet.ts:1736-1740); (b) polling sync — addressHistorySchema (IHistoryTxSchema.array()) is applied as an axios transformResponse, so one such tx anywhere in the wallet's history poisons every subsequent history page load (src/api/wallet.ts:44-47,86-89); (c) stream sync — IHistoryTxSchema.parse throws (src/sync/stream.ts:754); (d) /transaction fetch for input resolution fails (src/utils/transaction.ts:1104-1117). Since the tx stays in the address history forever, this is a persistent, attacker-triggerable sync failure / missed-funds condition. Additionally src/shielded/types.ts:48 types ephemeral_pubkey as required and src/shielded/processing.ts:122 does Buffer.from(shieldedOutput.ephemeral_pubkey,'hex') OUTSIDE the per-output try (line 133), so even if the schema were relaxed, one keyless output aborts decryption of every other shielded output in the tx.
findings.evidence: wallet-lib: src/schemas.ts:106 `ephemeral_pubkey: z.string()` (AmountShieldedOutputSchema), :116 (FullShieldedOutputSchema), :191-192 `ephemeral_pubkey: z.string(); decoded: IHistoryOutputDecodedSchema` (IHistoryShieldedOutputSchema, no .optional()); src/api/schemas/txApi.ts:113-114 `ephemeral_pubkey: z.string(), decoded: shieldedDecodedSchema` required; src/new/wallet.ts:1736-1740 parse failure → `return` (tx dropped); src/sync/stream.ts:754 `IHistoryTxSchema.parse(wsData.data)`; src/shielded/processing.ts:122 vs try at :133. hathor-core: hathorlib/hathorlib/transaction/shielded_tx_output.py:103-104,158-166 (33 zero bytes = absent, consensus-valid); hathor/transaction/base_transaction.py:82-113 ('ephemeral_pubkey' omitted when empty; 'decoded' only when decode_script and script parses); verification accepts absent eph pubkey (transaction_verifier.py:787-798 only validates when present).
findings.recommendation: Make `ephemeral_pubkey` optional (default '') and `decoded` optional in all four shielded schemas (schemas.ts:101-126, 174-197; txApi.ts:96-124) and in IShieldedOutput (shielded/types.ts:48). In processing.ts, skip outputs with empty/absent ephemeral_pubkey (they are unrecoverable by design) and move the buffer decoding inside the per-output try/catch so one malformed output cannot abort the loop. Add a regression test feeding a tx with a keyless and a script-unparseable shielded output through the WS, polling and txApi paths.

findings.title: Stream-based history sync (XPUB_STREAM_WS / MANUAL_STREAM_WS) never covers shielded-spend addresses — restored wallets miss all shielded receives
findings.description: The polling sync path correctly derives the shielded pair at every BIP32 index and includes the spend-derived P2PKH in the address set sent to the fullnode (utils/storage.ts:117-131). The two streaming modes do not: `loadAddressesCPUIntensive` is documented as 'only contemplates P2PKH addresses for now' and derives solely from the account-0 xpub (`accessData.xpubkey`, m/44'/280'/0'); `generateNextBatch` sends only those addresses in manual mode; in xpub mode the fullnode derives from the same account-0 xpub server-side. Shielded spend addresses live on a different account (m/44'/280'/2'/0, SHIELDED_SPEND_ACCT_PATH), so the fullnode can never match them. The stream consumer does derive and save the shielded address pair locally per streamed address (stream.ts:566-585), which masks the gap — addresses exist in storage but the history vertices paying ONLY shielded-spend scripts are never streamed. A wallet restored from seed using a streaming sync mode reports zero shielded funds and may double-show/missing-track balances when those UTXOs are later spent. There is no guard refusing stream modes when shielded keys exist (new/wallet.ts:3784-3801 falls back to polling only when the node lacks the capability).
findings.evidence: wallet-lib: src/sync/stream.ts:234-253 (`loadAddressesCPUIntensive ... This only contemplates P2PKH addresses for now`, derives `new BitcoreAddress(key.publicKey)` from `this.xpubkey`), :482-486 (manual batch sends only that list), :566-585 (shielded pair saved locally only); contrast src/utils/storage.ts:117-131 (polling path pushes `shieldedResult.spendAddress.base58` into the queried address set). hathor-core: the WS streamer matches vertices against the addresses/xpub provided (hathor/websocket/streamer.py:300) — it has no knowledge of the wallet-side scan/spend account convention (core defines no shielded address format at all, hathor/conf/mainnet.py:23-24).
findings.recommendation: In manual-stream mode, interleave the spend-derived P2PKH addresses (account 2') into each `sendManualStreamingHistory` batch. For xpub-stream mode, either send a second stream for the spend xpub or refuse/downgrade to polling when access data carries spendXpubkey. At minimum, hard-fail `syncHistory` for stream modes when shielded keys are present instead of silently missing funds.

findings.title: Multisig wallets derive and expose single-sig shielded addresses — the shielded receive chain silently bypasses the P2SH multisig policy
findings.description: `generateAccessDataFromSeed` derives the shielded scan/spend keys unconditionally from the LOCAL participant's root seed, including when walletType === MULTISIG (the multisig branch only affects the legacy account derivation). `loadAddresses` then derives and saves shielded address pairs for every wallet that has scanXpubkey/spendXpubkey with no wallet-type gating (utils/storage.ts:117-131), and `getCurrentAddress({legacy:false})`/`getAddressAtIndex(i,{legacy:false})` happily hand out these addresses on a multisig wallet. The on-chain spend script for a shielded address is a plain P2PKH over the single spend pubkey (utils/shieldedAddress.ts:91-92), and the signing path signs shielded-spend inputs with the single spend-chain key (utils/transaction.ts:413-416). Net effect: funds received at a 'multisig wallet's' shielded address are controlled by exactly one cosigner's seed — the m-of-n policy is bypassed — and the other cosigners (who hold different seeds, hence different scan/spend chains) can neither see nor co-sign them. Core offers no shielded multisig concept to lean on (no shielded address format exists in consensus), so the wallet must gate this itself; nothing in the integration does.
findings.evidence: wallet-lib: src/utils/wallet.ts:686-692 (multisig branch for legacy key) vs :710-715,726-729 (scan/spend keys derived unconditionally from rootXpriv); src/utils/storage.ts:117-131 (no WalletType check before deriveShieldedAddressFromStorage); src/utils/address.ts:178-211 (no multisig gating); src/utils/transaction.ts:413-416 (shielded-spend inputs signed single-sig via getSpendXPrivKey); src/utils/shieldedAddress.ts:91-92 (spend address = P2PKH of one pubkey). hathor-core: hathor/conf/mainnet.py:23-24 — only P2PKH/MULTISIG version bytes exist; consensus is agnostic, so the custody policy is purely wallet-side and currently violated.
findings.recommendation: Skip shielded scan/spend key derivation (or at least shielded address chain loading and `{legacy:false}` address exposure) when walletType === MULTISIG, until a multisig-aware shielded scheme exists (e.g. P2SH spend scripts + shared scan key). Throw a clear error from getCurrentAddress/getAddressAtIndex with legacy:false on multisig wallets.

findings.title: Shielded change exists only for HTR — partial spends of shielded custom-token UTXOs always emit transparent change, revealing the remaining balance (and the token for FullShielded holdings)
findings.description: The only shielded-change mechanism is `convertHtrChangeIfRequested`, which is explicitly filtered to `token === HTR_UID && isChange` and additionally no-ops when the tx has no other shielded outputs. Custom-token change is produced by `_prepareSendTokensData`/`prepareSendManyTokensData` as a plain transparent output (sendTransaction.ts:1164-1178) with no conversion hook and no `changeShieldedMode` participation. So every partial spend of a shielded custom-token UTXO publicly discloses the wallet's remaining balance of that token as a transparent change output; for tokens held as FullShielded the transparent change also forces the token UID into `tokens[]` (the privacy guard at sendTransaction.ts:593-604 adds any token referenced by a transparent output), undoing the asset-hiding the user paid 2 HTR per output for. The node enforces none of this (privacy hygiene is explicitly wallet territory per the client guide), and the wallet auto-selects shielded UTXOs by default (utils/utxo.ts:99-110 comment), making the leak the default outcome rather than an informed choice. A user can only avoid it by hand-picking inputs and appending an explicit shielded self-output.
findings.evidence: wallet-lib: src/new/sendTransaction.ts:1076-1080 (`withToken.token === HTR_UID && withToken.isChange === true`), :1069 (`if (shieldedOutputDefs.length === 0) return`), :1164-1178 (transparent change output for any token), :593-604 (transparent change pulls token into tokens[]); src/utils/utxo.ts:99-110 (selector mixes shielded UTXOs into any send by default). hathor-core/guide: SHIELDED_INTEGRATION_REVIEW/reference/client-guide-checklist.md:125 (transaction-layer privacy hygiene is client-side); hathor/verification has no rule on change visibility — wallet's responsibility (ground truth §10 'Address/script reuse, output ordering, decoy values ... entirely client-side').
findings.recommendation: Extend changeShieldedMode (or a per-token option) to convert custom-token change to a shielded output to a fresh own shielded address, mirroring convertHtrChangeIfRequested (add the per-output fee, keep the ≥2-outputs invariant). Until then, default the UTXO selector to prefer transparent UTXOs for transparent sends (the `shielded` filter already exists in IUtxoFilterOptions, types.ts:606-610) and document the leak.

findings.title: Readonly/xpub wallets have zero shielded capability and there is no scan-key (view-key) import path — balances silently incomplete
findings.description: `generateAccessDataFromXpub` produces access data with no scanXpubkey/spendXpubkey/scanMainKey/spendMainKey, and there is no start option to provide them. Consequently `deriveShieldedAddressFromStorage` returns null (utils/address.ts:182-186), no shielded chain is loaded (utils/storage.ts:382,425), and rewind is impossible anyway because decryption requires the PIN-encrypted scan xpriv (storage.ts:968-976, processing.ts:65). A readonly wallet opened over a seed that actively uses shielded outputs therefore reports balances that silently exclude all shielded funds — there is no warning and no 'has shielded' signal surfaced (the node's `has_shielded` hints on address_balance/address_search are also not consumed anywhere in src/). The RFC's design point that the scan key grants view-only access (separate account 1' exactly so it can be shared) is not exploitable: even handing the wallet a scan xpriv has no entry point. migrateShieldedAccessData explicitly bails for xpub-only records (utils/wallet.ts:771 `if (!accessData.words) return false`).
findings.evidence: wallet-lib: src/utils/wallet.ts:476 (generateAccessDataFromXpub — no shielded fields in its return), :768-771 (migration no-op without seed); src/utils/address.ts:182-186 (null without scanXpub/spendXpub); src/storage/storage.ts:968-976 (getScanXPrivKey requires scanMainKey+PIN); grep for has_shielded in src/ → no hits. hathor-core: /v1a/thin_wallet/address_balance and address_search expose `has_shielded` (hathor/wallet/resources/thin_wallet/address_balance.py:103-157, address_search.py:138-146) precisely as the transparent-only-balance warning hint; RFC scan/spend split (rfc-summary.md:11) exists to enable view-only wallets.
findings.recommendation: Either (a) add a start path accepting scanXpub+spendXpub (watch addresses, flag funds as shielded-undecodable) and optionally an encrypted scan xpriv for full view-only decoding, or (b) at minimum surface a 'shielded activity present but not visible' indicator (the fullnode's has_shielded flag or detection of shielded_outputs in history) so readonly users are not shown silently wrong balances.

findings.title: Wallet-service wallet has no shielded support and the new core events-API plumbing is not consumed anywhere in wallet-lib
findings.description: Core's alpha-v4 HEAD commit exists specifically to carry shielded data through the event-queue API that the wallet-service stack consumes (pydantic ShieldedTxOutput, TxData.shielded_outputs, TxInput.spent_output smart union). On the wallet-lib side, HathorWalletServiceWallet received only defensive tolerance: getTxBalance skips inputs without `token` (shielded inputs) and the storage proxy passes type='shielded' entries through untouched. There is: no shielded address derivation (getAddressAtIndex at wallet.ts:1495 implements the updated IHathorWallet signature without the IAddressChainOptions param — callers passing {legacy:false} silently get legacy addresses), no crypto-provider hook, no rewind/decoding, no shielded create path in its sendTransaction flows, and no balance contribution from shielded receives. Wallet-lib as a whole contains zero consumers of the event-queue API (grep for v1a/event / NEW_VERTEX_ACCEPTED → none), which is fine for the fullnode wallet (legacy WS is shielded-aware) but means the events groundwork has no wallet-lib counterpart at all. Result: a user moving funds to shielded via the fullnode wallet sees them vanish if they open the same seed in the wallet-service wallet — same silent-incompleteness failure mode as COMP-05, with no guard or warning.
findings.evidence: wallet-lib: src/wallet/wallet.ts:763-780 (only change: `if (txin.token === undefined) continue;`), :1495 `async getAddressAtIndex(index: number)` (no opts despite IHathorWallet at src/wallet/types.ts:363 declaring `opts?: IAddressChainOptions`); src/wallet/walletServiceStorageProxy.ts:192-199 (pass-through only); grep 'shielded' in src/wallet/wallet.ts → 2 hits (both the balance skip); grep -rln 'v1a/event|NEW_VERTEX_ACCEPTED' src/ → empty. hathor-core: hathor/event/model/event_data.py:46-69,160-172 (ShieldedTxOutput model + TxData.shielded_outputs added at HEAD 00a4532b so the event stream carries shielded receives to the wallet-service daemon).
findings.recommendation: Document the wallet-service wallet as explicitly non-shielded for this release and make it fail loudly: throw from getAddressAtIndex/getCurrentAddress when shielded opts are passed, and surface a warning when service history contains shielded entries the wallet cannot value. Track full parity (shielded address chain registration with the service, blinding storage, decode via provider) as the follow-up the core events plumbing anticipates.

findings.title: Minting token supply directly into shielded outputs (incl. shielded TCT) is unreachable — core supports it, wallet-lib has no producer
findings.description: hathor-core explicitly supports creating/minting supply straight into shielded outputs: a TCT may carry a ShieldedOutputsHeader and MUST then carry a MintHeader with token_index=1 (token_creation_transaction_verifier.py:30-75), and MintHeader entries extend the surjection domain so freshly minted assets can be claimed by FullShielded outputs (transaction_verifier.py:892-897). Wallet-lib's `_attachShieldedHeaders` even contains the TCT branch for txData.shieldedOutputs (utils/transaction.ts:879-884) and the createToken MintHeader sentinel logic (utils/transaction.ts:996-1071) — but no flow ever populates `shieldedOutputs` on a create-token or mint txData: `prepareCreateTokenData`/`prepareMintTxData` build only transparent outputs (src/utils/tokens.ts has zero shielded references), `prepareCreateNewToken` exposes no shielded option (src/new/wallet.ts:2243-2313), and passing a shielded address as mint/recipient address is silently downgraded to the transparent spend-derived P2PKH (integration test K.1/K.4 pin this as the intended behavior). So the supported flows are only 'shielded-funded' TCT/mint (shielded HTR inputs → UnshieldBalanceHeader, handled by prepareTransaction's detection at utils/transaction.ts:1338-1422). Minting privately requires two transactions (mint transparent, then shield), doubling fees and leaking the minted amount's initial owner on-chain — a real feature gap versus core.
findings.evidence: wallet-lib: grep 'shielded' src/utils/tokens.ts → no hits; src/new/wallet.ts:2243-2313 (CreateTokenOptions has no shielded fields); src/utils/transaction.ts:879-884,996-1071 (TCT header attach + NEW_TOKEN_KEY sentinel exist but `txData.shieldedOutputs` has no producer in any token flow); __tests__/integration/shielded_outputs/token_creation.test.ts:7-12 ('Group K ... resolves the shielded recipient to the corresponding spend-derived P2PKH' — i.e. transparent). hathor-core: hathor/verification/token_creation_transaction_verifier.py:30-75 (shielded TCT requires MintHeader token_index 1 — a fully supported construction); hathor/verification/transaction_verifier.py:892-897 (mint entries extend surjection domain for minting into FullShielded); vertex_verifier.py:247-260 (TCT may carry all four shielded headers).
findings.recommendation: Add a `shielded`/`shieldedMode` option to prepareCreateTokenData and prepareMintTxData that routes the minted amount through createShieldedOutputs (plus the required second shielded output and per-output fee) and emits the MintHeader entry, reusing the existing _attachShieldedHeaders TCT branch. Until implemented, document that shielded addresses in token flows produce transparent outputs.

findings.title: Timelocked shielded receives are treated as never locked — wallet offers them for spending and builds txs the node rejects
findings.description: The wallet can CREATE timelocked shielded outputs (ShieldedOutputProposal.timelock is threaded into the on-chain script at src/shielded/creation.ts:326-338 and exposed via ProposedOutput.timelock at src/new/wallet.ts:1997). On the receive side, however, the decoded entry stored for a wallet-owned shielded output copies only what the fullnode sent: core's _shielded_output_to_json emits `decoded: {address}` with NO timelock for shielded outputs (ground truth §1.1/gotcha 5), and processNewTx builds the entry as `decoded: { ...so?.decoded, address: result.address }` without parsing the locally-available script hex for a timelock. `isOutputLocked` then sees `decoded.timelock === undefined` → unlocked (utils/transaction.ts:305-316), so the UTXO is saved unlocked and the selector will pick it before the lock expires; the resulting spend fails fullnode script verification. Two wallets exchanging timelocked shielded payments (a flow the send side explicitly supports) hit this on the receiving end.
findings.evidence: wallet-lib: src/utils/storage.ts:990 (`decoded: { ...so?.decoded, address: result.address }` — no script parse for timelock), :1032-1033 (isOutputLocked on that decoded); src/utils/transaction.ts:305-316 (undefined timelock → unlocked); send side supports timelock: src/shielded/creation.ts:331 (`timelock: proposal.timelock ?? null` in the output script), src/new/wallet.ts:1997. hathor-core: hathor/transaction/base_transaction.py:82-113 — shielded `decoded` carries only `address` (no timelock), and spent shielded scripts are evaluated identically to transparent ones (hathor/verification/transaction_verifier.py:202-203), so the node rejects an early spend.
findings.recommendation: When appending decoded shielded entries in processNewTx, parse the (already hex-normalized) script with the existing script parser and populate decoded.timelock/type from it instead of trusting the fullnode's minimal decoded block. Add a unit test: receive a timelocked shielded output, assert the UTXO is reported locked until the timelock.

findings.title: No pre-validation that shielded values are in [1, 2^40) — out-of-range amounts fail deep inside native crypto with a misleading error
findings.description: ct-crypto range proofs are pinned at 40 bits with min_value=1: `create*ShieldedOutput` throws for value 0 or ≥ 2^40, and the client guide makes the range check an explicit wallet responsibility ('the binding accepts any u64'). createShieldedOutputs validates scanPubkey length and token UID lengths up front (lines 272-309) precisely to avoid confusing downstream failures, but performs no value-range check; neither does the sendTransaction shielded-def collection (src/new/sendTransaction.ts:240-257) nor convertHtrChangeIfRequested. A user shielding ≥ 10,995,116,277.76 HTR-equivalent units (well within the 2^63 transparent OutputValueType range) or constructing a 0-value shielded output gets a wrapped native error ('Failed to create shielded output i/N ... RangeProof...') after the wallet has already done UTXO selection and N-1 expensive proofs, instead of an immediate, actionable validation error. No node-side message exists either — the failure is purely local but cryptic.
findings.evidence: wallet-lib: src/shielded/creation.ts:272-309 (upfront validation block lacks any value check), :91-98/:187-195 (values passed straight to provider). ct-crypto: hathor-core-shielded/hathor-ct-crypto/src/rangeproof.rs:12-16,44-53 (RANGE_PROOF_BITS=40, min_value=1); ground-truth ct-crypto reference §4 'Caller responsibilities: values must be in [1, 2^40)'; client-guide-checklist.md (wallet must not attempt amounts ≥ 2^40).
findings.recommendation: Add `if (proposal.value < 1n || proposal.value >= 1n << 40n) throw` to the upfront validation loop in createShieldedOutputs (and mirror it where shielded defs are first accepted in sendManyOutputsSendTransaction), with an error message stating the 40-bit CT limit.

findings.title: PartialTx (atomic swap) and transaction-template paths have no shielded awareness
findings.description: Neither the PartialTx multi-party proposal flow nor the transaction-template engine (src/template/transaction/*) was extended for shielded outputs/inputs: grep over both subsystems finds zero shielded references. Templates cannot emit shielded outputs or the new headers, and a PartialTx that references an input spending a shielded slot (input.index ≥ len(parent.outputs), the combined index space the node uses) will resolve the wrong output or fail when it positionally indexes the parent's outputs — the sparse-decode-aware `findSpentOutput` helper added for the main pipeline (utils/transaction.ts:129-157) is not used there. These are secondary, explicitly multi-party/advanced paths, so this is recorded as a scoping gap rather than a bug in the primary flows; but nothing guards them (no throw when a shielded address or shielded-spending input enters a swap/template), so failures will be confusing rather than fail-fast. The user-side TODO_FIX_40 (partial-tx shielded address validation) confirms this is known but unlanded.
findings.evidence: wallet-lib: grep -rn 'shielded' src/models/partial_tx.ts src/template/ → no hits; combined-index lookups in PartialTx use positional outputs access while the main pipeline needed transactionUtils.findSpentOutput (src/utils/transaction.ts:129-157) for correctness. hathor-core: input index space is unified across transparent+shielded (hathor/transaction/base_transaction.py:348-363), so any path resolving spent outputs positionally is wrong for shielded-bearing parents.
findings.recommendation: Short term: validate-and-throw — reject shielded addresses in template/PartialTx output builders and reject inputs whose index falls in the parent's shielded range. Long term: route PartialTx spent-output resolution through findSpentOutput and design shielded support for swaps separately (blinding-factor exchange between parties is non-trivial).

result.notes:
CONFIRMED. Both sides verified against source of truth.

CORE (omits key / empty pubkey is consensus-valid):
- /tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor/transaction/base_transaction.py:101-102 — `if output.ephemeral_pubkey: data['ephemeral_pubkey'] = output.ephemeral_pubkey.hex()`. Key is OMITTED from JSON when the pubkey is empty.
- This serializer (_shielded_output_to_json) is reached by both wallet-facing paths: to_json_extended (base_transaction.py:888-896, decode_script=True) which is published as `wallet:address_history` (indexes/address_index.py:88-90, the onNewTx real-time path) AND the stream vertex serialization (websocket/streamer.py:300 to_json_extended).
- Empty ephemeral_pubkey is consensus-valid: verifier validates the pubkey ONLY `if output.ephemeral_pubkey` (transaction_verifier.py:787-798). Absence is never rejected.
- Wire format: hathorlib shielded_tx_output.py:104 writes 33 zero bytes when empty, and :166 decodes `b'\x00'*33` back to `b''` (absent). vertex_exporter.py:573 even constructs consensus-valid outputs with `ephemeral_pubkey = b''` when no recipient pubkey is available.

WALLET-LIB (requires the key):
- src/schemas.ts:106 (AmountShieldedOutputSchema), :116 (FullShieldedOutputSchema), :191 (IHistoryShieldedOutputSchema): all `ephemeral_pubkey: z.string()` — required, no .optional().
- src/api/schemas/txApi.ts:113: `ephemeral_pubkey: z.string()` — required.
- Stream path: src/sync/stream.ts:754 `IHistoryTxSchema.parse(wsData.data)` runs inside the 'stream' event listener (buildListener, registered at :814) with NO surrounding try/catch — a ZodError propagates out of the listener and `resolve()` (passed in) is never reached, hanging the sync promise. Parse happens BEFORE any normalize (normalizeShieldedOutputs runs later in storage/onNewTx), so the required-field check fails first.
- Real-time path: src/new/wallet.ts:1736-1740 `IHistoryTxSchema.safeParse(wsData.history)` → on failure logs and `return`s, silently dropping the entire tx (including any transparent outputs that pay the wallet).

A consensus-valid tx with a shielded output carrying no ephemeral pubkey (e.g., recipient pubkey unknown / burn-style) that also touches a subscribed/victim address fails all branches of the IHistoryOutput/shielded schema, sinking the whole IHistoryTx parse. Remote-triggerable by any third party broadcasting such a tx.

Severity high is appropriate: remote-triggerable, causes either a SYNCING hang (uncaught throw, stream) or silent loss of funds-bearing txs (safeParse drop, real-time). Recommendation in finding (make ephemeral_pubkey .optional() in all four schemas + treat absence as non-recoverable skip) is sound; I found no existing handling that makes the field optional anywhere (src/types.ts:271,349 also type it as required `string`).

All six factual claims verified against both codebases. NODE (source of truth): hathor/transaction/base_transaction.py:101-102 omits ephemeral_pubkey from JSON when empty (`if output.ephemeral_pubkey:`); this serializer is used by to_json_extended:891 which the WS streamer emits (streamer.py:300). hathor/verification/transaction_verifier.py:786 validates the pubkey only `if output.ephemeral_pubkey`, so an empty (33-zero-byte = absent) ephemeral pubkey is consensus-valid; hathorlib shielded_tx_output.py:104,166 confirm zero-bytes==absent on wire, decoded to b''. WALLET-LIB: schemas.ts:106/116/191 and api/schemas/txApi.ts:113 all declare `ephemeral_pubkey: z.string()` (required) — notably these same schemas already mark mode/token_data/asset_commitment/spent_by optional, so this is an inconsistency. Node serializes shielded outputs into outputs[] (base_transaction.py:891), parsed by IHistoryOutputSchema union (schemas.ts:122); a shielded entry without ephemeral_pubkey fails both shielded branches and the transparent branch (TransparentOutputSchema requires `value`, schemas.ts:90), so the whole tx fails IHistoryTxSchema. Stream sync: stream.ts:754 IHistoryTxSchema.parse is uncaught in the buildListener 'stream' handler (connection.ts:102 emit -> websocket/index.ts:47 emit, no surrounding try/catch); the throw skips manager.addTx(:755) so lastSeenSeq/lastProcSeq never advance and the vertex is never ACKed (ACK flow control stream.ts:508-533), which can stall the window and hang the wallet in SYNCING. Real-time: wallet.ts:1736-1740 safeParse -> log + return silently drops the whole tx, so a tx that also pays the wallet transparently becomes invisible. Trigger is remote and not even strictly adversarial — any burn-style/empty-ephemeral shielded output (legitimately producible by the node) in a tx touching a victim address trips it; an attacker can deliberately craft one. Funds remain recoverable on-chain (not lost), but remote-triggerable sync stall + silent drop of funds-bearing txs justify high. Recommendation (.optional() in all four schemas + skip-rewind on absence + regression test) is correct and consistent with existing optional-field handling in the same schemas.

CONFIRMED. validateAddress (/tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/models/address.ts:99-106 length 25-or-71; :122-128 version byte via isVersionByteValid, /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/models/network.ts:169-176 accepts any of p2pkh/p2sh/shielded) checks length and version byte independently — any cross combination with a recomputed checksum validates. getType (address.ts:144-159) keys purely on first byte, so a 25-byte address with byte 0x3c/0x5d returns 'shielded'; getScanPubkey (address.ts:185-191) then returns a clamped 24-byte subarray(1,34) (includes checksum bytes; Buffer.subarray never throws) and getSpendPubkey (:202-208) returns an empty buffer. No compensating validation exists anywhere: grep over the worktree shows all callers (src/utils/address.ts:132, src/utils/transaction.ts:813, src/new/sendTransaction.ts:1092-1107, nano_contracts/fields/address.ts) use the same validateAddress; there is no decodeShieldedAddress validator; encode side (src/utils/shieldedAddress.ts:40-51) enforces 33-byte 02/03 pubkeys but decode does not; __tests__/models/address.test.ts has no cross-combination test. Source-of-truth check confirms hathor-core defines no shielded address format (only P2PKH_VERSION_BYTE/MULTISIG_VERSION_BYTE, hathor/conf/settings.py:95; hathor/crypto/shielded/ has none), so wallet-lib alone enforces this convention. ONE MECHANISM DETAIL REFUTED: for a 71-byte address with the p2pkh version byte, P2PKH.createScript (src/models/p2pkh.ts:69-70,79) uses slice(1,-4) = 66 bytes, not bytes [1,21) — the resulting script pushes a 66-byte "hash" and is non-standard (P2PKH.identify p2pkh.ts:112 rejects it) and provably unspendable (HASH160 yields 20 bytes, OP_EQUALVERIFY always fails), i.e. a silent burn rather than a "spendable-looking output paying an arbitrary hash". This slightly weakens the worst-case impact but the core finding (no length↔version binding; malformed addresses validate and produce garbage pubkeys/scripts instead of validation errors) stands. Severity low is appropriate: requires a crafted address with deliberately recomputed checksum, attacker controls destination anyway, and downstream ct-crypto's 33-byte check or script unspendability bounds the damage. Recommendation (bind version==shielded ⇔ length==71, and validate 0x02/0x03 pubkey prefixes on decode) is sound.

CONFIRMED. (1) Wallet schemas require `decoded`: /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/api/schemas/txApi.ts:114 `decoded: shieldedDecodedSchema` (not optional) and src/schemas.ts:192 `decoded: IHistoryOutputDecodedSchema` (not optional). (2) Core omits the key for non-parsing scripts: /tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor/transaction/base_transaction.py:108-112 — `if decode_script: ... if script_type: data['decoded'] = {...}`; GET /transaction serializes via tx.to_json(decode_script=True) (resources/transaction.py:61 → base_transaction.py:816-820), so shielded outputs with non-standard scripts ship without `decoded`. (3) Such scripts are consensus-valid: only size (≤1024, vertex_verifier.py:185-189; hathorlib shielded_tx_output.py:27,143-148) and sigop checks; no standardness enforcement in the verifier, so the condition is remote-triggerable. (4) Impact path verified: txApi.getTransaction validates via transactionApiSchema (txApi.ts:86,175-183) through transformJsonBigIntResponse (txApi.ts:38; bigint.ts:133 = schema.parse, throws ZodError) → breaks getFullTxById (new/wallet.ts:3244) and the spent-tx fetch in convertTransactionToHistoryTx (utils/transaction.ts:1104-1117), which is also invoked in the wallet's own send flow (sendTransaction.ts:841). (5) Fix is safe: processing.ts:106 already null-chains `shieldedOutput.decoded?.address`. Minor correction to the finding: the schemas.ts:192 citation is latent rather than remote-triggerable — node history paths (to_json_extended: ws factory.py:215, streamer.py:300, address_history.py:262) merge shielded outputs into `outputs`, and those merged schemas (schemas.ts:101-126) do not require `decoded`; only the txApi /transaction path carries the separate shielded_outputs array with conditional `decoded`. Severity medium is appropriate: availability-only (tx-inspection/spent-tx-resolution DoS on specific lookups), no funds at risk, and main history sync is unaffected.

Confirmed on all points. (1) Wallet-lib encodes Base58(version(1)||scan_pubkey(33)||spend_pubkey(33)||checksum(4)): /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/utils/shieldedAddress.ts:28 (doc) and :53-57 (encode). (2) Version bytes 0x3c/0x5d defined only in /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/models/network.ts:17,24,31. (3) RFC sketches a different format scan_pubkey(33)||hash(spend_pubkey)(20): rfc-summary.md:11. (4) hathor-core defines no shielded address format or version byte — /tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor/conf/mainnet.py:23-24 has only P2PKH 0x28 and MULTISIG 0x64; grep for scan_pubkey/spend_pubkey across core Python returns nothing. (5) The official client integration guide (/tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor-ct-crypto/SHIELDED-OUTPUTS-CLIENT-GUIDE.md) contains no address format section at all, so a second client has no spec to follow except the divergent RFC sketch. Consensus is indeed agnostic (only script + ephemeral_pubkey go on-chain), so nothing breaks today — info severity is correct; this is a real cross-client interop standardization gap, not a code bug.

findings.title: Voided/reorged spending txs permanently destroy the wallet's UTXO records: unconditional input-delete loop in processHistory + irreversible local spent_by stamping
findings.description: Two new mechanisms break reorg/voided-tx recovery, for both transparent and shielded UTXOs. (1) processHistory's new per-tx input-deletion loop runs for EVERY tx in history, including voided ones — processNewTx returns early for voided txs (line 906) but the delete loop right after it has no is_voided guard, so a voided spender deletes the origin tx's perfectly-unspent UTXO on every reprocess. (2) processNewTx now locally stamps `origOutput.spent_by = tx.tx_id` on the stored origin tx and persists it via store.saveTx. When the spending tx is later voided, nothing ever clears that stamp: cleanMetadata wipes utxos/metadata but NOT history (memory_store.ts:1121-1126), and the rebuild loop skips saveUtxo for any output with `spent_by !== null` (utils/storage.ts:1105). On master, spent_by came only from fullnode payloads, so a processHistory after voiding resurrected the UTXO. Now the UTXO is never resurrected, while the balance (computed additively from the output loop, which skips the voided tx's debits) still shows the funds — the wallet displays a balance it cannot spend until a full storage wipe + resync. For shielded UTXOs it is worse: the decoded entry in the stored origin tx keeps the stale spent_by, and the onNewTx merge logic (src/new/wallet.ts:1782-1798) deliberately preserves the stored decoded entries over the wire form, so even a fullnode redelivery of the origin tx with spent_by=null cannot fix it. Voided txs are routine in Hathor (double-spend conflict resolution), and initial sync also delivers voided txs, so a fresh sync containing one voided spend of an own output already exhibits the bug.
findings.evidence: wallet-lib: /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/utils/storage.ts:511-533 (`for (const input of tx.inputs) { ... await store.deleteUtxo({...}) }` inside the historyIter loop, after processNewTx — no `tx.is_voided` check; processNewTx's own voided early-return is at lines 905-911); src/utils/storage.ts:1259-1272 (`origOutput.spent_by = tx.tx_id; ... await store.saveTx(origTx)`) with no reversal path; src/utils/storage.ts:1105 (`if (output.spent_by === null)` gates UTXO re-creation); src/storage/memory_store.ts:1121-1126 (cleanMetadata wipes utxos but not this.history, so stamps survive every reprocess); src/new/wallet.ts:1825-1830 (voided update routes to processHistory — the path that can no longer resurrect). git diff master...HEAD shows both blocks are new additions (master had neither the delete loop nor the stamping).
findings.recommendation: Gate the processHistory input-delete loop on `!tx.is_voided`. For the stamping: when processing a voided-state change, clear `spent_by` on origin outputs whose recorded spender is the now-voided tx (or only trust fullnode-provided spent_by and keep the local stamp in a separate, rebuildable metadata map instead of mutating persisted history). Add a regression test: receive UTXO → spend it → void the spending tx → assert the UTXO is selectable again and balance matches the UTXO set.

findings.title: onNewTx 'safety net' triggers a full processHistory on every websocket event whenever history contains shielded outputs the wallet can never decode — non-convergent O(N) reprocess per tx
findings.description: After every processed websocket tx (and every sender-local insert, which goes through the same enqueueOnNewTx path), onNewTx scans the ENTIRE tx history and, if any stored tx has `shielded_outputs` but no decoded shielded entry in outputs[], runs a full storage.processHistory. The retry condition is permanently true for txs whose shielded outputs simply do not belong to this wallet: (a) the wallet sends shielded outputs to external recipients with transparent change — processShieldedOutputs only appends entries for outputs whose decoded.address is the wallet's (src/shielded/processing.ts:107-111), so the sender's own tx never gets a decoded entry; (b) any incoming tx that pays the wallet transparently while also carrying shielded outputs to third parties. Once one such tx exists, EVERY subsequent websocket event triggers cleanMetadata + full history replay, including a PIN-based decryptData (KDF) call per shielded output per tx (processing.ts:65 getScanXPrivKey is invoked per output) and native rewind attempts that are guaranteed to fail again. State never converges, so the cost is paid forever: O(history × shielded outputs × KDF/crypto) per incoming tx, plus the full-metadata rewrite races and re-runs the STATE-01 deletion bug each time. The same false-positive also flips `shieldedNewlyAvailable` (line 1815) for every metadata-only update (e.g. first_block confirmation) of such a tx, forcing processHistory through the update branch too.
findings.evidence: wallet-lib: /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/new/wallet.ts:1851-1866 (`for await (const storedTx of this.storage.txHistory()) { ... if (!hasDecoded) { needsRetry = true; ... } } if (needsRetry) await this.storage.processHistory(this.pinCode)`) — no marker distinguishing 'decryption never attempted' from 'attempted, nothing is ours'; src/new/wallet.ts:1811-1815 (shieldedNewlyAvailable uses the same hasDecoded test); src/shielded/processing.ts:105-111 (ownership gate: outputs to non-wallet addresses are skipped, so sent-to-others txs can never satisfy hasDecoded); src/utils/storage.ts:963-1011 (decoded entries are the only thing that sets hasDecoded). hathor-core context: ≥2 shielded outputs are mandatory (transaction_verifier.py:1219-1227), so 'wallet pays one external shielded recipient' always produces ≥2 undecodable outputs in the sender's history.
findings.recommendation: Persist a per-tx marker after a decryption attempt (e.g. `shieldedProcessedAt` or `shieldedDecodeAttempted: true` on the stored IHistoryTx, or a count of owned outputs found) and make the safety net retry only txs never attempted with the current provider+pin. Also scope `shieldedNewlyAvailable` to txs where shielded_outputs were genuinely absent from the stored copy, not merely undecoded.

findings.title: Default UTXO selection mixes shielded UTXOs into nano-contract and template transactions, which never compute the excess blinding factor — node-rejected txs
findings.description: selectUtxos defaults to returning shielded UTXOs together with transparent ones (`undefined (default) → all UTXOs`, types.ts:606-610) and bestUtxoSelection/fastUtxoSelection intentionally do not filter (utxo.ts comment: 'Select any UTXO (transparent or shielded)'). The unshield machinery that makes this legal — computing the excess blinding factor and attaching the UnshieldBalanceHeader — lives only in SendTransaction.prepareTxData (sendTransaction.ts:543-571) and transactionUtils.prepareTransaction (transaction.ts:1347-1421). But two builder paths bypass prepareTransaction entirely: (1) NanoContractTransactionBuilder selects UTXOs via wallet.getUtxosForAmount (builder.ts:377, 760) and constructs the tx via createTransactionFromData (builder.ts:819, 868) — `_attachShieldedHeaders` only attaches an UnshieldBalanceHeader when `txData.excessBlindingFactor` is already set (transaction.ts:961-970), which nothing in the nano path computes — then signs directly through prepareNanoSendTransaction (nano_contracts/utils.ts:70-84); (2) the transaction-template interpreter selects from `this.wallet.storage.selectUtxos(newOptions)` with no shielded filter (template/transaction/interpreter.ts:252, 262) and builds the tx itself. hathor-core rejects any tx whose input index points into a parent's shielded_outputs when it has no shielded outputs and no UnshieldBalanceHeader: 'shielded inputs + no shielded outputs ⇒ header required' (transaction_verifier.py:1020-1045, invariant 2; is_shielded() in transaction.py:182-193 is triggered purely by input index ≥ len(spent_tx.outputs)). bestUtxoSelection prefers the single largest UTXO ≥ amount, so as soon as a wallet's largest HTR UTXO is shielded, every nano deposit and template-built tx fails after PoW with ShieldedBalanceMismatchError-class rejections.
findings.evidence: wallet-lib: /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/utils/utxo.ts:99-110 (no `shielded` filter in options, comment asserting core 'accepts shielded inputs in transparent-output-only txs' — true only WITH the header); src/types.ts:606-610 (`undefined (default) → all UTXOs (transparent + shielded)`); src/nano_contracts/builder.ts:377,760,819,868 (selection + createTransactionFromData, no excess computation); src/nano_contracts/utils.ts:70-84 (signs and sends without prepareTransaction); src/template/transaction/interpreter.ts:252,262; src/utils/transaction.ts:961-970 (UnshieldBalanceHeader only when txData.excessBlindingFactor pre-set). hathor-core: /tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor/verification/transaction_verifier.py:1020-1045 (mutual-exclusion invariant 2: shielded inputs with no shielded outputs MUST carry UnshieldBalanceHeader); hathor/transaction/transaction.py:182-193 (is_shielded via input index).
findings.recommendation: Either default `shielded: false` in selectUtxos for all callers that have not opted in (nano builder, template interpreter, any getUtxosForAmount consumer), or move the excess-blinding-factor computation of utils/transaction.ts:1347-1421 into createTransactionFromData/_attachShieldedHeaders so every construction path that can receive shielded inputs attaches the header. Add integration tests: nano deposit and template send while the wallet's largest UTXO is shielded.

findings.title: Plain transparent sends silently auto-unshield: shielded UTXOs are spent without user opt-in, permanently revealing previously-confidential amounts
findings.description: Because selectUtxos defaults to mixing shielded and transparent UTXOs, an ordinary `sendTransaction`/`sendManyOutputs` to a transparent recipient will pick shielded UTXOs whenever they rank first by value, and SendTransaction then quietly builds a full-unshield tx (excess blinding factor + UnshieldBalanceHeader). The tx is consensus-valid, but it deanonymizes the user's shielded holdings without consent: with the common single-shielded-input case the transparent outputs + fee publicly equal the shielded input's hidden value, and the revealed excess scalar IS that input's value blinding factor — hathor-core's own header docstring flags this leak and explicitly delegates the hygiene decision to wallets ('the leak is acceptable... leaving multi-input aggregation/hygiene to wallets', unshield_balance_header.py:42-47; rule inventory §10 'Unshield privacy leak'). A user who deliberately shielded funds has them silently unshielded by an unrelated transparent payment; there is no option on SendTransaction, sendManyOutputs, or IUtxoFilterOptions consumers' public APIs to say 'transparent funds only', and no warning/event when auto-unshield happens. This also contradicts the project's own convention of explicit options over inference for send/build APIs.
findings.evidence: wallet-lib: /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/utils/utxo.ts:99-110 (deliberate no-filter selection feeding bestUtxoSelection used by _prepareSendTokensData, sendTransaction.ts:1132); src/new/sendTransaction.ts:543-571 (silent full-unshield branch: `if (shieldedOutputs.length === 0 && blindedInputsArr.length > 0)` computes and ships the excess with no opt-in flag); src/types.ts:606-610 (default = all UTXOs). hathor-core: /tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor/transaction/headers/unshield_balance_header.py:42-47 (excess from a single shielded input ≈ that input's blinding factor; privacy hygiene is wallet responsibility); transaction_verifier.py docstring 931-950 (full-unshield equation makes the unblinded sum public).
findings.recommendation: Make spending shielded UTXOs in non-shielded sends opt-in: default `shielded: false` in the selection options used by _prepareSendTokensData and expose an explicit `allowUnshield`/`spendShielded` option on SendTransaction/sendManyOutputs (consistent with the explicit-options convention). When the caller opts in, surface that an unshield will occur (event or returned metadata) so UIs can warn the user.

findings.title: Decrypted values and blinding factors are persisted in plaintext in tx history and UTXO records
findings.description: processNewTx appends decoded shielded entries containing the plaintext recovered `value`, `blindingFactor` (hex) and `assetBlindingFactor` to tx.outputs and persists them via store.saveTx; saveUtxo likewise stores `blindingFactor`/`assetBlindingFactor` on every shielded IUtxo. Wallet key material is PIN-encrypted at rest (mainKey/scanMainKey/spendMainKey, types.ts:494-505), but these confidential-transaction openings are written unencrypted to whatever IStore backend the consumer uses (IndexedDB/LevelDB/AsyncStorage on desktop/mobile). Anyone with read access to the storage file can (a) read all confidential amounts and (b) — strictly worse than the legacy plaintext-history situation — extract the blinding factors, which constitute cryptographic PROOF of the amounts to third parties and remain valid forever on-chain (the commitment can be re-opened publicly). The PIN-gating of decryption (`pinCode !== undefined` at line 951-956) protects the scan key but not its output: after first decode, the PIN is no longer needed to read everything. hathor-core/ct-crypto never stores blindings; custody policy is explicitly a wallet responsibility (rule inventory §10 'Blinding-factor custody & sharing').
findings.evidence: wallet-lib: /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/utils/storage.ts:1005-1007 (`blindingFactor: result.decrypted.blindingFactor.toString('hex'), assetBlindingFactor: ...` on the entry appended to tx.outputs), 1010-1011 (`await store.saveTx(tx)` persists it), 1138-1144 (saveUtxo spread with shielded/blindingFactor/assetBlindingFactor); src/types.ts:472-474 (IUtxo.blindingFactor 'hex, 32 bytes' plaintext); contrast src/types.ts:503-505 (scanMainKey/spendMainKey ARE encrypted with PIN). hathor-core: blinding factors never touch the node; storage/encryption is wallet policy (hathor/crypto/shielded/balance.py is sender-side only; client-guide-checklist.md privacy section).
findings.recommendation: Encrypt the stored openings (blindingFactor/assetBlindingFactor, and ideally the recovered value) with the PIN-derived key, the same way access-data private keys are protected — or derive blinding factors deterministically from the scan key + ephemeral pubkey so they never need persisting and can be recomputed on demand. At minimum document the at-rest exposure for store implementors. (Tracked already as TODO_FIX_38 — should block release.)

findings.title: Timelocked shielded outputs are recorded as unlocked: receive path never parses the shielded script, so locked funds count as available and get selected for spends the node rejects
findings.description: wallet-lib's own send path supports timelocks on shielded outputs (sendManyOutputsSendTransaction forwards `timelock` for shielded outputs, and creation.ts builds the on-chain script with `timelock: proposal.timelock ?? null`), and the consensus script is evaluated identically to transparent ones on spend (transaction_verifier verify_script). But on the receive side, hathor-core's `_shielded_output_to_json` decoded object contains ONLY `{address}` — no timelock (base_transaction.py:82-113; API reference gotcha #5) — and processNewTx builds the decoded entry as `decoded: { ...so?.decoded, address: result.address }` without ever parsing the locally-available script hex. The resulting UTXO is saved with `timelock: output.decoded.timelock || null` = null, isOutputLocked returns false (line 1033), so the funds are counted in the UNLOCKED balance, never enter saveLockedUtxo/processUtxoUnlock, and selectUtxos offers them immediately. A spend attempt before the timelock expires is signed and mined, then rejected by the fullnode's script evaluation — and because the spend went through the local-insert flow, the wallet may already have deleted/marked the UTXO locally. Note the sender-local insert path DOES parse the script (`convertTransactionToHistoryTx`: `decoded: (parsed?.toData() ?? {})`, transaction.ts:1270), so self-sends and websocket receives of the same output disagree about its locked state.
findings.evidence: wallet-lib: /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/utils/storage.ts:990 (`decoded: { ...so?.decoded, address: result.address }` — no script parse), 1033 (isOutputLocked on the timelock-less decoded), 1136 (`timelock: output.decoded.timelock || null` → null); src/shielded/creation.ts:326-338 (sender embeds timelock in the shielded script); src/new/wallet.ts sendManyOutputsSendTransaction (`...(o.timelock != null ? { timelock: o.timelock } : {})` for shielded outputs); src/utils/transaction.ts:1260-1271 (local-insert path parses script — inconsistent with WS path). hathor-core: /tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor/transaction/base_transaction.py:82-113 (`decoded` = {address} only); transaction_verifier.py:202-203 (spent shielded scripts evaluated like transparent — timelock enforced on spend).
findings.recommendation: In the processNewTx decode block (and processShieldedOutputs), parse `so.script` with parseScript(network) and merge the full decoded data (address, timelock, type) into the appended entry, mirroring convertTransactionToHistoryTx. Add a unit test receiving a timelocked shielded output via the WS shape and asserting it lands in the locked balance and saveLockedUtxo.

findings.title: Stream history-sync modes (xpub stream and manual stream) never request history for shielded spend addresses — shielded funds invisible under these sync modes
findings.description: Shielded receives land on spend-derived P2PKH addresses from account m/44'/280'/2' (SHIELDED_SPEND_ACCT_PATH, constants.ts:356), a different BIP44 account than the legacy xpub (account 0'). The stream sync paths only communicate the legacy chain to the fullnode: xpub mode sends `accessData.xpubkey` (the account-0 xpub, stream.ts:416-423) from which the fullnode derives addresses — it cannot derive account-2' children; manual mode generates batches with `loadAddressesCPUIntensive(..., this.xpubkey, ...)` (stream.ts:482-487), again legacy-only. The integration added local derivation+saving of shielded address pairs when address items arrive (stream.ts:566-587), which makes the wallet LOOK shielded-aware, but the spend P2PKHs are never sent to the fullnode, so neither historical txs paying them nor the fullnode-side real-time subscription (which the streamer creates only for streamed addresses) cover them — `connection.subscribeAddresses` is only invoked from the polling apiSyncHistory path (utils/storage.ts:177). Net effect: a wallet configured with MANUAL_STREAM_WS or XPUB_STREAM_WS (the recommended modes for large wallets, used by headless) silently misses all shielded receives — balances omit them with no error, and the final `processHistory(pinCode)` (stream.ts:829-832) has nothing to decode.
