Funds appear only if the wallet is later restarted in polling mode.
findings.evidence: wallet-lib: /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/sync/stream.ts:420-423 (`const { xpubkey } = accessData; ... this.xpubkey = xpubkey` — legacy account xpub only), 482-495 (manual batches derived from this.xpubkey, no spend-chain addresses ever sent via sendManualStreamingHistory), 566-587 (shielded addresses saved locally but never transmitted); src/constants.ts:350-356 (scan account 1', spend account 2' — disjoint from the streamed xpub); src/utils/storage.ts:177 (subscribeAddresses only in apiSyncHistory/polling path). hathor-core: the address index is keyed by on-chain script address (base_transaction.py:508-512) — only addresses the fullnode knows about are streamed/subscribed; it has no way to derive account-2' addresses from an account-0 xpub.
findings.recommendation: In manual stream mode, interleave the spend-derived P2PKH addresses (same indexes) into the batches sent via sendManualStreamingHistory; for xpub mode, either also send the spend-chain xpub (m/44'/280'/2'/0) if the fullnode stream API supports multiple xpubs, or force-fallback to manual/polling when shielded keys exist. Until then, hard-error (or at least warn loudly) when historySyncMode is a stream mode and accessData.spendXpubkey is set.

findings.title: Shielded decryption is silently skipped when the wallet instance has no cached pinCode — start({pinCode}) does not enable it, and no error or warning is surfaced
findings.description: Every receive-side decode path is gated on `this.pinCode`: onNewTx passes `this.pinCode ?? undefined` to processNewTx/processHistory, the initial-sync and reload paths do the same, and processNewTx's decode block requires `pinCode !== undefined` (utils/storage.ts:951-956). `this.pinCode` is only ever set in the constructor (wallet.ts:387); `start({ pinCode })` reads the option into a local (line 2054) and uses it for access-data generation/migration but never assigns `this.pinCode`. A consumer that supplies the PIN per-operation (a long-standing supported pattern — every send method accepts `options.pinCode` precisely so the PIN need not live on the instance) gets: shielded addresses derived and subscribed, scan keys migrated into access data, but zero decryption — incoming shielded outputs are stored raw, no UTXOs are saved, balances silently omit shielded funds, and the wallet's own shielded change becomes untracked after a send (the local insert also decodes via this.pinCode). No log line or error distinguishes 'no provider' from 'no pin'; the STATE-02 safety net cannot fire either since it is also gated on this.pinCode. This is an availability gap with a confusing failure mode rather than data loss (a later restart with constructor pinCode recovers via processHistory), but it deserves an explicit contract.
findings.evidence: wallet-lib: /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/new/wallet.ts:387 (`this.pinCode = pinCode` — constructor only), 2052-2055 (start() uses a local `pinCode` and never persists it to the instance), 1824/1829/1865 and 1657 (`this.pinCode ?? undefined` at every processing site), onConnectionChangedState sync call (diff hunk at wallet.ts:652-670, `this.pinCode ?? undefined`); src/utils/storage.ts:951-956 (decode block requires `pinCode !== undefined` — silent skip otherwise). Contrast: send paths accept per-call pin (`const pin = newOptions.pinCode || this.pinCode`, e.g. wallet.ts:1973), so per-call-PIN integrations are an expected usage pattern.
findings.recommendation: Set `this.pinCode = pinCode` in start() when provided (or add an explicit `wallet.setPinCode()` / `enableShieldedSync(pin)` API), and log a clear warning once when shieldedCryptoProvider is set but no pin is available while txs with shielded_outputs arrive. Document that shielded receive-tracking requires the PIN to be available to the instance.

result.notes: CONFIRMED. (1) Bounds check verified at /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/utils/transaction.ts:1111-1113: `if (input.index >= response.tx.outputs.length) reject('Index outside of tx output array bounds')` — uses only transparent outputs. Core mandates a combined index space: /tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor/transaction/base_transaction.py:361-363 (is_shielded_output) and :347-359 (resolve_spent_output). The /transaction endpoint the wallet hits (resources/transaction.py:62, get_tx_extra_data) serializes via to_json(), which emits shielded outputs ONLY as a separate top-level `shielded_outputs` key (base_transaction.py:815-820), never inline in outputs[] — so every input spending a shielded output of an API-fetched parent is rejected at :1111 before the shielded-slot machinery (:1129-1215) can run. (2) convertFullNodeTxToHistoryTx verified at transaction.ts:1580-1625: histTx literal (:1598-1614) has no shielded_outputs key despite the schema parsing it (src/api/schemas/txApi.ts:150; getTransaction uses transactionApiSchema at txApi.ts:84-86). The isShieldedOutputEntry check inside outputs.map (:1594) never fires on this endpoint because to_json never inlines shielded entries. No normalizeShieldedOutputs call rescues this path. Minor imprecision in the finding: with the bounds check fixed, there is a UTXO-record fallback at :1196-1213 before the 'references a shielded slot' throw (:1198-1202); but in the API-fetch scenario the parent is absent from storage so the UTXO record is almost certainly absent too — the throw is the realistic outcome. Impact verified: callers are the fire-and-forget sender-local insert (src/new/sendTransaction.ts:839-850, un-awaited IIFE with no catch → unhandled rejection; tx still pushes, but wallet misses its own tx until WS delivery) and the template interpreter (src/template/transaction/interpreter.ts:281). Trigger requires the parent tx absent from local storage while spending its shielded output — genuine edge path, so medium severity stands.

Confirmed. (1) /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/utils/transaction.ts:193-195 infers mode from asset_commitment presence in normalizeShieldedOutputs case (a) and never reads output.mode; IShieldedOutputEntry (src/types.ts:339-361) doesn't even declare a mode field. (2) hathor-core sends the discriminator in exactly this shape: /tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor/transaction/base_transaction.py:96 ('mode': output.mode().value) emitted by _shielded_output_to_json, whose entries to_json_extended appends to outputs[] (lines 888-896); docstring at lines 85-90 verbatim instructs consumers to rely on mode rather than infer from asset_commitment/surjection_proof. (3) Currently harmless: OutputMode enum (hathorlib/hathorlib/transaction/shielded_tx_output.py:30-34) only has 1 and 2, and mode-2 JSON always carries asset_commitment (base_transaction.py:105-107). (4) Refutation attempts failed: txApi.ts:104 zod schema parses mode but only for the separate shielded_outputs[] field (case b, preserved in place); src/shielded/processing.ts:130-131 already implements the recommended mode-first-with-fallback pattern, proving intent, but case (a) entries reach it with an inferred always-defined mode so the node's value is lost. ensureHex heuristic at transaction.ts:260-266 also verified as described. Severity [info] is right: equivalent behavior today, one-line forward-compat fix.

CONFIRMED. Every cited line checks out and no compensating control exists anywhere in the worktree.

Evidence verified:
1. Plaintext persistence into history: /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/utils/storage.ts:987 (`value: result.decrypted.value`), :1005 (`blindingFactor: result.decrypted.blindingFactor.toString('hex')`), :1006 (`assetBlindingFactor: ...toString('hex')`), persisted at :1011 (`await store.saveTx(tx)`). Also re-propagated on tx re-delivery at :713.
2. Plaintext persistence into UTXO store: storage.ts:1131-1148 — `store.saveUtxo({... shielded: true, blindingFactor: (output as IShieldedOutputEntry).blindingFactor, assetBlindingFactor: ...})` at :1143-1145.
3. Type carriers are plain hex with no encryption marker: src/types.ts:352-353 (IShieldedOutputEntry) and :472-474 (IUtxo) — comments say "hex, 32 bytes — value blinding factor from decryption".
4. No encryption layer on the round-trip: read paths decode the stored string directly as raw hex — src/utils/transaction.ts:1366 and src/new/sendTransaction.ts:480 both do `Buffer.from(utxo.blindingFactor, 'hex')` with no decryptData. If the value were encrypted these would produce garbage, so storage is definitively plaintext.
5. Contrast confirmed: `encryptData` is used exclusively for key material (src/utils/wallet.ts:596, 620, 631 `scanMainKey`, 636 `spendMainKey`, 642-650, 694-697, 727-729, 807-859); grep shows zero encryptData calls touching blindingFactor/assetBlindingFactor/decrypted values anywhere in src/.
6. The design inconsistency is real and sharp: the scan xpriv (viewing capability) IS PIN-encrypted at rest (src/storage/storage.ts:976 `decryptData(accessData.scanMainKey, pinCode)`, :988 spendMainKey), meaning the architecture intends shielded viewing to be PIN-gated — but the cached plaintext openings (value + blinding factor) nullify that protection for every already-scanned output. An attacker with store read access but no PIN cannot rewind (scan key encrypted) yet reads all amounts directly and holds provable Pedersen openings (commitment is public on-chain; r + v fully open C = v·H + r·G).
7. Core side confirmed: hathor-core never persists blindings (hathor/crypto/shielded/ is sender/verifier-side computation only; no storage of blinding factors found), so custody/encryption is wallet policy exactly as the finding states — core neither saves nor forbids, the wallet owns this decision.
8. Export-surface claim holds: the fields live on IHistoryTx outputs (IShieldedOutputEntry), so getTx()/history consumers carry the openings; src/new/wallet.ts:1154-1172 reads them straight off history outputs.

Severity: high stands. No fund loss (spend key remains PIN-encrypted), but it fully defeats the confidentiality property the feature exists to provide, against exactly the at-rest attacker the scan-key encryption was designed to resist, and the blinding factor escalates beyond mere disclosure to third-party-provable amounts. One could argue medium since the same attacker already sees all transparent history, but transparent history is publicly recoverable from the chain while shielded openings are not — the asymmetry justifies high.

findings.title: Timelock is dropped when receiving a shielded output — balance over-reports available funds and premature spends are node-rejected
findings.description: The wallet supports building timelocked shielded outputs (the timelock flows into the on-chain P2PKH script via createOutputScript), but on RECEIPT the timelock is lost. hathor-core's `_shielded_output_to_json` only emits `decoded = {address}` for shielded outputs (no timelock — base_transaction.py:108-110), and the wallet never parses the timelock back out of `so.script`. In processNewTx the decoded shielded entry is built as `decoded: { ...so?.decoded, address }` (storage.ts:990) and the UTXO is saved with `timelock: output.decoded.timelock || null` (storage.ts:1139), which is always null for a received shielded output. isOutputLocked (utils/transaction.ts) then reads `output.decoded.timelock === undefined` and treats it as unlocked, so the value is credited to `tokens.unlocked` and the UTXO is offered by selectUtxos before the timelock expires. The node evaluates shielded output scripts identically to transparent ones (verify_script, transaction_verifier.py:202-203), so the timelocked script enforces the lock and the early spend is rejected post-PoW.
findings.evidence: wallet-lib drops timelock: src/utils/storage.ts:990 `decoded: { ...so?.decoded, address: result.address }` and src/utils/storage.ts:1139 `timelock: output.decoded.timelock || null`. Send-side timelock is honored: src/shielded/creation.ts:331 `timelock: proposal.timelock ?? null`, src/utils/transaction.ts:827-829 (shielded branch builds P2PKH WITH timelock). Note self-sends DO recover it via parseScript (src/utils/transaction.ts:1260,1270), proving the script carries it — only the receive path drops it. Core: hathor/transaction/base_transaction.py:108-110 emits only `{'address': ...}` in decoded; shielded scripts verified like transparent at hathor/verification/transaction_verifier.py:202-203.
findings.recommendation: On the receive path, parse `so.script` with parseScript and copy `timelock` into the decoded entry (and into the saved UTXO's `timelock`) exactly as the self-send path already does, so locked shielded UTXOs are excluded from available balance and UTXO selection until the timelock expires.

findings.title: No upper-bound (2^40) value validation before building shielded outputs — large amounts fail with an opaque crypto error
findings.description: Range proofs are fixed at 40 bits in ct-crypto (RANGE_PROOF_BITS=40), so any shielded output value >= 2^40 (~1.0995e12 base units) causes `create*ShieldedOutput`/`createRangeProof` to throw inside Rust. createShieldedOutputs validates scanPubkey length, token UID length, and output count, but never checks that `proposal.value` is within [1, 2^40). Since OutputValueType is bigint and custom tokens can have supplies up to 2^63, a wallet shielding a large balance hits a cryptic native throw routed through the generic `Failed to create shielded output i/n` wrapper (creation.ts:358-364) far from the real cause. The client guide's stated [1, 2^64) range (reference checklist line 111-112) is wrong; core/ct-crypto win at 40 bits.
findings.evidence: wallet-lib: src/shielded/creation.ts:256-309 validates pubkey/token/count but has no value-range guard; the only value check anywhere is the post-rewind `recoveredValue <= 0n` in src/shielded/processing.ts:203. ct-crypto source of truth: /tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor-ct-crypto/src/rangeproof.rs:12-15 (RANGE_PROOF_BITS=40); ct-crypto API ref §4 'Value range for proofs: range proofs are fixed 40-bit, min_value=1 ... values must be in [1, 2^40)'. Consensus inventory §10 'Value fits 40 bits ... A wallet must not attempt amounts >= 2^40'.
findings.recommendation: Validate each `proposal.value` (and the converted shielded HTR change) is in [1, 2^40) before calling the crypto provider, throwing a clear wallet-level error naming the value and the 40-bit limit.

findings.title: Multisig wallets silently receive single-key (P2PKH) shielded addresses — shielded funds bypass the multisig quorum
findings.description: generateAccessDataFromSeed derives the shielded scan (account 1') and spend (account 2') keys unconditionally, regardless of walletType, and the on-chain shielded address is always a single-sig P2PKH derived from the spend pubkey (deriveShieldedAddressFromStorage -> spendAddress). Spending a shielded UTXO signs a plain single-key P2PKH signature with the spend key chain (getSignatureForTx shielded-spend branch), never a multisig redeem script. A user of a MULTISIG wallet therefore has shielded receives controlled by ONE key (the spend key derived from a single participant's root seed), not the m-of-n quorum that governs all their other funds. There is no guard preventing multisig wallets from deriving/using shielded addresses, so this is a silent downgrade of the wallet's security model.
findings.evidence: wallet-lib: src/utils/wallet.ts:710-729 derives scan/spend keys with no walletType check (same in generateAccessData at 624-637); src/utils/address.ts:200-208 builds the on-chain address as a P2PKH from the spend pubkey ('shielded-spend'); src/utils/transaction.ts:413-430 signs shielded inputs single-sig (`derivedKey = spendXprivkey.deriveNonCompliantChild(...)`, pushes one signature+pubkey, never building a multisig script). Core has no shielded multisig concept — only P2PKH/MULTISIG version bytes exist (conf/mainnet.py:23-24) and shielded scripts are parsed as standard scripts (base_transaction.py:509-510).
findings.recommendation: Either gate shielded key derivation/usage off for MULTISIG wallets (throw on shielded send/receive), or explicitly derive the shielded spend script as a multisig redeem script so shielded funds inherit the same quorum; at minimum document that shielded funds in a multisig wallet are single-key.

findings.title: Full-unshield excess computation in SendTransaction includes authority outputs' mask value, diverging from the prepareTransaction path
findings.description: When building the UnshieldBalanceHeader excess scalar, the full-unshield branch iterates `outputs` and pushes every entry's `out.value` as a transparent output entry with no authority filter. Authority outputs carry the authority mask (1n/2n, TOKEN_MINT_MASK/TOKEN_MELT_MASK) as their `value`, not a real token amount — the node and ct-crypto skip authority/zero-value entries in the balance equation (balance.rs:56-57,73-74; verifier authorities skipped). The sibling implementation in utils/transaction.ts:1392-1393 correctly guards with `out.value > 0n && (out.authorities ?? 0n) === 0n`, showing the intended behavior. Because compute_balancing_blinding_factor requires sum(input values) == sum(output values), an included authority value would yield a wrong excess and a node-rejected tx. Currently latent (sendManyOutputs cannot emit authority outputs) but a correctness divergence if the path is reused.
findings.evidence: wallet-lib buggy loop: src/new/sendTransaction.ts:556-562 `for (const out of outputs) { transparentOutputEntries.push({ value: out.value, ... }) }` with no authority/zero filter. Correct sibling: src/utils/transaction.ts:1392-1393 skips `(out.authorities ?? 0n) !== 0n`. Mask constants: src/constants.ts:129,135. Verifier/ct-crypto skip authority+zero entries: /tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor-ct-crypto/src/balance.rs:56-57,73-74; values must balance per balance.rs test_full_unshield_to_transparent_with_excess (inputs and outputs both 1000).
findings.recommendation: Mirror the prepareTransaction guard: only push transparent output entries where `out.value > 0n && (out.authorities ?? 0n) === 0n` in the sendTransaction full-unshield excess loop.

findings.title: Shielding an entire balance to a single recipient is impossible — the ≥2-output rule plus the 1-min range proof leave no decoy option
findings.description: hathor-core requires 0 or >=2 shielded outputs (TrivialCommitmentError, transaction_verifier.py:1219-1227). The wallet enforces this by throwing when exactly one shielded output is requested. The core docs suggest wallets add a second 'possibly zero-value' shielded output to shield to a single recipient, but ct-crypto range proofs reject value 0 (min_value=1), so a zero-value decoy shielded output cannot be built. Consequently, to shield to a single recipient the user MUST have either a second recipient or shielded change. When the selected funds exactly cover value+fee (no change), even `changeShieldedMode` cannot rescue it (convertHtrChangeIfRequested no-ops with no change output), and the send fails hard with 'At least 2 shielded outputs are required'. There is no graceful path or guidance for 'shield my whole balance to one address'.
findings.evidence: wallet-lib hard throw: src/new/sendTransaction.ts:504-509 and src/shielded/creation.ts:264-268. Change conversion can't help with no change: src/new/sendTransaction.ts:1077-1088 (returns addedFee 0n when no change output). Zero-value decoy impossible: ct-crypto API ref §4 'create*ShieldedOutput/createRangeProof with value 0 ... fails inside RangeProof::new (throws)'. Core rule: /tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded ... transaction_verifier.py:1219-1227 (>=2 shielded outputs). RFC privacy rule rfc-summary.md:12.
findings.recommendation: Document the limitation and, where a privacy-preserving auto-split is desired, have the wallet split the single recipient amount into two (or more) shielded outputs to the same address (each value >= 1) instead of throwing, so 'shield entire balance to one recipient' is achievable.

result.notes: CONFIRMED. Plaintext persistence verified: /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/utils/storage.ts:987 (value), :1005-1006 (blindingFactor/assetBlindingFactor as hex), :1011 store.saveTx(tx); UTXO path :1141-1146 saveUtxo with shielded:true + both blinding factors. Store layer applies no encryption (src/storage/memory_store.ts:864-866 raw Map set; :543 raw saveTx). Whole-tree grep: encryptData used ONLY for key material — src/utils/wallet.ts:596,620,631,636,727,729 (incl. new scanMainKey/spendMainKey) and src/storage/storage.ts:976 decrypts scan key — never in the shielded persistence path; no redaction/sanitization of blindingFactor anywhere, and the fields ride IShieldedOutputEntry (src/types.ts:352-353) and IUtxo (src/types.ts:472-474) so they surface via getTx()/history export. Aggravating detail: decryption is PIN-gated (storage.ts:955 pinCode !== undefined) but the recovered secrets are then stored in the clear, defeating the PIN gate. Source-of-truth check: hathor-core never persists blindings (hathor/crypto/shielded/balance.py is transient sender-side math; shielded_tx_output.py rewinds transiently); the client guide (SHIELDED_INTEGRATION_REVIEW/reference/client-guide-checklist.md:32,36,80) explicitly marks blinding factors 'kept secret (needed for spending)' — custody is wallet policy, as the finding states. Nuance: plaintext `value` is partly inherent (balances/UTXO selection must work without PIN, and transparent history is plaintext too); the avoidable exposure is the blinding factors, needed only at spend time when the PIN is available, and uniquely enabling provable commitment openings. Severity high is defensible (medium arguable since it requires DB/export compromise): the library demonstrably treats DB compromise as in-scope by PIN-wrapping every other secret, and this leak defeats the core property of the shielded feature.

Code mechanics check out (gate at /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/new/sendTransaction.ts:543, excess computed at :571-577, header attached in src/utils/transaction.ts:961-969, no min-input/warn logic), but the privacy claim is refuted by the source of truth on three independent grounds.

1) The reviewer MISQUOTED hathor-core. The actual docstring at /tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor/transaction/headers/unshield_balance_header.py:42-46 reads: "Privacy: with exactly one shielded input the scalar is effectively r_in of that input, but the transparent outputs of a full unshield already expose the spent amount, so nothing previously-private is additionally leaked." The claimed text "leaving multi-input aggregation/hygiene to wallets" does not exist anywhere in core — grep for "hygiene"/"aggregation" across hathor/transaction/headers/ and hathor/verification/ returns zero hits. Core explicitly asserts NO privacy regression, the opposite of what the finding attributes to it.

2) The official client integration guide agrees: /Users/pedroferreira/Hathor/hathor-wallet-lib/SHIELDED_INTEGRATION_REVIEW/reference/client-guide-checklist.md:50 — "Privacy: single shielded input → excess = that input's r (input already spent, no regression); multiple → only sum revealed." The guide imposes no wallet-side minimum-input or warning requirement (line 124 likewise just notes the sum property).

3) The "unlinkability" mechanics are wrong. Hathor shielded outputs have no nullifier/anonymity-set scheme (grep for nullifier/anonymity in /tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor/ is empty): a spend publicly references the exact (tx_id, index) of the shielded UTXO in the input list, so "WHICH shielded commitment this unshield spends" is already public regardless of r. And the input's value v needs no 40-bit brute force from r: in a full unshield, v = sum(transparent outputs) + fee − sum(transparent inputs), all public by construction (verifier equation at transaction_verifier.py:936-938). Publishing r therefore reveals nothing an observer cannot already compute. The only real "leak" — the amount — is inherent to the user's choice of a full unshield (transparent outputs ARE the amount) and exists identically with 1 or N shielded inputs.

The recommendation (warn / prefer shielded change) is at best an optional UX nicety, not a security gap; per environment rules core wins, and core says single-input full unshield leaks nothing previously private. If the team wanted to track the UX suggestion it would be info-level, but as a security finding it is refuted.

Confirmed. JSON receive path has no size bounds on shielded proof fields: src/schemas.ts:104-106,114-118,183-194 and src/api/schemas/txApi.ts (fullnodeTxApiShieldedOutputSchema) use plain z.string() with no .max() (grep over both files finds zero .max() calls). These are hex-decoded and handed to native crypto with no length check: src/shielded/processing.ts:122-124 (Buffer.from(...,'hex')), :144-150 rewindFullShieldedOutput, :189-195 rewindAmountShieldedOutput. The wire deserializer IS hardened exactly as claimed: src/models/shielded_output.ts:175-176 (MAX_RANGE_PROOF_SIZE 3328), :186-189 (script cap), :218-221 (MAX_SURJECTION_PROOF_SIZE 4096), constants at src/constants.ts:254,260, matching hathor-core hathorlib/hathorlib/transaction/shielded_tx_output.py:24-25,130-132,186-188. The native layer does not defend early either: hathor-ct-crypto/src/rangeproof.rs:143-147 deserialize_range_proof is a bare RangeProof::from_slice that copies the entire input into a Vec with no cap (the only MAX_RANGE_PROOF_SIZE reference in Rust is in a #[cfg(test)] block), so allocations (JS string -> Buffer -> NAPI copy -> Rust Vec) all occur before rejection. Severity low is correct (not higher): requires a malicious/compromised fullnode (already trusted), the oversized string is already in memory from JSON.parse so amplification is ~2.5x per output (max 32 outputs/tx), and processing only decodes outputs whose decoded.address matches the wallet (processing.ts:106-111), though a malicious node can trivially satisfy that. Borders on info, but the deliberate wire-path hardening makes the JSON-path gap a legitimate low; the recommended .max()/decoded-length checks are cheap and mirror existing limits.

CONFIRMED. All cited evidence verified exactly: /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/utils/storage.ts:987 (plaintext value), :1005-1006 (blindingFactor/assetBlindingFactor as raw hex), :1011 (store.saveTx), :1141-1146 (saveUtxo with plaintext blindings); type carriers at src/types.ts:352-353 and :472-474. Exhaustive grep shows encryptData is applied ONLY to key material (src/utils/wallet.ts:596,620,631,636,694-697,727,729,807,809) and never to blindings — the asymmetry is real. Openings propagate through IHistoryTx: spend path reads plaintext utxo.blindingFactor (src/new/sendTransaction.ts:472-480, src/utils/transaction.ts:1363-1366) and src/new/wallet.ts:1119-1188 serves them from stored history, so any history export/API (headless) carries commitment openings. Core side confirmed: /tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor/crypto/shielded/ is pure functions, no persistence — custody is wallet policy as claimed. Fix is feasible: decode already requires pinCode (storage.ts:955) and blindings are re-derivable via rewind from the PIN-encrypted scan key (src/shielded/processing.ts:144-153). SEVERITY ADJUSTED high→medium: privacy-only (no fund theft possible); the attacker model (local DB read or history export) already sees ALL amounts via the architecturally-required plaintext `value` field — the entire history/balance store is plaintext by long-standing wallet-lib design, so the marginal new capability from unencrypted blindings is third-party provability/on-chain linkage, not first disclosure of amounts; and an attacker with DB access can brute-force the PIN on the co-located encrypted scan key to re-derive blindings anyway. Real, concrete, worth fixing (especially excluding openings from default history serialization), but the high rating over-credits the incremental harm.

CONFIRMED with one mechanism correction. (1) Missing validation is real: creation.ts:272-309 upfront loop checks scanPubkey/token-UID lengths only, never proposal.value; sendTransaction.ts:504-515 checks count only; wallet.ts:1981-2019 maps shielded outputs with no value check; greps for 2**40/1099511627776/RANGE_PROOF_BITS/MAX_SHIELDED_VALUE over src/ find nothing (only MAX_SHIELDED_OUTPUTS/MAX_RANGE_PROOF_SIZE). The transparent Output positivity check (src/models/output.ts:64) never applies — the phantom output is removed pre-model. (2) value=0 throws in native code as claimed: ct-crypto pins min_value=1 (hathor-ct-crypto/src/rangeproof.rs:44; node bindings src/rangeproof.rs:43,57) and libsecp256k1-zkp rejects min_value>value (secp256k1-zkp-sys rangeproof_impl.h:215); wrapped non-actionably at creation.ts:358-364. (3) CORRECTION: values >= 2^40 do NOT make RangeProof::new throw — min_bits is a floor (rangeproof_impl.h:163-165: mantissa = max(bits(v), min_bits)), so values in (2^40, 2^63) yield a successful LARGER proof; core verification only checks range.start>=1 (hathor-ct-crypto/src/rangeproof.rs:95-101, transaction_verifier.py:800-827 has no max), so slightly-over values are accepted on-chain with an oversized proof, leaking value magnitude and defeating the fixed-proof-size design (rangeproof.rs:9-14; node test test_proof_size_is_constant only covers up to 10^12 < 2^40). Larger values trip wallet-lib's MAX_RANGE_PROOF_SIZE=3328 check (src/models/shielded_output.ts:174-176). Hard native failures only at 0 and > 2^63-1 (rangeproof_impl.h:130). (4) Evidence nit: client-guide-checklist.md:12,57 says [1, 2^64), not [1, 2^40) — the 40-bit bound comes from core's RANGE_PROOF_BITS, which wins. Recommendation (MAX_SHIELDED_VALUE = 2^40-1 upfront check) remains correct and is mildly strengthened: the over-bound case is a silent privacy/size-channel divergence rather than a loud error. Severity low is appropriate.

CONFIRMED. (1) V.4 at /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/__tests__/integration/shielded_outputs/shielded_negative.test.ts:105-147 is named 'second send of an already-spent shielded UTXO is rejected' but performs two sequential successful sends; lines 140-142 state 'We mainly assert no error is thrown'. No code path deliberately re-spends a consumed shielded slot or asserts rejection. Minor nuance: the test is not fully vacuous (if the wallet re-selected the spent UTXO the node would reject tx2 and the test would fail), but the rejection behavior the test is named for is never exercised — matching the reviewer's exact phrasing. (2) Suite header (line 15) promises 'server-side (fullnode rejects on push_tx)' coverage; all rejections in the file (V.1 line 62, V.3 line 97) are client-side build-time throws. (3) A.10 (ws_message_ordering.test.ts:243-251) and C.18 (cross_wallet.test.ts:209-212) void via hand-fed `{...stored, is_voided: true}` through onNewTx; A.10's own comment (lines 232-235) admits it simulates rather than drives a real conflict. (4) Exhaustive grep across all 23 shielded integration suites + unit tests for push_tx/conflict/already-spent/rejects found no test that builds a tx re-spending a consumed shielded slot and asserts fullnode rejection, and none that creates a real on-chain conflict between two wallets: core.test.ts:1730-1832 (L.5) is a positive bookkeeping test ('Final guard: a second self-send must succeed'); utxo_selector.test.ts:377 rejects on insufficient funds; edge_cases.test.ts rejections are client-side validation; token_creation.test.ts K.8 and concurrency.test.ts are positive no-double-spend tests. (5) Source of truth confirms the untested behavior is real: /tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor/verification/transaction_verifier.py:181-192 (shielded slot indexing in _verify_inputs) and :205-210 (ConflictingInputs); cross-tx conflict→void is consensus behavior the wallet's shielded UTXO bookkeeping must survive but is never driven end-to-end. Severity medium is right: a test-quality/coverage gap (not a product bug) but in the exact area where a real regression existed (L.5 spent-shielded-UTXO deletion bug).

CONFIRMED on every element. (1) Schema strictness verified: src/schemas.ts:191-192 requires ephemeral_pubkey + decoded in IHistoryShieldedOutputSchema; src/schemas.ts:106,116 require ephemeral_pubkey in both shielded variants of IHistoryOutputSchema (a core shielded output without it matches no union member since it also lacks value/token for the transparent variant); src/api/schemas/txApi.ts:113-114 same; txApi.ts:149 declares shielded_inputs. (2) Test absence verified: grep over __tests__ for all schema names returns zero matches; __tests__/new/hathorwallet.test.ts:979,994 builds history objects with `as unknown as IHistoryTx` (bypasses schemas) and always includes ephemeral_pubkey + decoded; integration tests replay wallet-lib-produced payloads which always set both. (3) Core divergence verified: hathor/transaction/base_transaction.py:101-102 omits ephemeral_pubkey when falsy and :108-112 omits decoded when parse_address_script fails; to_json_extended (base_transaction.py:888-896) feeds these shapes into outputs[] of the wallet-history JSON. Absent ephemeral pubkey is consensus-valid (hathorlib shielded_tx_output.py:158-166: 33 zero bytes = absent; verifier only checks it `if output.ephemeral_pubkey`, transaction_verifier.py:787-798) and no script-standardness rule exists for shielded outputs (verify_commitments_valid, transaction_verifier.py:749-798), so unparseable scripts (no decoded) are valid too. (4) shielded_inputs is dead: core inlines spent shielded outputs into inputs[] (hathor/transaction/resources/transaction.py:97-105, base_transaction.py:865-880); no core code emits a top-level shielded_inputs key (it's .nullish() so harmless but untestable, as claimed). Impact path verified: src/new/wallet.ts:1736-1740 safeParse failure silently drops the tx; src/sync/stream.ts:754 uses .parse which throws in the stream vertex handler — a consensus-valid third-party tx with absent ephemeral_pubkey or non-standard script that touches a wallet address breaks realtime + stream sync, with no test pinning. Severity high is appropriate: this is not just a test gap — the schemas are demonstrably wrong vs core's canonical emission, enabling a cheap sync-griefing vector.

Confirmed. ws_message_ordering.test.ts:171-173 reads `const balAfter = await fresh.getTxBalance(after!); expect(typeof balAfter).toBe('object');` — getTxBalance (src/new/wallet.ts:3040-3045 → src/utils/transaction.ts:611) returns Promise<Record<string, IBalance>>, so the typeof assertion can only fail via a thrown exception; it verifies "no crash," not decryption. The test's own comment (line 172) concedes this. A.9 in the same file (lines 200-227) proves the recommended technique: wipe walletB's stored tx + shielded UTXOs (store.history.delete + utxo loop), replay ws events, assert exact 50n credit — directly applicable to full→bare ordering. Seed claim verified: generateWalletHelper() takes a distinct unused precalculated wallet per call (wallet-precalculation.helper.ts:268-273), so `fresh` cannot decrypt walletB's outputs and the current test can never observe a credit. Searched the whole shielded suite: core.test.ts:1644, A.4, A.7, and realtime_vs_reload.test.ts all cover bare→full or re-delivery to an already-decoded wallet; no test deterministically covers full-payload-as-first-event with a credit assertion. Minor mitigation: `expect(after).not.toBeNull()` (line 168) does assert the tx is stored, so the test isn't entirely assertion-free — but the stated contract (decrypt correctly) is unverified. Severity low is correct (test-quality gap on the historically buggy decode-then-clobber path; no production defect).

PARTIALLY REAL — the central "non-rotating address" mechanism is refuted; only the positional (no-shuffle, change-always-last) leak survives. CONFIRMED: sendTransaction.ts:1090 uses getCurrentAddress({}, {legacy:false}) without markAsUsed and :1102 appends the converted change as the LAST shielded output; only transparent outputs are shuffled (sendTransaction.ts:419-426, comment at :424 explains the shuffle exists precisely to hide change position); no shuffle of shieldedOutputDefs exists anywhere in src/; the last shielded output always carries the balancing vbf (src/shielded/creation.ts:74 and :169); hathor-core enforces no ordering/reuse rule. REFUTED: the claim that the shielded change address never rotates ("repeated shielded sends reuse the same P2PKH"). Rotation happens through tx-history processing, identical to the legacy transparent chain: spend-derived P2PKH addresses are saved as addressType 'shielded-spend' and subscribed (src/utils/storage.ts:119-131); on the wallet's own tx, onNewTx calls storage.processNewTx(newTx, this.pinCode ?? undefined) (src/new/wallet.ts:1824), the decryption block appends the decoded change output to tx.outputs with decoded.address = spend P2PKH (src/utils/storage.ts:985-990), the output loop advances shieldedMaxIndexUsed for 'shielded-spend' addresses (src/utils/storage.ts:1040-1043), and updateWalletMetadataFromProcessedTxData advances shieldedCurrentAddressIndex past it (src/utils/storage.ts:824-834). So the next send's change goes to a fresh address; reuse only occurs for back-to-back sends before the first tx is processed — the same known caveat as transparent change, which also defaults to getCurrentAddress() (src/storage/storage.ts:344). FRAMING OVERSTATED: per RFC summary (SHIELDED_INTEGRATION_REVIEW/reference/rfc-summary.md, Design section) AmountShielded "hides amount, reveals recipient/token" — recipient P2PKH scripts are visible by design; shielded outputs provide amount/token confidentiality, not address unlinkability, so nothing is "defeated". The balancing-bf position is cryptographically unobservable (blinding factors are secret). Residual real issue: deterministic last-position change is a change-detection heuristic an observer can use for clustering; shuffling shielded outputs (or randomizing the balancing position — any output can carry the balancing vbf) is a cheap, valid hardening. The "fresh internal change chain" recommendation contradicts the wallet's existing architecture (no BIP44 internal/change chain exists anywhere in Hathor wallets). Severity adjusted low → info.

CONFIRMED on all counts. (1) Schemas require fields core omits: src/schemas.ts:106,116 (ephemeral_pubkey required in both IHistoryOutputSchema shielded union variants), src/schemas.ts:191-192 (IHistoryShieldedOutputSchema requires ephemeral_pubkey + decoded), src/api/schemas/txApi.ts:113-114 (same in fullnodeTxApiShieldedOutputSchema). (2) Core omits both: /tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor/transaction/base_transaction.py:101-102 (ephemeral_pubkey only if present) and 108-112 (decoded only if parse_address_script succeeds). Absence is consensus-valid: hathor/verification/transaction_verifier.py:787 (validates only when present, no presence rule), hathorlib/hathorlib/transaction/shielded_tx_output.py:104,158-166 (33 zero bytes = absent). (3) Real exposure: hathor/websocket/streamer.py:300 sends to_json_extended() with shielded outputs inlined in outputs[] (base_transaction.py:888-896); an absent-ephemeral output fails all three IHistoryOutputSchema union variants → src/sync/stream.ts:754 hard .parse() throws, breaking stream sync; src/new/wallet.ts:1736-1739 safeParse failure silently DROPS the consensus-valid tx (normalizeShieldedOutputs at wallet.ts:1745 runs only after parse); txApi get_tx (hathor/transaction/resources/transaction.py:62 → to_json(decode_script=True), base_transaction.py:815-820) emits top-level shielded_outputs hitting the required decoded/ephemeral_pubkey in fullnodeTxApiShieldedOutputSchema. (4) shielded_inputs never emitted by core: transaction.py:97-105 inlines spent shielded outputs into inputs[]; zero grep hits for shielded_inputs in hathor/transaction/resources/ and hathor/websocket/ — dead schema member (harmless via .nullish() but unpinned). (5) Test absence verified: grep for IHistoryTxSchema|fullnodeTxApi|IHistoryShieldedOutputSchema|IHistoryOutputSchema|transactionApiSchema over __tests__ → zero matches; the only fixtures use ephemeral_pubkey:'' (key present — a shape core never emits), __tests__/new/hathorwallet.test.ts:979,994; integration tests only exercise wallet-authored txs which always set ephemeral_pubkey and standard P2PKH scripts. Minor nuance: current core always emits mode (base_transaction.py:96) — schemas already special-case old-node mode omission, showing omit-semantics were known but missed for ephemeral_pubkey/decoded. Severity high is right: attacker-craftable consensus-valid tx aborts stream sync or silently diverges wallet history, with no test pinning any of these schemas.

Confirmed on all points. (1) src/sync/stream.ts:566-587: shielded derivation in StreamManager.processQueue is wrapped in a swallow-all try/catch (catch at 581-587 only calls this.logger.error); a null return at line 573 is skipped with no log at all. Confirmed. (1) Ownership gate is exactly `if (!output.blindingFactor) continue;` at src/new/wallet.ts:1154, with comment (lines 1144-1147) admitting the gate relies on undecoded entries never being appended to tx.outputs[] — a storage convention, not an ownership check. (2) The implicit guarantee is real and unpinned: blindingFactor is only ever persisted via the decryption path (src/utils/storage.ts:1005, from result.decrypted.blindingFactor); sender-local insert (src/utils/transaction.ts:1257-1273) emits shielded_outputs without value/blindingFactor; send-time blindings in sendTransaction.ts:472-480 are transient. A future change persisting send-time blindings would flip the mixed case into leaking recipient openings with zero test failures. (3) Exhaustive grep: getShieldedUnblindingForTx is called at exactly 4 integration sites, all in unblinding_encoder.test.ts — P.1:75, P.2:136 (recipient, all-owned), P.3:212 (self-send, all-owned), P.4:266 (sender, none-owned). No mixed-ownership call exists. The mixed tx shape IS built in multi_token_shielded.test.ts Q.3/Q.4 (lines 230-246, 303: recipient output to walletB + sender's shielded change via changeShieldedMode) and sparse_decode.test.ts N.1/N.3, but those assert only balances/spendability, never the unblinding payload. (4) Minor nuance: the unit suite does test a mixed mock tx (hathorwallet.test.ts:999-1039, foreign output at line 1018 excluded alongside two owned ones), so inclusion+exclusion-simultaneously IS covered at the filter level — but with a hand-built post-decryption storage shape (makeTx, lines 940-997), so it pins the filter, not the pipeline guarantee; the finding explicitly acknowledges this. (5) Recommended P.5 is feasible: changeShieldedMode exists (src/new/sendTransaction.ts:138,168; src/new/types.ts:391) and Q.3 demonstrates the exact construction. No current bug — severity medium is appropriate for a missing end-to-end regression guard on the user-stated privacy invariant.

VERIFIED: all cited code exists exactly as claimed. src/utils/storage.ts:1005-1006 writes blindingFactor/assetBlindingFactor as raw lowercase hex onto the IShieldedOutputEntry (persisted via store.saveTx at :1011); same plaintext factors saved on IUtxo records at storage.ts:710-715 and :1144-1145. src/types.ts:352-353 and :473-474 declare them plain string hex, while key material at types.ts:494-505 (mainKey/scanMainKey/spendMainKey) uses IEncryptedData under the PIN. encryptData appears only in src/utils/crypto.ts and src/utils/wallet.ts — never in src/shielded/ or any blinding-factor write path. getShieldedUnblindingForTx (src/new/wallet.ts:1119) reads them with no PIN gate. Client guide checklist lines 32/36/121 confirm blinding factors are wallet-held secrets. HOWEVER, severity is overstated: storage.ts:987-991 persists the decrypted value, token UID, and address in plaintext on the SAME entry, so any attacker with store read access already recovers the wallet's full confidential history without the blinding factors — consistent with the wallet's existing design of storing all (transparent) history unencrypted. The marginal exposure from plaintext vbf/abf is only the cryptographically verifiable opening (provable third-party deanonymization), and the factors cannot spend funds (spend key stays PIN-encrypted). The proposed fix (encrypt vbf/abf only) would not restore confidentiality while value/token/address remain plaintext. Neither hathor-core nor the client guide mandates at-rest encryption. Real hardening gap, but privacy-marginal given the baseline: severity low.

CONFIRMED. (1) Required fields verified: src/schemas.ts:106,116 (ephemeral_pubkey required in both IHistoryOutputSchema shielded union variants), src/schemas.ts:191-192 (IHistoryShieldedOutputSchema requires ephemeral_pubkey AND decoded), src/api/schemas/txApi.ts:113-114 (same), txApi.ts:149 (shielded_inputs key). (2) Core divergence verified: hathor-core base_transaction.py:101-102 emits ephemeral_pubkey only `if output.ephemeral_pubkey` and base_transaction.py:108-112 emits decoded only when parse_address_script succeeds; absent ephemeral pubkey is consensus-valid (hathorlib shielded_tx_output.py:158-166 maps 33 zero bytes -> b''; verifier transaction_verifier.py:787-797 validates only IF present; no verifier rule requires parseable scripts on shielded outputs). shielded_inputs is never emitted by core — spent shielded outputs are inlined into inputs[] (resources/transaction.py:97-105, base_transaction.py to_json_extended ~864-880); harmless (.nullish) but dead as claimed. (3) Impact reachable on ALL three sync surfaces, all of which serialize through to_json_extended -> _shielded_output_to_json: polling (address_history.py:262 -> addressHistorySchema, src/api/schemas/wallet.ts:16), stream sync (streamer.py:300 -> src/sync/stream.ts:754 hard .parse inside ws listener -> throw), realtime (factory.py:215 -> src/new/wallet.ts:1736-1740 safeParse failure SILENTLY DROPS the tx). A shielded entry without ephemeral_pubkey fails every union member (TransparentOutputSchema needs value/decoded/token/spent_by), so a consensus-valid third-party tx pairing an absent-ephemeral-pubkey shielded output with a dust transparent output to a victim address makes the victim wallet drop the tx (missed funds) and breaks stream reload — cheap griefing vector. normalizeShieldedOutputs runs AFTER parse (wallet.ts:1745), nothing repairs the payload. (4) Test absence verified: grep for IHistoryTxSchema|fullnodeTxApi|IHistoryShieldedOutputSchema|IHistoryOutputSchema over __tests__ returns zero matches; the closest fixture (__tests__/new/hathorwallet.test.ts:940-997) is cast `as unknown as IHistoryTx` bypassing zod and uses ephemeral_pubkey:'' (present empty string, not the omitted-key shape); integration tests only exercise wallet-lib-produced payloads which always set ephemeral_pubkey and standard P2PKH scripts. The omit-when-absent hazard was clearly known — mode and token_data got .optional() with explanatory comments (schemas.ts:182,190) — yet ephemeral_pubkey/decoded were left required and unpinned by any test. Minor caveats not changing verdict: decoded requiredness only bites on the top-level shielded_outputs path (schemas.ts:192) and txApi (txApi.ts:114), not the inline outputs[] union (AmountShielded/FullShielded variants omit decoded); and the schemas ARE indirectly exercised by integration onNewTx flows, just never against the divergent canonical core shapes, which is the substance of the finding. Severity high is justified: the test gap conceals a live, trivially-triggerable sync-DoS/missed-funds bug.

CONFIRMED on all points. (1) Shielded chains derived with no walletType gate at src/utils/wallet.ts:627-637 (generateAccessDataFromXpriv, contrast type-gated multisig branches at 582-591/598-608), src/utils/wallet.ts:710-729 (generateAccessDataFromSeed derives unconditionally even with multisig set), and src/utils/wallet.ts:758-813 (migrateShieldedAccessData, ungated caller at src/new/wallet.ts:2121). (2) Multisig wallets actually expose shielded addresses: loadAddresses at src/utils/storage.ts:117-132 derives the shielded pair for the P2SH branch too, and getAddressAtIndex({legacy:false}) at src/new/wallet.ts:841-849 has no walletType check; deriveShieldedAddressFromStorage (src/utils/address.ts:178) gates only on scan/spend xpub presence. (3) Custody downgrade is real: spend address is single-sig P2PKH from spend_pubkey (src/utils/address.ts ~200-208), and hathor-core has no multisig concept in shielded crypto (grep -i multisig over /tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor/crypto/shielded/ and hathor/transaction/shielded_tx_output.py is empty) — so an m-of-n wallet's shielded address is spendable by one participant's seed alone; core neither blocks nor defines this, the contract is wallet-side and unpinned. (4) Test gap confirmed: grep -rln -i multisig across __tests__/integration/shielded_outputs and __tests__/shielded (25 files) returns empty; the read-only single-sig test exists as claimed at __tests__/integration/shielded_outputs/core.test.ts:1363. (5) WS proxy claim confirmed: isShieldedOutputEntry passthrough at src/wallet/walletServiceStorageProxy.ts:193; test diffstat is exactly +1 line, and that line (__tests__/wallet/walletServiceStorageProxy.test.ts:20) mocks isShieldedOutputEntry to return false — the shielded-passthrough branch is never exercised true in any test. Severity: medium is right for the finding as scoped (coverage/contract gap); the underlying P2SH-custody-downgrade behavior itself could merit a separate higher-severity product decision.

CONFIRMED on every cited point. (1) S.1 (mint_melt_shielded.test.ts:99-133): minting happens transparently via createTokenHelper at line 106; the body at 113-126 is sendManyOutputsTransaction of already-minted supply — resulting tx is Fee+ShieldedOutputs with no MintHeader, so the name 'mint more tokens to FS output' overstates. (2) K.4 (token_creation.test.ts:120-139) calls mintTokens with a shielded address and asserts only balance 125n; K.5 (411-433) asserts only HTR balance 100n. The mint/melt flows (src/new/wallet.ts:2450-2513 → tokenUtils.prepareMintTxData) have no shielded support (zero 'shielded' matches in src/utils/tokens.ts), and createOutputScript at src/utils/transaction.ts:823-830 silently converts the shielded address to a spend-derived P2PKH script — with transparent inputs, isShieldedTx (transaction.ts:993-995) is false, so K.4/K.5 txs carry NO shielded headers; neither test inspects headers or output shape. (3) Exhaustive grep of __tests__: the only header combos exercised are Fee+ShieldedOutputs, Unshield+Mint (S.0/S.4), Unshield+Melt (S.2/S.3); no integration or unit test builds Mint/MeltHeader + ShieldedOutputsHeader together (__tests__/headers/* are serialization-only; __tests__/utils/transaction.test.ts:369-380 only uses mint/melt as utxo-type strings). (4) The auto-declare branch in _attachShieldedHeaders (src/utils/transaction.ts:993-1076) explicitly counts shielded outputs in the delta (1035-1038) to support that combination, yet only its unshield path is ever exercised. (5) hathor-core source-of-truth confirms the combo is legal and verifier-critical: verify_mint_melt_requires_shielded accepts ShieldedOutputsHeader for Rule M1; transaction_verifier.py extends the surjection-proof domain per MintHeader entry 'so a FullShieldedOutput can claim a freshly-minted asset' (~line 893); Rule M4 _fold_mint_melt_entry folding (~998-1019) applies with shielded outputs present. Minor nuance: wallet-lib's public API may not currently produce the combo (mintTokens has no shielded option; shielded sends don't select authority inputs), making it 'unsupported and unpinned' rather than 'supported but untested' — but the finding's recommendation already covers that alternative, and the misleading test names plus unexercised declaration branch stand either way. Medium severity is correct.

CONFIRMED (headline), with two sub-claims corrected by empirical testing.

Confirmed claims:
1. No client-side 40-bit bound exists anywhere in wallet-lib. Exhaustive grep over /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src and __tests__ for 2**40 / 1099511627776 / MAX_SHIELDED_VALUE / RANGE_PROOF_BITS finds nothing (the only "40" hits are 40n test amounts and a comment at core.test.ts:88). src/shielded/creation.ts passes proposal.value straight to the provider (lines 78, 84, 93, 173-190); src/new/sendTransaction.ts:505-515 guards only output COUNT (min 2 / MAX_SHIELDED_OUTPUTS); no value guard in wallet.js, new/types.ts, or zod schemas. MAX_OUTPUT_VALUE (2^63, constants.ts:107) is enforced only in buffer.ts:287 for transparent output serialization — shielded values never pass through it.
2. Largest shielded amount in any test is 80_000_000n (~2^26.3): __tests__/integration/shielded_outputs/core.test.ts:101-102.
3. The 'Cannot create more than' >32-output message at sendTransaction.ts:510-515 is asserted by zero tests (grep over __tests__ is empty; only 'At least 2 shielded outputs' is asserted, at __tests__/shielded/creation.test.ts:138, and that pins the duplicate guard in src/shielded/creation.ts:266, not the sendTransaction one).
4. Source of truth: ct-crypto pins RANGE_PROOF_BITS=40 with min_value=1 (hathor-ct-crypto/src/rangeproof.rs:16,45); node-side proof size cap MAX_RANGE_PROOF_SIZE=3328 (hathorlib/hathorlib/transaction/shielded_tx_output.py:24,130-132).

Corrected sub-claims (failure mode is WORSE than the reviewer described, not better):
A. "create throws for value >= 2^40" is FALSE. I ran the installed @hathor/ct-crypto-node binding: createAmountShieldedOutput(2^40-1) → 3213-byte proof; (2^40) → 3213 bytes (fits via min_value=1 offset); (2^40+12345) → SUCCEEDS with 3309-byte proof (41-bit mantissa, silent); (2^42) → SUCCEEDS with 3373 bytes; (0) → opaque Rust throw. secp256k1-zkp auto-expands the mantissa beyond min_bits instead of erroring. Consequences: a 41-bit value yields a 3309-byte proof, UNDER the node's 3328 cap, and node verification only enforces range.start>=1 (rangeproof.rs:95-101; transaction_verifier.py:800-827 checks nothing else) — so an over-limit value can be accepted on-chain while breaking the constant-proof-size privacy invariant (3309 vs 3213 leaks value magnitude). Values >= ~2^42 are rejected only by the node deserializer's size cap AFTER the wallet has built/signed/mined the tx. Wallet-lib's own 3328 check (src/models/shielded_output.ts:174-176) is deserialize-only; serialize (lines 92-116) has no size check. This makes the missing client-side bound strictly more important than "opaque throw".
B. The "crypto throw happens AFTER UTXO selection has marked inputs as selected / cleanup untested" sub-claim is REFUTED on mechanics: storage.utxoSelectAsInput is only called from updateOutputSelected (sendTransaction.ts:983,1006), which is first invoked in mineTx (line 763) — i.e., AFTER prepareTx/prepareTxData where createShieldedOutputs runs (line 517). prepareSendManyTokensData (line 1233) selects but does not mark. A crypto-stage failure therefore leaves no UTXOs marked-selected; no cleanup is needed there.

Severity assessment: keep medium. As a test-coverage finding it is real, and the underlying gap (no client-side [1, 2^40] bound, silent variable-size proofs above it) is genuine and has a privacy/consensus edge; but triggering it requires custom-token amounts > ~1.1e12 base units, so it is not high. Recommendation should be amended: the right fix is a client-side value bound (reject value < 1 or value > 2^40, matching min_value=1 semantics where 2^40 itself still yields a 40-bit proof) plus boundary tests at 2^40/2^40+1, and a sendTransaction-layer assertion for the 33-output message; the "released after crypto-stage failure" integration variant is unnecessary given marking happens only at mineTx.

Code divergence CONFIRMED: wallet builds the surjection domain from every input with a token, no authority skip, no mint extension (src/new/sendTransaction.ts:459-468; src/shielded/creation.ts:129-145 iterates inputGenerators verbatim; sole caller sendTransaction.ts:517). Core skips authority inputs (transaction_verifier.py:868-869) and appends MintHeader entries (transaction_verifier.py:894-897). MintHeader is attached after proof creation (src/utils/transaction.ts:1070-1071). Mechanism sound: hathor-ct-crypto/src/surjection.rs:40-45 wraps libsecp256k1-zkp surjectionproof_verify, which fails on domain length/order mismatch. HOWEVER, both failing scenarios are currently UNREACHABLE through wallet-lib: (1) authority inputs are rejected before crypto work — checkUnspentInput (sendTransaction.ts:1287-1295, 'XXX: We are NOT enabling authority outputs for now') runs on every user-supplied input (user-input tokens forced to chooseInputs=false at sendTransaction.ts:309, checked at :1191), and auto-selection filters authorities:0n (src/utils/utxo.ts:58,110); (2) MintHeader+FullShielded cannot co-occur — regular-tx MintHeader requires a mint-authority input (transaction.ts:1042,1056), impossible per (1), and createToken txs never carry shieldedOutputs (only SendTransaction.prepareTxData populates them, sendTransaction.ts:495-616, DEFAULT_TX_VERSION only; the TCT shielded-funding path uses excessBlindingFactor, mutually exclusive with shielded outputs per transaction.ts:961-968, so no surjection proofs exist). For every tx wallet-lib can actually build, the two domains coincide. Real divergence from source of truth and the recommendation is valid hardening, but it is a latent/forward-compat hazard (becomes high the moment authority-input or shielded-mint support is enabled), not an active bug — severity adjusted medium→low.

CONFIRMED. Every cited claim verified against the code; no compensating mechanism found anywhere in the worktree.

Evidence chain:
1. Spend addresses live on a different BIP44 account: /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/constants.ts:356 (SHIELDED_SPEND_ACCT_PATH = m/44'/280'/2'), src/utils/wallet.ts:714,728 (spendXpubkey derived from that path), src/utils/address.ts:178-211 (deriveShieldedAddressFromStorage builds spendAddress P2PKH from spendXpub, addressType 'shielded-spend').
2. Polling path DOES cover them: src/utils/storage.ts:117-131 pushes shieldedResult.spendAddress.base58 into the queried address set, and src/utils/storage.ts:177 subscribes them.
3. Stream paths do NOT: src/sync/stream.ts:237-254 loadAddressesCPUIntensive ("only contemplates P2PKH addresses for now") derives solely from one xpub; src/sync/stream.ts:416-423 sets this.xpubkey = accessData.xpubkey (account-0 xpub only); src/sync/stream.ts:482-495 (generateNextBatch) and :661-690 (sendStartMessage) send only that list / that xpub to the fullnode. src/sync/stream.ts:566-587 saves the shielded pair locally only — no subscription, no history fetch — which exactly masks the gap as claimed.
4. Source of truth: hathor-core's streamer matches and auto-subscribes ONLY the addresses it iterates (manual list or server-side derivation from the provided account-0 xpub) — /tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor/websocket/streamer.py:60-61 ("Each pushed address is automatically subscribed"), :276 (subscribe_address(item.address)). Core has no concept of the wallet-side scan/spend account convention, so vertices paying only spend-derived P2PKH scripts (account 2') are never streamed nor subscribed.
5. No guard: src/utils/storage.ts:71-84 getSupportedSyncMode allows MANUAL_STREAM_WS/XPUB_STREAM_WS for any P2PKH wallet regardless of shielded keys; src/new/wallet.ts:3784-3801 only falls back to polling when the node lacks the 'history-streaming' capability. Grepped the whole src/ for spendXpub/spendAddress/SHIELDED_SPEND in sync code — the only stream-side handling is the local-save at stream.ts:566-587.

Mitigating factor (keeps this at high rather than critical): the wallet's default sync mode is POLLING_HTTP_API (src/new/wallet.ts:430); streaming is opt-in via setHistorySyncMode. But known consumers (e.g. desktop wallet) enable streaming, and the failure mode is silent — a restored wallet shows zero shielded funds with no error. Severity high is correct.

CONFIRMED. Source of truth supports the finding's premise:
- hathorlib/hathorlib/transaction/shielded_tx_output.py:158-166 deserializes 33 zero bytes to ephemeral_pubkey=b'' (absent); :104 serializes zeros when empty. hathor/transaction/shielded_tx_output.py:26-27 re-exports these as canonical.
- hathor/verification/transaction_verifier.py:787-798 validates ephemeral_pubkey ONLY when present (`if output.ephemeral_pubkey:`); no other verify_* rejects an absent one => such an output is consensus-valid.
- hathor/transaction/base_transaction.py:101-102 omits 'ephemeral_pubkey' JSON key when empty; :108-112 adds 'decoded' only when parse_address_script returns truthy. These serializers feed the exact sync paths: WS streamer (websocket/streamer.py:300, factory.py:215), thin_wallet address_history (address_history.py:262), /transaction resource (transaction.py:101).

Wallet-lib confirmed: schemas.ts:106,116 require ephemeral_pubkey in Amount/FullShieldedOutputSchema; :191-192 require ephemeral_pubkey AND decoded in IHistoryShieldedOutputSchema (no .optional()); api/schemas/txApi.ts:113-114 require both. Consequences verified: WS new-tx safeParse failure returns early dropping the whole tx incl. transparent outputs (new/wallet.ts:1736-1740); polling uses addressHistorySchema=IHistoryTxSchema.array() as axios transformResponse (api/schemas/wallet.ts:16; api/wallet.ts:46,87,109) which throws; stream parse throws (sync/stream.ts:754); /transaction input resolution (utils/transaction.ts:1104-1117). shielded/types.ts:48 types ephemeral_pubkey as required; processing.ts:122 Buffer.from(...ephemeral_pubkey...) is at line 122, BEFORE the per-output try at :133, so a malformed/absent key aborts decryption of all other shielded outputs in the tx.

Minor imprecision (does not weaken finding): the finding emphasizes AmountShieldedOutputSchema/FullShieldedOutputSchema in the outputs[] union as the extended-JSON path; in practice the shielded_outputs[] entries are parsed by IHistoryShieldedOutputSchema (schemas.ts:215), which ALSO requires both keys, so the conclusion holds either way. A keyless shielded entry in outputs[] fails all three IHistoryOutputSchema union branches (schemas.ts:122-126; TransparentOutputSchema needs `value`/`token` which shielded entries lack), so IHistoryTxSchema fails for the whole tx.

Consumer impact verified: (a) WS — src/new/wallet.ts:1736-1740 safeParse fails → logger.error + return, the entire tx (including transparent outputs paying the wallet) silently dropped; normalizeShieldedOutputs runs only AFTER parse (wallet.ts:1745), so it cannot rescue. Exhaustive grep for fallback handling found only src/utils/storage.ts:1002 (`so?.ephemeral_pubkey ?? ''`) — that runs post-decryption when appending decoded entries, far after the failing parse, so it does not mitigate.

Impact scenario is concrete: a third party crafts a consensus-valid shielded tx with a zeroed ephemeral_pubkey (the deserializer/serializer pair explicitly supports it) and includes a transparent output to a victim address; the tx enters the victim's address history forever, silently dropping it on WS and hard-failing all polling/stream history loads — persistent sync DoS plus missed transparent funds. CONFIRMED. The up-front validation loop in /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/shielded/creation.ts:270-309 checks scanPubkey length, token UID lengths, and FullShielded input presence but has no value-range check; proposal.value flows unchecked into the crypto provider (creation.ts:83-97, 178-195) and errors are wrapped opaquely at creation.ts:358-364. Exhaustive search found no value-bound validation anywhere else: wallet entry (src/new/wallet.ts:1980-1995), sendTransaction phantom outputs, napi binding (hathor-ct-crypto/src/napi_bindings.rs:13-23 bigint_to_u64 accepts any u64), and no tests in __tests__/shielded/creation.test.ts. Source of truth confirms the [1, 2^40) domain: hathor-ct-crypto/src/rangeproof.rs:15-16 (RANGE_PROOF_BITS=40) and :45 (min_value=1). One mechanism detail is inaccurate but makes the finding stronger, not weaker: per vendored secp256k1-zkp (rangeproof_impl.h:215, value=0 fails as claimed; but min_bits=40 is only a floor — mantissa widens for larger values), proof creation does NOT throw for value in [2^40, 2^63-1]; it succeeds with a 41+-bit proof. Wallet-lib's ShieldedOutput.serialize does not enforce MAX_RANGE_PROOF_SIZE (only deserialize does, src/models/shielded_output.ts:175), so the wallet would sign and push the tx and the node rejects oversized proofs at deserialization — and borderline 41-bit proofs (~3309 B vs 3328 cap) may even be accepted by core, whose verify_range_proof (rangeproof.rs:85-103) enforces only min>=1 with no max-bits check, silently breaking the constant-proof-size privacy property. The recommended fix (validate 1 <= value < 2^40 in the up-front loop and for converted HTR change) is correct. Severity low is appropriate: 2^40 centi-units (~1.1e10 HTR) exceeds realistic amounts and value=0 is pathological, but the failure mode for [2^40, 2^41) is worse than the finding states (potential network-accepted proof with degraded privacy rather than a local throw).

Confirmed end-to-end. (1) Send side creates timelocked shielded outputs: src/new/wallet.ts ~1997 threads o.timelock for shielded ProposedOutputs; src/shielded/creation.ts:331 bakes `timelock: proposal.timelock ?? null` into the on-chain script via createOutputScript. (4) Selector treats it as spendable: memory_store.ts:764 `isTimeLocked(utxo.timelock || 0)`, and src/utils/utxo.ts:102-114 (bestUtxoSelection) deliberately auto-selects shielded UTXOs through that same path. (5) Node rejects the early spend: verify_script→script_eval is shielded-aware (hathor-core scripts/execute.py:118 resolve_spent_output) so the script timelock opcode is enforced identically to transparent. No compensating validation found anywhere in the worktree. Severity medium is appropriate: no fund loss (spendable after lock expiry), but balances misreport locked funds as unlocked and auto-selection builds node-rejected transactions. Recommended fix (parse the locally available script hex for timelock when appending the decoded entry) is sound.

CONFIRMED (core of finding). (1) Zero shielded references in the PartialTx subsystem or template engine: grep -rn -i 'shielded' over /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/models/partial_tx.ts, src/wallet/partialTxProposal.ts, src/wallet/api/swapService.ts, and src/template/ returns no hits. (2) Positional output resolution is real and wrong for shielded-bearing parents: PartialTx.validate() at src/models/partial_tx.ts:483-486 does `if (data.tx.outputs.length <= input.index) resolve(false)` then `data.tx.outputs[input.index]` — per core, inputs spending shielded outputs use index >= len(outputs) (hathor-core/hathor/transaction/base_transaction.py:347-363 resolve_spent_output, unified index space), and core's to_json puts shielded outputs in a separate `shielded_outputs` field (base_transaction.py:815-819), so validate() returns false (confusing rejection) for any legitimate shielded-spending input. (3) Templates: src/template/transaction/executor.ts:150 (`origTx.outputs[index]` on IHistoryTx from interpreter.getTx, interpreter.ts:268-273) and src/template/transaction/context.ts:130-134 (addBalanceFromUtxo) index positionally; wallet storage appends decoded shielded entries into history outputs[] with onChainIndex != array position (src/utils/storage.ts:1007-1008), so positional access can resolve the wrong output or undefined — exactly what findSpentOutput (src/utils/transaction.ts:139-157) was added to prevent, and it is not used in either subsystem. (4) Templates emitting to a shielded address do NOT fail fast: they call createOutputScriptFromAddress (executor.ts:401,474,645,978,1072; utils.ts:52), whose shielded branch (src/utils/address.ts:144-149) silently builds a transparent P2PKH(spend_pubkey) script — a transparent output that leaks the amount and is sent to an address the recipient's wallet won't scan. PARTIALLY REFUTED detail: the claim "no throw when a shielded address enters a swap" is wrong for the proposal API — PartialTxProposal.addOutput (src/wallet/partialTxProposal.ts:212-222) has a P2SH/P2PKH-only switch whose default throws AddressError('Unsupported address type'), so shielded addresses entering the swap output builder DO fail fast (generically, not shielded-aware). Also TODO_FIX_40_PARTIAL_TX_SHIELDED_ADDRESS_VALIDATION.md no longer exists at the project root (it appeared in an earlier git status snapshot). Net: the scoping gap is real — input-side (combined index space) handling is broken in both subsystems and template output-side silently degrades to transparent — but the swap output-side has a fail-fast throw the reviewer missed. Severity low remains correct for these secondary multi-party/advanced paths.

CONFIRMED. (1) wallet.ts:1851-1866 matches the quote: every onNewTx (websocket events AND sender-local inserts via sendTransaction.ts:849 → enqueueOnNewTx) scans the full txHistory and triggers storage.processHistory(this.pinCode) if ANY stored tx has shielded_outputs but no decoded entry; needsRetry is a local — no persisted attempt-marker exists anywhere in the worktree (grepped shieldedProcessed/DecodeAttempted/retry variants: zero hits outside this loop). (2) Ownership gate verified at src/shielded/processing.ts:106-111 (getAddressInfo check skips non-wallet addresses) and src/utils/storage.ts:963-1011 is the only code appending decoded entries — so txs whose shielded outputs all belong to third parties can never satisfy hasDecoded; the retry condition is permanently true. (4) processHistory cost verified at src/utils/storage.ts:468-542: cleanMetadata() + full processNewTx replay + nested per-input getTx loop = O(history × inputs) per incoming event, non-convergent. The "re-runs STATE-01" sub-claim was not adjudicated (depends on STATE-01) but the finding stands without it. CONFIRMED. (1) Safety net exists verbatim at /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/new/wallet.ts:1851-1866 and runs after every onNewTx (websocket new-tx, metadata updates, and sender-local inserts — all serialized through enqueueOnNewTx at wallet.ts:1721-1728); no marker anywhere in the codebase distinguishes 'decryption never attempted' from 'attempted, nothing is ours' (grep for shieldedProcessedAt/decodeAttempted/etc. is empty). (3) Scenario (a) is a supported flow: sendTransaction.ts:504-508 requires ≥2 shielded outputs (matching core transaction_verifier.py verify_trivial_commitment_protection), changeShieldedMode defaults to null (transparent change), and the sender-local insert (utils/transaction.ts:1257-1287) emits shielded_outputs with no decoded entries — so an all-external shielded send permanently poisons the sender's history. CONFIRMED. (1) Unconditional input-delete loop verified at /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/utils/storage.ts:511-533: runs inside processHistory's historyIter loop for every tx (historyIter has no voided filter, memory_store.ts:467-526) with no tx.is_voided guard, while processNewTx's voided early-return is at storage.ts:905-911. With asc ordering the origin UTXO is re-saved first, then the voided spender's iteration deletes it. git show master:src/utils/storage.ts proves master's processHistory had no such loop (its delete loop lived only in processSingleTx, which runs only for new unvoided txs). (2) Stamping verified at storage.ts:1259-1272 with gate at storage.ts:1105 and cleanMetadata not touching history (memory_store.ts:1121-1126); grep over src/ finds no code clearing spent_by. Nuance found while attacking: hathor-core DOES redeliver the origin tx with spent_by=null when the spender is voided (core address_index.py:55-71 + transaction_metadata.py:142-154 exclude voided spenders), and addTx's wire form overwrites the transparent stamp — so mechanism 2 self-heals for transparent outputs. But this does not refute the finding: mechanism 1 re-deletes the UTXO on every subsequent processHistory regardless, and for shielded outputs the merge at src/new/wallet.ts:1785-1798 preserves the stored decoded entries with the stale stamp exactly as claimed. Impact is actually slightly worse than stated for transparent UTXOs: core's address index includes voided txs in history (address_index.py:33 include_voided=True), so even a full wipe+resync re-delivers the voided spender and the delete loop destroys the UTXO again at sync-end processHistory — permanent until a code fix. Balance stays credited (storage.ts:1095-1101 has no spent_by check; voided spender contributes no debit), so the wallet displays funds it cannot spend. Voided txs are routine (double-spend conflict resolution), wallet.ts:1825-1830 routes voided changes to processHistory, and no test exercises void-then-resurrect (__tests__/utils/storage.test.ts only has is_voided:false fixtures). Severity high is correct: persistent wallet-state corruption and unspendable-balance display triggered by routine network events, but no on-chain fund loss and recoverable with fixed software, so not critical.

CONFIRMED. (1) Unconditional input-delete loop verified at src/utils/storage.ts:511-533 — runs for every historyIter tx with no tx.is_voided guard (processNewTx's voided early-return at 905-911 only skips processing, not the loop). git diff master...HEAD confirms both the loop and the spent_by stamping (storage.ts:1259-1272) are new. Walkthrough: processHistory saves the origin UTXO (spent_by===null, line 1105) then the voided spender's loop deletes it; balance is credited unconditionally (1095-1101) with no debit from the voided tx → unspendable displayed balance. WORSE than claimed: even full wipe+resync does not fix it, since refetched history includes voided txs (core address_index.py SCOPE include_voided=True) and the loop re-runs. (2) Stamping verified (no clearing code anywhere; cleanMetadata at memory_store.ts:1121-1126 spares history). One partial refutation: for TRANSPARENT outputs the stamp is NOT irreversible — hathor-core republishes the origin tx when a spender voids (address_index.py:64-69 walks voided tx inputs; get_output_spent_by at transaction_metadata.py:142-154 excludes voided spenders → wire spent_by=null), wallet addTx (storage.ts:402-406) overwrites the stamped tx, and processMetadataChanged (storage.ts:673-717) re-saves the UTXO. For SHIELDED the irreversibility holds exactly as claimed: wallet.ts:1785-1791 merges stale stamped decoded entries back onto redelivered txs and the alreadyDecoded skip (storage.ts:950) prevents refresh. Regardless, claim (1) re-destroys both transparent and shielded UTXOs on every later processHistory (reload, other voided changes via wallet.ts:1825-1830, shielded retry safety net 1851+). Realism caveat: in plain double-spends the winner spends the same output (no bug for that input); the bug needs an input left genuinely unspent after voiding (multi-input loser, propagated voiding, reorg) — still routine. Severity high is correct: wallet-local corruption showing unspendable balance, not healed by resync, but no on-chain fund loss.

CONFIRMED. (1) Unconditional delete loop verified at /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/utils/storage.ts:511-533, new vs master (git diff master...HEAD), no is_voided guard; processNewTx's voided early-return is at storage.ts:905-911 so a voided spender contributes no debit yet still deletes the origin UTXO on every processHistory. memory_store.ts:467-495 historyIter yields voided txs; no code removes voided txs from history. (2) Local spent_by stamping verified at storage.ts:1260-1272 with no reversal path (only write sites: 1267 and 999 per grep); cleanMetadata wipes utxos but not history (memory_store.ts:1121-1127); UTXO rebuild gated on spent_by===null at storage.ts:1105; voided update routes to processHistory at wallet.ts:1825-1830; onNewTx merge preserves stale decoded shielded entries at wallet.ts:~1786-1799, and the alreadyDecoded gate at storage.ts:950 prevents spent_by refresh for shielded entries. Source-of-truth check strengthens the finding: hathor-core's get_output_spent_by (hathor/transaction/transaction_metadata.py:142-154) excludes voided spenders, and hathor/indexes/address_index.py:55-69 actively republishes origin txs when a spender's voided_by changes ("their spent_by was affected") — core's canonical post-void state is "unspent" and clients are expected to resurrect; the branch's unconditional delete loop re-deletes the UTXO on every processHistory regardless of what the fullnode sends. Partial mitigation exists for transparent outputs only (redelivered origin tx overwrites the stamp; processMetadataChanged at storage.ts:657-720 can re-save the UTXO) but it is race-dependent and undone by any subsequent processHistory, and does not apply to shielded entries.
