Minor correction to the finding: wipe+resync does not fix it either, since initial sync delivers the voided spender and HTTP history also computes spent_by excluding voided spenders — the bug reappears after resync. Severity high is appropriate (unspendable displayed balance, persistent accounting divergence; no on-chain fund loss).

CONFIRMED with one mechanism correction. (1) Missing validation is real: the upfront validation block at /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/shielded/creation.ts:270-309 checks scanPubkey length (274), token UID length (283), and inputGenerators token length (304) but has NO value-range check; values flow straight to the provider at creation.ts:83-98 and 178-195. The shielded-def collection at /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/new/sendTransaction.ts:240-257 also has no value check, and exhaustive grep of the whole src tree found no [1,2^40) or value-positivity check anywhere on the shielded send path (only MAX_SHIELDED_OUTPUTS and MAX_RANGE_PROOF_SIZE byte-size checks). The Output model's falsy-value guard (src/models/output.ts:63) never applies: phantom IDataOutputs are removed (sendTransaction.ts:431-433) and createShieldedOutputs runs (sendTransaction.ts:517) before any Output model is built. (2) Value 0 behaves exactly as claimed: ct-crypto pins min_value=1 (hathor-ct-crypto/src/rangeproof.rs:44) and libsecp256k1-zkp rejects value<min_value at sign time (secp256k1-zkp-sys rangeproof_impl.h:215 'min_value > value' returns 0), so the user gets the wrapped cryptic error at creation.ts:361 after UTXO selection and prior proofs. (3) CORRECTION on the >=2^40 mechanism: the finding claims native creation throws for >=2^40; it does not. In libsecp256k1-zkp, min_bits is a FLOOR, not a cap (rangeproof_impl.h:162-165: mantissa = max(bits(v), min_bits)), and there is no explicit 2^40 check anywhere in hathor-ct-crypto/src/ (ecdh.rs create paths call create_range_proof with no value guard; verify only enforces range.start >= 1 at rangeproof.rs:95-101, ffi.rs:193, napi_bindings.rs:177; ct-crypto tests deliberately stop at u64::MAX>>24 = 2^40-1, rangeproof.rs:307,328). Consequence: values in [2^40, 2^41) produce a 41-bit ~3309-byte proof that passes BOTH the wallet's and core's MAX_RANGE_PROOF_SIZE=3328 cap (wallet src/models/shielded_output.ts:174-176; core hathorlib/transaction/shielded_tx_output.py:24) and verifies on the node — the tx is silently accepted while breaking the constant-proof-size privacy property ct-crypto's RANGE_PROOF_BITS comment promises (rangeproof.rs:7-14, test_proof_size_is_constant rangeproof.rs:323). Values >= ~2^41 yield >3328-byte proofs rejected late by the wallet's own serializer with the cryptic 'range proof size N exceeds maximum 3328' (shielded_output.ts:175-176). So the missing pre-validation is real and the recommended [1, 2^40) check is correct per the source of truth (core/ct-crypto intent), but the failure mode for [2^40,2^41) is silent acceptance with a size-distinguishable proof rather than an error — arguably slightly worse than the finding states. Severity low remains appropriate: triggering requires value 0 or amounts above ~10^12 base units, and the homomorphic balance still holds. Note the local reference doc client-guide-checklist.md:12 says '[1, 2^64)' (older Bulletproof text); core wins with the 40-bit Borromean design.

CONFIRMED. Every cited claim checks out in the code. (1) /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/utils/storage.ts:1005-1006 writes `blindingFactor: result.decrypted.blindingFactor.toString('hex')` and `assetBlindingFactor: ...toString('hex')` onto the decoded entry pushed into tx.outputs (line 985-1008), and storage.ts:1010-1012 persists it via `await store.saveTx(tx)`. (2) storage.ts:1141-1147 (finding cited 1138-1144; actual span is 1131-1148 — trivial offset) spreads `{ shielded: true, blindingFactor, assetBlindingFactor }` into every shielded `store.saveUtxo` call. (3) Type definitions confirm plaintext: src/types.ts:473-474 (IUtxo.blindingFactor/assetBlindingFactor are bare hex strings) and src/types.ts:352 (IShieldedOutputEntry.blindingFactor hex). The asymmetry with key material is real: src/types.ts:494-505 shows mainKey/scanMainKey/spendMainKey as IEncryptedData (PIN-encrypted), so the PIN gate at storage.ts:951-956 protects the scan key but its decryption OUTPUT is stored unprotected. (4) Exhaustive refutation attempt found no encryption layer anywhere: grep for "encrypt" across src/shielded/* and src/utils/storage.ts in non-decrypt contexts returned nothing; src/storage/memory_store.ts and src/storage/storage.ts never reference blindingFactor — they persist IUtxo/IHistoryTx objects verbatim, so any IStore backend (IndexedDB/LevelDB/AsyncStorage) receives plaintext openings. (5) Persistence is intentional, not incidental: src/utils/transaction.ts:1363-1366 and src/new/sendTransaction.ts:472-480 read utxo.blindingFactor at spend time and throw if missing. (6) Source-of-truth side holds: hathor-core never persists blindings (hathor/crypto/shielded/balance.py is sender-side computation only), and the client guide (SHIELDED_INTEGRATION_REVIEW/reference/client-guide-checklist.md:32,36) explicitly states blinding factors are returned to the client to be "kept secret (needed for spending)" — custody is wallet policy, and this wallet's policy is plaintext-at-rest. The recommendation is also technically sound: the blinding factor is recovered by range-proof rewind with the PIN-gated scan key (that is exactly how processShieldedOutputs obtained it), so on-demand recomputation instead of persistence is feasible. Severity medium is correct: threat model requires local storage read access (same as the pre-existing plaintext tx history), no fund-loss path (spend key remains PIN-encrypted), but the exposure is strictly worse than plaintext history because blinding factors constitute permanent third-party-verifiable cryptographic proof of amounts against the on-chain commitments. Not high (no remote vector, no theft); not low (irrevocable privacy proof escalation over the legacy baseline). Already self-acknowledged in the repo as TODO_FIX_38_BLINDING_FACTORS_PLAINTEXT_STORAGE.md.

1) Default selection mixes shielded into plain sends, deliberately: /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/utils/utxo.ts:102-113 — bestUtxoSelection's IUtxoFilterOptions omits the `shielded` filter, with a comment explicitly saying "Select any UTXO (transparent or shielded)" and justifying it only on consensus grounds (core accepts it), not privacy. fastUtxoSelection (utxo.ts:56-62) likewise omits it. _prepareSendTokensData defaults to bestUtxoSelection (src/new/sendTransaction.ts:1132,1158).

2) The store DOES support the filter — memory_store.ts:825-826 honors `options.shielded === false` → transparent-only — but no send-path caller ever sets it. Grep of the whole worktree found zero uses of `shielded: false` in src/ outside the store implementation itself.

3) Wallet-owned shielded UTXOs land in the same spendable pool with unblinded values: src/utils/storage.ts:700-717 saves them via store.saveUtxo with `shielded: true` and the real value, same token uid, so they compete head-to-head in selection (order_by_value 'desc' means a large deliberately-shielded chunk is often picked).

4) The full-unshield branch is silent: src/new/sendTransaction.ts:543-577 (`if (shieldedOutputs.length === 0 && blindedInputsArr.length > 0)`) computes the excess and attaches it with no flag, no event, no returned metadata. SendTransaction's emitted events (sendTransaction.ts:776-862) include nothing unshield-related.

5) No opt-in/opt-out anywhere in the public API: SendTransactionFullnodeOptions (src/new/types.ts:367-371: changeAddress/token/pinCode only), SendManyOutputsOptions (types.ts:386-392: no input-selection control; changeShieldedMode only affects change output), IUtxoSelectionOptions (src/types.ts:619-624: token/changeAddress/chooseInputs/utxoSelectionMethod). No allowUnshield/spendShielded/transparentOnly exists under any name (exhaustive grep).

6) Core side confirms the privacy consequence: /tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor/transaction/headers/unshield_balance_header.py:42-47 — with exactly one shielded input the revealed excess scalar "is effectively r_in of that input" and a full unshield's transparent outputs "expose the spent amount". Minor citation inaccuracy: the docstring does NOT contain the quoted phrase "leaving multi-input aggregation/hygiene to wallets" — it instead argues the scalar leaks nothing beyond what the unshield itself exposes. This weakens the "core explicitly delegates hygiene to wallets" framing but not the substance: the unshield itself publicly reveals the previously-confidential amount, and the wallet performs it without consent.

7) Intentionality confirmed but doesn't refute: __tests__/integration/shielded_outputs/edge_cases.test.ts:165 ("Plain transparent send — must consume shielded UTXOs") and unshielding.test.ts:46 (U.1) show auto-unshield is the designed unshield UX. Note for the fix: a transparent send is currently the ONLY unshield path, so flipping the default to `shielded: false` must ship together with the explicit opt-in option, or unshielding becomes impossible.

Impact scenario is concrete and realistic: a user who deliberately shielded funds makes any routine transparent payment; selection picks the (typically large) shielded UTXO; tx publicly reveals the hidden amount (single-shielded-input case: exact value = transparent outputs + fee − transparent inputs, and the excess scalar equals that input's blinding factor) — permanently, on-chain, with no warning. For a feature whose entire purpose is amount confidentiality, silent deanonymization via the default code path justifies HIGH (not critical: no fund/key loss, only wallet-owned blindings revealed, consensus-valid tx). The recommendation also matches the project's own explicit-options-over-inference convention.

CONFIRMED. (4) Wallet's own send path produces timelocked shielded scripts: src/new/wallet.ts:1997 forwards timelock, src/shielded/creation.ts:331 embeds it in the on-chain script. (5) Core enforces timelock on spend: transaction_verifier.py:143-146,203 evaluates spent shielded scripts via verify_script like transparent ones; no core restriction forbids timelocked shielded scripts. (6) Inconsistency confirmed: local-insert path src/utils/transaction.ts:1260-1270 DOES parse the script (P2PKH.toData() includes timelock), so self-sends disagree with WS-received state for the same output. Confirmed end-to-end. (1) Core emits only {address} in decoded for shielded outputs: /tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor/transaction/base_transaction.py:108-112. (2) Wallet receive path spreads that decoded and never parses the script: /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/utils/storage.ts:990 (`decoded: { ...so?.decoded, address: result.address }`); UTXO saved with always-null timelock at storage.ts:1139 (`timelock: output.decoded.timelock || null`). No parseScript call exists anywhere in the receive path (grep across src/). (3) isOutputLocked treats undefined timelock as unlocked (src/utils/transaction.ts:311-315), so value lands in tokens.unlocked (storage.ts:1095-1100) and the UTXO passes selection (memory_store.ts:764 `isTimeLocked(utxo.timelock || 0)`). (4) Send side genuinely supports timelocked shielded outputs: src/new/types.ts:350 (IShieldedOutputProposal.timelock?), src/shielded/creation.ts:331, and the shielded branch of createOutputScript builds P2PKH WITH timelock (src/utils/transaction.ts:823-830). (5) Self-send local-insert path DOES recover the timelock via parseScript/toData (src/utils/transaction.ts:1260,1270; P2PKH.toData includes timelock at src/models/p2pkh.ts:93), proving the script carries it and only the fullnode-delivered receive path drops it. Severity high stands: balance over-reporting plus node-rejected spends, but no permanent fund loss (UTXO becomes spendable after lock expiry). Recommended fix (parse so.script on receipt like the self-send path) is correct.

Confirmed on both wallet-lib and hathor-core sides. Wallet-lib: /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/sync/stream.ts:420-423 (setupStream uses only accessData.xpubkey), :237-254 (loadAddressesCPUIntensive derives legacy P2PKH only — comment says so), :482-495 and :672-684 (manual batches sent via sendManualStreamingHistory contain only legacy addresses), :566-587 (shielded/spend pairs saved locally, never transmitted). CONFIRMED. Stream sync modes never communicate shielded spend-chain addresses to the fullnode. Evidence: (1) stream.ts:416-423 setupStream uses only accessData.xpubkey (legacy account); grep shows no scanXpubkey/spendXpubkey reference anywhere in stream.ts. (2) Manual mode batches at stream.ts:482-495 and the start message at stream.ts:672-684 both use loadAddressesCPUIntensive (stream.ts:237-254), which derives plain P2PKH children of the legacy xpub only — spend P2PKHs never appear in sendManualStreamingHistory payloads. (3) stream.ts:566-587 saves the shielded/spend address pair to local storage only; nothing transmitted. (4) connection.subscribeAddresses has exactly one src call site, utils/storage.ts:177 (polling apiSyncHistory path), and only the polling loadAddresses (utils/storage.ts:127-131) adds spendAddress.base58 to the subscribed/history-fetched list. (5) No guard or warning: getSupportedSyncMode (utils/storage.ts:71-84) permits stream modes for all P2PKH wallets; syncHistory (src/new/wallet.ts:3784-3801) falls back to polling only on missing 'history-streaming' capability, never on shielded keys; gap-extension (wallet.ts:1666-1677) reuses the same stream mode. (6) Core side confirms impact: hathor/websocket/iterators.py:97-111 derives only xpub.subkey(idx) children of the single provided xpub (cannot reach hardened account 2'); hathor/websocket/streamer.py:61,276 auto-subscribes only streamed (legacy) addresses; hathor/transaction/base_transaction.py:509-512 indexes shielded outputs by their on-chain script address, i.e. the spend P2PKH (src/utils/shieldedAddress.ts:22-23,91-92). Net effect as claimed: under XPUB_STREAM_WS/MANUAL_STREAM_WS, historical shielded receives are never streamed and no real-time subscription covers spend addresses; stream.ts:831-832 processHistory has nothing shielded to decode. Severity high (not critical): funds are not lost or exposed — they reappear in polling mode, which is the constructor default (wallet.ts:430); impact requires opting into stream modes (typical for headless/large wallets), and the failure is silent.

CONFIRMED — every link in the chain verified against both wallet-lib and hathor-core.

1) Core never serializes timelock for shielded outputs: /tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor/transaction/base_transaction.py:109-113 — `_shielded_output_to_json` with decode_script=True emits only `data['decoded'] = {'address': script_type.address}` even though `parse_address_script` has the full script. Cross-checked all other serializers: to_json_extended (base_transaction.py:888-896) and event shapes (hathor/event/model/event_data.py:47-69, ShieldedTxOutput.decoded comes from the same function) — no path emits timelock for shielded outputs.

2) Wallet receive path drops it: /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/utils/storage.ts:990 `decoded: { ...so?.decoded, address: result.address }` — so?.decoded is the fullnode JSON (address-only). Grep of src/shielded/processing.ts and src/shielded/unblinding.ts: zero hits for `timelock` or `parseScript`; nothing parses so.script on receipt. UTXO then saved with `timelock: output.decoded.timelock || null` at storage.ts:1139 (same pattern at 529, 635, 708 for the history-reload paths) — always null for received shielded outputs.

3) Lock check treats null as unlocked: src/utils/transaction.ts:305-316 isOutputLocked returns false when decoded.timelock is undefined/null → value credited to unlocked balance (transaction.ts:634+) and saveLockedUtxo never called (storage.ts:1149-1153), so the UTXO is offered for selection prematurely.

4) Send side really does build timelocked shielded scripts: proposal types carry timelock (src/shielded/types.ts:58,97; src/new/types.ts:350); src/shielded/creation.ts:331 passes `timelock: proposal.timelock ?? null` into createOutputScript; src/utils/transaction.ts:823-830 'shielded' branch builds `new P2PKH(spendAddress, { timelock: output.timelock })` — so the lock is embedded in the on-chain script. The asymmetry is proven by the self-send path: src/utils/transaction.ts:1259-1271 uses `parseScript(so.script, ...)` and `decoded: parsed?.toData()`, and P2PKH.toData (src/models/p2pkh.ts:89-95) includes timelock — only the receive/websocket path drops it.

5) Core enforces the lock on spend: /tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor/transaction/scripts/execute.py:118 ("Use resolve_spent_output for shielded-aware lookup") — script_eval evaluates shielded output scripts identically to transparent ones (called from transaction_verifier.py verify_script), so a pre-expiry spend is rejected by the node after PoW.

Severity high is appropriate: wallet over-reports available balance and silently selects locked UTXOs, producing node-rejected transactions (wasted PoW, confusing failures); no permanent fund loss (lock eventually expires), so not critical. Recommendation in the finding (parse so.script with parseScript on the receive path, as the self-send path already does) is correct and minimal.

CONFIRMED. (1) `this.pinCode` is assigned exactly once, in the constructor: /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/new/wallet.ts:387; no setPinCode/enableShieldedSync exists anywhere in src/. (2) start() at wallet.ts:2053-2054 reads `const pinCode = options.pinCode || this.pinCode` into a LOCAL, uses it for access-data generation (2086-2105) and migrateShieldedAccessData (2115-2129, shielded scan keys migrated), but never persists to the instance; the guard at 2057 accepts the local pin, so a seed wallet constructed without pinCode and started via start({pinCode}) is a legal, reachable state. (3) Every receive-side decode site passes `this.pinCode ?? undefined`: wallet.ts:659, 1657, 1674, 1824, 1829, 3832; the safety-net at 1851/1865 is explicitly gated on `this.pinCode` so it cannot recover either. (4) The decode gate at src/utils/storage.ts:950-955 requires `pinCode !== undefined` and silently skips — no else branch, no warning; the warnings in src/shielded/processing.ts (77/177/204) are only reachable when a pin was supplied. (5) Sender-local insert: sendTransaction.ts:819-851 enqueues via wallet.enqueueOnNewTx → onNewTx → processNewTx(newTx, this.pinCode ?? undefined), and convertTransactionToHistoryTx emits raw shielded_outputs wire form (utils/transaction.ts:1250-1273, comment explicitly defers to "processNewTx's decryption gate") — so the wallet's own shielded change is untracked even though the send accepted a per-call pin (wallet.ts:1973). Per-call-pin is a pervasive supported pattern (1973, 2270, 2471, 2587, 2691, 2807, 3529, 3622, 3725, 3913). (6) No doc/contract mitigates: the only nearby comment (wallet.ts:2144-2148) covers provider registration, not pin availability. Core/RFC side irrelevant — scan keys are pin-encrypted in access data, so pin is genuinely required; this is a wallet-lib key-availability contract gap. Severity medium is correct: recoverable via restart-with-constructor-pin (processHistory re-decodes), no data loss, but silent and confusing.

CONFIRMED on every claim, with corroborating evidence on both wallet-lib and hathor-core sides.

Wallet-lib evidence:
1. /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/sync/stream.ts:416-423 — setupStream reads only `accessData.xpubkey` (legacy account chain) into `this.xpubkey`. No spend/scan xpub is ever read here.
2. stream.ts:482-495 (generateNextBatch) and stream.ts:671-684 (sendStartMessage, MANUAL_STREAM_WS case) — manual batches are produced exclusively by `loadAddressesCPUIntensive(..., this.xpubkey, ...)`; no spend-derived P2PKH is ever included in `sendManualStreamingHistory`. stream.ts:663-669 — XPUB mode sends only `this.xpubkey`.
3. stream.ts:566-587 — shielded/spend addresses are derived and saved LOCALLY when an address item arrives, but never transmitted to the fullnode. This is exactly the "looks shielded-aware but isn't" pattern the finding describes.
4. Exhaustive grep: `connection.subscribeAddresses` is called from exactly one place in src/: /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/utils/storage.ts:177, inside apiSyncHistory (polling path only). The polling path DOES include spend addresses — utils/storage.ts:117-132 pushes `shieldedResult.spendAddress.base58` into the fetched/subscribed list, with a comment (lines 127-130) explicitly stating the fullnode indexes by on-chain script address, confirming the intended design that stream modes fail to replicate.
5. No guard exists: getSupportedSyncMode (utils/storage.ts:71-84) allows both stream modes for any P2PKH wallet regardless of shielded keys; the only fallback to polling (src/new/wallet.ts:3788-3801) is triggered by missing fullnode 'history-streaming' capability, not by shielded keys. No warning/error anywhere for the stream+shielded combination (grep for MANUAL_STREAM/XPUB_STREAM in src/shielded/ and src/utils/shieldedAddress.ts: zero hits).
6. constants.ts:350 (scan account 1') and 356 (spend account 2') — hardened accounts disjoint from the streamed account-0 xpub.
7. No integration test covers stream sync + shielded (__tests__/integration/shielded_outputs/ has no stream-mode usage).

Hathor-core evidence:
8. /tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor/websocket/iterators.py:97-111 — xpub streaming derives only `xpub.subkey(idx)` children of the single provided xpub; hardened account-2' children are unreachable from an account-0 xpub (hardened derivation requires the private key).
9. /tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor/websocket/streamer.py:61,276 — the fullnode-side streamer subscribes for real-time updates ONLY the addresses it streams, so spend addresses get neither historical txs nor real-time events under stream modes.
10. Shielded outputs carry an on-chain P2PKH script on the spend pubkey (hathorlib ShieldedOutput, re-exported via /tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor/transaction/shielded_tx_output.py; wallet-side confirmation at /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/utils/address.ts:201-208: "on-chain scripts reference this P2PKH, not the shielded address"). So a pure shielded receive touches no legacy-chain address and is fully invisible under stream sync.

Impact: a wallet with shielded keys configured for MANUAL_STREAM_WS or XPUB_STREAM_WS silently misses all shielded receives — missing from history, balance, and real-time events — with no error or warning; final processHistory (stream.ts:831-832) has nothing to decode. Funds reappear if re-synced via polling, so this is silent invisibility, not loss. Mitigating factor: default mode is POLLING_HTTP_API (src/new/wallet.ts:430), so stream modes require explicit opt-in — but that opt-in is the documented recommendation for large wallets (headless). High severity is appropriate: silent wrong balances on a supported configuration, no detection path.

Confirmed. Buggy loop: src/new/sendTransaction.ts:556-562 pushes every out.value into transparentOutputEntries with no authority/zero-value filter. Correct sibling: src/utils/transaction.ts:1392-1399 guards with `out.value > 0n && (out.authorities ?? 0n) === 0n` and documents why ("Authority outputs are skipped because their value field is the authority mask"). Asymmetry exists within sendTransaction itself: the input side at line 485 DOES filter authority/zero utxos for transparentInputEntries. Source of truth agrees: core verifier skips authority outputs in the balance equation (transaction_verifier.py:979-980 `if output.is_token_authority(): continue`) and ct-crypto skips zero-value entries (balance.rs:56-57, 73-74), so an excess scalar computed with an authority mask value included would mismatch the verifier and the tx would be rejected. The bug is latent as the finding states: all entries reaching the loop have authorities hardcoded to 0n in prepareTxData (sendTransaction.ts:237, 252, 275) and change outputs are value outputs, so sendManyOutputs cannot currently hit it. Severity low is correct; recommendation (mirror the utils/transaction.ts guard) is sound.

Confirmed. (1) Core unconditionally requires >=2 shielded outputs when any exist: /tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor/verification/transaction_verifier.py:1219-1227 (TrivialCommitmentError, "conservatively require >= 2 shielded outputs always"). (2) Wallet throws hard on exactly one: /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/new/sendTransaction.ts:505-509 and src/shielded/creation.ts:264-268. (3) Zero-value decoy is cryptographically impossible: /tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor-ct-crypto/src/rangeproof.rs:44 and :95-99 enforce min_value>=1 at proof creation (and :128 at verification), so a 0-value second shielded output cannot be built. (4) changeShieldedMode cannot rescue the exact-funds/no-change case: convertHtrChangeIfRequested returns addedFee 0n when no HTR change output exists (src/new/sendTransaction.ts:1081) or change <= fee (:1084-1088). (5) Exhaustive grep found no auto-split, decoy, or other graceful path in wallet-lib. Two caveats: (a) the sub-claim that core docs suggest a "possibly zero-value" second output could not be found anywhere in the core repo — minor framing inaccuracy; (b) a manual workaround exists: callers can explicitly pass two shielded outputs to the same address, so the limitation is a UX/guidance gap rather than a hard impossibility, consistent with the stated low severity. Recommendation (document + optional auto-split into two >=1 outputs to the same address) is sound.

CONFIRMED. (1) src/utils/wallet.ts:710-729 (generateAccessDataFromSeed) and :627-637 (generateAccessDataFromXpriv) derive shielded scan (1')/spend (2') keys with no walletType check — the MULTISIG branch at :686-688 only affects the legacy mainKey. (2) src/utils/storage.ts:117-132 (loadAddresses): the only walletType branch (:108-112) covers legacy addresses; shielded address pairs are then derived, saved, and the spend P2PKH subscribed unconditionally for every wallet type including MULTISIG. src/new/wallet.ts:840-850 also hands out shielded addresses with no guard. (3) src/utils/address.ts:200-208: on-chain spendAddress is a single-key P2PKH from the spend pubkey. (4) src/utils/transaction.ts:412-430 signs 'shielded-spend' inputs with one key from the spend chain; signTransaction (:498-503) wraps it via createInputData(sig,pubkey) — plain P2PKH input data, never a multisig redeem script. (5) Exhaustive grep: zero multisig handling in src/shielded/, src/utils/shieldedAddress.ts, or sendTransaction's shielded path (its walletType check at sendTransaction.ts:1114-1123 is only for transparent change); no multisig+shielded tests. (6) Core agrees with the finding's premise: no multisig concept in hathor/crypto/shielded/ or shielded headers; shielded scripts parsed as standard address scripts (base_transaction.py ~509-512), only P2PKH/MULTISIG version bytes exist (conf/mainnet.py:23-24). Aggravating detail beyond the finding: in a multisig wallet each participant has a different root seed, so each participant derives a DIFFERENT shielded scan/spend pair — participants don't even agree on the wallet's shielded address, and shielded funds received by one participant's pair are invisible to and unspendable by the others. Severity medium is right: silent quorum bypass, but requires the user to actively share a shielded address, and funds remain spendable by the deriving participant.

CONFIRMED. src/shielded/creation.ts:263-309 validates output count, scanPubkey length (274), token UID length (283), and inputGenerators token UIDs (299-308), but never checks proposal.value against [1, 2^40). Exhaustive greps across src/ and __tests__/ for 2**40 / 1099511627776 / RANGE_PROOF / value-bound patterns found no range guard anywhere in the shielded build path; the only value constant is MAX_OUTPUT_VALUE=2n**63n (src/constants.ts:107) used solely by outputValueToBytes for transparent outputs. The lone value check is the post-rewind recoveredValue <= 0n at src/shielded/processing.ts:203, exactly as claimed. The provider/bindings add nothing: @hathor/ct-crypto-provider/lib/abstract.js:37-42 passes value through; napi_bindings.rs:13-19 only rejects negative/>u64; ecdh.rs:152-184 has no amount check. Source of truth confirms the 40-bit consensus limit: /tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor-ct-crypto/src/rangeproof.rs:15 (RANGE_PROOF_BITS=40) and hathorlib/hathorlib/transaction/shielded_tx_output.py:24 (MAX_RANGE_PROOF_SIZE=3328, "Borromean @ 40-bit: 3213 B + headroom"); the client guide's [1, 2^64) (checklist lines 12, 57) is stale — core wins. One mechanism correction that does not refute the finding: rangeproof.rs:52 passes 40 as min_bits, and secp256k1-zkp expands the mantissa for larger values rather than failing, so values >= 2^40 usually fail later via the opaque "range proof size N exceeds maximum 3328" throw at src/models/shielded_output.ts:174-176 (mirrored in core), not inside createRangeProof itself; values just over 2^40 (41-bit proofs, ~3290 B) may even fit under 3328 and pass fullnode verification since verify_range_proof (rangeproof.rs:83-103; transaction_verifier.py:800-827) only enforces min>=1 with no upper-bit cap. Either way the wallet emits no clear error naming the value or the 40-bit limit, so the missing upfront [1, 2^40) guard and the recommendation stand. Medium severity is right: no fund loss (pre-broadcast failure), but >2^40 custom-token amounts are reachable and the boundary behavior is inconsistent/opaque.

All cited evidence verified and the finding survives. (1) src/utils/wallet.ts:674-678 sets walletType=MULTISIG, lines 686-692 branch on it only for the legacy account key, then lines 712-715 and 726-729 derive scan/spend xprivs unconditionally from the local participant's rootXpriv. (2) src/utils/storage.ts:117-131 derives/saves shielded address pairs for every wallet with keys, no walletType check (only the legacy branch at 108-112 checks type). (3) src/utils/address.ts:178-186 gates only on key presence. (4) src/new/wallet.ts:837-850 getAddressAtIndex({legacy:false}) and 897-911 getCurrentAddress expose shielded addresses with no multisig gating; storage.ts:308-310 is a pass-through. (5) src/utils/shieldedAddress.ts:91-92 — on-chain spend script is P2PKH over the single spend pubkey. (6) src/utils/transaction.ts:413-419 signs shielded-spend inputs single-sig via getSpendXPrivKey. Additional evidence beyond the claim: migrateShieldedAccessData (src/utils/wallet.ts:758-813) retrofits shielded keys onto EXISTING multisig wallets on upgrade (gates only on accessData.words, line 773; called unconditionally from src/new/wallet.ts:2121-2129), and the shielded change conversion in src/new/sendTransaction.ts:1095-1109 routes a multisig wallet's change to the local participant's single-sig shielded chain. Exhaustive grep: zero multisig/getWalletType references in src/shielded/*, shieldedAddress.ts; the only WalletType.MULTISIG check in sendTransaction.ts (line 1119) is for transparent change script type. No test covers multisig+shielded gating. Source of truth confirmed: /tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor/conf/mainnet.py defines only P2PKH_VERSION_BYTE and MULTISIG_VERSION_BYTE; hathor/crypto/shielded/ has no multisig concept — consensus is agnostic, so the wallet must gate this itself and does not. Severity high is correct: m-of-n custody is silently bypassed for shielded funds, but exploitation requires active use of the shielded feature on a multisig wallet.

CONFIRMED. (1) stream.ts:233-254: loadAddressesCPUIntensive derives only account-0 P2PKH children from the passed xpub ("This only contemplates P2PKH addresses for now"); both stream modes use accessData.xpubkey (stream.ts:416-423). Manual mode sends only that batch (stream.ts:482-495, sendStartMessage ~672-684); xpub mode sends only the account-0 xpub (stream.ts:662-668) and core derives from it server-side (hathor-core websocket/iterators.py:97-114 iter_xpub_addresses uses xpub.subkey(idx) only). (2) Shielded spend addresses are P2PKH of pubkeys on a different account, m/44'/280'/2'/0 (constants.ts:356, types.ts:504, shieldedAddress.ts:64,91-92), so streamed account-0 addresses can never match them. (3) Polling path contrast verified: utils/storage.ts:117-131 pushes shieldedResult.spendAddress.base58 into the queried set. (4) No guard or backfill exists anywhere: getSupportedSyncMode (utils/storage.ts:71-84) allows both stream modes for P2PKH wallets regardless of spendXpubkey; syncHistory (new/wallet.ts:3784-3801) downgrades only on missing node capability; streamSyncHistory ends with processHistory only (stream.ts:830-833); checkGapLimit's shielded branch (utils/storage.ts:424-427) keys off shieldedLastUsedAddressIndex which never advances under stream sync (no shielded vertices arrive), so it cannot trigger a backfill; stream.ts:566-587 saves the shielded address pair locally but never their history. (5) Core-side premise verified: shielded output recipient addresses ARE indexed (hathor-core base_transaction.py get_related_addresses parses shielded_out.script, ~line 508), so streaming the spend addresses would work — the wallet just never sends them. Result: a wallet restored via XPUB_STREAM_WS or MANUAL_STREAM_WS silently misses all history vertices that pay only shielded-spend scripts. Mitigating: default historySyncMode is POLLING_HTTP_API (new/wallet.ts:430), so only consumers opting into stream modes (e.g. desktop wallet) are affected — but the failure is silent loss of fund visibility on restore, so high severity stands.

CONFIRMED. (1) Stream modes derive only from the transparent account xpub: src/sync/stream.ts:237-254 (loadAddressesCPUIntensive, 'only contemplates P2PKH addresses for now', derives hdpubkey.deriveChild(i)), :416-423 (this.xpubkey = accessData.xpubkey), :482-495 and :661-690 (manual batches / xpub start message send only that). Shielded spend addresses live on a separate chain m/44'/280'/2'/0 (src/constants.ts:356, src/types.ts:502-504). (2) hathor-core's streamer derives strictly from the single provided xpub (hathor/websocket/iterators.py:97-113, xpub.subkey(idx)) and subscribes only streamed addresses (hathor/websocket/streamer.py:276) — it cannot match spend-chain addresses. (3) Shielded receives are on-chain P2PKH scripts of the spend key (src/utils/transaction.ts:823-829), so vertices paying only those scripts are never streamed. (4) Polling path compensates by pushing shieldedResult.spendAddress.base58 into the queried set (src/utils/storage.ts:117-131); the stream consumer only saves the address pair locally (src/sync/stream.ts:566-587), masking the gap exactly as claimed. (5) No guard: getSupportedSyncMode (src/utils/storage.ts:71-84) allows both stream modes for P2PKH wallets regardless of shielded keys; syncHistory (src/new/wallet.ts:3784-3801) downgrades only on missing fullnode capability. Grep across the worktree found no other compensation (no spendXpub/spendAddress handling in sync/connection paths). Impact qualifier: default sync mode is POLLING_HTTP_API (src/new/wallet.ts:430), so only consumers opting into streaming (e.g. desktop wallet uses xpub streaming) are affected, and funds remain recoverable via a polling resync — hence high, not critical. Silent zero-balance on seed restore in a shipped sync mode keeps it at high.

Confirmed at every cited location. (1) Stream modes derive/send only account-0 xpub addresses: /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/sync/stream.ts:233-254 (loadAddressesCPUIntensive, comment "only contemplates P2PKH addresses for now", derives from this.xpubkey set at stream.ts:416-423 from accessData.xpubkey), :482-495 and :672-684 (manual batches), :664-669 (xpub mode sends only that xpub). (2) Shielded spend keys are a sibling hardened account m/44'/280'/2' (src/constants.ts:356, src/utils/wallet.ts:628-636); core's xpub streamer derives via single xpub.subkey(idx) (hathor-core hathor/websocket/iterators.py:97-114) so it can never reach them. (3) Core matches shielded-receive vertices by the address parsed from shielded_out.script (hathor-core hathor/transaction/base_transaction.py:506-512), and wallet-lib builds that script as P2PKH of the spend pubkey (src/utils/address.ts:144-148, 178-211) — so vertices paying only shielded outputs are indexed solely under account-2' addresses the stream never covers. (4) Polling path asymmetry confirmed: src/utils/storage.ts:117-131 pushes spendAddress.base58 into the queried set; stream path only saves the pair locally (stream.ts:566-587), masking the gap exactly as claimed. (5) No guard: getSupportedSyncMode (src/utils/storage.ts:71-84) allows both stream modes for any P2PKH wallet regardless of shielded keys; syncHistory (src/new/wallet.ts:3784-3801) falls back to polling only on missing node capability. Exhaustive grep found no shielded handling elsewhere in stream/sync. Mitigating factor (keeps it high rather than critical): default historySyncMode is POLLING_HTTP_API (src/new/wallet.ts:430) and funds are recoverable by re-syncing with polling; but consumers opting into stream sync (e.g., desktop) silently show zero shielded balance after seed restore.

Verified evidence:
1. /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/utils/wallet.ts:686-692 — the MULTISIG branch only switches the legacy account path (P2SH_ACCT_PATH vs P2PKH_ACCT_PATH). Lines 710-715 then derive scanXpriv/spendXpriv unconditionally from the local rootXpriv, and lines 726-729 store them, regardless of walletType. The migration path migrateShieldedAccessData (wallet.ts:758-813) is equally unconditional — it only checks field presence and `words`, never walletType, so even pre-existing multisig wallets get shielded keys injected on upgrade.
2. /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/utils/storage.ts:117-131 — loadAddresses calls deriveShieldedAddressFromStorage(i, storage) for every index with only a comment "if keys are available"; no wallet-type check. Both the shielded and spend-P2PKH addresses are saved and the spend address is subscribed.
3. /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/utils/address.ts:178-211 — deriveShieldedAddressFromStorage gates only on scanXpub/spendXpub presence (lines 182-186), which multisig wallets now always have.
4. /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/new/wallet.ts:837-850 — getAddressAtIndex with opts.legacy===false derives and returns a shielded address with no walletType check (the only MULTISIG check nearby, line 879, is in getAddressPathForIndex and is skipped entirely when legacy:false, line 872-876). getCurrentAddress (897-911) delegates to storage with no gating; storage.getAddressAtIndex (src/storage/storage.ts:257-262) is a plain store passthrough.
5. /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/utils/transaction.ts:413-419 — inputs whose addressInfo.addressType === 'shielded-spend' are signed with a single key derived from getSpendXPrivKey; no P2SH/multisig path for shielded spends.
6. /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/utils/shieldedAddress.ts:91-92 — on-chain spend script is publicKeyToP2PKH(spendKey.publicKey), i.e. single-sig P2PKH over one cosigner's spend pubkey.
7. Core side: /tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor/conf/mainnet.py:23-24 defines only P2PKH_VERSION_BYTE and MULTISIG_VERSION_BYTE; hathor/transaction/shielded_tx_output.py contains no address concept and hathor/crypto/shielded/ has zero multisig references — consensus is custody-agnostic, so the gating must be wallet-side, and it is absent.
8. Exhaustive search: grep across src/ for any file combining shielded+multisig logic found only incidental co-occurrences (legacy output-type selection in sendTransaction.ts:1114-1123, type imports). No throw/guard exists for legacy:false on multisig wallets, and no test asserts such gating (__tests__/utils/wallet.test.ts shielded tests are migration-only).

Net effect as claimed: a multisig wallet exposes single-sig shielded receive addresses (getCurrentAddress/getAddressAtIndex with legacy:false, and convertHtrChangeIfRequested at src/new/sendTransaction.ts:1090 would even auto-route HTR change there), funds at those addresses are controlled by one cosigner's seed alone, and other cosigners (different seeds → different scan/spend chains) cannot see or co-sign them. Severity high is appropriate: it is a real custody-policy bypass with potential cosigner-visible fund loss, but exploitation/footgun requires the multisig wallet to actually use the shielded ({legacy:false}) flow, so it stops short of critical.

CONFIRMED on all material points. Core legitimately omits both keys: /tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor/transaction/base_transaction.py:101-102 emits ephemeral_pubkey only when truthy and :108-112 emits decoded only when parse_address_script succeeds; hathorlib/hathorlib/transaction/shielded_tx_output.py:158-166 maps 33 zero bytes to b'' (absent); hathor/verification/transaction_verifier.py:787-798 validates eph pubkey only when present, so keyless outputs are consensus-valid; no verifier rule requires the shielded script to parse. All wallet-facing endpoints use this serializer (address_history.py:262, websocket/factory.py:215, websocket/streamer.py:300 via to_json_extended at base_transaction.py:888-896; resources/transaction.py:101). Wallet-lib schemas make the keys required exactly as cited: src/schemas.ts:106,116 (ephemeral_pubkey in both outputs[] union shielded branches — a keyless entry matches no branch since TransparentOutputSchema at :90-98 requires value/token, so the whole IHistoryTxSchema fails), :191-192 (IHistoryShieldedOutputSchema requires ephemeral_pubkey AND decoded), src/api/schemas/txApi.ts:113-114 (both required; wired into transactionApiSchema at :150 and applied in getTransaction transformResponse, src/api/txApi.ts:86). Consequences verified: src/new/wallet.ts:1736-1740 safeParse failure silently drops the entire tx including transparent outputs paying the wallet (normalizeShieldedOutputs with its ?? '' fallbacks runs only after parse at :1745); src/api/wallet.ts getAddressHistory/getAddressHistoryForAwait apply addressHistorySchema in axios transformResponse so one such tx poisons polling sync; src/sync/stream.ts:754 IHistoryTxSchema.parse throws; src/utils/transaction.ts:1104-1117 input-resolution fetch rejects. Also confirmed src/shielded/processing.ts:122 Buffer.from(ephemeral_pubkey,'hex') sits outside the per-output try starting at :133 (partially mitigated by the decoded.address guard at :106-107, which skips script-unparseable outputs before reaching :122). No defaulting, optional handling, or tests for these shapes exist anywhere in the worktree (grepped all ephemeral_pubkey sites; src/utils/storage.ts:1002 fallback is post-parse). One minor overstatement: missing `decoded` alone does NOT break the WS/polling/stream paths (the outputs[] union shielded branches at schemas.ts:101-120 don't require decoded) — it only breaks the /transaction txApi shape; missing ephemeral_pubkey breaks all paths. Severity high is appropriate: attacker-triggerable persistent sync failure and missed-funds display, but recoverable with a schema fix and no fund/key compromise.

CONFIRMED. (1) Only shielded-change mechanism is convertHtrChangeIfRequested, hard-filtered to HTR: src/new/sendTransaction.ts:1076-1080 (`withToken.token === HTR_UID && withToken.isChange === true`), no-ops when shieldedOutputDefs empty (:1069); sole call site :401 operates on the HTR fee leg only; option documented as "HTR fee-change" (src/new/types.ts:379-384). (2) Custom-token change is always a transparent IDataOutput from _prepareSendTokensData (src/new/sendTransaction.ts:1164-1178) with no conversion hook. (3) tokens[] guard adds any token with a transparent output (:593-598); comment at :588-592 itself acknowledges transparent change pulls an otherwise-FullShielded token into tokens[]. (4) Default selector deliberately mixes shielded UTXOs into any send (src/utils/utxo.ts:102-113) and never uses the existing `shielded` filter (src/types.ts:606-610), then auto-builds the full-unshield path — so the leak is the silent default. Source-of-truth check: hathor-core fully supports shielded outputs for custom tokens (hathor/transaction/shielded_tx_output.py:77-105) and hathor/verification has no change-visibility rule, so shielded custom-token change is feasible and entirely wallet responsibility — nothing in core mitigates or forbids this. Minor imprecisions only: "remaining balance" is the residual of the selected inputs (not necessarily the wallet's whole balance), and checklist.md:125 says nodes enforce only the three tx-layer invariants rather than literally "privacy hygiene is client-side" — substance unchanged. High severity stands: default flow silently undoes the privacy users paid 2 HTR/output for, avoidable only by hand-picking inputs and appending a manual shielded self-output.

Every cited claim verified against the code; no refuting gate exists anywhere in the worktree.

Verified evidence:
1. /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/utils/wallet.ts:686-692 — multisig branch only switches the legacy account path (P2SH_ACCT_PATH vs P2PKH_ACCT_PATH); lines 710-715 then derive scan (account 1') and spend (account 2') xprivs unconditionally from the local participant's rootXpriv, and lines 726-729 store them for ALL wallet types including WalletType.MULTISIG. The legacy migration helper migrateShieldedAccessData (wallet.ts:758-813) is equally ungated — any pre-shielded multisig wallet with stored words gets shielded keys written on next wallet.start (src/new/wallet.ts:2117-2121).
2. /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/utils/storage.ts:117-131 — loadAddresses derives, saves, AND subscribes the shielded-spend P2PKH chain for every wallet whose access data has scanXpubkey/spendXpubkey, with zero walletType check; so a multisig wallet automatically tracks single-sig shielded addresses without any explicit API call.
3. /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/new/wallet.ts:837-850 — getAddressAtIndex(i, {legacy:false}) derives and returns shielded addresses with no multisig gating; getCurrentAddress (897-911) and getNextAddress/getAllAddresses delegate to storage with the same opts, also ungated. Grep for any "shielded not supported for multisig"-style guard across src/ found nothing.
4. /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/utils/shieldedAddress.ts:91-92 — on-chain spend script is publicKeyToP2PKH(spendKey.publicKey), i.e. single-key custody; /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/utils/transaction.ts:413-419 signs 'shielded-spend' inputs with the single spend-chain key (getSpendXPrivKey + deriveNonCompliantChild).
5. Core side confirmed: /tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor/conf/mainnet.py:23-24 defines only P2PKH_VERSION_BYTE/MULTISIG_VERSION_BYTE; grep for multisig/p2sh across hathor/crypto/shielded/ and hathor/transaction/shielded_tx_output.py and headers returns nothing — core offers no shielded multisig concept, so the custody policy is entirely wallet-side and is currently unenforced.

Aggravating detail the reviewer missed: /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/new/sendTransaction.ts:1095-1109 — when shielded change conversion runs, change is sent to the wallet's own shielded address (P2PKH over the single spend pubkey). Since nothing gates shielded sends on walletType (getOutputTypeFromWallet at :1114-1123 happily handles MULTISIG), a multisig wallet performing a shielded send silently moves its change from m-of-n P2SH custody to 1-of-1 custody of the local participant's seed.

Tests: __tests__ contain multisig tests and shielded tests but no test covering the interaction; no gating behavior is asserted anywhere.

Severity assessment: high is correct. Concrete scenario is realistic — any consumer app that enables shielded receive/send UI on a multisig wallet (the lib gives no signal not to) silently downgrades custody: funds at the shielded address are spendable by one cosigner alone, and the other cosigners (different seeds, hence different scan/spend chains) can neither see nor co-sign them. Not critical because no third-party theft occurs and funds remain spendable by a legitimate participant, but it silently defeats the entire purpose of an m-of-n wallet and hides funds from auditing cosigners.

CONFIRMED. Every load-bearing claim checks out against the code:

1. HTR-only shielded change: /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/new/sendTransaction.ts:1076-1080 — `convertHtrChangeIfRequested` filters strictly on `withToken.token === HTR_UID && withToken.isChange === true`, and line 1069 no-ops when `shieldedOutputDefs.length === 0`. Its single call site (sendTransaction.ts:401-407) runs only on `partialHtrTxData` (the HTR fee pass at :384-392). The option doc itself admits the scope: src/new/types.ts:379 ("If set, the HTR fee-change output ..."), src/new/sendTransaction.ts:157.

2. Custom-token change is always transparent: `_prepareSendTokensData` emits a plain `IDataOutput` change (sendTransaction.ts:1169-1178, `isChange: true`), and `prepareSendManyTokensData` (sendTransaction.ts:1233-1253) just loops `prepareSendTokensData` per token with no shielded hook. Exhaustive grep for `changeShieldedMode`/conversion helpers across src/ finds nothing else (only wallet.ts:1969/1977/2026, types.ts:391, sendTransaction.ts); the template interpreter (src/template/transaction/) has zero shielded support either. No alternative mechanism exists.

3. Token-UID exposure for FullShielded holdings: sendTransaction.ts:593-599 — any transparent output (including the change added by prepareSendManyTokensData, per the comment at :588-592) adds its token to `tokensWithVisibleOutput`, which gates `tokens[]` at :613. The privacy-guard comment at :580-586 explicitly states that listing the token in `tokens[]` "would defeat that privacy guarantee" — the transparent custom-token change forces exactly that.

4. Default leak, not informed choice: `bestUtxoSelection` (src/utils/utxo.ts:102-113) deliberately mixes shielded UTXOs in (comment at :102-107) and does not set the existing `shielded` filter (src/types.ts:606-610; honored in src/storage/memory_store.ts:825-826). Shielded custom-token UTXOs are stored with the recovered token UID (src/shielded/processing.ts:141-198), so the selector spends them transparently for any custom-token send.

5. Source-of-truth check: nothing in hathor-core verification constrains change visibility — change is a pure wallet-side construct, and the client guide checklist (SHIELDED_INTEGRATION_REVIEW/reference/client-guide-checklist.md, "Full nodes must enforce the three transaction-layer invariants — not automatic from crypto verification" and §8/§9) puts everything beyond those invariants on the client. Core cannot save the wallet here.

Minor wording nit (does not change verdict): the leak reveals the remaining amount of the *selected inputs*, not necessarily the wallet's whole token balance — though with best-UTXO selection (smallest UTXO larger than the amount) the change frequently approximates the bulk of a holding.

Severity HIGH is appropriate: for a confidential-transactions feature, a partial spend of a FullShielded custom-token holding silently publishes both the token UID and the change amount on-chain by default, undoing privacy the user paid 2 HTR/output for, with no opt-in available (unlike HTR, which at least has `changeShieldedMode`). The only mitigations are manual input picking plus an explicit shielded self-output. Not critical because no funds/keys are at risk and consensus is unaffected — it is a privacy-defeating default, exactly the wallet-side responsibility the integration was meant to cover.

CONFIRMED on every link of the chain.

Wallet-lib side (schemas require the key):
- /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/schemas.ts:106 (AmountShieldedOutputSchema), :116 (FullShieldedOutputSchema), :191 (IHistoryShieldedOutputSchema) all declare `ephemeral_pubkey: z.string()` with no `.optional()`. Same in /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/api/schemas/txApi.ts:113 — notably that schema deliberately relaxed `mode` and `token_data` to optional for node-shape tolerance but left `ephemeral_pubkey` required.
- A node-shaped shielded entry without the key fails all three branches of the IHistoryOutputSchema union (schemas.ts:122-126): TransparentOutputSchema requires `value`/`token`/`spent_by` (schemas.ts:91-99), and both shielded branches require `ephemeral_pubkey`. So the whole tx fails IHistoryTxSchema (outputs: IHistoryOutputSchema.array() at schemas.ts:207; shielded_outputs at :214).
- No pre-parse normalization exists: `normalizeShieldedOutputs` runs AFTER parse in both paths (src/new/wallet.ts:1745, src/storage/storage.ts:405). utils/storage.ts:1002 (`so?.ephemeral_pubkey ?? ''`) shows downstream code already tolerates absence, but the zod gate fails first.
- Parse sites confirmed: src/sync/stream.ts:754 `IHistoryTxSchema.parse(wsData.data)` inside the event-emitter listener built by buildListener (stream.ts:733) — no try/catch in the listener; the surrounding promise (stream.ts:798-826) only resolves on stream end/abort, so a thrown ZodError escapes to the emitter and the sync promise never resolves. src/new/wallet.ts:1736-1740 uses safeParse → logger.error → return, silently dropping the entire tx including any transparent outputs paying the wallet.

Source-of-truth side (core genuinely omits the key and accepts its absence):
- /tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor/transaction/base_transaction.py:100-101: `if output.ephemeral_pubkey: data['ephemeral_pubkey'] = ...` in `_shielded_output_to_json` — key omitted when empty.
- That serializer feeds exactly the wallet-facing payloads: to_json (base_transaction.py:816-820), and to_json_extended (base_transaction.py:888-897) which appends shielded entries into `ret['outputs']`; to_json_extended is what the wallet WS sends (hathor/websocket/factory.py:215) and what the history stream sends (hathor/websocket/streamer.py:300 `data=item.vertex.to_json_extended()`).
- Wire format: /tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathorlib/hathorlib/transaction/shielded_tx_output.py:103-104 (serializes b'' as 33 zero bytes), :158-166 and :197-205 (33 zero bytes decoded back to b'' = "not present"); field defaults to b'' (:44, :59).
- Consensus: /tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor/verification/transaction_verifier.py:787-798 validates the pubkey only `if output.ephemeral_pubkey` — absence passes verification; grep confirms no other verifier touches ephemeral_pubkey. So any third party can broadcast a consensus-valid shielded tx with zeroed ephemeral pubkeys (range proofs don't require it; it exists only for recipient recovery), making this remotely triggerable against any wallet whose subscribed address appears in the tx.

Severity: high stands. Remote-triggerable sync hang (stream path) plus silent drop of fund-bearing txs (real-time path) — availability/correctness, not fund theft, so not critical. Recommendation (make the field `.optional()` in all four schemas, treat absence as not-recoverable in processing.ts) is consistent with core semantics and with hathor/transaction/shielded_tx_output.py:70-71, which raises 'no ephemeral_pubkey for ECDH recovery' for the same case in Python.

CONFIRMED. All four schemas require ephemeral_pubkey: src/schemas.ts:106 (AmountShieldedOutputSchema), :116 (FullShieldedOutputSchema), :191 (IHistoryShieldedOutputSchema), src/api/schemas/txApi.ts:113 — verified by exhaustive grep; no .optional()/default anywhere (the tolerant `so?.ephemeral_pubkey ?? ''` at src/utils/storage.ts:1002 sits downstream of the schema gate and never runs). Core side fully verified: hathor/transaction/base_transaction.py:101-102 omits the JSON key when empty; to_json_extended (base_transaction.py:888-897) embeds shielded outputs in outputs[] with that serializer and is exactly what feeds the wallet via WS address_history (hathor/websocket/factory.py:215) and the history streamer (hathor/websocket/streamer.py:300); hathorlib/hathorlib/transaction/shielded_tx_output.py:104,158-166 treats 33 zero bytes on wire as absent (decoded to b''); hathor/verification/transaction_verifier.py:787-797 is the ONLY verifier touchpoint and validates pubkey only `if output.ephemeral_pubkey` — absence is consensus-valid (grep over all hathor/verification/ found no other requirement). CONFIRMED. Wallet-lib requires ephemeral_pubkey in all four schemas: src/schemas.ts:106 (AmountShieldedOutputSchema), :116 (FullShieldedOutputSchema), :191 (IHistoryShieldedOutputSchema), src/api/schemas/txApi.ts:113 — all `z.string()`, no `.optional()` (notably, sibling fields `mode`/`token_data`/`spent_by` in the same schemas WERE made optional with node-compat comments, so this field was simply missed). The omission reaches both wallet paths: streamer.py:300 uses to_json_extended(), which appends shielded outputs into outputs[] via _shielded_output_to_json (base_transaction.py:888-896). A keyless shielded entry fails all three IHistoryOutputSchema union branches (TransparentOutputSchema at schemas.ts:90-99 requires `value`/`token`, absent from shielded JSON), so the whole tx fails IHistoryTxSchema.
