Confirmed both legs. (1) /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/utils/transaction.ts:1111-1113 rejects any input.index >= response.tx.outputs.length on the API fallback of convertTransactionToHistoryTx. Core's /transaction endpoint serializes with tx.to_json() (/tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor/transaction/resources/transaction.py:62), which emits shielded outputs as a separate 'shielded_outputs' key (base_transaction.py:815-820), not in 'outputs'; combined index space per base_transaction.py:347-363 means shielded-spending inputs always have index >= len(outputs), so they are always rejected on this path. Wallet schema parses shielded_outputs separately (src/api/schemas/txApi.ts:150); no transform merges them (src/api/txApi.ts:34-48). (2) convertFullNodeTxToHistoryTx (transaction.ts:1580-1625) never copies tx.shielded_outputs — histTx literal at 1598-1614 lacks the key and normalizeShieldedOutputs is not applied (direct resolve at 1114). findSpentOutput (transaction.ts:139-157) only inspects parentTx.outputs. Minor nuance vs the claim: before the 'references a shielded slot' throw (1198-1202) there is a storage.getUtxo fallback at 1196 that could theoretically recover, but on this path the parent tx is absent from storage so the UTXO record is unlikely to exist; and the bounds check rejects first anyway. Same shielded_outputs drop affects src/template/transaction/interpreter.ts:281. Impact: the sender-local insert at src/new/sendTransaction.ts:839-850 is a fire-and-forget IIFE with no catch, so the rejection is unhandled and the wallet fails to record its own just-sent tx (WS delivery may compensate later). Edge path (parent usually in storage), so medium severity is appropriate.

CONFIRMED. Core claim verified at every cited location: (1) /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/models/address.ts:99-106 — length check is `!== 25 && !== 71` with no version correlation; :122-128 — version byte check accepts any of p2pkh/p2sh/shielded via Network.isVersionByteValid (/tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/models/network.ts:169-176), so a checksum-valid 25-byte address with the shielded byte (0x3c mainnet / 0x5d testnet, network.ts:17,24) or a 71-byte address with the p2pkh/p2sh byte passes validateAddress. (2) getType (address.ts:148-158) keys purely on first byte after validateAddress, so cross combinations classify as the wrong type. (3) getScanPubkey (address.ts:185-191) does `subarray(1, 34)` — Buffer.subarray clamps, so on a 25-byte address it silently returns 24 bytes, as claimed. (4) Exhaustive grep found NO other layer binding length to version byte: shieldedAddress.ts only validates on the ENCODE side (utils/shieldedAddress.ts:40-51); callers (sendTransaction.ts:1092-1107, new/wallet.ts:1984-1995, utils/address.ts:129-151, utils/transaction.ts:827, storage/storage.ts:333-339) all rely on isShielded()/getType(). (5) Source-of-truth confirmed: hathor-core defines only P2PKH_VERSION_BYTE/MULTISIG_VERSION_BYTE (/tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor/conf/mainnet.py:23-24); the shielded address format never appears on-chain, so wallet-lib is the sole enforcement point.

Two mechanism details in the finding are inaccurate but do not change the verdict: (a) For the 71-byte/p2pkh-byte case, P2PKH.createScript (/tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/models/p2pkh.ts:69-70) uses `addressBytes.slice(1, -4)` = 66 bytes, not bytes [1,21); the resulting script pushes a 66-byte blob (push-length 0x42), producing a malformed/unspendable script (fails P2PKH.identify length 25/31, p2pkh.ts:112; can never satisfy OP_EQUALVERIFY against HASH160's 20 bytes) — funds are burned rather than "paying an arbitrary hash". Impact is comparable (fund loss after passing validation). (b) For the 25-byte/shielded-byte case, real call sites (sendTransaction.ts:1095, wallet.ts:1989) call getSpendAddress() before getScanPubkey(); getSpendPubkey returns an empty buffer (subarray(34,67) on 25 bytes) and bitcorePublicKey throws there — so failure typically surfaces as a confusing bitcore error before reaching ct-crypto's 33-byte check, not "only inside ct-crypto". Either way validation should have rejected the address up front. Severity low is correct: requires a corrupted/buggy encoder or crafted address (checksum trivially computable), attacker controls destination anyway, and outcomes are late confusing errors or unspendable outputs rather than redirection. Recommendation in the finding (bind version⇔length, validate 0x02/0x03 pubkey prefixes on decode) is sound.

CONFIRMED. /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/utils/storage.ts:1005-1006 writes vbf/abf as raw hex onto the IShieldedOutputEntry persisted via store.saveTx (line 1011); same plaintext fields on UTXO records at storage.ts:713 and storage.ts:1144. Type declarations match the citation exactly (src/types.ts:352-353 history output, :473-474 IUtxo, both plain string hex). Contrast verified: signing material is PIN-encrypted IEncryptedData (types.ts:494, :503, :505 mainKey/scanMainKey/spendMainKey). Exhaustive grep found NO encryptData/IEncryptedData usage anywhere in blinding-factor write/read paths — consumers read raw hex (src/new/sendTransaction.ts:480, src/utils/transaction.ts:1366), so no hidden decrypt-on-read exists. getShieldedUnblindingForTx (src/new/wallet.ts:1119) takes only txId, no PIN gate, and returns unblinding payloads from the plaintext fields (wallet.ts:1154,1172-1173). Source of truth agrees: client-guide-checklist.md sections 2.1/2.2 say value/asset blinding factors are "kept secret (needed for spending)"; hathor-core never persists them. Mitigation is cheap: vbf/abf are only consumed at spend time (PIN already present) and in user-initiated export. One caveat tempering the impact claim: the same record already stores decrypted value (storage.ts:987) and token (storage.ts:991) in plaintext — unavoidable for balance display — so a store-read attacker learns amounts/tokens regardless; the blinding factors' incremental harm is producing publicly verifiable unblinding proofs (explorer payload) and commitment recomputation, not new knowledge, and they confer no spend authority. "Defeats the entire confidentiality guarantee" is mildly overstated, but the at-rest asymmetry vs PIN-encrypted keys is real. Medium severity is correct.

Confirmed on all points. (1) Wallet-lib encodes shielded addresses as Base58(version(1)||scan_pubkey(33)||spend_pubkey(33)||checksum(4)): /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/utils/shieldedAddress.ts:28 (doc) and :53-57 (implementation); the 71-byte decode side lives in src/models/address.ts:83-84,149-150, same convention. (2) Version bytes 0x3c/0x5d are defined only in /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/models/network.ts:17,24,31 — grep of hathor-core and hathor-ct-crypto finds them nowhere (only unrelated OP_13=0x5d opcode at hathor/transaction/scripts/opcode.py:71). (3) Core defines no shielded address format: /tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor/conf/mainnet.py:23-24 has only P2PKH_VERSION_BYTE=0x28 and MULTISIG_VERSION_BYTE=0x64; grep for scan_pubkey/spend_pubkey/shielded_address across all core .py files returns zero hits; hathor/crypto/shielded/ contains only crypto primitives; the official SHIELDED-OUTPUTS-CLIENT-GUIDE.md (both the core-repo copy and the fetched checklist) never mentions an address format. Consensus only sees script + ephemeral_pubkey (shielded_tx_output.py:70-73). (4) RFC sketches a conflicting format scan_pubkey(33)||hash(spend_pubkey)(20): SHIELDED_INTEGRATION_REVIEW/reference/rfc-summary.md:11. So the wallet-lib format is indeed a repo-local convention diverging from the RFC sketch with no upstream ratification — a real interop/standardization gap, but not a current bug. Severity info is appropriate (nothing breaks today; wallet-lib is the only client); recommendation to ratify the format + version-byte registry upstream stands.

CONFIRMED: no value-range pre-validation exists anywhere in wallet-lib. The upfront validation block at /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/shielded/creation.ts:270-309 checks scanPubkey length (274), token UID length (283), and inputGenerators token UIDs (299-308), but never proposal.value. The shielded-def collection at /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/new/sendTransaction.ts:240-257 passes output.value straight into the phantom output with no check. Exhaustive grep of src/ for 40-bit/positivity checks found only: MAX_SHIELDED_OUTPUTS, MAX_RANGE_PROOF_SIZE=3328 (constants.ts:254), and proof-size/script-size checks in models/shielded_output.ts:175,187 — no value-range check in zod schemas, utils, or models. The installed @hathor/ct-crypto-node provider.js (line 47-48) and napi_bindings.rs (bigint_to_u64, lines 12-19, only rejects negative/>u64) add nothing either.

SOURCE OF TRUTH CONFIRMED: /tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor-ct-crypto/src/rangeproof.rs:15 (RANGE_PROOF_BITS=40), :44 (min_value=1). Tests only exercise up to u64::MAX>>24 = 2^40-1 (rangeproof.rs:307,328). Core verifier (hathor/verification/transaction_verifier.py:800-827) only checks proof validity + min_value>=1 (rangeproof.rs:95-101); it never checks the proven range's upper bound or exact proof size.

MECHANISM CORRECTION (makes it worse, not refuted): the finding claims values >= 2^40 'fail deep inside native crypto'. Only value=0 does (C sign rejects min_value > value, rangeproof_impl.h:215; test_zero_amount_rejected at rangeproof.rs:167-181). For values above 2^40, libsecp256k1-zkp treats min_bits as a FLOOR, not a cap: secp256k1-zkp-sys-0.10.1/depend/secp256k1/src/modules/rangeproof/rangeproof_impl.h:162-165 sets mantissa = max(bits_needed(value-1), min_bits=40). Consequences: (a) values in (2^40, ~2^41] silently produce a 41-bit proof (~3309 B, under the 3328 cap on both wallet shielded_output.ts:175 and node side) that the node ACCEPTS — breaking the constant-proof-size privacy invariant (rangeproof.rs:9-14, test_proof_size_is_constant) and leaking that the value is huge; (b) larger values produce ~3373+ B proofs that fail wallet-lib's own serialization cap with a cryptic 'range proof size exceeds maximum 3328' error after all expensive proofs. (Note: value exactly 2^40 still fits 40 bits due to the min_value=1 offset — proven range is [1, 2^40] inclusive.)

VERDICT: finding is real; the recommendation (upfront `value < 1n || value >= 1n << 40n` check in createShieldedOutputs and at sendManyOutputsSendTransaction def acceptance) is correct and slightly conservative (rejects exactly 2^40, which is safe). Severity should be raised from low to medium: this is not merely a misleading error — a window of out-of-range values yields node-accepted transactions with a privacy-degrading variable-size range proof, and nothing on the node side rejects them.

CONFIRMED. (1) /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/utils/transaction.ts:193-195 reads exactly `mode: output.asset_commitment ? ShieldedOutputMode.FULLY_SHIELDED : ShieldedOutputMode.AMOUNT_SHIELDED` inside normalizeShieldedOutputs case (a) (nested-in-outputs[] shape), discarding the node-sent `mode` int. The wire-entry type IShieldedOutputEntry (src/types.ts:339-361) does not even declare `mode`, though the zod schemas DO accept it (src/api/schemas/txApi.ts:104 and src/schemas.ts:182, both `mode: z.number().optional()`), so the value is available but dropped. (2) Source of truth confirmed: /tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor/transaction/base_transaction.py `_shielded_output_to_json` sends `'mode': output.mode().value` and its docstring says verbatim that mode "is the canonical mode discriminator on the wire; downstream consumers should rely on it instead of inferring mode from the presence of asset_commitment/surjection_proof". (3) Equivalence-today confirmed: core's OutputMode enum (hathorlib/hathorlib/transaction/shielded_tx_output.py:30-34) has only AMOUNT_ONLY=1 / FULLY_SHIELDED=2, and `asset_commitment` is emitted iff FullShieldedOutput; wallet enum values match (node_modules/@hathor/ct-crypto-provider/src/types.ts:11-14: AMOUNT_SHIELDED=1, FULLY_SHIELDED=2). So no current bug — info severity is correct. (4) Nuances: the downstream consumer src/shielded/processing.ts:130-131 ALREADY implements the recommended pattern (prefer explicit mode, fall back to asset_commitment only when mode===undefined), but the case (a) normalizer overwrites mode before processing sees it; case (b) (separate shielded_outputs[]) preserves the node's mode untouched (transaction.ts:228+ only hex-converts fields). A hypothetical mode 3 would still be misrouted in BOTH shapes: case (a) re-infers it to 1/2; case (b) preserves 3 but processing.ts:130 evaluates isFullShielded=false and feeds it to rewindAmountShieldedOutput — so "silently misclassified as AmountShielded" holds. (5) ensureHex confirmed at transaction.ts:260-266 — pure character-set heuristic ([0-9a-fA-F]+ → hex, else base64); core encodings per _shielded_output_to_json are commitment/ephemeral_pubkey/asset_commitment=hex and range_proof/script/surjection_proof=base64, so misdetection requires a base64 payload composed entirely of hex chars ((22/64)^len, negligible for these sizes). Finding stands as written; recommendation (`output.mode ?? inference` in the case (a) extraction, plus explicit unknown-mode rejection in processing.ts) is sound forward-compat hygiene.

CONFIRMED as a code-level divergence, but impact is latent (unreachable today), so severity should drop from medium to low.

Verified claims:
1. Wallet domain construction: /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/new/sendTransaction.ts:459-467 pushes one InputGeneratorInfo for EVERY input with `inp.token` — no authority skip — and src/shielded/creation.ts:129-145 (buildSurjectionDomain) consumes the list verbatim, in order. Confirmed.
2. Node verification domain: /tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor/verification/transaction_verifier.py:868-869 `if spent_output.is_token_authority(): continue` (authority inputs excluded) and :894-897 (MintHeader entry tags appended). Confirmed exactly as claimed.
3. MintHeader timing: src/utils/transaction.ts:1070-1071 attaches MintHeader inside _attachShieldedHeaders, called from createTransactionFromData — after createShieldedOutputs ran at sendTransaction.ts:517. Proof can never include mint-entry generators. Confirmed.
4. Proof sensitivity: hathor-ct-crypto/src/surjection.rs:40-51 verifies over the exact generator list; secp256k1-zkp surjection verification fails on domain length/order mismatch. Mechanism confirmed.

Why severity drops — both failure scenarios are unreachable via the wallet:
- sendTransaction.ts:517 is the ONLY caller of createShieldedOutputs (grep of whole worktree). Token mint/melt flows (src/utils/tokens.ts) and transaction templates contain zero shielded-output support.
- Authority inputs are categorically blocked on this path: user-supplied inputs go through checkUnspentInput (called at sendTransaction.ts:1191), which rejects any authority output at sendTransaction.ts:1287-1295 ("XXX: We are NOT enabling authority outputs for now"); automatic selection filters authorities: 0n (src/utils/utxo.ts:58 and :110).
- A MintHeader on a regular shielded tx requires a mint-authority input (transaction.ts:1040-1044 populates mintAuthorityTokens only from inp.authorities), which cannot be present per above. The createToken branch (unconditional mint declaration, transaction.ts:1002/1056) only coexists with excessBlindingFactor (full unshield: shielded inputs, NO shielded outputs — sendTransaction.ts:543 gates on shieldedOutputs.length === 0), and surjection proofs exist only for FullShielded OUTPUTS, so no proof/domain exists to mismatch there.
- For the reachable case (no authority, no mint), wallet domain order (allInputs = [...partialInputs, ...partialHtrTxData.inputs], sendTransaction.ts:440) matches final tx input order (fullTxData.inputs, sendTransaction.ts:609), and shielded-input generators match the node (blinded asset_commitment for FullShielded via assetBlindingFactor at :464-466 vs transaction_verifier.py:879-880; derived tag for transparent/AmountShielded vs :871/:890). No current-behavior bug.

Conclusion: the finding's code observation and source-of-truth divergence are accurate, and the recommendation (mirror node domain: skip authority inputs, append mint-entry tags) is correct future-proofing — _attachShieldedHeaders's mint-detection machinery shows the codebase anticipates mint+shielded coexistence. But no transaction constructible through the wallet's current public flows can hit the rejection, so this is a latent divergence/hardening issue, not a user-facing failure: low, not medium.

CONFIRMED. (1) Safety net verified verbatim at src/new/wallet.ts:1851-1866: every onNewTx (all websocket events route via enqueueOnNewTx, wallet.ts:935) scans the FULL txHistory() and runs storage.processHistory(pin) if ANY stored tx has shielded_outputs but no decoded shielded entry in outputs[]. No marker/debounce/retry-guard exists anywhere in src/ (grep for shieldedProcessedAt/decodeAttempted/retry returns nothing). (2) Permanently-true condition verified: processing.ts:105-111 skips outputs whose decoded.address is not in the wallet (getAddressInfo gate), and utils/storage.ts:963-1011 is the only code path that appends decoded entries — so txs whose shielded outputs all belong to third parties can NEVER satisfy hasDecoded. Core serializer (hathor-core base_transaction.py:82-114) confirms shielded outputs carry decoded.address, so the ownership gate is the sole filter. (3) Scenarios real: core mandates >=2 shielded outputs (transaction_verifier.py verify_trivial_commitment_protection), and wallet-lib sendTransaction.ts:505-509 only enforces count — shielded change is opt-in (changeShieldedMode, sendTransaction.ts:157/394-403), so a send to external shielded recipients with transparent change yields a permanently-stuck tx; an incoming transparent payment in a tx carrying third-party shielded outputs does too, with no action by this wallet. (4) Non-convergence verified: processHistory (utils/storage.ts:468-534) = cleanMetadata + full chronological replay with nested getTx per input; replay can never decode non-owned outputs, so the full rebuild repeats forever on every event. (5) shieldedNewlyAvailable false positive verified at wallet.ts:1811-1815/1825 — metadata-only updates (first_block) of such txs also force processHistory via the update branch. One overstatement: the "KDF per shielded output per tx" cost claim is too strong — getScanXPrivKey runs only for wallet-owned addresses (processing.ts:110-114 gates before derivation) and already-decoded txs skip decryption on replay (utils/storage.ts:950); the dominant recurring cost is the O(history) metadata rebuild per websocket event, not the crypto. Severity high stands: unbounded repeated full-history reprocessing + full-metadata rewrite races on every tx event, but no direct fund loss.

CONFIRMED: no [1, 2^40) value bound anywhere on the wallet-lib shielded creation path. Verified exhaustively: (1) /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/shielded/creation.ts:270-309 upfront loop validates scanPubkey length, token UID length, and inputGenerators only — never proposal.value; the per-mode helpers (creation.ts:59-120, 153-240) pass proposal.value straight to the crypto provider. (2) sendTransaction pre-crypto checks cover only count (src/new/sendTransaction.ts:505-515). (3) sendManyOutputsSendTransaction (src/new/wallet.ts:1981-2019) maps outputs without value checks. (4) Grep over all of src/ finds no 2^40 / 1099511627776 / RANGE_PROOF_BITS constant; constants.ts has only MAX_OUTPUT_VALUE=2^63 (constants.ts:107), enforced in outputValueToBytes (src/utils/buffer.ts:287) which shielded outputs never traverse (value is committed, not serialized; the phantom transparent output at sendTransaction.ts:248-257 is removed before serialization). models/output.ts:64 positive-value check applies to transparent Output instances only. (5) Neither installed provider (@hathor/ct-crypto-provider) nor the node bindings (/Users/pedroferreira/Hathor/hathor-ct-crypto-node/src/rangeproof.rs) validate the amount. ct-crypto side confirmed: RANGE_PROOF_BITS=40 and min_value=1 (/tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor-ct-crypto/src/rangeproof.rs:15,44; node binding rangeproof.rs:16,49).

ONE MATERIAL CORRECTION that makes the finding WORSE, not refuted: the claimed failure mode ("values 0 or >= 2^40 make RangeProof::new throw, failure is loud, no invalid tx reaches the node") is only correct for value=0 (sign_impl rejects min_value > value) and value > 2^63 (proveparams returns 0 when min_value!=0 && value > INT64_MAX). For 2^40 <= value <= 2^63 creation SUCCEEDS: in libsecp256k1-zkp, min_bits is a floor, and the mantissa grows to fit the value (secp256k1-zkp-sys-0.10.1 rangeproof_impl.h, range_proveparams: `*mantissa = *v ? 64 - clz64(*v) : 1; if (*min_bits > *mantissa) *mantissa = *min_bits;` — i.e. mantissa = max(bits_needed, 40)). The wallet then builds and pushes the tx. Consequences: a 42+-bit proof (~3370 B) exceeds the MAX_RANGE_PROOF_SIZE=3328 cap (mirrored at src/models/shielded_output.ts:175 from hathorlib) and is rejected by the node AFTER push; a 41-bit proof (values in [2^40, ~2^41), ~3306 B) fits under the cap and core's verify_range_proofs (/tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor/verification/transaction_verifier.py:800-827 plus ct-crypto verify_range_proof rangeproof.rs:97) only enforces range.start >= 1, never a 40-bit max — so such a tx is likely ACCEPTED on-chain with a proof whose distinct size leaks that the value exceeds 2^40, defeating the constant-size privacy goal the 40-bit pin exists for (node binding rangeproof.rs:7-15). So the finding's mitigating claim "no invalid tx reaches the node" is false for the upper bound; the recommendation (validate 1n <= value <= (1n<<40n)-1n upfront in createShieldedOutputs) is exactly right and more important than stated. Minor evidence nit: the client guide actually says range proofs cover [1, 2^64) (client-guide-checklist.md:12,57 — RFC-era text); the 40-bit bound comes from ct-crypto/core, which wins per the rules. Severity adjusted low -> medium: beyond a bad error message, amounts >= 2^40 of a large-supply custom token produce either a node-rejected push or an on-chain proof-size privacy leak; amounts only need ~1.1e12 base units (~1.1e10 tokens at 2 decimals), attainable for custom tokens.

Confirmed all four legs of the finding by direct code reading. (1) New unconditional input-delete loop in processHistory at src/utils/storage.ts:511-533 has no tx.is_voided guard (processNewTx's voided early-return is at 906-911, but the loop runs after it for every history tx); git diff master...HEAD confirms master's processHistory had no such loop (master's delete loop existed only in processSingleTx, the live-tx path). memory_store.deleteUtxo (memory_store.ts:743-745) deletes by txId:index key unconditionally, so a voided spender in history destroys the origin UTXO on every reprocess — including after a full wipe+resync, since fullnode address history includes voided txs. (2) Local stamping origOutput.spent_by = tx.tx_id at storage.ts:1259-1272 is the ONLY writer of spent_by in src/ (grep verified); no clearing path exists. cleanMetadata (memory_store.ts:1121-1126) wipes utxos/metadata but not history, and the rebuild gate `if (output.spent_by === null)` at storage.ts:1105 permanently skips saveUtxo for stamped outputs. (3) Voided updates route to processHistory at src/new/wallet.ts:1825-1830; the shielded merge at wallet.ts:1785-1798 preserves stored decoded entries (with stale spent_by) over wire data, so origin-tx redelivery cannot fix shielded entries; for transparent outputs redelivery could overwrite the stamp via addTx (wallet.ts:1801), but the next processHistory delete loop re-deletes the UTXO anyway. (4) Balance is credited in the output loop (storage.ts:1095-1101) before the 1105 gate, and the voided spender's debit is skipped — so the wallet shows balance with no selectable UTXO. Severity: high is correct — routine trigger (double-spend voiding, reorgs, voided txs in initial sync), persistent unspendable-balance state not fixed even by resync (delete-loop leg), but no on-chain fund loss (funds spendable from another wallet implementation), so not critical. Recommended fixes in the finding (gate delete loop on !tx.is_voided; clear/relocate the local spent_by stamp; regression test receive→spend→void→assert UTXO selectable) are appropriate.

VERIFIED via static trace of both mechanisms. (1) Unconditional input-delete loop: src/utils/storage.ts:489-534 — deleteUtxo loop runs inside historyIter for EVERY tx with no is_voided guard; processNewTx's voided early-return is at 905-911 but the loop runs after it returns. historyIter (memory_store.ts:467-509) yields voided txs unfiltered. git show master:src/utils/storage.ts confirms master's processHistory had NO input deletion (the loop only existed in processSingleTx, old lines 430-466, never invoked on the voided path) — both blocks are new on this branch. Sequence: cleanMetadata wipes UTXOs → origin processNewTx re-saves UTXO → voided spender's delete loop removes it. This defeats even full wipe+resync since fullnode history includes voided txs. (2) Irreversible stamp: storage.ts:1267 `origOutput.spent_by = tx.tx_id` + saveTx at 1271 is the ONLY local spent_by assignment in src/ (grep), with no clearing path on voiding; saveUtxo gate at storage.ts:1105 then skips re-creation; cleanMetadata (memory_store.ts:1121-1126) does not wipe this.history so the stamp survives; wallet.ts:1785-1798 merges stored decoded shielded entries (with stale stamp) over wire form on redelivery, so fullnode redelivery cannot fix shielded entries. Balance inconsistency confirmed: output credit at storage.ts:1095-1101 applies regardless of spent_by while the voided spender contributes no debit → unspendable phantom balance. Trigger path confirmed: wallet.ts:1825-1830 routes voided-state changes to processHistory; no voided-reversal/resurrection code exists anywhere in src/. Caveat: static analysis only (review worktree has no node_modules); recommended regression test (receive → spend → void spender → assert UTXO selectable) was not executed, but the code chain is unambiguous. Severity high is correct — funds become unspendable in this client (even after resync, due to mechanism 1) though not permanently lost (a fixed client or different implementation can spend them).

CONFIRMED with a mechanism correction. Missing validation verified: the up-front loop in createShieldedOutputs (/tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/shielded/creation.ts:270-309) checks scanPubkey length, token-UID length and inputGenerators, but has no value-bound check; proposal.value flows unchecked into createAmountShieldedOutput (creation.ts:83-97) and createShieldedOutputWithBothBlindings (creation.ts:178-195), wrapped by the generic error at creation.ts:358-364. Exhaustive search found no value check elsewhere: src/new/sendTransaction.ts only checks output count (line 510) and change>fee (1084); src/utils/buffer.ts:285 outputValueToBytes positivity never applies because shielded phantom outputs are removed before serialization and ShieldedOutput carries no value field. Ground truth confirmed: hathor-ct-crypto/src/rangeproof.rs:15 RANGE_PROOF_BITS=40, line 44 min_value=1. HOWEVER, the claimed mechanism is partially wrong — empirical test of the installed @hathor/ct-crypto-node binding shows: value=0 throws natively (claim correct); value=2^40 and 2^41 SUCCEED (3213/3309-byte proofs, both under MAX_RANGE_PROOF_SIZE=3328, and core's verify_range_proof at rangeproof.rs:94-101 enforces only range.start>=1 with no upper bound, so these would likely be accepted on-chain); value=2^42..~2^62 succeed at creation but produce oversized proofs rejected by wallet-lib's own clear size check at src/models/shielded_output.ts:175 (late but not opaque); value=2^63 throws natively. So 'proof creation fails for any value >= 2^40' is false for 2^40..2^62, but the core finding — no up-front [1, 2^40) validation, opaque native error for value=0 and extreme values — stands, and the recommended check matches RANGE_PROOF_BITS. Severity low is appropriate (throws rather than misbuilds; realistic amounts far below bound).

CONFIRMED. (1) Safety net exists verbatim at /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/new/wallet.ts:1851-1866: full txHistory() scan; needsRetry=true for any tx with shielded_outputs but no decoded entry; then storage.processHistory(pinCode). No decode-attempt marker exists anywhere in src/ (grep: zero hits for shieldedDecodeAttempted/shieldedProcessedAt/etc.). (3) Scenario (a) verified: sender-local insert (src/utils/transaction.ts:1257-1287) emits wire-form shielded_outputs but no decoded entries; default change is transparent (changeShieldedMode=null, src/new/sendTransaction.ts:157). Scenario (b) is independent of wallet behavior. (4) Core source-of-truth confirms ≥2 shielded outputs mandatory: /tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor/verification/transaction_verifier.py:1219-1227 (verify_trivial_commitment_protection raises TrivialCommitmentError if <2). (5) processHistory is genuinely O(history): src/utils/storage.ts:468-527 = cleanMetadata() + full chronological replay through processNewTx + per-input getTx. Non-convergent: retry can never clear the condition, so it fires on every onNewTx (all WS events route through enqueueOnNewTx, wallet.ts:935/1721). pinCode gate satisfied in any shielded-enabled wallet (set at construction, wallet.ts:387). CONFIRMED. (1) Unguarded delete loop verified at src/utils/storage.ts:511-533 inside processHistory's historyIter loop; processNewTx's voided early-return is at 905-911 but the loop has no is_voided guard; memory_store.ts historyIter (467-492) yields voided txs; no removeTx/deleteTx prunes them. git diff master...HEAD confirms the loop and the stamping block are both new. (2) Stamping verified at storage.ts:1259-1272 (origOutput.spent_by = tx.tx_id + saveTx); line 1105 gates saveUtxo on spent_by===null; memory_store.ts:1121-1126 cleanMetadata wipes utxos/metadata but NOT history; repo-wide grep finds no code that ever clears spent_by (only hit, transaction.ts:1245, constructs a fresh entry). wallet.ts:1825-1829 routes voided changes to processHistory. (3) Source of truth confirms the divergence: hathor-core get_output_spent_by EXCLUDES voided spenders (transaction_metadata.py:142-154, line 150), so core reports spent_by=null once the spender is voided; the local stamp contradicts core semantics with no reconciliation, and initial sync (origin delivered with spent_by=null) hits the bug via mechanism (1) alone. (4) Shielded unhealable confirmed: decoded entry spent_by set once at decode (storage.ts:999); wallet.ts:1785-1798 merge preserves stored decoded entries on redelivery (alreadyOnNewTx blocks re-decode), so even fullnode redelivery can't refresh the stamp. ONE NUANCE the finding overstates: for TRANSPARENT outputs in the real-time void path, hathor-core's address index republishes the origin tx when its spent_by changes (hathor/indexes/address_index.py:55-69, has_spent_by_changed_since_last_call), and the wallet routes that non-voided update to processMetadataChanged (wallet.ts:1831-1833) which re-saves the UTXO when wire spent_by===null (storage.ts:673-700), with addTx overwriting the transparent stamp — a transient heal. But the voided spender remains in history forever, so the next processHistory (any voided change, shielded safety-net retry at wallet.ts:1836+, or wallet restart) re-deletes the UTXO via the unguarded loop with no fresh republish to heal it. End state matches the finding: balance credited (output-loop credit at storage.ts:1066-1101 precedes the spent_by gate; voided debit skipped) while the UTXO is gone until wipe+resync. No regression test covers void-then-resurrect. Severity high is correct (funds shown but unspendable, voided txs routine; recoverable by full resync so not critical).

CONFIRMED. (1) Safety net verified at /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/new/wallet.ts:1851-1866: scans entire txHistory per onNewTx event; any stored tx with shielded_outputs but no decoded entry (isShieldedOutputEntry) triggers full storage.processHistory(this.pinCode). (2) Permanent false positive verified: src/shielded/processing.ts:106-111 skips every output whose decoded.address fails storage.getAddressInfo (non-wallet recipients) — so txs whose shielded outputs all belong to third parties can NEVER satisfy hasDecoded; processHistory re-runs the same gate → non-convergent, full reprocess on every subsequent websocket event forever. (3) Trigger state is default-path reachable: hathor-core requires ≥2 shielded outputs (verify_trivial_commitment_protection in /tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor/verification/transaction_verifier.py; mirrored at sendTransaction.ts:505-508) and shielded change is opt-in (changeShieldedMode=null default, sendTransaction.ts:157), so 'send shielded to external recipient with transparent change' produces a permanently-undecodable tx in the sender's own history; sender-local insert (utils/transaction.ts:1257-1273) stores shielded_outputs with no decoded entries. (4) Cost verified: processHistory does store.cleanMetadata() then replays all history (utils/storage.ts:468-495 — full metadata wipe + O(N) processNewTx); getScanXPrivKey runs uncached PBKDF2 decryptData per call (storage.ts:971-977); pinCode is retained from constructor (wallet.ts:387) so the net runs for every shielded-enabled wallet. (5) shieldedNewlyAvailable claim verified: wallet.ts:1815 uses the same storedHasDecodedShielded test and lines 1796-1798 merge shielded_outputs from storage into bare re-deliveries, so metadata-only updates (e.g. first_block) of such a tx also route to processHistory via line 1825-1829. ONE INACCURACY (does not change severity): the 'KDF per shielded output per tx' claim is overstated — deriveScanPrivkeyForAddress/getScanXPrivKey runs only for outputs passing the ownership check (processing.ts:110-114), so the undecodable third-party outputs themselves cost no KDF/rewind; the dominant per-event cost is the unconditional cleanMetadata + full-history replay plus KDF per OWNED shielded output. Severity high is correct: no direct fund loss by itself, but a never-converging O(history) reprocess per websocket event with metadata wipe/replay races, triggered by the default send path.

CONFIRMED. (1) bestUtxoSelection deliberately omits the shielded filter: /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/utils/utxo.ts:102-113 (comment justifies it purely by consensus validity); fastUtxoSelection same at utxo.ts:56-62. Minor nit: the finding's core-docstring quote is a paraphrase, not literal text, but substance holds. Severity high is right: silent deanonymization of deliberately-shielded holdings on any ordinary transparent send, with no API to prevent or detect it; not critical since no funds at risk and only the wallet's own outputs are exposed.

CONFIRMED. (1) Default selection mixes pools: /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/utils/utxo.ts:102-113 — bestUtxoSelection's IUtxoFilterOptions omits `shielded`, with an explicit comment justifying mixing only by consensus-validity ("hathor-core accepts shielded inputs in transparent-output-only txs"); it is the default selector at src/new/sendTransaction.ts:1132 and the options built at sendTransaction.ts:375-378 pass no selector/filter. (2) The shielded filter EXISTS (src/types.ts:606-610; enforced in src/storage/memory_store.ts:825-826) but is never set anywhere in the send path — repo-wide grep for `shielded: false` at selectUtxos call sites returns zero hits. (3) Silent full-unshield: src/new/sendTransaction.ts:543-578 — `if (shieldedOutputs.length === 0 && blindedInputsArr.length > 0)` computes the excess blinding factor and attaches it (line 616) with no opt-in flag, no emitted event, no returned metadata. Change defaults to transparent (sendTransaction.ts:1169-1178); convertHtrChangeIfRequested only runs on explicit changeShieldedMode opt-in (sendTransaction.ts:401-407), which controls change shape, not input selection. (4) Exhaustive search for an opt-in confirms none exists: SendTransactionFullnodeOptions (src/new/types.ts:367-371), SendManyOutputsOptions (src/new/types.ts:386-392), UtxoOptions (src/new/types.ts:92-110), IUtxoSelectionOptions (src/types.ts:619-624) — no allowUnshield/spendShielded/transparent-only knob; grep for those names finds nothing. Core permits but never requires mixing — the hygiene decision is the wallet's, and the wallet defaults to deanonymizing. One evidence nit: the reviewer's docstring quote ("the leak is acceptable... leaving multi-input aggregation/hygiene to wallets") is a paraphrase — "hygiene"/"wallets" appear nowhere in unshield_balance_header.py; the literal text says the transparent outputs "already expose the spent amount, so nothing previously-private is additionally leaked", which presupposes a deliberate unshield and thus reinforces (not refutes) the finding that auto-building one without consent is the leak. Severity HIGH stands: the default send path silently destroys the core guarantee of the shielded feature (amount confidentiality) and irreversibly publishes it on-chain; it also violates the project's own explicit-options-over-inference convention. Confirmed. /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/utils/storage.ts:1005-1006 appends plaintext hex blindingFactor/assetBlindingFactor (and plaintext value, line 987) to tx.outputs; lines 1010-1012 persist via store.saveTx; lines 1141-1147 spread shielded/blindingFactor/assetBlindingFactor onto every shielded IUtxo in saveUtxo. Type contract is plaintext: src/types.ts:472-474 (IUtxo.blindingFactor 'hex, 32 bytes'), while scanMainKey/spendMainKey at types.ts:503-505 ARE IEncryptedData (PIN-encrypted) — the asymmetry claim holds. Exhaustive grep found no encryption anywhere in the path: MemoryStore.saveTx/saveUtxo (src/storage/memory_store.ts:543-557, 864-866) are direct Map sets; consumers read the hex back with no decryption (src/new/sendTransaction.ts:480, src/utils/transaction.ts:1366). PIN gate at storage.ts:951-956 only gates the decode step, not subsequent reads. Source of truth agrees: core/ct-crypto never store blindings; client-guide-checklist.md (lines 32, 36, 80) says blinding factors must be 'kept secret (needed for spending)' — custody is wallet policy. The recommendation is feasible: processing.ts:144-197 recovers blindings via rewind from scan key + ephemeral pubkey, so they are recomputable on demand. Counterargument that transparent history is already plaintext does not refute it: blinding factors are transferable cryptographic proof of amounts, strictly worse than readable values. Minor nit: TODO_FIX_38 file referenced in the finding no longer exists on disk.

CONFIRMED on every load-bearing point; finding survives the attack.

Mechanism verified end-to-end:
1. Default selection mixes shielded UTXOs into ordinary sends, deliberately: /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/utils/utxo.ts:102-113 — bestUtxoSelection's comment explicitly says "Select any UTXO (transparent or shielded)" and the IUtxoFilterOptions it builds (lines 108-113) omits the `shielded` field. fastUtxoSelection (lines 56-62) likewise. The opt-out filter EXISTS (src/types.ts:606-610 `shielded?: boolean`, honored by memory_store.ts:825-826) but no selection path uses it.
2. _prepareSendTokensData defaults to bestUtxoSelection (src/new/sendTransaction.ts:1132) and selected shielded UTXOs flow into blindedInputsArr via the utxo.shielded branch at sendTransaction.ts:471-484.
3. Silent full-unshield branch confirmed at sendTransaction.ts:526-578: `if (shieldedOutputs.length === 0 && blindedInputsArr.length > 0)` computes the excess blinding factor and ships it (line 616) with no flag, no error, no event. The only events SendTransaction emits are generic lifecycle ones (sendTransaction.ts:776-862: mine-tx-started, send-tx-start, send-tx-success, send-error) — nothing signals an unshield.
4. No public opt-in/opt-out anywhere: SendManyOutputsOptions is exactly {inputs, changeAddress, startMiningTx, pinCode, changeShieldedMode} (src/new/types.ts:386-392); SendTransaction's constructor takes no selection/shielded option (sendTransaction.ts:161-179); sendTransaction/sendManyOutputsTransaction (src/new/wallet.ts:1940-1947, 2039-2045) pass nothing else through. Exhaustive grep for allowUnshield/spendShielded/transparentOnly/onlyTransparent found no hits. Only workaround is manually supplying explicit `inputs`, which is not a default-safe API.
5. Shielded UTXOs are stored in the same spendable UTXO set with decoded values and `shielded: true` (src/utils/storage.ts:700-712 and 1131-1143), so with `order_by_value: 'desc'` they rank first whenever they are the largest — making the scenario (user shields funds, then any ordinary transparent payment auto-unshields them) realistic, not contrived.

Source-of-truth check: hathor-core fully permits and mechanizes this (unshield_balance_header.py:33-47; transaction_verifier.py:931-950 — full-unshield equation sum(C_in)=sum(C_out)+excess*G+fee*H makes the previously-hidden amount publicly derivable; the core docstring itself states "the transparent outputs of a full unshield already expose the spent amount" and that with one shielded input the scalar is effectively that input's r_in). So consensus-validity is not in question — this is purely a wallet privacy-hygiene decision, which is exactly the finding's point.

One inaccuracy in the finding (does not change the verdict): the quote "the leak is acceptable... leaving multi-input aggregation/hygiene to wallets" is a paraphrase — that exact text does not appear at unshield_balance_header.py:42-47 in this core worktree; the actual docstring frames the single-input blinding-factor reveal as adding nothing beyond what the transparent outputs already expose. The substantive leak (the confidential amount becomes public) is real and acknowledged by core regardless.

Severity: high is appropriate. The entire purpose of shielded outputs is amount confidentiality; the default send path silently and irreversibly publishes the protected amounts on-chain without consent, contradicting the project's own explicit-options-over-inference convention. Not critical (no fund loss, no consensus violation), but a direct defeat of the feature's security property under default usage.

CONFIRMED end-to-end; every cited line checks out and exhaustive search found no mitigation.

Selection mixes shielded UTXOs by default:
- /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/types.ts:606-610 — `shielded?: boolean`, "undefined (default) → all UTXOs (transparent + shielded)".

- src/utils/utxo.ts:56-62 and 108-113 — neither fastUtxoSelection nor bestUtxoSelection sets `shielded`; the comment at utxo.ts:102-107 ("hathor-core accepts shielded inputs in transparent-output-only txs") is only true WITH an UnshieldBalanceHeader.

Header only attached when excess is pre-computed:
- src/utils/transaction.ts:961-970 — `_attachShieldedHeaders` pushes UnshieldBalanceHeader only if `txData.excessBlindingFactor` is already set.
- The excess computation exists in exactly two places: transactionUtils.prepareTransaction (src/utils/transaction.ts:1347-1421) and SendTransaction.prepareTxData (src/new/sendTransaction.ts:~530-578). Grep for `excessBlindingFactor` in src/nano_contracts/ and src/template/ returns nothing; in fact the string "shielded" does not appear anywhere in either directory.

Bypassing paths confirmed:
- Nano: src/nano_contracts/builder.ts:370-377 (getUtxosForAmount with only {token, filter_address}) and :760; buildTransaction at :819/:868 calls createTransactionFromData directly. wallet.getUtxosForAmount → getAvailableUtxos → storage.selectUtxos with no shielded filter (src/new/wallet.ts:1460-1481, 1428-1430). Then src/nano_contracts/utils.ts:70-84 (prepareNanoSendTransaction) signs and wraps in SendTransaction({transaction}), and src/new/wallet.ts:3498-3508 runs `sendTransaction.runFromMining()` — prepareTxData/prepareTransaction never execute, so no excess is ever computed.
- Template: src/template/transaction/interpreter.ts:220-224 (build + signTransaction directly), :250-253 (getUtxosForAmount) and :262 (storage.selectUtxos, no filter).

Core source of truth confirms rejection:
- /tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor/transaction/transaction.py:182-189 — has_shielded_inputs() is True iff any input.index ≥ len(spent_tx.outputs); purely structural, exactly what the wallet produces for shielded UTXOs.
- /tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor/verification/transaction_verifier.py:1038-1042 — invariant 2: "a full-unshield tx (shielded inputs, no shielded outputs) must carry an unshield balance header" → ShieldedBalanceMismatchError. So any nano deposit or template tx that picked a shielded UTXO is rejected post-PoW.

No countervailing evidence: zero occurrences of `shielded: false` anywhere in src/; no nano/template shielded integration test exists (__tests__/integration/shielded_outputs/ has no nano or template coverage). Signing itself succeeds for shielded-spend P2PKH inputs (same signTransaction path used by the working full-unshield flow), so the failure really lands at node validation after wasted PoW, as claimed.

Severity high is correct: deterministic node rejection of nano-contract deposits and template transactions for any wallet whose shielded UTXOs are among the largest for the requested token (bestUtxoSelection/getUtxosForAmount order desc), but no fund loss or consensus impact.

CONFIRMED on every element. (1) Send path supports timelocked shielded outputs: /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/new/wallet.ts:1997 forwards `...(o.timelock != null ? { timelock: o.timelock } : {})` in the shielded branch of sendManyOutputsSendTransaction, and src/shielded/creation.ts:331 embeds `timelock: proposal.timelock ?? null` into the on-chain script via createOutputScript; ShieldedOutputProposal has `timelock?: number` (src/shielded/types.ts:97). (2) Core never sends timelock for shielded outputs: /tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor/transaction/base_transaction.py:108-112 — `_shielded_output_to_json` sets `data['decoded'] = {'address': script_type.address}` only, even though `script_type` (P2PKH) has `.timelock` available (hathor/transaction/scripts/p2pkh.py:48,54 show transparent decoded DOES include timelock via to_human_readable). to_json_extended (base_transaction.py:891) uses the same serializer. (3) Receive path never parses the script: src/utils/storage.ts:990 builds `decoded: { ...so?.decoded, address: result.address }`; no parseScript call exists anywhere in src/utils/storage.ts or src/shielded/processing.ts (grep confirmed). (4) isOutputLocked (src/utils/transaction.ts:305-316) depends solely on `output.decoded.timelock`, so storage.ts:1033 evaluates false; the value lands in unlocked balance (storage.ts:1099), the UTXO is saved with `timelock: output.decoded.timelock || null` = null (storage.ts:1139 — finding cited 1136, minor offset), and saveLockedUtxo (storage.ts:1149-1153) is skipped, so processUtxoUnlock never runs. (5) No downstream defense: memory_store.selectUtxos filters on stored `utxo.timelock` (src/storage/memory_store.ts:764) which is null; canUseUtxo (src/utils/transaction.ts:1505) re-checks the same timelock-less decoded entry. (6) Inconsistency confirmed: the sender-local-insert path DOES parse — src/utils/transaction.ts:1260-1270 (`parseScript(so.script, ...)`, `decoded: (parsed?.toData() ?? {})`), so self-sends carry timelock while WS/API-delivered receives of the identical output do not. (7) Core enforces the timelock on spend: hathor/verification/transaction_verifier.py:202-203 calls verify_script → script_eval, and hathor/transaction/scripts/execute.py:118-128 resolves the shielded output via resolve_spent_output and evaluates its script exactly like transparent ones, so a premature spend is rejected. (8) No existing test covers timelocked shielded receive (grep of __tests__ found nothing). Minor correction to the finding's consequence narrative: the premature spend is rejected at push_tx script verification, not "signed and mined, then rejected" — so the practical impact is incorrect locked/unlocked balance display plus send failures (node rejection) until the timelock expires, with no permanent fund loss. Severity medium is appropriate.

(2) Xpub mode sends only that xpub (stream.ts:663-669); core derives children non-hardened via xpub.subkey(idx) (hathor-core hathor/websocket/iterators.py:97-111), so hardened account-2' spend addresses (SHIELDED_SPEND_ACCT_PATH, constants.ts:356) are underivable. (3) Manual mode batches come exclusively from loadAddressesCPUIntensive(this.xpubkey) (stream.ts:237-254, 482-495, 672-684) — spend addresses never sent via sendManualStreamingHistory. (4) Shielded/spend addresses derived in processQueue are only saved locally (stream.ts:566-587). (5) Fullnode streamer auto-subscribes only streamed addresses (streamer.py:61,276) and wallet-side subscribeAddresses exists only in polling path (utils/storage.ts:177), so real-time receives are also missed. (6) Polling path proves the asymmetry: loadAddresses pushes spendAddress.base58 for subscription+history fetch (utils/storage.ts:117-131) — stream paths have no equivalent. (7) Shielded receives do land on spend-pubkey P2PKH on-chain scripts (shieldedAddress.ts:92) and core indexes txs by parsed shielded_out.script address (base_transaction.py:509-512). (8) No guard: getSupportedSyncMode (utils/storage.ts:71-84) allows stream modes for any P2PKH wallet; wallet.ts:3784-3801 falls back to polling only on missing fullnode capability, never on shielded keys. No shielded coverage in __tests__/stream.test.ts. Severity: high stands — silent balance omission under stream modes, but default mode is polling (wallet.ts:430) and funds are recoverable by restarting in polling mode, so not critical.

Confirmed end-to-end. (1) Receive path drops timelock: src/utils/storage.ts:990 builds decoded as { ...so?.decoded, address } and core's _shielded_output_to_json emits only {'address': ...} for shielded outputs (hathor-core base_transaction.py:108-110); no parseScript/toData anywhere in utils/storage.ts or src/shielded/processing.ts. (2) Consequence: isOutputLocked (utils/transaction.ts:311-315) treats undefined timelock as unlocked → credited to tokens.unlocked (storage.ts:1099-1100) and UTXO saved with timelock null (storage.ts:1139), so selectUtxos offers it before expiry. (3) Send side genuinely supports timelocked shielded outputs: shielded/types.ts:58,97, new/types.ts:350, creation.ts:331, and utils/transaction.ts:822-829 build the on-chain P2PKH WITH timelock for shielded addresses. (4) Self-send path recovers timelock via parseScript().toData() (utils/transaction.ts:1260,1270; p2pkh.ts:93 includes timelock in toData), proving the script carries it — only the network-receive path loses it. (5) Node enforces the lock: transaction_verifier.py:144-146 resolves shielded scripts for inputs and verify_script/script_eval evaluates them identically to transparent (scripts/execute.py:118 resolve_spent_output is shielded-aware), so premature spends are rejected post-PoW. No alternative timelock backfill exists anywhere in the worktree (checked schemas, sync, processing). High severity is right: balance over-reporting + rejected spends on a supported feature, but no permanent fund loss (spendable after expiry), so not critical.

Confirmed at every cited site. (1) this.pinCode assigned only at src/new/wallet.ts:387 (constructor); grep finds no other assignment and no setPinCode/enableShieldedSync API in src/. (2) start() (wallet.ts:2052-2054) reads options.pinCode into a local, uses it for access-data generation (2092-2106) and migrateShieldedAccessData (2121-2129) — shielded scan keys get migrated — but never sets this.pinCode; clearSensitiveData (2897-2900) only clears xpriv/seed. (3) All receive-side decode paths pass `this.pinCode ?? undefined`: wallet.ts:1657, 1674, 1824, 1829, 3832, 659; STATE-02 safety net gated on this.pinCode at 1851/1865 so it cannot fire either. (4) utils/storage.ts:951-956 silently skips the decode block when pinCode === undefined — no else branch, no warning; the only logger.error (storage.ts:1016) is inside the block's catch and never runs when skipped. (5) Pin is genuinely required: scan key is pin-encrypted (storage.ts:976 decryptData(accessData.scanMainKey, pinCode), called from shielded/processing.ts:65); no pinless view-key path exists. (6) Sender's own shielded change affected: post-push local insert (sendTransaction.ts:849 enqueueOnNewTx → onNewTx → this.pinCode) does not receive the per-call signing pin (sendTransaction.ts:674), while send methods explicitly support per-call pin (wallet.ts:1973, 2270, 2471, 2587), confirming per-call-PIN integrations are an expected pattern. Verified against both wallet-lib and hathor-core. (1) Stream sync transmits only the legacy account xpub/addresses: stream.ts:416-423 (this.xpubkey = accessData.xpubkey), 663-669 (xpub mode sends single xpub), 237-254 + 482-495 (manual batches via deriveChild on legacy xpub only). (2) Shielded receives land on account-2' spend P2PKH: utils/shieldedAddress.ts:84-99 (spendAddress = P2PKH(spend pubkey from m/44'/280'/2'/0)); send-side sets output.address = spendAddress.base58 (sendTransaction.ts:263-264, 1095-1104). (3) Fullnode cannot derive those addresses from the account-0 xpub: hathor-core websocket/iterators.py:97-114 derives stream addresses via xpub.subkey(idx) on the one xpub given; shielded outputs are indexed by on-chain script address (base_transaction.py get_related_addresses ~508-512). (4) Stream path saves shielded/spend addresses locally only (stream.ts:566-587) — never sent, never subscribed; connection.subscribeAddresses has exactly one call site, the polling path (utils/storage.ts:177), and the polling path's loadAddresses explicitly pushes spendAddress.base58 with a comment confirming the design intent (utils/storage.ts:120-131). (5) No guard/fallback: getSupportedSyncMode (utils/storage.ts:71-84) allows stream modes regardless of shielded keys; the only stream fallback is missing fullnode capability (wallet.ts:3788-3801). __tests__/stream.test.ts has zero shielded coverage. Impact: stream-mode wallets silently omit all shielded receives (history + realtime) with no error; funds reappear only after a polling-mode resync, so high (not critical — no fund loss, and stream modes are opt-in, default is POLLING_HTTP_API). Severity high is correct.

Finding verified end-to-end; every cited line checks out and no refuting mechanism exists anywhere in the worktree.

Wallet-lib evidence (all in /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded):
- src/sync/stream.ts:416-423 — setupStream reads only `accessData.xpubkey` (legacy account-0 xpub) into this.xpubkey. spendXpubkey/scanXpubkey are never read by the stream manager.
- src/sync/stream.ts:237-254 — loadAddressesCPUIntensive derives plain P2PKH children of the passed xpub only ("This only contemplates P2PKH addresses for now"); used for both the first manual batch (stream.ts:672-684) and subsequent batches (stream.ts:482-495). XPUB mode sends only that xpub (stream.ts:663-669; src/new/connection.ts:195-219).
- src/sync/stream.ts:566-587 — shielded + spend addresses are derived via deriveShieldedAddressFromStorage and saved LOCALLY in processQueue; nothing is ever transmitted to the fullnode for them.
- Receives really do land on the static spend-derived P2PKH: src/new/sendTransaction.ts:263-264 and 1095-1104 set output.address = spendAddress.base58 (no one-time/stealth keys); src/utils/shieldedAddress.ts:92 (spendAddress = HASH160(spend_pubkey)). src/constants.ts:350,356 confirm scan=account 1', spend=account 2' — hardened accounts unreachable from the account-0 xpub.
- Polling path is the contrast: src/utils/storage.ts:119-131 pushes spendAddress.base58 into the address list used for both history fetch (loadAddressHistory, line 178) and subscription (line 177). connection.subscribeAddresses has exactly ONE call site in src/ (utils/storage.ts:177, polling-only).
- No safeguard exists: src/utils/storage.ts:71-84 getSupportedSyncMode allows all three modes for P2PKH wallets regardless of shielded keys; src/new/wallet.ts:3784-3801 falls back to polling only when the fullnode lacks the 'history-streaming' capability — never because shielded keys are present. No warning/error anywhere (grepped spendXpubkey across src/new/wallet.ts: zero sync-mode interaction).
- No test covers stream sync + shielded (stream.test.ts has no shielded streaming assertions; integration shielded tests run under the polling default, wallet.ts:430).

Source-of-truth (hathor-core, /tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded):
- hathor/websocket/iterators.py:97-113 — iter_xpub_addresses derives only non-hardened children of the single xpub sent; it cannot produce account-2' spend addresses, and gap_limit_search (lines 126-148) looks up history strictly by those derived base58 addresses in the address index.
- hathor/websocket/streamer.py:61 ("Each pushed address is automatically subscribed for real-time updates") and :276 — only streamed (legacy) addresses get fullnode-side real-time subscriptions, so the real-time path misses spend P2PKHs too.

Net effect as claimed: under MANUAL_STREAM_WS / XPUB_STREAM_WS, historical txs paying spend P2PKHs are never fetched and never subscribed, so shielded receives are silently invisible; stream.ts:831-832 processHistory has nothing to decode.

Severity: high is correct. Not critical because (a) default mode is POLLING_HTTP_API (src/new/wallet.ts:430) so a wallet must opt into stream modes, and (b) no funds are lost — restarting in polling mode recovers them. But silent balance omission with zero error under the modes recommended for large wallets justifies high.

CONFIRMED. Core side: hathor/transaction/base_transaction.py:108-113 emits decoded={'address'} only for shielded outputs (no timelock); no core rule forbids timelocked shielded scripts (hathorlib/hathorlib/transaction/shielded_tx_output.py bounds only script size, line 143-145) and spend-time enforcement is generic script_eval with shielded-aware resolve_spent_output (hathor/verification/transaction_verifier.py:203; hathor/transaction/scripts/execute.py:118-120), so a premature spend of a timelocked shielded UTXO IS node-rejected. Wallet side: src/utils/storage.ts:990 builds decoded as {...so?.decoded, address} (so.decoded never carries timelock from the node) and storage.ts:1139 saves the UTXO with timelock null; isOutputLocked (src/utils/transaction.ts:305-316) then treats it as unlocked, crediting tokens.unlocked (storage.ts:1099) and skipping saveLockedUtxo (storage.ts:1149-1153). Exhaustive grep confirms no timelock recovery in src/shielded/processing.ts or unblinding.ts; the ONLY shielded script parse is the self-send local-insert path (src/utils/transaction.ts:1260,1270 via parseScript + P2PKH.toData at src/models/p2pkh.ts:89-94, which includes timelock), proving the script carries the lock and only the fullnode-receive path drops it. Send side supports timelocked shielded outputs (src/shielded/creation.ts:331; src/utils/transaction.ts:823-829 shielded branch builds P2PKH WITH timelock; proposal types src/shielded/types.ts:58,97), so wallet-to-wallet usage can trigger this. The zod schema even reserves decoded.timelock (src/api/schemas/txApi.ts:85) but core never populates it. Impact: balance over-report + locked UTXOs offered by selection + node-rejected early spends (post-PoW); self-healing after lock expiry, no fund loss — high (not critical) is the right severity. Recommendation in finding (parse so.script on receive like the self-send path) is sound.

All cited evidence verified. (1) Wallet throws on exactly 1 shielded output: /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/new/sendTransaction.ts:505-509 and src/shielded/creation.ts:264-268. (2) Core requires >=2 shielded outputs whenever any exist: /tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor/verification/transaction_verifier.py:1219-1227 (TrivialCommitmentError); core is stricter than RFC (rfc-summary.md:12 allowed a transparent output as alternative — core does not). (3) Zero-value decoy impossible: ct-crypto hardcodes min_value=1 in create_range_proof (/tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor-ct-crypto/src/rangeproof.rs:42-44; test at 168-179 asserts amount=0 creation fails), verify also rejects range.start<1 (rangeproof.rs:95-101). (4) convertHtrChangeIfRequested no-ops with no HTR change output (sendTransaction.ts:1081) or change<=fee (1084-1088). (5) No auto-split/decoy logic anywhere in wallet-lib src/. Caveat on framing: "impossible" is slightly overstated — the caller can manually split the amount into two shielded outputs to the same address (each >=1) via the existing API, so this is a missing graceful path/UX/documentation gap rather than a hard impossibility (except the true edge case of total value 1, which cannot be split). Low severity is correct; the recommendation (document + optional auto-split) is appropriate.

Confirmed. src/new/sendTransaction.ts:556-562 pushes every out.value into transparentOutputEntries with no authority/zero filter, while the input side of the same excess computation DOES filter (sendTransaction.ts:485: `(utxo.authorities ?? 0n) === 0n && utxo.value > 0n`) — an internal asymmetry. The sibling path src/utils/transaction.ts:1392-1399 applies the correct guard with a comment explaining authority values are masks, not amounts. Source of truth agrees: hathor-core verifier skips authority outputs from the balance equation (transaction_verifier.py:979-980 `if output.is_token_authority(): continue`) and ct-crypto skips zero-amount entries (balance.rs:56-57,73-74), so an excess computed over authority mask values (TOKEN_MINT_MASK=1n/TOKEN_MELT_MASK=2n, src/constants.ts:129,135) would not satisfy the verifier's equation and the tx would be rejected (ShieldedBalanceMismatchError). Latency also confirmed: prepareTxData hard-codes authorities: 0n on every output it builds (sendTransaction.ts:237,252,275) and change outputs are never authority, so the path is currently unreachable with authority outputs — severity low is correct. Recommended fix (mirror the utils/transaction.ts guard) is appropriate.

CONFIRMED on all cited points; no refuting guard exists anywhere in the worktree.

Evidence verified:
1. /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/utils/wallet.ts:710-729 — generateAccessDataFromSeed computes walletType (P2PKH vs MULTISIG at 673-678) but then unconditionally derives scanXpriv/spendXpriv from the local participant's rootXpriv and stores scanMainKey/spendMainKey for BOTH wallet types. Same in generateAccessData at 624-637 (only condition is argXpriv.depth === 0, not walletType).
2. /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/utils/address.ts:200-208 — deriveShieldedAddressFromStorage builds spendAddress as a single-key P2PKH from the spend pubkey (addressType 'shielded-spend'); no multisig variant exists.
3. /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/utils/transaction.ts:413-430 — getSignatureForTx signs 'shielded-spend' inputs with a single derived key (spendXprivkey.deriveNonCompliantChild), pushing one signature+pubkey; never a redeem script.
4. /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/utils/storage.ts:117-131 — loadAddresses derives AND saves the shielded address pair at every index for ALL wallet types (the p2pkh/p2sh branch at 108-112 only affects the legacy address; the shielded derivation after it is unconditional) and subscribes the spend P2PKH for tx notifications. So multisig wallets silently get active single-key shielded addresses without any user opt-in at the lib level.
5. /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/new/wallet.ts:837-850 — getAddressAtIndex(legacy:false) derives shielded addresses with no walletType check; getAddressPathForIndex:871-875 returns the single spend path for shielded even on multisig wallets.
6. Exhaustive grep for multisig guards in shielded paths found only /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/new/sendTransaction.ts:1114-1123 (getOutputTypeFromWallet, generic change-output type selection, unrelated) — no shielded send/receive path checks WalletType.MULTISIG.

Source of truth: core has no shielded multisig concept — /tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor/conf/mainnet.py:23-24 defines only P2PKH_VERSION_BYTE/MULTISIG_VERSION_BYTE, and base_transaction.py:509-510 parses shielded output scripts as standard scripts. Core would technically accept a P2SH script in a shielded output, so the wallet COULD build a multisig redeem script — it just doesn't. The reference docs (client-guide-checklist.md, rfc-summary.md) are silent on multisig.

Aggravating detail beyond the finding: in a multisig wallet each participant has a different root seed, so each participant's lib instance derives DIFFERENT shielded scan/spend keys — shielded funds received via one participant's shielded address are invisible to and unrecoverable by the other n-1 participants (different scan keys), making this also a funds-availability hazard, not just a quorum bypass. The user's working tree already contains TODO_FIX_37_MULTISIG_SHIELDED_KEYS_DERIVED.md acknowledging this exact issue.

Severity: medium is fair. Exploitation requires a multisig user to actively obtain/share a shielded receive address (default receive address remains P2SH), but the lib derives and subscribes these addresses automatically with no guard or warning; borders on high due to the single-seed recoverability hazard.

CONFIRMED. All cited evidence checks out. (1) Receive path drops timelock: /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/utils/storage.ts:990 builds `decoded: { ...so?.decoded, address: result.address }` and the node's so.decoded never contains timelock — verified at /tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor/transaction/base_transaction.py:108-112 where _shielded_output_to_json emits only `{'address': ...}`. storage.ts:1139 then saves the UTXO with `timelock: output.decoded.timelock || null` (always null). Exhaustive grep of src/shielded/processing.ts, unblinding.ts, sync/stream.ts, api/schemas/txApi.ts found NO timelock recovery from so.script anywhere on the receive path. (2) isOutputLocked (src/utils/transaction.ts:305-316) returns false for undefined/null timelock, so storage.ts:1033/1095-1100 credits the value to tokens.unlocked and the UTXO passes the isTimeLocked filter in src/new/wallet.ts:1396 — over-reported available balance and the locked UTXO offered for spending. (3) Node enforces the lock: shielded scripts evaluated identically to transparent via verify_script (hathor/verification/transaction_verifier.py:202-203) → script_eval with shielded-aware resolve_spent_output (hathor/transaction/scripts/execute.py:118-120); OP_GREATERTHAN_TIMESTAMP enforced (scripts/opcode.py:81,659). Premature spend is rejected post-PoW. (4) Send-side asymmetry confirmed: src/utils/transaction.ts:823-829 builds shielded P2PKH WITH timelock, src/shielded/creation.ts:331 plumbs proposal.timelock (ShieldedOutputProposal.timelock at src/shielded/types.ts:97), and the self-send path recovers it via parseScript (src/utils/transaction.ts:1260,1270) — only the receive path drops it. SEVERITY ADJUSTMENT to medium: the wallet's own typed public send API cannot produce timelocked shielded outputs — ISendShieldedOutput (src/new/sendTransaction.ts:83-90) has no timelock field and the phantom output hardcodes timelock:null (sendTransaction.ts:251). Triggering requires direct use of exported createShieldedOutputs with a timelock proposal, an untyped JS caller, or an external/custom sender. Impact is balance over-reporting plus node-rejected spends (wasted PoW); funds are not lost and become spendable after the timelock expires. Real correctness/parity bug and a griefing vector, but narrow realistic trigger surface today. Recommended fix (parse so.script with parseScript on receipt, as the self-send path already does) is correct.

CONFIRMED. ws_message_ordering.test.ts:167-173 (A.6) asserts only `expect(after).not.toBeNull()` and `expect(typeof balAfter).toBe('object')`; src/new/wallet.ts:3040-3053 shows getTxBalance always returns a Record<string, bigint> object, so the typeof assertion is vacuously true — decryption correctness is unverified (the test's own comment at line 172 admits "we don't crash" is the bar). The finding's seed claim is also correct: generateWalletHelper() consumes a distinct precalculated wallet per call (wallet-precalculation.helper.ts:268-275), so `fresh` has a different seed than walletB; the in-test comment "Same wallet seed-space → cannot rely on credit" (line 169) is wrong — fresh deterministically cannot decrypt walletB's outputs (credit would be 0n), and a deterministic 50n assertion is achievable via the A.9 wipe technique (same file, lines 200-227: store.history.delete + utxo wipe, then assert exact 50n) or by sending to fresh's own shielded addresses. Mitigating nuance keeping severity at low: the genuine full-first decode path IS credit-asserted (50n) in setupReceivedShieldedTx (line 107) via real ws delivery, so only the synthetic full→bare permutation in A.6 is under-asserted. Severity low stands.

CONFIRMED with corrected mechanism. The validation gap is real: /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/shielded/creation.ts:270-309 validates scanPubkey/token-UID lengths and output count but never checks proposal.value; exhaustive grep of the worktree found no 2^40 guard anywhere (only recoveredValue<=0n at src/shielded/processing.ts:203 and the transparent-only MAX_OUTPUT_VALUE=2n**63n check at src/utils/buffer.ts:287, which shielded values never pass through). Source of truth confirms 40 bits: RANGE_PROOF_BITS=40 and min_value=1 at /tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor-ct-crypto/src/rangeproof.rs:15,44; the client guide's [1,2^64) (reference/client-guide-checklist.md:12,57) is wrong, core wins. HOWEVER the finding's stated failure mode is empirically false: min_bits=40 is a floor in secp256k1-zkp, not a cap. Running the installed @hathor/ct-crypto-node binding: values 2^40 and 2^41 create successfully (proof 3213/3309 bytes), pass verifyRangeProof, and fit MAX_RANGE_PROOF_SIZE=3328 — they succeed SILENTLY end-to-end (verification enforces only min>=1, no bits cap: rangeproof.rs:85-103, ffi.rs:187-200; core's verify_range_proofs at hathor/verification/transaction_verifier.py:800-827 ignores the proven range). Values 2^42..2^63-1 also create and crypto-verify fine but produce 3373-5070 byte proofs rejected late by the wallet's own size check at src/models/shielded_output.ts:175-176 ("range proof size X exceeds maximum 3328") — a late error that doesn't name the value, but not an opaque native throw. Only value >= 2^63 throws the claimed opaque Rust error ("failed to generate range proof"). Net: the recommendation (guard [1, 2^40) before calling the provider) stands and is arguably MORE important than claimed for the (2^40, 2^41] window, where the wallet silently emits outputs exceeding the documented 40-bit consensus intent with a size-distinguishable range proof. Severity stays medium: practical impact requires custom-token amounts >= ~1.1e12 base units; the mid-range failure is a clear-ish (if mislocated) wallet error, and the silent window, while a spec violation, is still accepted by core verification as written.

CONFIRMED. (1) V.4 (__tests__/integration/shielded_outputs/shielded_negative.test.ts:105-147) is two sequential successful sends; lines 138-146 literally say "We mainly assert no error is thrown" and the only assertions are not-null checks — it cannot fail if the wallet re-selected a spent shielded UTXO unless the node rejects, which is never asserted. Suite header line 15 promises "server-side (fullnode rejects on push_tx)" tests; every .rejects in the file (lines 62, 97) is client-side build refusal. No test builds a raw tx with explicit inputs referencing a spent shielded slot and pushes it. Documented for manual testing." CONFIRMED on all material points. (1) Required fields verified: src/schemas.ts:191-192 (ephemeral_pubkey + decoded required in IHistoryShieldedOutputSchema, wired into IHistoryTxSchema at schemas.ts:215), schemas.ts:106/116 (ephemeral_pubkey required in both shielded variants of IHistoryOutputSchema union), src/api/schemas/txApi.ts:113-114 (both required), txApi.ts:149 (shielded_inputs). (3) Absence is consensus-valid: hathorlib/hathorlib/transaction/shielded_tx_output.py:103-104 + 158-166 (33 zero bytes = not present, deserialized to empty); hathor/verification/transaction_verifier.py:787 validates pubkey only when present. (5) shielded_inputs is dead: core inlines spent shielded outputs into inputs[] (base_transaction.py:865-873; resources/transaction.py:97-105); no core code emits a top-level shielded_inputs key. (6) Test absence: no test references the schemas by name. One nuance the finding slightly overstates: the schemas ARE exercised indirectly — __tests__/integration/shielded_outputs/crypto_failures.test.ts calls walletB.onNewTx (lines 102/144/194/264) which runs IHistoryTxSchema.safeParse — but test J.41 (line 142) uses a PRESENT '00'.repeat(33) string (passes z.string()), never the omitted-key shape core canonically emits, so the failing variants remain untested. Severity high stands: silent drop of consensus-valid third-party txs touching wallet addresses (including their transparent outputs → balance divergence) plus stream-sync throw is a cheap griefing vector with zero coverage.

CONFIRMED on all points. (1) Schemas require fields core omits: src/schemas.ts:106,116 and :191-192 plus src/api/schemas/txApi.ts:113-114 mark ephemeral_pubkey (and decoded where cited) required, while hathor-core's _shielded_output_to_json omits ephemeral_pubkey when absent (base_transaction.py:101-102) and decoded when the script doesn't parse (base_transaction.py:108-112). Absent ephemeral pubkey is consensus-valid: 33 zero bytes deserialize to b'' (hathorlib/hathorlib/transaction/shielded_tx_output.py:103-104,158-166) and the verifier only validates it if present (hathor/verification/transaction_verifier.py:787-797). (2) The shapes really hit the schemas: to_json_extended inlines shielded outputs into outputs[] (base_transaction.py:888-896); an absent-ephemeral output fails ALL three IHistoryOutputSchema union branches → whole tx rejected at src/new/wallet.ts:1736-1739 (safeParse failure → silent drop, including transparent outputs to the wallet in the same tx) and src/sync/stream.ts:754 (bare .parse throws mid-stream). txApi path: /transaction resource uses tx.to_json(decode_script=True) with top-level shielded_outputs (resources/transaction.py:62) → fullnodeTxApiTxSchema (src/api/txApi.ts:86, txApi.ts:178) hits the same required ephemeral_pubkey/decoded (txApi.ts:113-114). (3) shielded_inputs (txApi.ts:149) is dead: core inlines spent shielded outputs into inputs[] (resources/transaction.py:97-105, base_transaction.py:862-880); no core code emits that key; harmless (.nullish) but untestable as claimed. (4) Test absence verified: grep -rE "IHistoryTxSchema|fullnodeTxApi|IHistoryOutputSchema|IHistoryShieldedOutputSchema" __tests__ → zero matches. Indirect onNewTx coverage exists but only replays wallet-produced shapes: ws_message_ordering.test.ts:30-42 (bare = empty outputs) and :49-62 (full replays stored ephemeral_pubkey/decoded, always set by wallet-lib send); unit fixtures (__tests__/new/hathorwallet.test.ts:940-997) always include ephemeral_pubkey:'' + decoded and bypass the schema via `as unknown as IHistoryTx`. __tests__/shielded/processing.test.ts:125 covers missing decoded only at the post-parse processing layer. Minor nitpick: this core version emits `mode` unconditionally (base_transaction.py:96), but the wallet schema already makes it optional — no impact. Severity high is appropriate: cheap griefing vector (one consensus-valid absent-ephemeral or non-standard-script shielded output in a tx also paying a victim address) causes silent tx loss on websocket sync and a thrown error on stream sync, with zero test coverage to catch the regression.

Verified all claims. (1) Filter exists as quoted: src/new/wallet.ts:1154 `if (!output.blindingFactor) continue;` with comment at 1144-1147 calling it "the ownership gate". (2) The invariant holds only by storage-shape accident: blindingFactor is persisted solely via own-key decryption (src/utils/storage.ts:1005), and the sender-local insert emits shielded_outputs WITHOUT blinding factors (src/utils/transaction.ts:1257-1273) — nothing pins non-persistence of send-time blindings. (3) Test gap confirmed by exhaustive grep: getShieldedUnblindingForTx is called in integration only at unblinding_encoder.test.ts:75,136,212,266 (P.1/P.2 recipient-only, P.3 all-owned self-send at lines 157-233, P.4 all-foreign at 235-275). sparse_decode.test.ts N.1 (line 66) builds exactly the mixed tx (own FS change + foreign recipient outputs) but only asserts spendability; multi_token_shielded.test.ts Q.3 uses changeShieldedMode but never calls the API; sender_local_insert.test.ts mentions it only in comments (lines 226, 297). (4) The unit mixed-case test (__tests__/new/hathorwallet.test.ts:1018, foreign entry without blindingFactor) is mock-based and bakes in the storage assumption — critically, it would STILL PASS if a future change persisted send-time blindings, so the proposed integration P.5 is the only test shape that would catch that regression mode. No current bug — implementation behaves correctly today — so this is a genuine test-gap finding; medium severity is appropriate given the privacy boundary (recipients' openings) is guarded only by an unpinned implicit invariant.

CONFIRMED (core claim): no test exercises the 40-bit shielded value ceiling and no client-side bound exists. Evidence: (1) ct-crypto pins the limit — /tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor-ct-crypto/src/rangeproof.rs:15 `RANGE_PROOF_BITS = 40` with min_value=1 (line 46); the reference doc (client-guide-checklist.md:12,57 says [1, 2^64) Bulletproof) is RFC-era and core wins, so [1, 2^40) is the real envelope. (2) Largest shielded value in any wallet-lib test is 80_000_000n (~2^26.3) at /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/__tests__/integration/shielded_outputs/core.test.ts:101-102. (3) Exhaustive grep of src/ and __tests__/ for 2**40 / 1099511627776 / MAX_SHIELDED_VALUE / 40-bit returns nothing; the only shielded bounds are MAX_SHIELDED_OUTPUTS=32, MAX_RANGE_PROOF_SIZE=3328, MAX_SHIELDED_OUTPUT_SCRIPT_SIZE=1024 (src/constants.ts:248-266). (4) src/new/sendTransaction.ts:504-515 guards only output COUNT (==1, >32), no value guard; src/shielded/creation.ts:78,173 passes proposal.value straight to the provider. (5) The `Cannot create more than` message (sendTransaction.ts:512) is asserted by zero tests (grep hits only the source line). (6) Node side gives no graceful error: hathor/verification/transaction_verifier.py:800-827 raises generic InvalidRangeProofError.

REFUTED sub-claim (this is why severity drops): the "crypto throw after UTXO selection has marked inputs as selected / untested selected-UTXO cleanup" concern is wrong about state. UTXO selection in prepareTxData (bestUtxoSelection via _prepareSendTokensData, sendTransaction.ts:1132-1160) only builds in-memory tx data — it never calls storage.utxoSelectAsInput. Marking happens exclusively in mineTx via updateOutputSelected(true) at sendTransaction.ts:763, and run() (sendTransaction.ts:932-952) orders prepareTx (which contains createShieldedOutputs at line 517) BEFORE mineTx. So a crypto-stage failure leaves no selected-UTXO state in storage to clean; no fund/state corruption is possible.
