Minor additional caveat: the claim "create throws for value >= 2^40" is not certain — secp256k1-zkp's rangeproof_sign treats min_bits=40 as a floor and may emit a larger proof for 41+ bit values instead of throwing (which would then hit the wallet's own MAX_RANGE_PROOF_SIZE=3328 check at src/models/shielded_output.ts:175-176, or slip past it for ~41-bit values); either way the failure is late and opaque, which supports the finding's substance.

Verdict: real missing-test + missing client-side guard (opaque late error for large custom-token amounts), but with the state-corruption angle refuted the impact is UX-only — adjusted from medium to low. The recommended tests (client-side rejection of 2^40, boundary success at 2^40-1, and a 33-output rejection-message assertion) remain valid.

Confirmed on all points. (1) S.1 (__tests__/integration/shielded_outputs/mint_melt_shielded.test.ts:113-126) is a sendManyOutputsTransaction of pre-minted supply — no mintTokens call, no MintHeader, despite the name. (2) K.4 (token_creation.test.ts:131-138) and K.5 (token_creation.test.ts:422-432) assert only token/HTR balances; mintTokens/meltTokens have no shielded output option (zero shielded references in src/utils/tokens.ts), and the shielded destination/change address is auto-converted to a transparent spend-P2PKH script at src/utils/address.ts:144-148, so both txs are fully transparent with no shielded headers — neither test asserts the produced shape. (3) The auto-declare logic at src/utils/transaction.ts:993-1076 (_attachShieldedHeaders; cited range 985-1071 is off by a few lines, substance identical) would emit MintHeader alongside ShieldedOutputsHeader (isShieldedTx at 993-995 includes shieldedOutputs.length>0; mint branch at 1056-1062), but exhaustive grep shows MintHeader/MeltHeader only appear in standalone header serialization unit tests (__tests__/headers/) and never coexist with ShieldedOutputsHeader in any built tx; the only exercised combos are Fee+ShieldedOutputs, Fee+Unshield, Unshield+Mint (S.0 TCT, S.4), Unshield+Melt (S.2, S.3). (4) hathor-core explicitly supports the combo: verify_mint_melt_requires_shielded (transaction_verifier.py ~672-692, Rule M1 satisfied by has_shielded_outputs()), MintHeader entries extend the surjection-proof domain for FS outputs of freshly-minted tokens (~892-897), M4 folding (~998-1019). Severity medium is right: a test gap (not a live bug) but in consensus-critical header construction, with three misleading test names; since no high-level wallet API currently reaches Mint/Melt+ShieldedOutputs, the recommendation to either add a real combo test or pin the auto-conversion and rename S.1/K.4/K.5 stands.

CONFIRMED. Every cited line checks out and no mitigating layer exists anywhere in the worktree.

Evidence verifying the claim:
1. Plaintext persistence in history tx: /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/utils/storage.ts:985-1008 pushes a decoded entry with `value: result.decrypted.value` (:987), `blindingFactor: result.decrypted.blindingFactor.toString('hex')` (:1005), `assetBlindingFactor: result.decrypted.assetBlindingFactor?.toString('hex')` (:1006), then persists it verbatim via `await store.saveTx(tx)` (:1011). No encryptData call exists anywhere in src/utils/storage.ts (grep returned zero hits).
2. Plaintext persistence in UTXO record: src/utils/storage.ts:1131-1148 — `store.saveUtxo({... shielded: true, blindingFactor: ..., assetBlindingFactor: ...})` at :1141-1146, raw hex strings.
3. Store layer applies no transformation: src/storage/memory_store.ts:864-866 `saveUtxo` does `this.utxos.set(key, utxo)` verbatim; saveTx (memory_store.ts:543) likewise. Grep for any redaction/omission of blindingFactor across src/: zero hits. The only decrypt/encrypt usage in src/storage/storage.ts (:948, :964, :976, :988, :1024) is for xpriv keys, not blindings.
4. Type carriers are plain hex with no encryption marker: src/types.ts:352-353 (IShieldedOutputEntry) and :473-474 (IUtxo), both commented "hex, 32 bytes".
5. Contrast confirmed — all key material IS PIN-encrypted: src/utils/wallet.ts:596, :620, :631 (`scanMainKey = encryptData(scanXpriv.xprivkey, pin)`), :636, :642-644, :727, :729, :807-809. The project's own threat model assumes store readers exist (that is why keys are encrypted), yet blinding factors get zero protection under the same assumption.
6. Source of truth supports the finding: the official client integration guide (SHIELDED_INTEGRATION_REVIEW/reference/client-guide-checklist.md:32, :36) explicitly states `value_blinding_factor` and `asset_blinding_factor` are "kept secret (needed for spending)". hathor-core has no blinding storage code at all (grep of /tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor/crypto/shielded/ for storage/persistence/custody: zero hits) — custody is entirely wallet policy, so the wallet-lib cannot lean on the node for protection.
7. Impact mechanics are correct: blinding factor + public on-chain commitment = commitment opening (provable disclosure + on-chain linkage). Without the plaintext copy, a DB reader gains nothing — blindings are only re-derivable via rangeproof rewind using the scan xpriv, which IS PIN-encrypted (src/storage/storage.ts:976). So persisting them plaintext is a strict downgrade of the at-rest protection the rest of the design provides. The fields also ride on IHistoryTx returned by getTx(), so any history export carries the openings.
8. Functional-necessity counterargument fails: the spend path (src/new/sendTransaction.ts:472-480, src/utils/transaction.ts:1363-1366) reads `utxo.blindingFactor` from the store, but it could equally decrypt-on-use (PIN is already available in the spend path, which is exactly how scan/spend xprivs are handled) or re-derive via rewind. Plaintext persistence is a convenience, not a requirement.

Severity: keep high. Defeating amount-confidentiality — the sole purpose of the shielded feature — for any store reader, plus enabling third-party provable disclosure, under the exact threat model the library already defends keys against, justifies high. (Mitigating note for context: wallet history including transparent amounts has always been stored plaintext in this design; the qualitative difference here is that blinding factors are cryptographic witnesses per the official guide's "kept secret" instruction, not display data.)

CONFIRMED on every point; the evidence is stronger than the finding claims.

1) Ungated shielded key derivation for multisig: /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/utils/wallet.ts:627-637 — generateAccessDataFromXpriv derives scan/spend chains for any `argXpriv.depth === 0`, with no walletType check, in deliberate contrast to the type-gated branches at lines 582-588 and 590-592 and the multisig branch at 598-608. Additionally (not cited by the reviewer), generateAccessDataFromSeed does the same unconditionally at src/utils/wallet.ts:710-715, so seed-started multisig wallets also get shielded chains.

2) No gate anywhere downstream: src/utils/storage.ts:117-132 (loadAddresses) derives and saves shielded+spend addresses on BOTH the p2pkh and p2sh branches whenever scan/spend xpubs exist (deriveShieldedAddressFromStorage at src/utils/address.ts:178-186 only checks key existence, never walletType). src/new/wallet.ts:837-850 (getAddressAtIndex with legacy:false) derives shielded addresses with no multisig check; the only MULTISIG check nearby (wallet.ts:878-879) is legacy path-string formatting in getAddressPathForIndex. The shielded send path has no gate either — the only multisig reference in src/new/sendTransaction.ts:1114-1123 is getOutputTypeFromWallet, which happily maps MULTISIG→'p2sh' and proceeds. So a multisig wallet silently exposes shielded addresses whose spend script is single-sig P2PKH on the local cosigner's spend chain (m-of-n custody downgraded to 1-of-1 for shielded receipts).

3) Zero multisig test coverage: `grep -rin multisig __tests__/integration/shielded_outputs __tests__/shielded` returns nothing (exit 1); `grep -rin 'multisig.*shield|shield.*multisig'` across all of __tests__ and src returns nothing. The multisig tests in __tests__/utils/wallet.test.ts (lines 413-480) predate shielded and never assert scan/spend key presence/absence. Read-only single-sig IS covered: __tests__/integration/shielded_outputs/core.test.ts:1363 ('should gracefully handle shielded outputs on read-only (xpub-only) wallet') — exactly as the finding states.

4) Wallet-service claim confirmed and WORSE than stated: src/wallet/walletServiceStorageProxy.ts:192-201 contains the new isShieldedOutputEntry passthrough; the diffstat for __tests__/wallet/walletServiceStorageProxy.test.ts is exactly +1 line, and that line is `isShieldedOutputEntry: jest.fn().mockReturnValue(false)` (test file line 20) — i.e., the new branch (line 193 passthrough) is mocked to NEVER fire, so the shielded passthrough path has literally zero unit-test execution.

5) Source of truth: hathor-core does not gate this either way — shielded outputs carry an arbitrary script (hathorlib MAX_SHIELDED_OUTPUT_SCRIPT_SIZE re-exported in /tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor/transaction/shielded_tx_output.py:27-43), and the scan+spend address format is inherently single-sig P2PKH on the spend pubkey. The multisig custody contract is purely a wallet-lib product decision, currently unpinned by any test, which is exactly the finding's point.

Severity: medium is appropriate for the test-gap framing. Note the underlying behavior (silent custody downgrade for multisig shielded receipts) is live, not hypothetical — if the team decides this should be blocked, the code fix itself would merit a higher-severity behavioral finding; as a coverage/contract-pinning issue, medium stands.

Confirmed at every cited location. Plaintext persistence: src/utils/storage.ts:987 (value), :1005-1006 (blindingFactor/assetBlindingFactor hex) saved via store.saveTx at :1011; UTXO copy at :1141-1147 via store.saveUtxo. Type carriers with no encryption: src/types.ts:352-353 and :472-474. Contrast verified: all xprivs PIN-encrypted (src/utils/wallet.ts:596,620,631 scanMainKey, :636 spendMainKey, :642-644 authKey; decrypted in src/storage/storage.ts:948,964,976,988). Repo-wide grep: zero encryptData calls on blinding factors, no redaction on any read path; getTx (src/new/wallet.ts:1093-1095) returns the raw stored IHistoryTx so openings flow into any history export/API. Source of truth agrees these are secret material under wallet custody: client-guide-checklist.md:32,36,80 ('blinding_factor ... kept secret'); hathor-core never stores blindings. Impact attack fails: unlike transparent history (public on-chain anyway), blinding factors are provable commitment openings not otherwise recoverable except via the PIN-encrypted scan key, so plaintext storage strictly downgrades the feature's only guarantee; fix is feasible since PIN is already present at the consumption sites (sendTransaction.ts:472-480, wallet.ts:1146-1172). Not critical (no fund/key loss); high stands.

All sub-claims verified. (1) Schema-too-strict CONFIRMED: src/schemas.ts:191-192 require `ephemeral_pubkey: z.string()` and `decoded: IHistoryOutputDecodedSchema` (no .optional()); same in AmountShieldedOutputSchema (schemas.ts:106), FullShieldedOutputSchema (schemas.ts:116), and src/api/schemas/txApi.ts:113-114. The git diff shows they deliberately made shielded *inputs* fields optional but left shielded *output* ephemeral_pubkey/decoded required. (2) Core omits these in consensus-valid cases CONFIRMED: hathor/transaction/base_transaction.py:101-102 only sets ephemeral_pubkey `if output.ephemeral_pubkey`; lines 108-112 only set `decoded` when decode_script AND parse_address_script succeeds. Absent ephemeral pubkey is consensus-valid (verifier transaction_verifier.py:787 validates only `if output.ephemeral_pubkey`; hathorlib shielded_tx_output.py:104,166 encode/treat all-zero 33B as absent). (3) Test absence CONFIRMED: grep of IHistoryTxSchema|fullnodeTxApi|IHistoryShieldedOutputSchema|transactionApiSchema over __tests__ returns nothing; the only shielded fixtures (hathorwallet.test.ts:979,994) hardcode ephemeral_pubkey:'' and always include decoded, and cast `as unknown as IHistoryTx`, bypassing zod entirely. (4) shielded_inputs dead key CONFIRMED: txApi.ts:149 declares it, but core to_json (base_transaction.py:815-820) emits only shielded_outputs and inlines spent shielded outputs into inputs[] (resources/transaction.py:97-101) — never a top-level shielded_inputs; it's nullish so harmless but untestable/dead. Impact path real: stream sync uses IHistoryTxSchema.parse() (THROWS, src/sync/stream.ts:754, inside WS listener) and onNewTx uses safeParse then silently drops the whole tx on failure (src/new/wallet.ts:1736-1740). A consensus-valid third-party tx with an omitted ephemeral pubkey (or unparseable shielded script) co-located with a transparent output to a wallet address would be rejected by the schema, dropping a legitimate payment from balance/history or breaking stream sync — a cheap griefing/DoS vector with no test coverage. Severity high is defensible (availability + fund-visibility impact via consensus-valid input); medium would also be reasonable since it requires an adversarial/atypical tx shape and the wallet never owns the absent-pubkey shielded output itself.

CONFIRMED. All cited code exists verbatim: /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/utils/storage.ts:987 (plaintext value), :1005-1006 (blindingFactor/assetBlindingFactor as raw hex), :1011 (store.saveTx persists them on history tx), :1141-1147 (store.saveUtxo persists shielded:true + both blinding factors); plus an additional persistence site the reviewer missed at src/utils/storage.ts:713 (re-delivery recovery path). Type carriers src/types.ts:352-353 and :472-474 have no encryption wrapper. Exhaustive grep for encryptData across src/ shows it is applied ONLY to xprivs/seed in src/utils/wallet.ts (:596,620,631,636,642-650,694-697,727,729,807,809,836-859) — never to blinding factors; no mitigation exists anywhere (storage layer, shielded module, zod schemas). Contrast claim accurate: scan/spend xprivs are PIN-decrypted at src/storage/storage.ts:976,988 (the finding's 'storage.ts:971-989' cite resolves to storage/storage.ts, not utils/storage.ts — minor citation slip, substance correct). Export-path claim confirmed: src/new/wallet.ts:1093-1094 getTx returns the stored IHistoryTx with openings intact. Source-of-truth check: hathor-core never persists blindings — hathor/transaction/shielded_tx_output.py:85-106 rewinds secrets transiently and balance.py is sender-side only; custody is wallet policy, and the client integration guide (SHIELDED_INTEGRATION_REVIEW/reference/client-guide-checklist.md:32,36) explicitly says value/asset blinding factors are 'kept secret (needed for spending)'. Severity nuance: not fund-loss (spending still requires the PIN-encrypted spend xpriv signature) and a DB reader already sees transparent history plaintext; but blinding factor + public Pedersen commitment is a commitment opening enabling PROVABLE third-party disclosure and on-chain linkage, defeating the feature's core guarantee under the same at-rest threat model that motivated PIN-encrypting all xprivs. Blindings are re-derivable on demand from the PIN-encrypted scan key, so plaintext persistence leaks exactly what the key-encryption design otherwise protects, and the recommended fix (encrypt or derive transiently) is feasible. High severity stands.

Code facts check out (src/new/sendTransaction.ts:542-543 gate, :571-577 excess computation, src/utils/transaction.ts:961-969 unconditional header attach, no min-input/warning logic anywhere in the worktree), but the finding's claimed privacy impact is refuted by the source of truth. (1) "Confirm WHICH shielded commitment this unshield spends / breaking unlinkability" is wrong: Hathor inputs explicitly reference the spent output by (tx_id, index) — see hathor/verification/transaction_verifier.py:958-973 where the verifier resolves the spent shielded commitment directly from the input pointer. There is no anonymity set; receive→spend linkage is always public, with or without the blinding factor. (2) "Publishing r lets any observer recover v" adds nothing: in a full unshield all outputs are transparent and the balance equation sum(C_in)=sum(C_out)+excess·G+fee·H (transaction_verifier.py:935-938) already makes the single input's value publicly derivable without r. (3) The core docstring the finding cites as supporting evidence actually disproves it — hathor/transaction/headers/unshield_balance_header.py:42-46 says: "with exactly one shielded input the scalar is effectively r_in of that input, but the transparent outputs of a full unshield already expose the spent amount, so nothing previously-private is additionally leaked." The quoted phrase "leaving multi-input aggregation/hygiene to wallets" does not exist anywhere in hathor-core (grep for hygiene/aggregat: zero hits) — it is fabricated. Caveat (reason for medium confidence and info severity rather than outright dismissal): there is a real but different second-order concern the finding never argues — a single-input excess reveals the spent commitment's effective G-scalar, which combined with the originating shield-tx's balance constraint (sum of output G-scalars pinned by transparent inputs) can expose a sibling output's scalar and hence its value via 40-bit search. If the reviewer wants to pursue that, it should be filed as a new finding with that mechanism; the finding as stated (linkability + v recovery of the spent input) describes information that is already public by protocol design and documented as such by core.

CONFIRMED. (1) JSON schemas unbounded: src/schemas.ts:101-120 and 174-197 — commitment/range_proof/script/ephemeral_pubkey/asset_commitment/surjection_proof are plain z.string() with no .max() or hex regex; identical in src/api/schemas/txApi.ts:96-124; even the shielded_outputs array (schemas.ts:215, .array().optional()) lacks a .max(32) cap. (2) Unchecked decode→native: src/shielded/processing.ts:122-124 Buffer.from(...,'hex') then rewindFullShieldedOutput (~:144) / rewindAmountShieldedOutput (~:191), called from the JSON history path at src/utils/storage.ts:963. (3) Wire-path contrast verified: src/models/shielded_output.ts:175-176 (rpLen ≤ MAX_RANGE_PROOF_SIZE=3328, constants.ts:254), :187-190 (script ≤ MAX_SHIELDED_OUTPUT_SCRIPT_SIZE), :218-221 (spLen ≤ MAX_SURJECTION_PROOF_SIZE=4096, constants.ts:260), mirroring hathor-core shielded_tx_output.py (imports those caps at :31-34). (4) No mitigation found anywhere else: size constants are used ONLY in the wire deserializer; ct-crypto Rust (hathor-ct-crypto/src/rangeproof.rs:143-147 deserialize_range_proof / RangeProof::from_slice) has no early size cap — a test comment at rangeproof.rs:298-302 explicitly notes size enforcement is delegated to the fullnode deserializer. Oversized strings are also persisted to storage (utils/storage.ts ~:1003 pushes range_proof/surjection_proof into stored tx.outputs). Severity low is correct: attacker must be the wallet's own compromised fullnode or MITM (already highly trusted), and "amplification" is only ~2-3x of attacker-transmitted bytes; still a real hardening gap inconsistent with the wallet's own wire path and core. Recommendation (zod .max() or decoded-buffer length checks in processShieldedOutputs) is sound.

PARTIALLY REAL — positional leak confirmed, address-reuse mechanism largely refuted. CONFIRMED: (1) change recipient = wallet.getCurrentAddress({},{legacy:false}) with no markAsUsed (src/new/sendTransaction.ts:1090; src/new/wallet.ts:897-906; memory_store.ts:395-419); (2) change appended last to shieldedOutputDefs (sendTransaction.ts:1102) and shielded outputs are NEVER shuffled — the shuffle at sendTransaction.ts:419-426 covers transparent outputs only (comment there says shuffling exists precisely so change isn't positionally identifiable; that mitigation is omitted for shielded), so shielded change is deterministically the last shielded output; (3) balancing bf on last output (creation.ts:74,169) — correct but cryptographically unobservable, only the position leaks; (4) shielded outputs carry cleartext P2PKH scripts (hathorlib re-exports in /tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor/transaction/shielded_tx_output.py:27-43), so reuse is on-chain visible; (5) core enforces nothing here — client-side, as claimed. REFUTED: the headline "repeated sends reuse the same address / every change output linkable" claim. The shielded cursor DOES rotate by use: processNewTx appends decoded wallet-owned shielded outputs with their 'shielded-spend' address (utils/storage.ts:985-1008), tracks shieldedMaxIndexUsed for addressType==='shielded-spend' (utils/storage.ts:1040-1043), and updateWalletMetadataFromProcessedTxData advances shieldedCurrentAddressIndex to maxIndexUsed+1 (utils/storage.ts:824-833). The wallet processes its OWN sent tx via the WS echo with this.pinCode (src/new/wallet.ts:1824), so the next send's change goes to a fresh address. This is identical to the wallet's transparent-change semantics (storage.ts:681 also uses getCurrentAddress without markAsUsed; rotation always happens via history processing, not at hand-out time). Residual real issues: change = current RECEIVE address (a payee handed that address links the next tx's change — one tx, not "every"; the wallet has no internal change chain anywhere, pre-existing design); back-to-back sends before the WS echo processes reuse the address; rotation fails if pinCode unavailable (read-only context) or decryption fails. The strongest surviving point is the deterministic last-position of shielded change, inconsistent with the deliberate transparent shuffle — recommendation to shuffle shielded outputs (or randomize positions) is valid and cheap. Severity: keep low; the "cluster every transaction" impact claim is overstated, but the positional fingerprint + first-cycle linkability are genuine privacy hardening gaps in a feature whose purpose is privacy.

areas.charter: Review tx weight calculation for shielded txs in /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/models/transaction.ts. `getOutputsSum()` (lines 370-382) adds plaintext shielded output values (`sumOutputs += shieldedOut.value`) into `calculateWeight()` (lines 329-360), but hathor-core's `minimum_tx_weight` (hathor/daa.py:176-207) uses `tx.sum_outputs` which is TRANSPARENT-ONLY (base_transaction.py:366-368) — the node cannot see shielded values. Weight is published on-chain (8-byte float in the graph struct) and the formula w = c*log2(size) + 4/(1+k/amount) + 4 is invertible given the public tx size: an observer solves for `amount`, subtracts the visible transparent sum, and recovers the EXACT total shielded value of every wallet-lib-built shielded tx — defeating amount confidentiality at tx-total granularity. Verify: (a) whether the kTerm is clamped by txMinWeight for realistic shielded tx sizes (~7KB+ means the size term alone exceeds the minimum, so no clamp — leak is live); (b) the correct fix direction (mirror core: transparent-only sum, which still satisfies the node's minimum since wallet weight currently only overshoots); (c) whether any test or integration assertion would catch this (none of the confirmed findings mention weight). Also check CreateTokenTransaction inherits the same leak.

Review the Mint/Melt header auto-declaration heuristic in /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/utils/transaction.ts `_attachShieldedHeaders` (lines 972-1076) against hathor-core's rules. Core's `verify_no_undeclared_mint_melt` (transaction_verifier.py:581-611) triggers on the TRANSPARENT-ONLY token_dict surplus/deficit whenever the tx spends the matching authority input; the wallet instead computes a delta that INCLUDES shielded output/input values (lines 1029-1046), so the two disagree whenever an authority input rides along with a shielding move (wallet declares nothing, node demands a header — and no balanced construction exists, so the wallet should refuse such input sets rather than build a doomed tx). Also audit: (a) tokenIndex mapping `tokensArray.indexOf(token)+1` uses the privacy-filtered tokens array from sendTransaction.ts:593-617 — line 597 excludes AUTHORITY outputs from `tokensWithVisibleOutput`, so a tx carrying an authority output of token X plus only FullShielded value outputs of X drops X from tx.tokens while the authority output's token_data still needs an index (broken serialization or wrong token reference); (b) melting a FullShielded-held token (shielded input + melt authority, no X outputs) — X never enters tokens[], so the required MeltHeader entry cannot be emitted; (c) Rule M3 (token in both headers), the TCT sentinel path (token_index==1, no melt of new token per token_creation_transaction_verifier.py:30-75), and core's UnusedTokensError interplay (verify_tokens counts AmountShielded but NOT FullShielded indexes as used). No confirmed finding covers production correctness of these headers — COMP-07 only covers the missing mint-into-shielded producer and TEST-06 only test naming.

Review token-administration flows in /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/utils/tokens.ts for shielded-UTXO contamination. `bestUtxoSelection` deliberately selects shielded UTXOs (utxo.ts:102-107), and tokens.ts uses it as the default at line 480 (createToken HTR deposit/fee), line 629 (melt amount selection), and line 691 (melt HTR deposit+fee) — yet tokens.ts contains ZERO shielded handling (no excess blinding factor computation, no blinded-input bookkeeping; grep 'shielded' returns nothing). Any mint/melt/create-token funded even partially by a shielded UTXO produces a tx that core classifies as shielded (`is_shielded()` = any input index >= len(spent_tx.outputs), transaction.py:182-193), which then requires an UnshieldBalanceHeader (verifier invariant transaction_verifier.py:1020-1045), MeltHeader/MintHeader declarations under verify_no_undeclared_mint_melt, and the shielded balance equation — none of which tokens.ts produces, since the excess scalar is only computed in SendTransaction.prepareTxData (sendTransaction.ts:543-571). Result: node-rejected token operations after PoW, plus silent forced deshielding attempts of confidential funds for admin operations. Confirm: (a) which wallet.ts entry points (mintTokens, meltTokens, createToken, delegateAuthority, destroyAuthority) route through these selectors; (b) whether `_attachShieldedHeaders`'s isShieldedTx gate (transaction.ts:993-995, keyed on shieldedOutputs/excessBlindingFactor — both absent here) means even the mandatory Mint/Melt declarations are skipped; (c) test coverage (none of the shielded integration suites exercise token ops with a shielded-UTXO-bearing wallet). Distinct from STATE-03 (nano/template only) and STATE-04 (plain sends, which DO compute the excess).

findings.title: Plaintext shielded output values enter tx weight, leaking exact total shielded amount on-chain
findings.description: getOutputsSum() folds the plaintext value of every shielded output into the sum used by calculateWeight(): `for (const shieldedOut of this.shieldedOutputs) sumOutputs += shieldedOut.value;` (transaction.ts:378-381). That sum becomes the `amount` term in the minimum-weight formula w = c*log2(size) + 4/(1+k/amount) + 4 (transaction.ts:341-360). The resulting weight is serialized as an 8-byte float in the graph struct (serializeGraphFields, transaction.ts:259-260) and is therefore public on-chain. hathor-core's minimum_tx_weight uses `tx.sum_outputs`, which is TRANSPARENT-ONLY (hathor/daa.py:203 `amount = max(1, tx.sum_outputs)`; hathor/transaction/base_transaction.py:366-368 `sum(output.value for output in self.outputs ...)` — shielded outputs live in a separate header and never enter sum_outputs). The node cannot see shielded values, so it never bakes them into weight. By matching core's formula but feeding it the secret total, wallet-lib publishes a weight that an observer can invert: the public tx size is known (it's on the wire), c/k/min are public network constants, so the observer solves the monotonic formula for `amount`, subtracts the visible transparent output sum, and recovers the EXACT total shielded value of every shielded tx wallet-lib builds. This defeats amount confidentiality (the core promise of confidential transactions) at tx-total granularity. The plaintext values are populated for real sends: _attachShieldedHeaders sets `tx.shieldedOutputs` from `so.value` (src/utils/transaction.ts:938-951), and prepareToSend -> calculateWeight runs on every send path (src/utils/transaction.ts:1428; src/new/sendTransaction.ts:681,738; src/wallet/wallet.ts:2031,2347,2551,2712,2793,3242; src/wallet/partialTxProposal.ts:422).
findings.evidence: wallet-lib leak: /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/models/transaction.ts:378-381 (`for (const shieldedOut of this.shieldedOutputs) { sumOutputs += shieldedOut.value; }`) feeding transaction.ts:341 `let sumOutputs = Number(this.getOutputsSum());` -> :348 `const amount = sumOutputs / 10 ** DECIMAL_PLACES;` -> :354 weight formula -> :259-260 weight serialized to 8-byte float on the public graph struct. Plaintext values are set on real sends at /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/utils/transaction.ts:945 (`so.value`). hathor-core contradiction: /tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor/daa.py:203 `amount = max(1, tx.sum_outputs) / (10 ** ...)` with /tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor/transaction/base_transaction.py:366-368 sum_outputs = transparent-only. Clamp check (a): default constants identical in /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/constants.ts:229-233 (txMinWeight 14, coeff 1.6, k 100) and /tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathorlib/hathorlib/conf/settings.py:88-94; a minimal 2-output AmountShielded tx is ~6.9KB (2x ~3310B range-proof-bearing outputs), so coeff*log2(6900) ~= 20.4 > 14 — `weight = Math.max(weight, txMinWeight)` (transaction.ts:357) never clamps, leaving the kTerm fully observable. Node acceptance bound (b): /tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor/verification/transaction_verifier.py:111-121 accepts weight in [min - WEIGHT_TOL, min + MAX_TX_WEIGHT_DIFF]; since the wallet's kTerm < 4 it only overshoots the transparent-only minimum within tolerance, so a transparent-only sum still passes. Tests (c): zero weight assertions in /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/__tests__/integration/shielded_outputs/ (grep `weight` = 0 hits across 23 suites); unit tests assert weight only on transparent txs (__tests__/models/transaction.test.ts:118,377).
findings.recommendation: Mirror hathor-core: exclude shielded output values from the weight `amount` term. Revert getOutputsSum() to transparent-outputs-only for the purpose of calculateWeight (drop the shieldedOutputs loop at transaction.ts:378-381), or compute the weight `amount` from transparent outputs alone (with the existing `Math.max(1, ...)` floor). The node's minimum-weight check still passes because the wallet weight only ever overshot the transparent-only minimum. Add a regression assertion that two shielded txs with identical structure but different hidden amounts produce identical weight.

findings.title: CreateTokenTransaction inherits the same shielded-value weight leak
findings.description: CreateTokenTransaction extends Transaction and does not override getOutputsSum, calculateWeight, or prepareToSend, so a shielded TCT computes its published weight from the same plaintext-shielded-value sum (GAP1-01). _attachShieldedHeaders explicitly supports the TCT path (it accepts `Transaction | CreateTokenTransaction` and sets `tx.shieldedOutputs`), so a token-creation tx that carries shielded outputs (alpha-v4 allows shielded TCTs, which must carry a MintHeader) will leak its total shielded value through weight exactly as a regular shielded tx does. Same invertibility, same privacy break, on a transaction type the integration explicitly enabled.
findings.evidence: /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/models/create_token_transaction.ts:39 `class CreateTokenTransaction extends Transaction` — no getOutputsSum/calculateWeight override (grep shows only serializeFundsFields/getFundsFieldsFromBytes overridden). Shared shielded-header attachment that populates tx.shieldedOutputs for both types: /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/utils/transaction.ts:904 `_attachShieldedHeaders(tx: Transaction | CreateTokenTransaction, ...)` -> :951 `tx.shieldedOutputs = shieldedModels`. Core allows shielded TCT and reconciles supply via the public MintHeader, not via weight: /tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor/verification/token_creation_transaction_verifier.py:30-75; weight still transparent-only via daa.py:203.
findings.recommendation: The transparent-only weight fix in GAP1-01 (applied at the base Transaction.getOutputsSum / calculateWeight level) automatically covers CreateTokenTransaction since it inherits the method; add an explicit shielded-TCT weight regression test to lock it in.

result.notes: CONFIRMED. Wallet-lib deliberately folds plaintext shielded values into the weight amount: /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/models/transaction.ts:378-381 (loop adding shieldedOut.value, comment "Shielded outputs also contribute to the weight calculation"), feeding calculateWeight at :341-354 and serialized publicly as an 8-byte double at :259-260. src/models/shielded_output.ts:54-55 even documents the value as "used for weight calculation", and _attachShieldedHeaders populates it with the real amount on every send (src/utils/transaction.ts ~:945). Source of truth contradicts: hathor-core's minimum_tx_weight (/tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor/daa.py:203) uses tx.sum_outputs which is transparent-only (/tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor/transaction/base_transaction.py:366-368 sums only self.outputs; shielded_outputs is a separate property); identical in hathorlib (base_transaction.py:268, daa.py:45). No shielded-aware weight logic exists anywhere in core, and the RFC/client-guide reference docs contain zero weight guidance — so core neither requires nor sanctions this. Clamp never masks it (defaults 14/1.6/100 match on both sides: wallet src/constants.ts:229-233 vs hathorlib conf/settings.py:88-94; KB-scale range-proof txs give c*log2(size)+4 > 14). Node accepts the inflated weight (kTerm overshoot < 4, within MAX_TX_WEIGHT_DIFF per transaction_verifier.py:111-121), so the bug is silent. Inversion is practical: weight is a full-precision double, tx size and constants are public, kTerm is monotonic in amount; recovery precision is sub-cent for realistic totals. Zero weight assertions in __tests__/integration/shielded_outputs/. Only mitigation: networks reporting min_tx_weight_k=0 (handled at transaction.ts:350-352) leak nothing, but defaults/mainnet use k=100. Severity critical stands — it defeats amount confidentiality (per-tx total) for the core promise of the feature. Recommended fix (exclude shielded values from the weight amount, mirroring core) is correct and remains node-compatible.

CONFIRMED. (1) Base leak exists: /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/models/transaction.ts:378-381 getOutputsSum() adds plaintext shielded values ("// Shielded outputs also contribute to the weight calculation"; sumOutputs += shieldedOut.value), and calculateWeight (line 341) publishes that sum through the invertible 4/(1+k/amount) term; prepareToSend (lines 499-501) is the real send path (src/new/sendTransaction.ts:681,738). (2) CreateTokenTransaction inherits it: /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/models/create_token_transaction.ts:39 extends Transaction; exhaustive method listing shows only constructor/serializeFundsFields/serializeTokenInfo/getTokenInfoFromBytes/getFundsFieldsFromBytes/createFromBytes/validateNft/createEmpty — no getOutputsSum/calculateWeight/prepareToSend override anywhere in the worktree. (3) Shielded TCT path is explicitly enabled: src/utils/transaction.ts:904 _attachShieldedHeaders(tx: Transaction | CreateTokenTransaction, ...) sets tx.shieldedOutputs and pushes ShieldedOutputsHeader; lines ~995-1010 build the MintHeader (token_index=1 via __create_token__ sentinel) for CREATE_TOKEN_TX_VERSION; comment at ~880 notes alpha-v3 lifted the TCT-can't-be-shielded restriction. (4) Core (source of truth) requires transparent-only weight: /tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor/daa.py:203 minimum_tx_weight uses tx.sum_outputs, and base_transaction.py:366-368 sums only self.outputs (core shielded outputs carry commitments, no value), so including shielded values is both unnecessary for acceptance and leaks the total shielded value (weight is invertible given public txSize + network constants; subtract transparent sum to get shielded total). (5) Core allows shielded TCT and reconciles supply via the public MintHeader, not weight: token_creation_transaction_verifier.py:30-75. Severity high is correct: same privacy break as GAP1-01 on a tx type the integration deliberately enabled; derivative of GAP1-01 (base-class fix covers both), so the recommended TCT-specific weight regression test is the right lock-in.

CONFIRMED. (1) Base leak exists: /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/models/transaction.ts:378-381 getOutputsSum() adds plaintext shielded values ("Shielded outputs also contribute to the weight calculation"); calculateWeight uses it at :341 and prepareToSend publishes the weight at :501. (2) CreateTokenTransaction inherits it: src/models/create_token_transaction.ts:39 extends Transaction and overrides only constructor/serializeFundsFields/serializeTokenInfo/getTokenInfoFromBytes/getFundsFieldsFromBytes/validateNft — no getOutputsSum/calculateWeight/prepareToSend; repo-wide grep shows no other weight computation. (3) Shielded TCT is explicitly enabled: utils/transaction.ts:883 calls _attachShieldedHeaders(ctTx,...), :904 signature accepts Transaction | CreateTokenTransaction, :951 sets tx.shieldedOutputs; :996-1072 builds the MintHeader for the createToken path (isCreateToken at :1002, shielded outputs counted at :1035-1038, unconditional mint authorization at :1056). (4) Core (source of truth) does NOT include shielded values in weight: /tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor/transaction/base_transaction.py:366-368 sum_outputs iterates transparent outputs only; daa.py:203 minimum_tx_weight uses it; no shielded-aware override exists in core. (5) Core allows shielded TCTs and reconciles supply via the public MintHeader, not weight: hathor/verification/token_creation_transaction_verifier.py:45-59. (6) Leak vector confirmed: src/models/shielded_output.ts:54 documents value as "used for weight calculation. Not serialized on-chain" (deserialize yields 0n at :242), so the published on-chain weight is the only artifact derived from the plaintext shielded sum, and the formula at transaction.ts:352-354 is invertible for the amount given txSize. Caveats: resolution degrades for very large amounts (kTerm saturates at 4) and is masked when weight clamps to txMinWeight, but on the shielded testnet's low constants the amount is recoverable. The recommendation is also correct: fixing getOutputsSum/calculateWeight in the base Transaction automatically covers CreateTokenTransaction via inheritance; a shielded-TCT weight regression test would lock it in.

CONFIRMED real. getOutputsSum() folds shielded plaintext values into the weight sum at /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/models/transaction.ts:378-381 (this loop is NEW on the branch per `git diff master...HEAD`). That sum drives calculateWeight (transaction.ts:341 Number(getOutputsSum()) -> :348 amount -> :352-354 formula -> :357 Math.max -> :260 weight serialized as 8-byte float on the public graph struct). Core uses transparent-only sum: base_transaction.py:366-367 sum_outputs filters self.outputs only; shielded outputs live in separate self.shielded_outputs and never enter sum_outputs; daa.py:203 minimum_tx_weight uses tx.sum_outputs. Value is genuinely hidden in BOTH modes — hathorlib/transaction/shielded_tx_output.py:80-100 serializes only mode|commitment|range_proof|script|[asset_commitment|surjection_proof], no plaintext value; value recovered only via range-proof rewind (recover_shielded_secrets). Constants match (14/1.6/100: wallet constants.ts:229-232 == hathorlib settings.py:88-94). Node acceptance bound confirms fix safety: transaction_verifier.py:111-121 accepts [min-WEIGHT_TOL, min+MAX_TX_WEIGHT_DIFF=4.0]; kTerm bounded in (0,4) so transparent-only minimum passes. Real send path: utils/transaction.ts:945 passes so.value into ShieldedOutput, :950-951 sets tx.shieldedOutputs, headers attached (:883/:888) before prepareToSend->calculateWeight (sendTransaction.ts:681,738). The monotonic weight formula plus on-wire tx_size and public constants let an observer invert for amount=sum_outputs and subtract transparent outputs to recover the exact total shielded value. One caveat (does not refute): kTerm saturates near 4 for very large totals, reducing recovery precision there; leak is strongest for small-to-moderate amounts but full double precision makes realistic totals recoverable. Critical severity justified — defeats amount confidentiality at tx-total granularity.

CONFIRMED. (1) Leak exists: /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/models/transaction.ts:379-381 adds plaintext shielded values into getOutputsSum(), whose sole src consumer is calculateWeight() (:341,:348,:354); prepareToSend (:499-501) bakes it into this.weight, serialized at full float64 precision on the public struct (:260). Values are real plaintext on sends (src/utils/transaction.ts:945 `so.value`; prepareToSend called at :1428). (2) Core contradiction verified: /tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor/daa.py:203-206 uses tx.sum_outputs, defined transparent-only at hathor/transaction/base_transaction.py:366-368 (same on hathorlib side: hathorlib/daa.py:44, hathorlib/base_transaction.py:268-270) — the node never includes shielded values in weight. (3) Invertible & accepted: weight formula is monotonic in amount; size/constants public (node even publishes min_tx_weight_k via hathor/version_resource.py:128); float64 precision allows exact-to-the-cent recovery up to ~2e7 HTR. Node accepts: overshoot = kTerm_wallet - kTerm_node < 4, and max-weight check only activates when min > MAX_TX_WEIGHT_DIFF_ACTIVATION=32 (transaction_verifier.py:111-121, hathorlib/conf/settings.py:314-318); typical shielded min ~24-28. Clamp never masks it: 1.6*log2(6900)≈20.4 > txMinWeight 14 (constants.ts:229-233). (4) Mitigating nuance (does not refute): current shielded test networks set MIN_TX_WEIGHT_K: 0 (hathorlib/conf/testnet.yml:18, nano_testnet.yml:18, localnet.yml:20) and wallet-lib's k=0 branch (transaction.ts:350-352) makes kTerm constant — no leak there IF storage.version is loaded; but wallet-lib falls back to hardcoded k=100 when version data is absent, and mainnet inherits default MIN_TX_WEIGHT_K=100 (hathorlib/conf/settings.py:94, no override in hathor/conf/mainnet.py). Severity stays critical: silent, permanent, exact on-chain disclosure of the total shielded amount per tx, defeating the feature's core confidentiality promise; fix is trivial (drop the shieldedOutputs loop) and cannot break node acceptance since the wallet only ever overshot the transparent-only minimum.

CONFIRMED technically, severity reduced to medium. Every cited fact checks out: (1) CreateTokenTransaction extends Transaction with no weight override — src/models/create_token_transaction.ts:39; only constructor/serializeFundsFields/serializeTokenInfo/getTokenInfoFromBytes/getFundsFieldsFromBytes/createFromBytes/validateNft/createEmpty are defined. (2) Inherited leak is real: src/models/transaction.ts:378-381 sums plaintext shieldedOut.value into getOutputsSum; calculateWeight uses it at :341; prepareToSend at :499-501. Core computes minimum weight from transparent outputs only (/tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor/transaction/base_transaction.py:366-368 sum_outputs iterates only self.outputs; daa.py:203) and verify_weight (transaction_verifier.py:111-121) accepts any weight in [min, min+MAX_TX_WEIGHT_DIFF], so the wallet's inflated weight is accepted on-chain and is invertible to the total shielded value (weight, txSize, transparent sum all public). (3) TCT shielded wiring exists: utils/transaction.ts:872-884 calls _attachShieldedHeaders(ctTx, txData); :904 accepts Transaction|CreateTokenTransaction; :951-952 sets tx.shieldedOutputs; mint-header auto-attach has an explicit CREATE_TOKEN_TX_VERSION branch (~:995). Core allows shielded TCT with mandatory MintHeader (token_creation_transaction_verifier.py:46-60). SEVERITY ATTENUATION: no current high-level wallet flow emits a TCT with shielded outputs — src/utils/tokens.ts (prepareCreateTokenData) has zero shielded code; createNewToken (src/new/wallet.ts:2243-2313) resolves shielded addresses to transparent P2PKH (documented in __tests__/integration/shielded_outputs/token_creation.test.ts:10-12); the shielded-output builder exists only in src/new/sendTransaction.ts (regular tx). The TCT leak is reachable only by consumers hand-building IDataTx with version=CREATE_TOKEN_TX_VERSION + shieldedOutputs via the public createTransactionFromData + prepareToSend — a supported but currently unexercised path. The finding is also derivative of GAP1-01: fixing getOutputsSum/calculateWeight in the base Transaction class fixes TCT automatically (the finding itself concedes this); the only independent deliverable is a shielded-TCT weight regression test. Real bug, but as a standalone finding the marginal impact beyond GAP1-01 is latent-path + missing-test, hence medium rather than high.

findings.title: FEE-version token mint/melt/create contaminated by shielded UTXOs builds txs hathor-core deterministically rejects (per-entry FEE_PER_OUTPUT fold never funded; chargeable-input accounting diverges)
findings.description: tokens.ts computes FEE-token fees with transparent semantics only (Fee.calculate = FEE_PER_OUTPUT per chargeable token output, src/utils/fee.ts:65-92; calculateFeeForMintAndCreateToken tokens.ts:946-995) and selects HTR inputs equal to exactly depositAmount+feeAmount (tokens.ts:478-491 mint/create, 688-701 melt). When bestUtxoSelection pulls in any shielded UTXO (which it does by design, utxo.ts:102-113), prepareTransaction classifies the tx as shielded and core runs verify_shielded_balance with Rule M4 folding. For FEE-version tokens, core adds an EXTRA FEE_PER_OUTPUT HTR to the output side per Mint/Melt header entry, ON TOP of the FeeHeader entries which are also added to the output side. The wallet never funds nor declares this fold, so the Pedersen equation is short by FEE_PER_OUTPUT x num_entries -> ShieldedBalanceMismatchError after PoW is spent. A second independent divergence hits FEE-token melts whose melted inputs are shielded: wallet Fee.calculate counts shielded token inputs as chargeable ('melt without outputs' rule, fee.ts:81-87), while core's _get_token_info_from_inputs SKIPS shielded inputs entirely, so chargeable_inputs=0 and expected_fee=0 != declared FEE_PER_OUTPUT -> InputOutputMismatch. DEPOSIT-version flows happen to balance (deposit fold mirrors the wallet's implicit burn) and are integration-tested (S.0-S.4); FEE-version flows have zero shielded test coverage anywhere (grep 'TokenVersion.FEE' in __tests__/integration/shielded_outputs/ returns nothing).
findings.evidence: Wallet: /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/utils/tokens.ts:480 'const selectedUtxos = await utxoSelection(storage, NATIVE_TOKEN_UID, requiredAmount)' with requiredAmount = depositAmount + feeAmount only (line 478); melt HTR selection tokens.ts:691; melt fee via Fee.calculate tokens.ts:682; src/utils/fee.ts:85-89 'if (hasInputs && outputCount === 0) { fee += FEE_PER_OUTPUT; } fee += BigInt(outputCount) * FEE_PER_OUTPUT' counts shielded IDataInputs as hasInputs. Core: /tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor/verification/transaction_verifier.py:987-990 adds FeeHeader entries to transparent_outputs, AND :1096-1104 _fold_mint_melt_entry FEE branch 'transparent_outputs.append((self._settings.FEE_PER_OUTPUT, htr_uid))' per entry for BOTH mint and melt (pinned by hathor_tests/tx/test_shielded_mint_melt.py:966-1038, two entries => two charges); /tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor/transaction/transaction.py:429-431 'if resolved.mode() != OutputMode.TRANSPARENT: # Shielded input: skip for token info' so chargeable_inputs excludes shielded inputs, vs wallet fee.ts counting them; exactness check transaction_verifier.py:427-430 'expected_fee != token_dict.fees_from_fee_header -> InputOutputMismatch'. No FEE-token shielded coverage: grep TokenVersion.FEE over /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/__tests__/integration/shielded_outputs/*.test.ts is empty.
findings.recommendation: In prepareMintTxData/prepareMeltTxData/prepareCreateTokenData, detect whether any selected UTXO has utxo.shielded === true; if so and the token is FEE-version, add FEE_PER_OUTPUT per prospective Mint/Melt header entry to requiredAmount (and keep it OUT of the FeeHeader so the exactness check still passes), and align Fee.calculate's chargeable-input counting with core by excluding shielded inputs. Alternatively (simpler and safer) exclude shielded UTXOs from token-administration selection by default (see GAP3-02) so the contaminated path cannot arise. Add integration tests: FEE-version mint, melt and createToken funded by shielded HTR and melting shielded FEE-token UTXOs.

findings.title: Token-administration flows silently force-deshield confidential funds with no opt-out: the shielded UTXO filter is dead code and wallet.ts never plumbs utxoSelection
findings.description: bestUtxoSelection and fastUtxoSelection select from the union of transparent and shielded UTXOs with no preference and no way to exclude shielded ones (the IUtxoFilterOptions.shielded flag exists and is implemented in MemoryStore but has ZERO call sites in src/). The token-admin entry points (mintTokens HTR deposit, meltTokens token-amount and HTR deposit/fee, createNewToken deposit) therefore can silently spend confidential UTXOs to fund an incidental 1% deposit or fee. The resulting full-unshield publishes the excess blinding scalar in the UnshieldBalanceHeader; with a single shielded input that scalar is the input's effective blinding factor, fully opening its Pedersen commitment — e.g. a 1 HTR token-creation deposit funded by a 1000 HTR FullShielded UTXO permanently reveals that the UTXO was worth 1000 HTR, plus address/amount linkage via the transparent change. hathor-core explicitly accepts this leak and delegates the hygiene decision to wallets (unshield_balance_header.py docstring; client guide: privacy hygiene is wallet territory). Unlike sends (where the user at least initiated a transfer), token admin ops give no signal that confidential funds will be consumed, and the public wallet API offers no escape hatch: tokens.ts accepts a utxoSelection override (tokens.ts:381, 578) but wallet.ts mintOptions/meltOptions/createToken options do not forward it, and no option exposes the shielded:false filter.
findings.evidence: Wallet: /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/utils/utxo.ts:108-113 options for bestUtxoSelection = {token, authorities, only_available_utxos, order_by_value} — no shielded filter (fastUtxoSelection identical, :56-62); src/types.ts:606-610 declares 'shielded?: boolean // false -> only transparent UTXOs'; src/storage/memory_store.ts:824-827 implements it; grep 'shielded: false|options.shielded' over src/ shows the only references are the memory_store implementation itself — no caller. wallet.ts:2495-2503 (mintOptions), 2609-2615 (meltOptions), 2299-2308 (createToken options) omit utxoSelection even though tokens.ts:381/578 accept it. Privacy consequence anchored in core: /tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor/transaction/headers/unshield_balance_header.py:42-47 (single-input excess == that input's blinding factor; leak accepted, hygiene left to wallets) and ground-truth §10 'Unshield privacy leak'. User-project rule 'Explicit options over inference' (feedback_explicit_options_over_inference) and 'Shielded unblinding scope' both point the same way.
findings.recommendation: Default token-administration selection to shielded:false (transparent-only) and surface an explicit opt-in (e.g. allowShieldedInputs / spendShielded option on MintTokensOptions, MeltTokensOptions, CreateTokenOptions) that switches the selector to the union, so deshielding confidential funds is always a conscious caller decision. At minimum, plumb utxoSelection through wallet.ts so integrators can supply a shielded-excluding selector, and wire the existing dead shielded filter into bestUtxoSelection/fastUtxoSelection.

findings.title: prepareTransaction's unshield fallback silently skips shielded inputs with missing UTXO record or missing blindingFactor, producing node-rejected txs where SendTransaction throws
findings.description: The excess-blinding-factor fallback that all token-admin flows rely on (prepareTransaction, transaction.ts:1338-1422) iterates txData.inputs and silently 'continue's when storage.getUtxo returns null (line 1361) or when a shielded UTXO lacks blindingFactor (line 1363). If every shielded input is skipped, no UnshieldBalanceHeader is attached at all and core rejects with 'a full-unshield tx ... must carry an unshield balance header'; if only some are skipped, the excess scalar is wrong and core rejects with ShieldedBalanceMismatchError — in both cases after PoW is already spent. The same condition in SendTransaction.prepareTxData throws a clear SendTxError ('Shielded input ... is missing blindingFactor — cannot satisfy the homomorphic balance equation') before any mining. The bare-record condition is not hypothetical: the codebase's own comment in processMetadataChanged documents that a metadata update used to overwrite a shielded UTXO with a record 'missing shielded: true / blindingFactor' and names exactly this fullnode rejection as the consequence; that write path was fixed, but the fallback still degrades silently instead of failing fast, and any future store path (or third-party IStore implementation) that drops the blinding fields reintroduces the post-PoW failure for mint/melt/createToken.
findings.evidence: Wallet: /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/utils/transaction.ts:1360-1363 'const utxo = await storage.getUtxo(...); if (!utxo) continue; if (utxo.shielded) { if (!utxo.blindingFactor) continue;' vs src/new/sendTransaction.ts:471-477 'if (!utxo.blindingFactor) { throw new SendTxError(`Shielded input ${inp.txId}:${inp.index} is missing blindingFactor ...`) }'. Self-documenting failure mode: src/utils/storage.ts:676-681 'overwrites a correctly-saved shielded UTXO with a bare record missing `shielded: true` / `blindingFactor`. The next send then can't compute the excess blinding factor ... and the fullnode rejects with "full-unshield tx ... must carry an unshield balance header"'. Core rejection: /tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor/verification/transaction_verifier.py:1038-1042 (header required) and :1048-1060 (ShieldedBalanceMismatchError).
findings.recommendation: Mirror SendTransaction's behavior in the prepareTransaction fallback: throw a descriptive error when an input's UTXO record cannot be found or a shielded record lacks its blindingFactor (and assetBlindingFactor for FullShielded), instead of continuing. This converts a guaranteed post-PoW node rejection into an immediate, actionable local error for all token-administration flows.

findings.title: Premise corrections: token flows DO route through the unshield fallback, Mint/Melt headers ARE declared, and DEPOSIT-version shielded token ops are integration-tested
findings.description: Recording confirmations so the suspected total gap is not double-reported. (a) All five entry points were traced: mintTokens (wallet.ts:2504-2512), meltTokens (:2616-2626), createNewToken (:2293-2312) build IDataTx via tokens.ts and then call transactionUtils.prepareTransaction, whose fallback (transaction.ts:1338-1422) computes the excess blinding factor for shielded-input/no-shielded-output txs and sets txData.excessBlindingFactor BEFORE createTransactionFromData runs; delegateAuthority (:2720-2728) and destroyAuthority (:2841-2842) spend only authority UTXOs, which can never be shielded (core forbids shielded authorities, ShieldedAuthorityError), so they stay transparent. (b) The isShieldedTx gate in _attachShieldedHeaders (transaction.ts:993-995) keys on shieldedOutputs OR excessBlindingFactor — and because the fallback sets excessBlindingFactor first, the gate fires for contaminated token ops and the Mint/Melt declaration logic (transaction.ts:996-1076) runs, producing entries that satisfy core's verify_no_undeclared_mint_melt, Rule M1 (Unshield header present), Rule M2 (transparent authority inputs present), the TCT one-entry/token_index==1 rule, and ascending header order Fee(0x11)<Unshield(0x13)<Mint(0x14)<Melt(0x15). The wallet's per-token delta correctly counts shielded input values (known to the wallet) and authority inputs/outputs are correctly excluded; for DEPOSIT tokens the core deposit/withdraw folds mirror the wallet's implicit HTR burn/withdraw output, so the balance equation closes. (c) Integration coverage exists and exercises exactly these paths against a real node: __tests__/integration/shielded_outputs/mint_melt_shielded.test.ts S.0 (createNewToken funded by ONLY shielded HTR), S.2/S.3 (melt FS/AS-held custom-token UTXOs), S.4 (mintTokens with shielded HTR funding the deposit), plus token_creation.test.ts. The real residual gaps are the FEE-version divergences (GAP3-01), the consent/opt-out gap (GAP3-02), and the silent-skip robustness gap (GAP3-03).
findings.evidence: Wallet: /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/new/wallet.ts:2510, 2624, 2310 all call transactionUtils.prepareTransaction; /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/utils/transaction.ts:1347-1421 (fallback sets txData.excessBlindingFactor), :1425 createTransactionFromData, :883/:888 _attachShieldedHeaders for TCT and regular tx, :1011-1075 delta computation and header construction. Core counterparts: /tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor/verification/transaction_verifier.py:581-611 (verify_no_undeclared_mint_melt), :672-691 (Rule M1), :1144-1202 (Rule M2 + TCT exemption); hathor/verification/token_creation_transaction_verifier.py:45-56 (TCT MintHeader rule). Tests: /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/__tests__/integration/shielded_outputs/mint_melt_shielded.test.ts:55,99,147,196,244.
findings.recommendation: No action beyond GAP3-01/02/03. When fixing those, keep the S.0-S.4 suite green and extend it with the FEE-version scenarios.

result.notes: CONFIRMED. (1) Silent skip verified verbatim at /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/utils/transaction.ts:1360-1363 ('if (!utxo) continue;' / 'if (!utxo.blindingFactor) continue;'). (2) Asymmetric strict path verified at src/new/sendTransaction.ts:471-477 (throws SendTxError for the identical missing-blindingFactor condition). (3) Reachability verified: prepareCreateNewToken (src/new/wallet.ts:2243→2310), prepareMintTokensData (:2450→2510), prepareMeltTokensData (:2566→2624), prepareDelegateAuthorityData (:2681→2728), prepareDestroyAuthorityData (:2797→2842) all call prepareTransaction directly, bypassing SendTransaction's check; the fallback's own comment (transaction.ts:1338-1340) confirms these flows depend on it. UnshieldBalanceHeader is attached only when excessBlindingFactor is set (transaction.ts:961-969); no other validation layer exists (exhaustive grep). (4) Core consequences verified: /tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor/verification/transaction_verifier.py:1038-1042 (header required for full-unshield) and :1048-1060 (ShieldedBalanceMismatchError on wrong excess) — both post-PoW rejections. (5) The bare-record trigger is documented in the codebase's own comment at src/utils/storage.ts:673-681, naming the exact fullnode error. Caveat keeping this medium not high: the known internal write-path trigger (processMetadataChanged) was already fixed, so today the condition requires a custom IStore or future regression — it is a fail-silent latent inconsistency, not an active bug. Recommendation (mirror SendTransaction's throw in the fallback) is sound.

CONFIRMED on every claim. (1) Selectors select from the union with no shielded filter: src/utils/utxo.ts:56-62 (fastUtxoSelection) and :108-113 (bestUtxoSelection) build IUtxoFilterOptions with only token/authorities/target_amount/only_available_utxos/order_by_value; the inline comment at utxo.ts:102 literally says "Select any UTXO (transparent or shielded)". (2) IUtxoFilterOptions.shielded (src/types.ts:606-610) is implemented only in src/storage/memory_store.ts:824-827 and has zero call sites in src/ — dead code; Storage.selectUtxos (src/storage/storage.ts:529-556) and matchBalanceSelection (storage.ts:580-590) never set it. (3) wallet.ts does not forward utxoSelection: mintOptions src/new/wallet.ts:2495-2503, meltOptions :2609-2615, createToken options :2299-2308 omit it even though tokens.ts accepts it (src/utils/tokens.ts:381, 578) and uses it for HTR deposit/fee selection (:480, :629, :691); MintTokensOptions/MeltTokensOptions/CreateTokenOptions (src/new/types.ts:157-168, 183-194, 411-426) expose no utxoSelection or shielded opt-out. (4) The silent full-unshield path is not only possible but explicitly engineered: src/utils/transaction.ts:1338-1421 (prepareTransaction) detects shielded inputs in token-admin txs ("notably createNewToken" per its own comment) and silently attaches the UnshieldBalanceHeader excess — no warning, no error, no opt-out. Shielded UTXOs sit in the same spendable pool (src/utils/storage.ts:700-717). (5) Core source of truth confirms hygiene is delegated to wallets: /tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor/transaction/headers/unshield_balance_header.py:42-46. Minor nuance: the core docstring notes the amount reveal in a full unshield comes primarily from the transparent outputs themselves (the single-input scalar adds "nothing previously-private"), so the finding's "scalar fully opens the commitment" framing is slightly overstated in isolation — but the claimed net outcome (confidential amount made public + address linkage, triggered silently by a 1% deposit) is accurate. Severity high is appropriate: the branch's entire purpose is confidentiality, and token-admin ops can permanently de-anonymize a large shielded UTXO with no API escape hatch; not critical since no funds are lost and txs remain valid.

All cited evidence verified. (1) Dead filter: IUtxoFilterOptions.shielded declared at src/types.ts:606-610, implemented only at src/storage/memory_store.ts:825-826; exhaustive grep over src/ shows zero call sites (storage.ts:712/1143 hits are saved-UTXO record fields, not filters). (2) Selection union: fastUtxoSelection (src/utils/utxo.ts:56-62) and bestUtxoSelection (:108-113) build options without shielded; comment at :102-107 documents shielded inputs are deliberately selectable. (3) No plumbing: tokens.ts accepts utxoSelection (src/utils/tokens.ts:381, :578; used at :480, :629, :691) but src/new/wallet.ts never forwards it — mintOptions :2495-2503, meltOptions :2609-2615, createToken options :2299-2308 all omit it; MintTokensOptions/MeltTokensOptions/CreateTokenOptions in src/new/types.ts have no such field; grep 'utxoSelection' in wallet.ts is empty. Send path DOES expose utxoSelectionMethod (src/new/sendTransaction.ts:1132), so token admin is uniquely without an escape hatch. (4) Consequence confirmed and self-documented: src/utils/transaction.ts:1338-1419 attaches UnshieldBalanceHeader with excess=sum(r_in) for shielded-input/transparent-only txs, with a comment explicitly naming createNewToken as the path it exists for. Core accepts the leak and delegates hygiene to wallets (/tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor/transaction/headers/unshield_balance_header.py:34-47). One refinement: per the core docstring (:42-46) the published scalar is not the marginal leak — the full-unshield's transparent outputs already expose the amount; the harm is the silent, irreversible full unshield itself (amount + transparent-change address linkage revealed). That refines mechanism wording, not impact. Realistic scenario stands: value-ordered, shielded-agnostic selection funds a 1% mint deposit from a large confidential UTXO with no signal, no opt-out, no public-API workaround. High severity appropriate for a privacy feature.

Every factual claim in this premise-correction finding checks out against the code. (a) Entry points: createNewToken (/tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/new/wallet.ts:~2293-2312), mintTokens (~2616-2628), meltTokens (~2615-2627), delegateAuthority (~2720-2729), and destroyAuthority (~2841-2842) all build IDataTx via tokens.ts and call transactionUtils.prepareTransaction — confirmed by direct read. (b) The full-unshield fallback at /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/utils/transaction.ts:1347-1421 scans inputs for shielded UTXOs, computes the excess via cryptoProvider.computeBalancingBlindingFactor (:1413-1418), and sets txData.excessBlindingFactor (:1420) BEFORE createTransactionFromData runs at :1425; createTransactionFromData calls _attachShieldedHeaders at :883 (TCT branch) and :888 (regular tx). (c) The isShieldedTx gate at transaction.ts:993-995 keys on `(txData.shieldedOutputs && length > 0) || !!txData.excessBlindingFactor`, so contaminated token ops do enter the Mint/Melt declaration block (:996-1076). The delta computation counts shielded output values (:1035-1038) and shielded input values via txData.inputs (:1039-1047, wallet-known values), excludes authority inputs/outputs (:1031, :1041-1044), gates mint on authority-or-TCT (:1056) and melt on melt authority (:1057), uses tokenIndex = tokensArray.indexOf+1 (:1060,:1065), and pushes Mint/Melt headers last (:1071,:1074) after ShieldedOutputsHeader (:952) and UnshieldBalanceHeader (:969), with FeeHeader arriving via txData.headers in the constructor (:865) — so the ascending order Fee(0x11)<Shielded(0x12)<Unshield(0x13)<Mint(0x14)<Melt(0x15) holds. (d) Core counterparts confirmed: /tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor/verification/transaction_verifier.py:581-612 verify_no_undeclared_mint_melt, :672-691 Rule M1 (requires ShieldedOutputsHeader OR UnshieldBalanceHeader — satisfied by the fallback-set excess), :1144-1202 Rule M2 with explicit TCT exemption for token_index==1; token_creation_transaction_verifier.py:45-56 requires exactly one MintHeader entry with token_index=1 for shielded TCTs — the wallet's NEW_TOKEN_KEY sentinel + tokenIndex remap (:1001-1009) satisfies this. Authority-can't-be-shielded confirmed by verify_authority_restriction (transaction_verifier.py:~1204), so delegate/destroyAuthority (authority-only inputs) correctly stay transparent. (e) Tests confirmed at exactly the cited lines: __tests__/integration/shielded_outputs/mint_melt_shielded.test.ts S.0:55, S.1:99, S.2:147, S.3:196, S.4:244, plus token_creation.test.ts in the same dir. The finding's conclusion — no mint/melt gap to report; residual issues are GAP3-01/02/03 — is supported. Severity info is correct for a premise-correction/confirmation entry.

CONFIRMED. (1) Selectors have no shielded exclusion: src/utils/utxo.ts:56-62 and :108-113 build options without `shielded`; the comment at utxo.ts:102-107 explicitly documents selecting "any UTXO (transparent or shielded)". Storage.selectUtxos (src/storage/storage.ts:529-557) adds no default filter. (2) Dead code confirmed: src/types.ts:606-610 declares the filter, src/storage/memory_store.ts:825-826 implements it, and an exhaustive grep of src/ shows zero callers ever set `shielded: true/false` in filter options (utils/storage.ts:712,1143 are UTXO-record writes that put shielded UTXOs INTO the selectable store, strengthening the finding). (3) Plumbing gap confirmed: tokens.ts:381 and :578 accept `utxoSelection`, but wallet.ts mintOptions (2495-2503), meltOptions (2609-2615) and prepareCreateNewToken options (2299-2308) omit it, and MintTokensOptions/MeltTokensOptions/CreateTokenOptions (src/new/types.ts:157,183,411) expose no related knob — unlike sends, which expose utxoSelectionMethod (sendTransaction.ts:1132). (4) The silent deshield completes by design: src/utils/transaction.ts:1338-1419 auto-detects shielded inputs in token-admin txData (comment names createNewToken explicitly), computes the excess scalar, and attaches UnshieldBalanceHeader (:955-969), so the op succeeds with no warning. Core accepts the leak (unshield_balance_header.py:34-47). One mechanism nuance: per the core docstring, the published scalar reveals nothing beyond what the full-unshield's transparent outputs already expose — the 1000 HTR value disclosure comes from the transparent change itself, not uniquely from the scalar; the reviewer's "fully opening its Pedersen commitment" framing is slightly overstated but the privacy outcome (silent, irreversible value+address disclosure of confidential funds with no opt-out) is exactly as claimed. Severity high is appropriate for a privacy feature whose token-admin paths silently negate confidentiality.

CONFIRMED on every element. (1) Contamination path real: bestUtxoSelection deliberately includes shielded UTXOs (src/utils/utxo.ts:102-113) and tokens.ts has no shielded exclusion; mint/create selects HTR for exactly depositAmount+feeAmount (src/utils/tokens.ts:478-480), melt likewise (tokens.ts:682, 688-701). (2) The contaminated tx really becomes shielded WITH mint/melt headers: prepareTransaction auto-computes the excess blinding factor for shielded inputs (src/utils/transaction.ts:1348-1421 -> UnshieldBalanceHeader) and _attachShieldedHeaders auto-declares MintHeader/MeltHeader from authority inputs + token delta (transaction.ts:996-1075), so core's Rule M4 fold triggers. (3) Core charges an extra per-entry FEE_PER_OUTPUT in HTR on the output side for FEE-version tokens on BOTH mint and melt (/tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor/verification/transaction_verifier.py:1096-1107 _fold_mint_melt_entry FEE branch; pinned by hathor_tests/tx/test_shielded_mint_melt.py:965-1037). The wallet never funds it (grep FEE_PER_OUTPUT over src/ shows no shielded-aware adjustment), and the excess scalar rides on G so it cannot absorb an H_HTR amount shortfall -> ShieldedBalanceMismatchError after PoW. (4) Second divergence confirmed: wallet Fee.calculate counts shielded token inputs as chargeable (src/utils/fee.ts:84-89; getDataInputFromUtxo at helpers.ts:619-628 gives them token/value/authorities=0) while core skips shielded inputs for token info (/tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor/transaction/transaction.py:427-431 'Shielded input: skip for token info'), and the fee check is exact-equality (transaction_verifier.py:427-431 InputOutputMismatch), so a full melt of shielded FEE-token UTXOs declares FEE_PER_OUTPUT vs expected 0 -> rejected. (5) Test gap confirmed: grep TokenVersion.FEE over __tests__/integration/shielded_outputs/ is empty; the only 'fee' mentions there are FS shielded-output fees, not FEE-version tokens (DEPOSIT-version mint/melt is covered in mint_melt_shielded.test.ts, masking the bug). Severity high stands: deterministic consensus-divergent rejection (wasted PoW, broken token-admin flows) whenever a FEE-version token operation pulls in any shielded UTXO, though no direct fund loss.

findings.title: Mint/Melt auto-declaration delta includes shielded values while core's undeclared-mint/melt check is transparent-only — authority input riding with a shielding move builds an unfixable, node-rejected tx instead of being refused
findings.description: The heuristic computes per-token delta over BOTH transparent and shielded sides (outputs add, inputs subtract) and declares a Mint/Melt header only when that all-sides delta is nonzero and a matching authority input is spent. hathor-core's `verify_no_undeclared_mint_melt` triggers on the TRANSPARENT-ONLY token_dict surplus/deficit (shielded inputs/outputs contribute nothing to token_dict) whenever the tx spends the matching authority. The two disagree exactly when an authority input rides along with a supply-preserving shielding move: e.g. transparent 100 X in -> FullShielded 100 X out, plus a melt-authority input of X (consolidating/destroying an authority in the same tx). Wallet: delta = +100 - 100 = 0 -> declares nothing. Core: transparent amount = -100, can_melt=true, has_been_melted -> demands a MeltHeader entry for X -> ShieldedMintMeltForbiddenError. Worse, NO valid construction exists: declaring melt 100 would add (100, X) to the output side of the Pedersen balance equation that the shielded output already balances, so the equation fails (and the heuristic's all-sides amount is the only one that satisfies verify_shielded_balance). The only correct behavior is to refuse such input sets before signing/PoW; the wallet has no such guard and ships a doomed tx. Reachability: blocked in the high-level send flow (checkUnspentInput rejects authority inputs, sendTransaction.ts:1287-1295), but `transactionUtils.prepareTransaction` is the public low-level API used by headless/custom-txData consumers, and the heuristic exists precisely to serve combined authority+shielded txs (it correctly serves the mint/melt flows when shielded UTXOs ride along).
findings.evidence: wallet-lib /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/utils/transaction.ts:1029-1047 — delta includes shielded outputs (`for (const so of txData.shieldedOutputs ?? []) { ... bumpDelta(so.token, so.value); }`, lines 1035-1038) and shielded inputs (line 1046, txData.inputs carry decoded shielded UTXO value/token); entries emitted only on all-sides delta sign at 1058-1068. The in-code rationale (lines 1021-1028) argues only about the BALANCE equation, not core's presence check. Core: /tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor/verification/transaction_verifier.py:581-611 — `verify_no_undeclared_mint_melt(tx, token_dict)` raises ShieldedMintMeltForbiddenError when `token_info.can_melt and token_info.has_been_melted() and token_uid not in melt_token_uids` where token_dict is transparent-only: /tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor/transaction/transaction.py:471 ('shielded inputs contribute nothing to token_dict'). Balance contradiction for any declared amount: transaction_verifier.py:992-1019 + 1062-1086 fold the entry's amount onto the commitment sums that the shielded output already balances. Authority-input block in the send flow: /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/new/sendTransaction.ts:1287-1295.
findings.recommendation: In `_attachShieldedHeaders`, additionally compute the transparent-only per-token delta (mirroring core's token_dict: transparent non-authority outputs minus transparent non-authority inputs). If a token has a transparent deficit with a melt authority spent (or surplus with mint authority) but the all-sides delta is zero or of the opposite sign, throw a descriptive error telling the caller to remove the authority input from the shielding tx (no consensus-valid construction exists). Emit headers only when both deltas agree in direction, keeping the all-sides amount (which is what verify_shielded_balance needs).

findings.title: FEE-version token mint/melt with any shielded input builds node-rejected txs: the per-entry FEE_PER_OUTPUT fold (core Rule M4, commit 040674bc) is never funded, and the wallet's FeeHeader counts shielded inputs that core's token_dict excludes
findings.description: When `mintTokens`/`meltTokens` runs while the wallet holds shielded UTXOs, utxo selection freely picks them (bestUtxoSelection/fastUtxoSelection set no `shielded` filter and the inclusion is deliberate per utxo.ts:102-107; memory_store passes shielded UTXOs when the option is undefined). The prepareTransaction fallback then computes the excess scalar and `_attachShieldedHeaders` auto-declares the Mint/MeltHeader — this works end-to-end for DEPOSIT-version tokens (integration tests S.2-S.4 prove it). For FEE-version tokens it is doomed in two independent ways. (a) Unfunded fold: core's `_fold_mint_melt_entry` adds one FEE_PER_OUTPUT of HTR to the OUTPUT side of the augmented balance equation for every Mint/Melt entry on a FEE token (added by core commit 040674bc, 2026-04-27). No wallet code funds it: tokens.ts selects HTR inputs covering only `depositAmount + feeAmount` (the FeeHeader), and the excess computation's output entries cover only real outputs + FeeHeader entries — the equation is short one FEE_PER_OUTPUT, so `verify_balance` returns false -> ShieldedBalanceMismatchError after signing and PoW. (b) Header-amount divergence: core checks FeeHeader EXACTNESS (`expected_fee == fees_from_fee_header`) against the transparent-only token_dict, while wallet `Fee.calculate` counts shielded IDataInputs (e.g. melting FS-held FEE-X with no X change: wallet charges the flat FEE_PER_OUTPUT for chargeable inputs; core sees zero chargeable inputs/outputs and expects 0) -> InputOutputMismatch. Either way the wallet builds, signs and mines a tx the node must reject; nothing refuses or adjusts up front. No FEE-version coverage exists in __tests__/integration/shielded_outputs/mint_melt_shielded.test.ts (all tokens are DEPOSIT-version).
findings.evidence: Core fold: /tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor/verification/transaction_verifier.py:1062-1105, FEE branch at 1096-1105 ('FEE-version tokens add FEE_PER_OUTPUT HTR on the output side for BOTH mint and melt'); introduced by commit 040674bc 'feat(shielded): charge FEE_PER_OUTPUT per Mint/Melt entry on FEE tokens'. Exactness check: transaction_verifier.py:407-430 (`expected_fee != token_dict.fees_from_fee_header -> InputOutputMismatch`) with token_dict transparent-only (/tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor/transaction/transaction.py:471) and calculate_fee semantics at /tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor/transaction/token_info.py:61-92. Wallet: zero FEE_PER_OUTPUT handling tied to header entries (grep over src/utils/transaction.ts and src/utils/tokens.ts: no hits); HTR funding = deposit+header fee only (/tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/utils/tokens.ts:478-491 mint, 682-695 melt); excess output entries = outputs + FeeHeader only (/tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/utils/transaction.ts:1388-1418); Fee.calculate counts shielded inputs uniformly (/tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/utils/fee.ts:43-89, flat charge at 85-87). Shielded UTXOs selectable by token flows: /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/utils/utxo.ts:56-62 and 102-113 (no `shielded` filter), /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/storage/memory_store.ts:825-826 (undefined -> include), used at tokens.ts:480 (mint HTR), 629 (melt token), 691 (melt HTR). DEPOSIT-only test coverage: /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/__tests__/integration/shielded_outputs/mint_melt_shielded.test.ts:99-244.
findings.recommendation: When `_attachShieldedHeaders` emits a Mint/MeltHeader entry for a FEE-version token, the pipeline must (1) fund one extra FEE_PER_OUTPUT of HTR per entry (extra HTR input selection or change reduction) so the Rule-M4 fold balances, and (2) compute the FeeHeader amount from core's transparent token_dict view (exclude shielded inputs/outputs from chargeable counts). Short term: detect FEE-version tokens in the heuristic and throw a clear error refusing shielded-input mint/melt of FEE tokens until both are implemented. Add FEE-version cases to the mint/melt integration suite.

findings.title: tokens[] privacy filter excludes authority outputs, so a token represented only by an authority output + FullShielded value outputs serializes the authority output with token_data 0x80 (an HTR authority output)
findings.description: `tokensWithVisibleOutput` skips authority outputs (line 597), so a tx carrying an authority output of token X whose only value outputs of X are FullShielded would drop X from `tokens[]`.
