But the authority output still needs an on-wire token_data index: `getTokenDataFromOutput` computes `tokensWithoutHathor.indexOf(output.token) + 1` which yields -1 + 1 = 0 for the missing token, then ORs the authority mask — producing token_data 0x80, i.e. an authority output over HTR. Core resolves token_data & 0x7F = 0 to HTR, and HTR authorities are consensus-invalid (token_dict construction/_check_token_permissions assert NATIVE has no authorities), so the tx is rejected — or worse, the output silently references the wrong token. The exclusion also buys zero privacy: an authority output's wire token_data must name the token anyway, and core's unused-token check explicitly counts ALL transparent outputs (including authorities) as using their token index, so listing X is both required and harmless. Currently latent: the send flow only creates authorities:0n outputs and rejects authority inputs, and token flows don't build shielded outputs — but `prepareTransaction`/`createTransactionFromData` are public APIs and any future flow combining authorities with FullShielded outputs trips it with no error raised wallet-side.
findings.evidence: wallet-lib: /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/new/sendTransaction.ts:594-598 — `if ((out.authorities ?? 0n) !== 0n) continue;` excludes authority outputs from the visible set; tokens filtered at line 613. /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/utils/transaction.ts:788-794 — `const tokenIndex = tokensWithoutHathor.indexOf(output.token) + 1; ... return tokenIndex | TOKEN_AUTHORITY_MASK;` with no indexOf===-1 guard (yields 0|0x80). Core: /tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor/verification/transaction_verifier.py:487-489 — `for txout in tx.outputs: seen_token_indexes.add(txout.get_token_index())` (authority outputs count as token usage, so including the token is consensus-required, not a leak); transparent token semantics: token index 0 = HTR, and NATIVE token asserts no mint/melt authorities (transaction_verifier.py:447-452).
findings.recommendation: Treat authority outputs as 'visible' in the tokens[] filter (their token is already public on the wire), i.e. delete the authorities exclusion at sendTransaction.ts:597. Independently, make `getTokenDataFromOutput` throw when `indexOf` returns -1 for a non-HTR token instead of silently emitting token_data 0 — that converts any future recurrence of this class of bug into an immediate local error.

findings.title: prepareTransaction's excess-blinding-factor fallback silently skips shielded inputs with missing UTXO or missing blindingFactor, producing an unbalanced unshield header instead of a local error
findings.description: The fallback that computes the UnshieldBalanceHeader excess for txs not built via SendTransaction.prepareTxData (mint/melt/createToken flows — the very paths that feed the Mint/Melt auto-declaration heuristic) iterates txData.inputs and does `if (!utxo) continue;` and `if (!utxo.blindingFactor) continue;`. A shielded input whose UTXO record is absent from storage, or present without its blinding factor (e.g. partially-migrated storage, re-synced wallet that decoded the output without persisting blindings), is silently dropped from the scalar sum. The resulting excess does not satisfy sum(r_in) - sum(r_out), so the fullnode rejects the tx (ShieldedBalanceMismatchError) after the wallet has signed and mined it. The parallel code in SendTransaction.prepareTxData treats the same condition as a hard error ('Shielded input ... is missing blindingFactor — cannot satisfy the homomorphic balance equation'), which is the correct behavior. Additionally, when ALL shielded inputs lack blinding factors the fallback computes no excess at all (shieldedInputs.length === 0), so `_attachShieldedHeaders` sees isShieldedTx=false and also skips the mandatory Mint/MeltHeader declaration — compounding into multiple consensus violations from one silently-degraded storage state.
findings.evidence: wallet-lib: /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/utils/transaction.ts:1360-1363 — `const utxo = await storage.getUtxo(...); if (!utxo) continue; if (utxo.shielded) { if (!utxo.blindingFactor) continue; ... }` vs the throwing equivalent at /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/new/sendTransaction.ts:471-477. Downstream gate that also degrades: /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/utils/transaction.ts:993-996 (isShieldedTx keys on shieldedOutputs/excessBlindingFactor only). Core rejection: /tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor/verification/transaction_verifier.py:1020-1060 (shielded inputs with no shielded outputs require the header and the excess must close the equation; verify_balance false -> ShieldedBalanceMismatchError).
findings.recommendation: Mirror SendTransaction's behavior: throw when an input is shielded (input.index addresses a shielded slot of the parent / utxo.shielded) but the stored UTXO or its blindingFactor is missing. Never silently exclude a shielded input from the excess computation.

findings.title: Melt-from-shielded with createAnotherMelt=false and no token change is unrelayable: the MeltHeader forces the token into tokens[] but header entries and FullShielded outputs do not count as token usage (UnusedTokensError); wallet has no guard
findings.description: Core's mempool-hardened `verify_tokens` requires every entry of tokens[] to be referenced by some output token_data — transparent outputs (including authorities) and AmountShielded outputs count; Mint/MeltHeader entries and FullShielded outputs do NOT. A MeltHeader entry, however, must reference the token via token_index into tokens[], so the token cannot be dropped. Therefore melting an exact amount of a FullShielded-held token with `createAnotherMelt: false` (no X change output, no X authority output, withdraw paid in HTR) produces tokens=[X] with zero X-referencing outputs -> UnusedTokensError at relay, after signing/PoW. prepareMeltTxData always sets tokens=[X] and nothing in the wallet detects or avoids the configuration (the default createAnotherMelt=true authority output is what accidentally saves the common case). The transparent melt-all shape shares the unused-tokens exposure (the core rule predates the shielded branch), but the shielded variant is strictly worse: dropping X from tokens[] is not even theoretically possible because the MeltHeader requires it listed, so the wallet must either add an X-referencing output or refuse.
findings.evidence: wallet-lib: /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/utils/tokens.ts:593 (`const tokensArray = [authorityMeltInput.token];` unconditional), 643-655 (X change only when foundAmount > amount), 657-667 (authority output only when createAnotherMelt); MeltHeader emission keyed on tokensArray membership at /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/utils/transaction.ts:1014-1019, 1063-1068. Core: /tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor/verification/transaction_verifier.py:486-504 — seen_token_indexes built from tx.outputs token indexes + AmountShielded token_data only ('Consider shielded output token indexes' counts AmountShieldedOutput, not FullShieldedOutput, not header entries) -> UnusedTokensError; MeltHeader token_index must point into tx.tokens (transaction_verifier.py:634-662, hathorlib mint_melt_header bounds).
findings.recommendation: In prepareMeltTxData, when the selected token inputs include shielded UTXOs (the MeltHeader path) and the tx would end up with no X-referencing output (no change and createAnotherMelt=false), either fail fast with a clear message or keep the melt-authority change output mandatory for shielded melts.

findings.title: FeeHeader for FEE-version tokens is computed over phantom shielded outputs and shielded inputs, diverging from core's transparent-only exact-fee equation — every shielded send of a FEE-version token is built and then rejected
findings.description: Discovered while auditing the mint/melt fee folds: in prepareTxData, `Fee.calculate` runs over `partialOutputs`, which still contains the phantom transparent stand-ins for every requested shielded output (phantoms are removed only at lines 428-433, after the fee calc), and over inputs that may include shielded UTXOs. For a FEE-version token X this charges FEE_PER_OUTPUT per shielded X output and counts shielded X inputs as chargeable. hathor-core computes `expected_fee = token_dict.calculate_fee(settings, shielded_fee)` over the TRANSPARENT-ONLY token_dict (shielded outputs live in the header, shielded inputs contribute nothing) plus the flat per-shielded-output fee (1 AS / 2 FS) — and enforces strict EQUALITY against the FeeHeader for shielded txs. Example: send 100 FEE-X into two FullShielded outputs, exact transparent X inputs, no change: wallet FeeHeader = 2*FEE_PER_OUTPUT (phantoms) + 2*FEE_PER_FULL_SHIELDED_OUTPUT; core expected = 1*FEE_PER_OUTPUT (flat chargeable-inputs rule, zero chargeable outputs) + 2*FEE_PER_FULL_SHIELDED_OUTPUT -> InputOutputMismatch ('Fee amount is different than expected'). The mismatch is structural — overpayment is not allowed on the token-side equation — so every FEE-token shielded send (and FEE-token unshield) is signed, mined and rejected. DEPOSIT-version tokens are unaffected (no per-output fee), which is why the existing shielded integration suites (all DEPOSIT tokens) pass.
findings.evidence: wallet-lib: /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/new/sendTransaction.ts:248-257 (phantom outputs pushed into txData.outputs), 338-346 (`Fee.calculate(partialInputs, partialOutputs, ...)` with phantoms still present), 354-364 (shieldedFee added on top), 428-433 (phantoms removed only after); /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/utils/fee.ts:43-89 (counts all non-authority elements with a token uniformly; flat input rule at 85-87). Core: /tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor/verification/transaction_verifier.py:407-430 — verify_token_rules for shielded txs: `if expected_fee != token_dict.fees_from_fee_header: raise InputOutputMismatch`; token_dict excludes shielded components (/tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor/transaction/transaction.py:471 'shielded inputs contribute nothing to token_dict'; shielded outputs are header-resident, never in tx.outputs); calculate_fee semantics at /tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor/transaction/token_info.py:61-92; shielded per-output fee constants hathor/conf/settings.py:36-37.
findings.recommendation: Compute the FEE-token portion of the FeeHeader from core's view: run Fee.calculate AFTER removing phantom outputs (or pass outputs with phantoms excluded) and exclude shielded inputs from chargeable-input counting, then add the flat chargeable-inputs rule exactly as token_info.calculate_fee does; keep the separate shieldedFee term. Add a FEE-version token case to the shielded send/unshield integration tests to pin equality with the node.

result.notes: CONFIRMED on both sides. Wallet: tokens.ts:478-491/629/682-701 fund exactly depositAmount+feeAmount with bestUtxoSelection (utxo.ts:102-113 includes shielded UTXOs by design; tokens.ts has zero shielded handling); fee.ts:84-92 counts shielded token inputs as chargeable. Pipeline wiring verified: prepareTransaction (transaction.ts:1348-1420) detects shielded inputs and sets excessBlindingFactor, _attachShieldedHeaders (transaction.ts:993-1080) auto-attaches MintHeader/MeltHeader, so the contaminated tx reaches core's shielded branch. Core: transaction.py:191-193 is_shielded() true on shielded inputs alone; verification_service.py:339 dispatches to verify_shielded_balance; transaction_verifier.py:1100-1110 _fold_mint_melt_entry FEE branch appends (FEE_PER_OUTPUT, HTR) to transparent_outputs per entry for both mint and melt (pinned by hathor_tests/tx/test_shielded_mint_melt.py:971-1038, two entries => two charges); the excess scalar only closes the G component, so the unfunded fold makes the H_HTR component short by FEE_PER_OUTPUT x entries -> ShieldedBalanceMismatchError. Second divergence also confirmed: transaction.py:428-431 skips shielded inputs (chargeable_inputs=0) while wallet declares FEE_PER_OUTPUT for all-shielded melts; verify_token_rules runs the fee exactness check on the shielded branch too (transaction_verifier.py:425-430) -> InputOutputMismatch. Coverage gap confirmed: grep TokenVersion.FEE over __tests__/integration/shielded_outputs/ is empty; mint_melt_shielded.test.ts and token_creation.test.ts use default DEPOSIT tokens. One refinement: the finding's phrase 'never funds nor declares this fold' overstates slightly — the wallet DOES auto-declare the MintHeader/MeltHeader; what it never funds is the per-entry FEE_PER_OUTPUT charge (and its FeeHeader can't carry it without breaking the exactness check, exactly as the recommendation notes). Severity high is right: deterministic post-PoW rejection of all FEE-version token admin ops for any wallet holding shielded HTR/FEE-token UTXOs, no opt-out in the token-admin selection path; no fund loss.

Verified on both sides. Wallet: bestUtxoSelection intentionally selects shielded UTXOs (src/utils/utxo.ts:102-113); prepareMintTxData funds only depositAmount+feeAmount (src/utils/tokens.ts:478-491, FeeHeader at 511-513); prepareMeltTxData funds depositAmount+Fee.calculate (tokens.ts:629, 682-691) where Fee.calculate counts shielded IDataInputs as chargeable via hasInputs (src/utils/fee.ts:81-89). The contaminated tx provably becomes shielded: transactionUtils.prepareTransaction (called from wallet.ts:2510/2624) detects shielded inputs and attaches UnshieldBalanceHeader (src/utils/transaction.ts:1338-1422), then _attachShieldedHeaders auto-declares MintHeader/MeltHeader from token deltas (transaction.ts:993-1076); the excess computation folds only transparent outputs + FeeHeader entries (transaction.ts:1392-1412), never the FEE-version per-entry term. Core (source of truth): is_shielded() is true for shielded-input txs (transaction.py:191-193), dispatching verify_shielded_balance (verification_service.py:333-344). _fold_mint_melt_entry FEE branch appends (FEE_PER_OUTPUT, HTR) to transparent_outputs per entry for BOTH mint and melt (transaction_verifier.py, fold fn ~1095-1110), pinned by hathor_tests/tx/test_shielded_mint_melt.py::test_fee_version_mint_charges_fee_per_output_on_outputs and ::test_fee_version_charges_per_entry_not_per_amount; FeeHeader entries also land on the output side (~987-990). Wallet never funds the fold term, so the HTR value equation is short by FEE_PER_OUTPUT x entries -> ShieldedBalanceMismatchError after PoW. Second divergence confirmed: _get_token_info_from_inputs skips shielded inputs ('Shielded input: skip for token info', transaction.py ~428-432) so chargeable_inputs=0 for fully-shielded melts; calculate_fee (token_info.py:83-90) then expects 0 while the wallet declares FEE_PER_OUTPUT -> InputOutputMismatch at verify_token_rules (transaction_verifier.py:427-431). DEPOSIT flows balance (fold mirrors wallet's implicit deposit/withdraw), matching the passing S-series integration tests. Coverage gap confirmed: grep TokenVersion.FEE over __tests__/integration/shielded_outputs/ is empty; FEE-token tests live only in transparent suites where contamination cannot occur. Impact: deterministic rejection of mint/melt/createToken for FEE-version tokens whenever any shielded UTXO is selected for funding — realistic for any wallet holding shielded HTR, since selection is indiscriminate by design. No fund loss/inflation, so not critical; high is the right severity for a reproducible functional break of an entire operation class on this branch.

CONFIRMED on every element. (1) Wallet heuristic computes an all-sides delta: /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/utils/transaction.ts:1029-1047 — transparent outputs add (1029-1034), shielded outputs add (1035-1038), all non-authority inputs subtract including decoded shielded (1046); headers emitted only on nonzero all-sides delta at 1058-1068. The design comment at 985-987 says "transparent delta" but the code computes all-sides — and no transparent-only cross-check exists anywhere in the worktree. (2) Core check is transparent-only: /tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor/verification/verification_service.py:339-341 calls verify_no_undeclared_mint_melt for every shielded tx; token_dict skips shielded inputs (hathor/transaction/transaction.py:429-431) and shielded outputs are header-borne (never in tx.outputs); transaction_verifier.py:599-611 raises ShieldedMintMeltForbiddenError when can_melt && has_been_melted (token_info.py:39-44, amount<0) without a header entry. (3) Divergence scenario (transparent 100 X in -> shielded 100 X out + melt-authority X input): wallet delta = 0 -> no header; core transparent amount = -100, can_melt=true -> rejected. (4) No-escape claim verified: entry amount >= 1 enforced at deserialize-time (transaction_verifier.py:637-638), and _fold_mint_melt_entry (transaction_verifier.py:1083-1085) appends (amount, X) to the transparent output side of verify_balance, which the shielded output already balances — any declared amount breaks the equation. Refusing the input set is the only correct behavior; the wallet has no such guard. (5) Reachability as stated: high-level send blocks authority inputs (sendTransaction.ts:1287-1295), but prepareTransaction (transaction.ts:1327) is public and the wallet's mint/melt flows (wallet.ts:2310/2510/2624) feed authority inputs through this heuristic. Severity adjusted high -> medium: the result is a node-rejected tx (wasted signing/PoW, no fund loss, recoverable by removing the authority input), and triggering it requires a low-level/custom-txData caller combining an authority input with a supply-preserving shielded move — the wallet's own flows never produce that combination (mint/melt flows always have nonzero same-direction deltas; send flow rejects authority inputs).

VERIFIED. (1) Wallet heuristic at /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/utils/transaction.ts:1029-1047 computes per-token delta over BOTH sides: shielded outputs added at 1035-1038, all non-authority inputs (incl. decoded shielded) subtracted at 1039-1047; headers emitted only on nonzero all-sides delta at 1056-1068. No transparent-only cross-check exists in _attachShieldedHeaders (904-1077) or anywhere else (grepped src/shielded/, src/schemas.ts, src/headers/). (2) Core diverges exactly as claimed: /tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor/verification/transaction_verifier.py:599-611 raises ShieldedMintMeltForbiddenError on transparent-only deficit/surplus (token_info.has_been_melted() == amount<0, token_info.py:39-44) whenever the authority is spent; token_dict skips shielded inputs (transaction.py:429-431) and counts only transparent self.outputs (transaction.py:466-494); check runs for every is_shielded() tx (verification_service.py:339-341). (3) Scenario confirmed: transparent 100 X in -> FullShielded 100 X out + melt-authority X input gives wallet delta=0 (no header) while core sees amount=-100, can_melt=True -> rejection. (4) "Unfixable" confirmed: _fold_mint_melt_entry (transaction_verifier.py:1083-1085) adds the declared amount as a public H_X term to the output side of verify_balance (1048-1058); the shielded commitment already carries 100*H_X there and blinding only adjusts the G component, so no declared amount balances — the only consensus-valid construction actually melts (shield 0), violating intent. Refusal pre-signing is the only correct wallet behavior. (5) Reachability as stated: high-level flow blocked at sendTransaction.ts:1287-1295 (authority inputs rejected), so trigger requires public low-level prepareTransaction (transaction.ts:1327) with custom txData (headless custom-tx path). SEVERITY ADJUSTED high->medium: tx is node-rejected post-PoW with no fund loss, no privacy leak, no consensus risk; no high-level wallet flow (mint/melt/send) can produce the divergent combination (their deltas are always nonzero same-sign and are served correctly by the heuristic). Impact is wasted PoW + confusing rejection in a niche low-level construction; fix is the recommended transparent-delta agreement guard with a descriptive error.

All cited code verified. (1) sendTransaction.ts:597 excludes authority outputs from tokensWithVisibleOutput; tokens[] filtered at :613. (2) utils/transaction.ts:790-794 has no indexOf===-1 guard; a dropped token yields token_data 0|0x80=0x80, reaching the wire via createTransactionFromData (transaction.ts:854). (3) Core rejects: token index 0 = HTR, and hathor-core transaction.py:483-486 raises InvalidToken ('output has mint authority, but no input has it') since HTR authority inputs cannot exist; transaction_verifier.py:447-450 asserts NATIVE has no mint/melt. (4) Privacy claim confirmed: transaction_verifier.py:487-489 counts authority outputs in seen_token_indexes, so listing the token is consensus-required and its wire token_data names the token anyway — the exclusion buys nothing. SEVERITY ADJUSTED medium→low: the trigger is unreachable through ANY current path, not just unlikely. SendTransaction.prepareTxData hardcodes authorities:0n on every output it builds (sendTransaction.ts:237,252,275) and ISendOutput (sendTransaction.ts:96) has no authority variant, making line 597 dead code in its own flow. The public APIs the finding cites as exposure (prepareTransaction/createTransactionFromData, transaction.ts:1425/841) bypass the filter entirely — callers supply tokens[] themselves — so they cannot trip it. Authority flows (delegate/mint/melt) build txData via tokenUtils.prepare*TxData and skip prepareTxData (wallet.ts:2720-2728, 2749-2756). Only a future modification of prepareTxData itself could expose the bug. Still real as a latent correctness defect with a sound fix (delete the :597 exclusion; throw on indexOf -1 in getTokenDataFromOutput), but it is defensive hardening, not a live bug.

CONFIRMED. Every element of the finding checks out against the code.

1. Quoted code exists verbatim: /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/utils/transaction.ts:1359-1370 — the prepareTransaction fallback loop does `if (!utxo) continue;` (1361) and `if (!utxo.blindingFactor) continue;` (1363), silently dropping shielded inputs from the excess scalar sum.

2. Asymmetry with SendTransaction confirmed: /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/new/sendTransaction.ts:471-477 throws SendTxError ('Shielded input ... is missing blindingFactor — cannot satisfy the homomorphic balance equation') for the identical condition. One nuance: the missing-UTXO half is actually symmetric — SendTransaction also silently skips when `getUtxo` returns undefined (`utxo?.shielded` falsy at sendTransaction.ts:471), so the hard-error asymmetry applies specifically to the missing-blindingFactor case. This does not refute the finding; it slightly narrows it.

3. The degraded state is reachable, not hypothetical: `blindingFactor?` is optional on both IUtxo (types.ts:473) and IShieldedOutputEntry (types.ts:352, 'populated after decryption'); the code's own comment at /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/utils/storage.ts:673-681 documents that bare shielded UTXO records missing `shielded: true`/`blindingFactor` have actually occurred (metadata-update overwrite — fixed at that one write site only) and explicitly names the downstream symptom ('fullnode rejects with "full-unshield tx … must carry an unshield balance header"').

4. The vulnerable flows can select such UTXOs: bestUtxoSelection (/tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/utils/utxo.ts:102-114) deliberately includes shielded UTXOs with no blindingFactor-presence check, and createToken/mint/melt all route through transactionUtils.prepareTransaction, not SendTransaction.prepareTxData (/tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/new/wallet.ts:2293→2310, 2504→2510, 2616→2624). I found no other validation layer (zod schema, caller check) that catches a shielded input with missing blindingFactor on these paths.

5. Core consequence confirmed: /tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor/verification/transaction_verifier.py:1037-1041 rejects a full-unshield tx without the header, and 1047-1059 raises ShieldedBalanceMismatchError when the excess does not close the equation — both post-signing/post-mining remote rejections.

6. Downstream compounding confirmed: /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/utils/transaction.ts:993-996 keys isShieldedTx on shieldedOutputs/excessBlindingFactor only, so when all shielded inputs are skipped (no excess set), the mandatory Mint/Melt declaration block is also bypassed. Minor caveat: in that all-skipped case the tx is rejected for the missing unshield header regardless (transaction_verifier.py:1037-1041), so the compounding adds redundant rule violations rather than a distinct new failure mode — the practical effect is the same single rejection.

Severity assessment: medium is correct. No fund loss and no invalid tx accepted by consensus; impact is a confusing remote ShieldedBalanceMismatchError after wasted signing/mining work instead of a clear local error, and it only triggers from an already-degraded storage state. The recommendation (mirror SendTransaction's throw) is sound.

CONFIRMED on both sides. Core: _fold_mint_melt_entry adds (FEE_PER_OUTPUT, HTR) to transparent_outputs for BOTH mint and melt on FEE-version tokens (/tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor/verification/transaction_verifier.py:1096-1104; commit 040674bc verified in git log); fold runs per header entry for every shielded tx (verifier:998-1019); the term is a value on H_HTR so the excess scalar (on G) cannot absorb it. FeeHeader exactness check at verifier:427-430 uses token_dict that skips shielded inputs entirely (transaction.py:430-431) and counts chargeable_inputs/outputs only from transparent slots (transaction.py:446-447, 496-497); get_complete_token_info (transaction.py:338-350) never folds mint/melt headers, so the per-entry charge is NOT in expected_fee — it must come from extra HTR input value, which the wallet never supplies. Wallet: _attachShieldedHeaders heuristic is token-version-blind (/tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/utils/transaction.ts:993-1075, no TokenVersion reference); HTR funding = depositAmount + feeAmount only (src/utils/tokens.ts:478-491 mint, 688-701 melt; FEE tokens have depositAmount=0); excess computation includes only real outputs + FeeHeader entries (src/utils/transaction.ts:1388-1418); grep for FEE_PER_OUTPUT across src/utils/transaction.ts and tokens.ts shows zero handling tied to mint/melt header entries, and grep for TokenVersion.FEE shows no guard refusing FEE tokens in shielded flows. Divergence (b) confirmed for melt: Fee.calculate (src/utils/fee.ts:43-90) counts shielded-origin IDataInputs (they carry token+value from wallet storage), flat FEE_PER_OUTPUT charge at fee.ts:85-87 when inputs exist with zero outputs, while core sees zero chargeable -> expects 0 -> InputOutputMismatch. (Nuance: (b) does not bite transparent FEE mints since the minted output is visible to core, matching the finding's own melt example; (a) independently dooms both mint and melt.) Shielded UTXOs freely selected: src/utils/utxo.ts:56-62 and 108-113 set no shielded filter (deliberate per comment at 102-107); src/storage/memory_store.ts:825-826 includes shielded when option undefined; used at tokens.ts:480/629/691. Test gap confirmed: __tests__/integration/shielded_outputs/mint_melt_shielded.test.ts creates tokens with default (DEPOSIT) version, zero TokenVersion/FEE references. Net effect as claimed: FEE-version mint/melt with any shielded input builds+signs+PoW-mines a deterministically node-rejected tx with no upfront error. Severity high is appropriate (silent guaranteed failure of a supported operation with wasted PoW; no fund loss).

CONFIRMED on every load-bearing claim; attempted refutations all failed.

Wallet side (verified): /tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/utils/transaction.ts:1029-1047 — the delta sums transparent outputs (1029-1034), shielded outputs (1035-1038, `bumpDelta(so.token, so.value)`), and subtracts non-authority inputs including decoded shielded inputs (1039-1046). Headers are emitted only when this ALL-SIDES delta is nonzero with matching authority (1056-1068). The in-code rationale (1021-1028) reasons only about the balance equation, never about core's transparent-only presence check. No compensating guard exists anywhere: grep for authority handling in src/utils/transaction.ts and src/shielded/*.ts found none; the only block is checkUnspentInput at src/new/sendTransaction.ts:1287-1295 ("XXX: We are NOT enabling authority outputs for now"), which gates only the high-level send flow. `prepareTransaction` (transaction.ts:1327) is public and calls `_attachShieldedHeaders` at 883/888.

Core side (verified, source of truth): (a) verify_no_undeclared_mint_melt runs for EVERY shielded tx — /tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor/verification/verification_service.py:339-341 — and raises ShieldedMintMeltForbiddenError when `can_melt and has_been_melted() and token_uid not in melt_token_uids` (transaction_verifier.py:599-611); has_been_melted is simply amount<0 (hathor/transaction/token_info.py:39-44). (b) token_dict is transparent-only: shielded inputs are explicitly skipped (hathor/transaction/transaction.py:420-431), and shielded outputs live in the ShieldedOutputsHeader (transaction.py:200-203), not in self.outputs, so neither contributes. So "transparent 100 X in -> FullShielded 100 X out + melt-authority-of-X input" yields wallet delta=0 (no header) but core token_dict[X].amount=-100 with can_melt=true -> rejection. The symmetric unshield direction (mint authority riding, shielded in -> transparent out) is equally doomed.

"No valid construction" claim (verified — this was my main refutation attempt): a zero-amount MeltHeader entry would have satisfied the presence check without disturbing the balance, but core enforces amount >= 1 at deserialize time (/tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathorlib/hathorlib/headers/mint_melt_header.py:57 and 112-114), and any amount >= 1 is folded onto the OUTPUT side of the balance equation (transaction_verifier.py:1084-1085 `primary_side.append((entry.amount, token_uid))`), adding amount*H_X that no blinding factor can cancel (blindings only span G) given the shielded output already commits the full transparent input. So for that exact input/output set, no header declaration passes both verify_no_undeclared_mint_melt and verify_shielded_balance — the only correct wallet behavior is to refuse upfront, which it does not.

Severity adjustment to medium: the failure is a consensus-rejected tx after signing + PoW (wasted work, opaque ShieldedMintMeltForbiddenError), not fund loss (rejected txs consume no UTXOs), and reachability requires the low-level `prepareTransaction` API with a custom txData that combines an authority input of token X with a supply-preserving (or opposite-sign) shielding/unshielding move of the SAME token X — none of the wallet's own mint/melt/delegate flows produce this combination (their all-sides deltas are correctly signed, verified: mint-to-shielded and melt-from-shielded both fold consistently on core's side). Still a real correctness gap in a public API whose documented purpose is exactly mixed authority+shielded txs; the recommended transparent-only-delta cross-check mirroring core's token_dict is the right fix.

Finding verified on both sides; no refuting code exists. CORE: _fold_mint_melt_entry adds FEE_PER_OUTPUT HTR on the output side per Mint/Melt entry for FEE-version tokens (/tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor/verification/transaction_verifier.py:1096-1104; commit 040674bc confirmed in git log), on top of FeeHeader entries already folded as outputs (987-990); the resulting H_HTR value imbalance cannot be absorbed by the excess scalar (G-component only, 938-943) -> ShieldedBalanceMismatchError (1049-1058). FeeHeader exactness for shielded txs: expected_fee != fees_from_fee_header -> InputOutputMismatch (407-430, called from verification_service.py:339-343), with token_dict transparent-only (shielded inputs skipped at /tmp/review-hathor-core-shielded/hathor/transaction/transaction.py:430-431; chargeable_inputs only for transparent inputs at 446-447). WALLET: mint funds only depositAmount+feeAmount (/tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/utils/tokens.ts:478-491), melt likewise (688-695); grep shows zero FEE_PER_OUTPUT handling tied to mint/melt header entries anywhere in src/; _attachShieldedHeaders declares entries version-blind (/tmp/review-wallet-lib-shielded/src/utils/transaction.ts:1049-1075) and excess covers only outputs+FeeHeader (1388-1418). Fee.calculate counts shielded IDataInputs uniformly (src/utils/fee.ts:43-90, flat charge 85-87; getDataInputFromUtxo at src/utils/helpers.ts:619-628 has no shielded marker), so FS-held FEE-token melt with no token change produces header=FEE_PER_OUTPUT vs core expected 0. Shielded UTXOs deliberately selectable (src/utils/utxo.ts:102-113 comment, no shielded filter at 56-62/108-113; src/storage/memory_store.ts:825-826 undefined->include); tokens.ts has zero 'shielded' mentions (no guard). Flow reaches the fallback: mintTokens/meltTokens -> prepareTransaction (src/new/wallet.ts:2504-2510, 2616-2624) -> _attachShieldedHeaders (transaction.ts:883/888). Tests: no FEE-version coverage in __tests__/integration/shielded_outputs/ (grep empty; default token version is DEPOSIT per tokens.ts:232). Minor refinement: divergence (b) only bites when there is no transparent change output of the FEE token (with transparent change the header happens to match), but divergence (a) — the unfunded fold — rejects EVERY FEE-version mint/melt that includes any shielded input, so the tx is always node-rejected after sign+PoW. Severity high is correct: no fund loss (tx rejected), but silent end-to-end failure with no upfront refusal and automatic shielded UTXO selection makes it realistic.
